User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to receive a welcome email with a Set Password link when my profile is created.
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to receive an automated welcome email from the Nemi Care platform immediately after the administrator creates their profile in the system. The welcome email contains a secure, one-time "Set Password" link that serves as the Provider's first point of entry into the platform. When the Provider clicks this link, they are redirected to the "Set Your Password" screen — a split-layout page featuring the Nemi Care logo and a care facility background image on the left, with a centered form on the right containing "New Password" and "Confirm Password" input fields and a blue "Set Password" button. This is essential because Providers do not self-register; their accounts are provisioned by the admin, and the welcome email is the only mechanism for them to activate their account and gain access to the Facility Portal. Without receiving this email, the Provider cannot log in, access resident data, or perform any clinical or administrative functions in the system.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When a new provider profile is created by the admin, Then the system must automatically send a welcome email with a "Set Password" link to the provider's registered email address.

2. If the Provider clicks the "Set Password" link and the link is still valid, Then the system must redirect them to the Set Password screen where they can create a new password.
   Else if the link has expired, Then the system must display an expiry error message with an option to request a new link.
   Else if the link has already been used, Then the system must display a message that the link is no longer valid.

3. If the provider profile has a valid email address, Then the welcome email must be delivered within 5 minutes of profile creation.
   Else if the email is missing or invalid, Then no email should be sent, and the admin should be notified of the delivery failure.

4. If the provider profile creation fails or is cancelled before saving, Then no welcome email should be triggered.

5. If the same email address is associated with multiple provider profiles, Then each profile creation must trigger its own independent welcome email with a unique Set Password link.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that when an administrator creates a new provider profile with a valid email (e.g., provider@clinic.com), a welcome email is delivered to that email address.
2. Verify the welcome email contains the Nemi Care logo, a personalized greeting using the provider's name, and a clearly visible "Set Password" button/link.
3. Verify that clicking the "Set Password" link in the welcome email opens the browser and redirects the Provider to the Nemi Care Set Password screen.
4. Verify the Set Password screen displays correctly with the split layout — care facility background image on the left, form panel with Nemi Care logo, "New Password" field, "Confirm Password" field, and blue "Set Password" button on the right.
5. Verify the Provider can successfully enter a new password, confirm it, click "Set Password", and get redirected to the Login screen with a success message.
6. Verify that after setting the password, the Provider can log in using their email and the newly created password.

Negative:
7. Verify that clicking the Set Password link after it has expired (e.g., after 24 hours) shows an error message like "This link has expired" with a "Resend Link" option.
8. Verify that clicking the Set Password link a second time (after already setting a password) shows a message like "This link has already been used."
9. Verify that no welcome email is sent if the admin creates a provider profile without an email address.
10. Verify that no welcome email is sent if the admin enters an invalid email format (e.g., "provider@", "provider.com").
11. Verify that no welcome email is triggered if the admin cancels or fails to save the provider profile.
12. Verify that entering mismatched passwords on the Set Password screen shows a "Passwords do not match" error and does not submit.
13. Verify that submitting the Set Password form with both fields empty shows appropriate required field errors.
14. Verify that entering a password that does not meet complexity requirements (e.g., too short, missing uppercase/number/special character) shows specific validation errors.

UI Scenarios:
15. Verify the welcome email renders correctly across major email clients (Gmail, Outlook, Apple Mail) with proper branding and formatting.
16. Verify the Set Password screen has the correct split layout: background image on the left half, form panel centered on the right half.
17. Verify the Nemi Care logo is displayed at the top of the form panel on the Set Password screen.
18. Verify both "New Password" and "Confirm Password" fields have visible labels, placeholder text, and show/hide visibility toggle icons.
19. Verify the "Set Password" button is displayed in the brand blue color, spans the full width of the form, and has white text.
20. Verify the password fields mask input by default (dots/asterisks) and the visibility toggle reveals/hides the text.
21. Verify the Set Password screen is responsive and displays correctly on tablet and mobile screen sizes.

Edge Cases:
22. Verify behavior when the same email address is used for two different provider profiles — each should receive its own independent welcome email with a unique link.
23. Verify the Set Password link behavior at exactly the expiry boundary (e.g., clicking at exactly 24 hours).
24. Verify behavior when the email delivery is delayed by the mail server and the Provider clicks the link close to expiry time.
25. Verify behavior when the Provider clicks the Set Password link on a different device or browser than where the email was received.
26. Verify behavior when the Provider's email inbox is full — does the system retry or notify the admin?

Security:
27. Verify the Set Password link uses a cryptographically secure, random token that cannot be guessed, predicted, or brute-forced.
28. Verify the Set Password link URL uses HTTPS protocol.
29. Verify the link token is permanently invalidated on the server after the password is successfully set.
30. Verify the link token is not exposed in server logs, browser history, or referrer headers in a way that could be exploited.
31. Verify that manipulating/tampering with the token in the URL does not grant access to the Set Password screen.
32. Verify that the password entered on the Set Password screen is transmitted over HTTPS.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the welcome email is delivered within 5 minutes of the admin saving the provider profile.
2. Verify the Set Password screen loads fully within 3 seconds after clicking the link.

User Story: As a Provider, on my first login I should be able to set password
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to establish their account password during their very first interaction with the Nemi Care platform. After clicking the Set Password link from the welcome email, the Provider is taken to the "Set Your Password" screen — a split-layout page with a care facility background image on the left side and a centered form panel on the right displaying the Nemi Care logo, the heading "Set your password", two password input fields ("New Password" and "Confirm Password") each with a show/hide visibility toggle icon, and a full-width blue "Set Password" button at the bottom. The password must meet the platform's security requirements (minimum 8 characters, at least one uppercase, one lowercase, one number, and one special character). Once the password is successfully set, the Provider's account becomes activated and they are redirected to the Login screen with a success confirmation message. This is the mandatory first step before the Provider can ever log in to the platform — without completing this step, the Provider's account remains in an inactive state and they cannot access any Facility Portal functionality including resident management, charting, or scheduling.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider arrives on the Set Password screen via the welcome email link, Then the system must display a form with "New Password" and "Confirm Password" fields along with a "Set Password" button.

2. If the Provider enters a valid password that meets all complexity requirements in both fields and the passwords match, Then the system must save the password, activate the account, and redirect the Provider to the Login screen with a success message.
   Else if the passwords do not match, Then the system must display "Passwords do not match" error and not submit the form.
   Else if the password does not meet complexity requirements, Then the system must display specific error messages for each unmet requirement.

3. If the Provider clicks "Set Password" without entering anything, Then the system must show required field errors for both "New Password" and "Confirm Password" and not submit the form.

4. If the Provider fills only "New Password" and leaves "Confirm Password" empty, Then the system must show a required field error for "Confirm Password" and not submit.
   Else if the Provider fills only "Confirm Password" and leaves "New Password" empty, Then the system must show a required field error for "New Password" and not submit.

5. After the password is successfully set, Then the Provider must be able to use the new password along with their email to log in through the Login screen.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify the Set Password screen loads correctly when the Provider clicks a valid Set Password link — showing the Nemi Care logo, "Set your password" heading, "New Password" field, "Confirm Password" field, and blue "Set Password" button.
2. Verify the Provider can enter a valid password (e.g., "SecurePass@123") in "New Password", enter the same password in "Confirm Password", click "Set Password", and get redirected to the Login screen.
3. Verify a success message (e.g., "Password set successfully") is displayed on the Login screen after successful password setup.
4. Verify the Provider's account is activated after setting the password — they can now log in with their email and new password.
5. Verify the Provider can use the show/hide visibility toggle on the "New Password" field to reveal and re-hide the entered password.
6. Verify the Provider can use the show/hide visibility toggle on the "Confirm Password" field independently from the "New Password" toggle.

Negative:
7. Verify that entering "SecurePass@123" in "New Password" and "DifferentPass@456" in "Confirm Password" shows a "Passwords do not match" error.
8. Verify that entering a 7-character password (e.g., "Pass@12") shows an error "Password must be at least 8 characters".
9. Verify that entering a password with no uppercase letter (e.g., "securepass@123") shows an error indicating an uppercase letter is required.
10. Verify that entering a password with no lowercase letter (e.g., "SECUREPASS@123") shows an error indicating a lowercase letter is required.
11. Verify that entering a password with no number (e.g., "SecurePass@abc") shows an error indicating a number is required.
12. Verify that entering a password with no special character (e.g., "SecurePass123") shows an error indicating a special character is required.
13. Verify that clicking "Set Password" with both fields completely empty shows "Password is required" for "New Password" and "Please confirm your password" for "Confirm Password".
14. Verify that entering a password only in "New Password" and leaving "Confirm Password" empty shows a required field error for "Confirm Password".
15. Verify that entering a password only in "Confirm Password" and leaving "New Password" empty shows a required field error for "New Password".
16. Verify that entering only spaces in the password fields is treated as invalid input.

UI Scenarios:
17. Verify the Set Password screen has the correct split layout — care facility background image on the left, form panel on the right.
18. Verify the Nemi Care logo is displayed at the top of the form panel.
19. Verify the heading "Set your password" is displayed above the form fields.
20. Verify both password fields have visible labels and placeholder text.
21. Verify both password fields have independent show/hide visibility toggle icons on the right side.
22. Verify the "Set Password" button is displayed in the brand blue color, spans the full width of the form, with white text.
23. Verify password fields mask input by default (shown as dots/asterisks).
24. Verify error messages appear inline below the respective fields in red text.
25. Verify the screen is responsive and displays correctly on tablet and mobile devices.

Edge Cases:
26. Verify behavior when entering the maximum allowed password length (e.g., 128 characters) — should be accepted if all requirements are met.
27. Verify behavior when entering a password consisting of only special characters (e.g., "@#$%^&*!") — should fail because it lacks uppercase, lowercase, and number.
28. Verify copy-paste functionality works in both "New Password" and "Confirm Password" fields.
29. Verify behavior when the browser auto-fills the password fields with a saved password.
30. Verify behavior when the password contains leading or trailing spaces — are spaces included or trimmed?
31. Verify behavior when the Provider refreshes the page during password entry — does the form reset?
32. Verify behavior when the Provider navigates away and returns using the browser Back button.

Security:
33. Verify the password is transmitted to the server over HTTPS and never in plaintext.
34. Verify the password is not stored or exposed in browser local storage, session storage, cookies, or the URL.
35. Verify the password is hashed (not stored in plaintext) on the server side.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Set Password screen loads fully within 3 seconds.
2. Verify the password save operation (click "Set Password" to redirect to Login) completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, after entering my credentials I should receive a One-Time Password (OTP) on my registered email
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to receive a One-Time Password (OTP) on their registered email address as part of a two-factor authentication (2FA) process after entering valid login credentials. When the Provider enters their email and password on the "Sign in to your account" screen and clicks the blue "Sign In" button, the system validates the credentials and, if correct, sends a 6-digit numeric OTP to the registered email. The Provider is then redirected to the OTP verification screen — a split-layout page with the care facility background image on the left and a centered form on the right showing the Nemi Care logo, the heading "Enter OTP", an informational message indicating the OTP was sent to the email, six separate single-digit input boxes for entering the OTP code, a blue "Verify" button, and a "Resend OTP" link below. Upon entering the correct 6-digit OTP and clicking "Verify", the Provider is authenticated and redirected to the Nemi Care Dashboard. This two-factor authentication step is critical for healthcare platform security, ensuring that even if credentials are compromised, unauthorized access is prevented without access to the Provider's email.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider enters valid credentials and clicks "Sign In", Then the system must send a 6-digit numeric OTP to the Provider's registered email and redirect them to the OTP verification screen.

2. If the Provider enters the correct 6-digit OTP and clicks "Verify", Then the system must authenticate them and redirect to the Nemi Care Dashboard.
   Else if the Provider enters an incorrect OTP, Then the system must display "Invalid OTP. Please try again." and allow retry.
   Else if the OTP has expired, Then the system must display "OTP has expired. Please request a new one." and highlight the "Resend OTP" link.

3. If fewer than 6 digits have been entered, Then the "Verify" button must remain disabled and the form must not be submittable.

4. When the Provider types a digit in any OTP box, Then the cursor must automatically advance to the next box.
   When the Provider presses Backspace, Then the current box must be cleared and the cursor must move back to the previous box.

5. If the Provider enters a non-numeric character (letter, symbol, space) in any OTP box, Then the input must be rejected and the box must remain empty.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that after entering valid email "provider@clinic.com" and correct password and clicking "Sign In", a 6-digit OTP is sent to "provider@clinic.com".
2. Verify the Provider is redirected to the OTP verification screen after successful credential validation.
3. Verify the OTP screen displays an informational message like "We've sent a verification code to pro***@clinic.com" with a masked email.
4. Verify entering the correct 6-digit OTP (e.g., "483927") and clicking "Verify" authenticates the Provider and redirects to the Dashboard.
5. Verify auto-focus: after entering "4" in the first box, the cursor automatically moves to the second box; after entering "8", it moves to the third, and so on through all 6 boxes.
6. Verify the "Verify" button becomes enabled only after all 6 digit boxes are filled.
7. Verify the OTP screen shows the Nemi Care logo, "Enter OTP" heading, 6 digit boxes, blue "Verify" button, and "Resend OTP" link.

Negative:
8. Verify entering an incorrect OTP (e.g., "000000" when the actual OTP is "483927") shows "Invalid OTP. Please try again." error.
9. Verify entering an expired OTP (waited more than 5 minutes) shows "OTP has expired. Please request a new one." error.
10. Verify the "Verify" button is disabled when only 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5 digits are entered (not all 6).
11. Verify entering a letter (e.g., "a") in any OTP box is rejected — the box stays empty.
12. Verify entering a special character (e.g., "@") in any OTP box is rejected.
13. Verify entering a space in any OTP box is rejected.
14. Verify that after 5 consecutive incorrect OTP attempts, the Provider sees an account lockout or rate-limit message.
15. Verify that entering the OTP from a previous login session (old OTP) is rejected as invalid.

UI Scenarios:
16. Verify the OTP screen has the correct split layout — care facility background image on left, form panel on right.
17. Verify the Nemi Care logo is displayed at the top of the form panel.
18. Verify the heading "Enter OTP" is prominently displayed.
19. Verify the informational message about email delivery is displayed below the heading.
20. Verify the 6 OTP digit boxes are displayed in a horizontal row, evenly spaced, and visually distinct.
21. Verify the blue "Verify" button is displayed below the OTP boxes, spans the full width of the form, with white text.
22. Verify the "Resend OTP" link is visible and clickable below the "Verify" button.
23. Verify the digit boxes have a visible border/outline that highlights on focus.

Edge Cases:
24. Verify pasting a 6-digit OTP from the clipboard (e.g., Ctrl+V "483927") auto-fills all 6 boxes correctly.
25. Verify pressing Backspace in the 3rd box clears the 3rd digit and moves cursor back to the 2nd box.
26. Verify pressing Backspace in the 1st box (already empty) does nothing — no error or crash.
27. Verify OTP expiry behavior at exactly the 5-minute boundary (e.g., entering OTP at 4:59 vs 5:01).
28. Verify behavior when the Provider leaves the OTP screen idle for 10 minutes and then enters the OTP.

Security:
29. Verify the OTP is NOT visible in the email subject line — only in the email body.
30. Verify the OTP expires after exactly 5 minutes and cannot be used after expiry.
31. Verify the OTP cannot be reused after successful verification (one-time use).
32. Verify the system locks the account or enforces a cooldown after 5 consecutive incorrect OTP attempts.
33. Verify the OTP is transmitted and verified over HTTPS.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the OTP email is delivered to the Provider's inbox within 30 seconds of clicking "Sign In".
2. Verify the OTP verification response (click "Verify" to Dashboard redirect) completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to login using valid credentials.
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to securely log in to the Nemi Care Facility Portal using their registered email address and password. The Login screen is the primary entry point to the platform, displayed as a split-layout page with a care facility background image showing elderly residents and staff on the left side, and a centered login form panel on the right. The form panel displays the Nemi Care logo at the top, the heading "Sign in to your account", an "Email" input field with placeholder text, a "Password" input field with a show/hide visibility toggle icon, a "Forgot Password?" link positioned below the password field, and a full-width blue "Sign In" button. When the Provider enters their registered email and correct password and clicks "Sign In", the system validates the credentials against the database. If valid, the Provider is directed to the OTP verification step (two-factor authentication) before gaining access to the Dashboard. If invalid, an error message is displayed without revealing whether the email or password was incorrect, as a security best practice. This login flow is the gateway to all Facility Portal functionality — resident management, clinical charting, scheduling, lead management, billing, and compliance — and must be secure, performant, and accessible to all authorized healthcare providers.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider enters a valid registered email and correct password and clicks "Sign In", Then the system must validate the credentials and redirect to the OTP verification screen.

2. If the email or password is incorrect, Then the system must display a generic error "Invalid email or password" (not specifying which field is wrong).
   Else if the account is locked, Then the system must display "Your account has been locked. Please contact your administrator."
   Else if the account is inactive/deactivated, Then the system must display "Your account is not active. Please contact your administrator."

3. If the Provider clicks "Sign In" with both Email and Password fields empty, Then the system must show required field errors for both fields and not submit.
   Else if only Email is empty, Then the system must show "Email is required."
   Else if only Password is empty, Then the system must show "Password is required."

4. If the Provider enters an email in an invalid format, Then the system must show "Please enter a valid email address." and not submit.

5. When the Provider clicks "Forgot Password?", Then the system must navigate them to the Forgot Password screen.

6. When the Provider presses the Enter key after filling in credentials, Then the system must treat it the same as clicking "Sign In."
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify the Provider can enter a valid email (e.g., "provider@clinic.com") and correct password, click "Sign In", and get redirected to the OTP verification screen.
2. Verify the Login screen displays correctly with the split layout — care facility background image on the left, form with Nemi Care logo, "Sign in to your account" heading, Email field, Password field, "Forgot Password?" link, and blue "Sign In" button on the right.
3. Verify the "Forgot Password?" link navigates the Provider to the Forgot Password screen.
4. Verify the Provider can use the show/hide visibility toggle on the Password field to reveal and re-hide the entered password.
5. Verify the Provider can submit the form by pressing the Enter key instead of clicking "Sign In".
6. Verify the Email field accepts valid email formats including special characters (e.g., "user+tag@domain.com", "user.name@domain.co.uk").

Negative:
7. Verify entering an incorrect password with a valid registered email shows generic "Invalid email or password" error — NOT "Incorrect password".
8. Verify entering a non-existent email with any password shows the same generic "Invalid email or password" error — NOT "Email not found".
9. Verify entering an invalid email format (e.g., "user@", "user.com", "@domain.com", "user@.com") shows "Please enter a valid email address.".
10. Verify clicking "Sign In" with both fields empty shows "Email is required" and "Password is required" errors.
11. Verify clicking "Sign In" with only Email filled and Password empty shows "Password is required" error.
12. Verify clicking "Sign In" with only Password filled and Email empty shows "Email is required" error.
13. Verify a locked account (after 5 failed attempts) shows "Your account has been locked. Please contact your administrator."
14. Verify an inactive/deactivated account shows "Your account is not active. Please contact your administrator."
15. Verify entering correct credentials for a locked account still shows the locked message (not OTP screen).

UI Scenarios:
16. Verify the Login screen has the correct split layout — care facility background image with elderly residents and staff on the left, form panel on the right.
17. Verify the Nemi Care logo (icon + "Nemi Care" text) is displayed at the top of the form panel.
18. Verify the heading "Sign in to your account" is displayed below the logo.
19. Verify the "Email" field has a visible label and placeholder text (e.g., "Enter your email").
20. Verify the "Password" field has a visible label, placeholder text (e.g., "Enter your password"), and a show/hide toggle icon on the right.
21. Verify the "Forgot Password?" link is visible, clickable, and right-aligned below the Password field.
22. Verify the "Sign In" button is displayed in the brand blue color, spans the full width, and has white text.
23. Verify the form panel is vertically centered within the right half of the screen.
24. Verify error messages appear inline below the respective fields in red text.

Edge Cases:
25. Verify login with an email containing the maximum allowed length (254 characters per RFC 5321) and a valid password.
26. Verify login with leading and trailing spaces in the email field — spaces should be trimmed before validation.
27. Verify behavior when the Provider clicks "Sign In" multiple times rapidly — should prevent duplicate authentication requests.
28. Verify behavior when the browser auto-fills the Email and Password from saved credentials.
29. Verify behavior when the user has Caps Lock on while typing the password — should the system warn them?

Security:
30. Verify credentials are transmitted over HTTPS and never visible in plaintext in network requests.
31. Verify the account is locked after 5 consecutive failed login attempts (brute-force protection).
32. Verify the error message never reveals whether the email exists in the system or whether the password was wrong (prevents email enumeration).
33. Verify SQL injection attempts in the Email field are safely handled (e.g., entering "' OR 1=1 --" does not bypass login).
34. Verify XSS payloads in the Email field are sanitized (e.g., entering "<script>alert(1)</script>" does not execute).
35. Verify rate limiting is enforced on login attempts from the same IP address.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Login screen fully loads (including background image) within 3 seconds on a standard connection.
2. Verify credential validation response (click "Sign In" to OTP screen or error) completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to resend the OTP if I did not receive it or if it has expired.
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to request a fresh One-Time Password (OTP) if the original OTP was not received due to email delivery delays, was accidentally deleted, or has expired past its validity window. On the OTP verification screen, below the blue "Verify" button, a "Resend OTP" link is prominently displayed. When the Provider clicks this link, the system invalidates any previously issued OTP, generates a new 6-digit numeric OTP, and sends it to the Provider's registered email address. A success confirmation (e.g., toast notification or inline message "OTP resent successfully") is displayed on screen, the six OTP digit input boxes are cleared for fresh entry, and a cooldown timer (e.g., "Resend available in 30s") is shown to prevent spam/abuse — during which the Resend link is temporarily disabled. Once the cooldown expires, the Resend link becomes active again. This feature is essential to ensure that Providers are never permanently locked out of the platform due to email delivery issues, while also implementing rate limiting to prevent OTP flooding attacks on the email system.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider clicks "Resend OTP", Then the system must invalidate all previously issued OTPs, generate a fresh 6-digit OTP, send it to the registered email, clear the OTP input boxes, and display "OTP resent successfully."

2. If the Provider enters the newly resent OTP and clicks "Verify", Then the system must authenticate them and redirect to the Dashboard.
   Else if the Provider enters the old/previous OTP, Then the system must reject it with "Invalid OTP. Please try again."

3. After clicking "Resend OTP", Then a cooldown timer (e.g., "Resend available in 30s") must be shown, and the "Resend OTP" link must be disabled until the timer expires.
   After the cooldown expires, Then the "Resend OTP" link must become active and clickable again.

4. If the Provider has reached the maximum number of resend attempts (e.g., 5), Then the system must display "Maximum resend attempts reached. Please try logging in again." and disable the Resend link permanently for that session.

5. After a resend, Then the OTP input boxes must be cleared and the cursor must be placed in the first digit box for fresh entry.

6. The newly resent OTP must have its own independent expiry timer (e.g., 5 minutes from the time of resend).
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify clicking "Resend OTP" sends a new 6-digit OTP email to the Provider's registered email.
2. Verify the confirmation message "OTP resent successfully" is displayed on the screen after clicking Resend.
3. Verify the six OTP digit input boxes are cleared (emptied) after clicking Resend OTP.
4. Verify the cursor/focus is placed on the first OTP digit box after the boxes are cleared.
5. Verify the Provider can enter the new OTP received via email, click "Verify", and get authenticated and redirected to the Dashboard.
6. Verify the cooldown timer (e.g., "Resend available in 30s") appears after clicking "Resend OTP" and counts down to 0.
7. Verify the "Resend OTP" link becomes active and clickable again after the cooldown timer reaches 0.

Negative:
8. Verify the old/previous OTP is rejected with "Invalid OTP. Please try again." after a new OTP has been resent.
9. Verify the "Resend OTP" link is visually disabled (grayed out) and not clickable during the cooldown period.
10. Verify clicking the disabled "Resend OTP" link during cooldown does nothing — no new email is sent, no error.
11. Verify that after reaching the maximum resend limit (e.g., 5 resends), clicking "Resend OTP" shows "Maximum resend attempts reached. Please try logging in again."
12. Verify the "Resend OTP" link remains permanently disabled for the session after the maximum limit is reached.
13. Verify that if the resend API call fails (e.g., server error), an appropriate error message like "Failed to resend OTP. Please try again." is shown.
14. Verify that entering the OTP from the first email after two consecutive resends is rejected (only the latest OTP should work).

UI Scenarios:
15. Verify the "Resend OTP" link is visible and positioned below the "Verify" button.
16. Verify the cooldown timer replaces the "Resend OTP" text during the countdown (e.g., "Resend available in 28s").
17. Verify the timer counts down smoothly (second by second) without flickering or lag.
18. Verify the "Resend OTP" link appears grayed out or in a disabled color during cooldown.
19. Verify the "OTP resent successfully" confirmation is displayed as a toast notification or inline message, styled consistently with the platform.
20. Verify the OTP input boxes visually clear (go from filled to empty) after resend.

Edge Cases:
21. Verify behavior when the Provider clicks "Resend OTP" multiple times rapidly before the cooldown activates — only one resend request should be processed.
22. Verify behavior at the exact cooldown boundary — clicking "Resend OTP" at exactly 0 seconds remaining should trigger a new resend.
23. Verify behavior when the Provider navigates away from the OTP screen (e.g., Back button) and returns — does the resend count reset?
24. Verify the new OTP has its own independent 5-minute expiry timer, not extending from the original OTP time.
25. Verify behavior when the Provider resends OTP, receives both old and new emails, and enters the OTP from the old email — should be rejected.

Security:
26. Verify rate limiting is enforced — the system does not allow unlimited resend requests even if the UI cooldown is bypassed (e.g., via API call).
27. Verify all previously issued OTPs (not just the most recent one) are invalidated when a new OTP is generated.
28. Verify the resend OTP API endpoint requires a valid session/token — it cannot be called by an unauthenticated user.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the resent OTP email arrives in the Provider's inbox within 30 seconds of clicking "Resend OTP".
2. Verify the "OTP resent successfully" confirmation appears within 2 seconds of clicking Resend.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to reset the password if I forgot the password.
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to recover access to their Nemi Care account when they have forgotten their password. From the Login screen, a "Forgot Password?" link is positioned below the Password field. Clicking this link navigates the Provider to the Forgot Password screen — a split-layout page with the care facility background image on the left and a centered form on the right displaying the Nemi Care logo, the heading "Forgot Password" or "Reset your password", an "Email" input field where the Provider enters their registered email address, a full-width blue "Send Reset Link" button, and a "Back to Login" link for easy navigation back. When the Provider submits a valid registered email, the system sends a secure, one-time password reset link to that email address. For security, the system displays a generic confirmation message ("If an account with this email exists, a reset link has been sent") regardless of whether the email is registered, to prevent email enumeration attacks. The Provider then clicks the reset link in their email, is taken to the Set Password screen to create a new password, and upon success is redirected back to the Login screen. This self-service password recovery flow is essential for healthcare providers who may work across multiple systems and need a quick, secure way to regain access without contacting an administrator.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider clicks "Forgot Password?" on the Login screen, Then the system must navigate them to the Forgot Password screen.

2. When the Provider enters an email and clicks "Send Reset Link", Then the system must always display the generic message "If an account with this email exists, a reset link has been sent" regardless of whether the email is registered or not.
   If the email is registered, Then a password reset email with a secure, one-time link must be sent.
   Else if the email is not registered, Then no email is sent, but the same generic message is still displayed (no email enumeration).

3. If the Provider clicks the reset link and the link is valid, Then the system must redirect them to the Set Password screen.
   Else if the link has expired, Then the system must show "This link has expired" with an option to request a new one.
   Else if the link has already been used, Then the system must show "This link has already been used."

4. After the Provider successfully sets a new password via the reset flow, Then the system must redirect them to the Login screen with the message "Password reset successfully. Please sign in with your new password."

5. If the Provider clicks "Send Reset Link" without entering an email, Then the system must show "Email is required" and not submit.
   Else if the Provider enters an invalid email format, Then the system must show "Please enter a valid email address" and not submit.

6. When the Provider clicks "Back to Login", Then the system must navigate them back to the Login screen.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify clicking "Forgot Password?" on the Login screen navigates the Provider to the Forgot Password screen.
2. Verify the Forgot Password screen displays correctly — split layout, Nemi Care logo, "Forgot Password" heading, Email field, blue "Send Reset Link" button, and "Back to Login" link.
3. Verify entering a valid registered email (e.g., "provider@clinic.com") and clicking "Send Reset Link" displays the generic confirmation message.
4. Verify a password reset email is received at "provider@clinic.com" after submitting the form.
5. Verify clicking the reset link in the email redirects the Provider to the Set Password screen.
6. Verify the Provider can enter a new valid password, confirm it on the Set Password screen, and click "Set Password".
7. Verify the Provider is redirected to the Login screen with the message "Password reset successfully. Please sign in with your new password."
8. Verify the Provider can log in with the new password (and the old password no longer works).
9. Verify clicking "Back to Login" on the Forgot Password screen navigates back to the Login screen.

Negative:
10. Verify clicking "Send Reset Link" with the email field empty shows "Email is required" error.
11. Verify entering an invalid email format (e.g., "user@", "user.com", "@domain.com") shows "Please enter a valid email address" error.
12. Verify entering an unregistered email (e.g., "nobody@clinic.com") still shows the same generic confirmation message — no way to tell the email doesn't exist.
13. Verify clicking an expired reset link (e.g., after 24 hours) shows "This link has expired" with an option to request a new link.
14. Verify clicking a reset link that has already been used shows "This link has already been used."
15. Verify the old password no longer works after the Provider has successfully reset to a new password.
16. Verify that entering mismatched passwords on the Set Password screen (via reset flow) shows a "Passwords do not match" error.

UI Scenarios:
17. Verify the Forgot Password screen has the correct split layout — care facility background image on left, form panel on right.
18. Verify the Nemi Care logo is displayed at the top of the form panel.
19. Verify the heading "Forgot Password" or "Reset your password" is displayed below the logo.
20. Verify the "Email" field has a visible label and placeholder text (e.g., "Enter your registered email").
21. Verify the "Send Reset Link" button is displayed in the brand blue color, spans the full width, with white text.
22. Verify the "Back to Login" link is visible and positioned below the button.
23. Verify the generic confirmation message is displayed inline on the same screen after submission (not a redirect).
24. Verify error messages appear inline below the Email field in red text.

Edge Cases:
25. Verify reset link expiry at exactly the boundary time (e.g., clicking at exactly 24 hours).
26. Verify that requesting multiple reset links in succession — only the latest link should work; all previous links must be invalidated.
27. Verify email with special characters in the local part (e.g., "user+test@domain.com", "user.name@domain.co.uk") is accepted.
28. Verify behavior when the Provider clicks "Send Reset Link" multiple times rapidly — should only send one email.

Security:
29. Verify the reset link uses a cryptographically secure, random, non-guessable token.
30. Verify the reset link is single-use — it cannot be used again after the password is changed.
31. Verify no email enumeration vulnerability — the response is identical for registered and unregistered emails (same message, same timing).
32. Verify rate limiting on password reset requests (e.g., max 3 requests per email per hour) to prevent abuse.
33. Verify the reset link URL uses HTTPS protocol.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the password reset email is delivered within 5 minutes of form submission.
2. Verify the Forgot Password screen loads within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to capture and view which source a lead originated from
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to capture and track the origination source of each lead within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. On the Lead List screen, the provider can view a table with columns including Name, email, Lead ID Source, Service Type, Status, and Added BY, where the Source column displays the channel through which the lead was acquired (e.g., Website ( caring.com, A place for MOM ), Referral, Walk-in, ). When adding a new lead via the "Add Lead" button, the Add Lead Form includes a Lead Source dropdown that allows the provider to select the appropriate source. The Lead Profile Header also displays the source information alongside other lead details such as name, status badge, service type tags, and contact information. This capability is essential for providers to analyze which marketing channels and referral networks are generating the most leads, enabling data-driven decisions about resource allocation and outreach strategies. Without source tracking, the provider would have no visibility into lead acquisition effectiveness across different channels.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider opens the Add Lead Form and clicks the Lead Source dropdown, Then the system must display all predefined lead source options.

2. If the provider selects a valid lead source and fills all required fields and clicks Save, Then the system must save the lead with the selected source and display it in the Lead List under the Source column.
   Else if the provider does not select a lead source and clicks Save, Then the system must display a validation error "Lead Source is required" and prevent form submission.

3. If the provider edits an existing lead and changes the Lead Source to a different value, Then the system must update the source and reflect the change in the Lead List and Lead Profile.
   Else if the provider edits the lead but does not change the source, Then the original source value must be retained.

4. If the lead was imported via bulk import with a source value, Then the system must display the imported source in the Source column.
   Else if the imported lead has no source value, Then the system must display "Unknown" or a default placeholder in the Source column.

5. When the provider views the Lead List table, Then every lead row must display a non-empty Source value.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking "Add Lead" opens the Add Lead Form with a Lead Source dropdown containing options: Caring.com, A place for mom, Referral, Walk-in.
2. Verify that selecting "Referral" as Lead Source, filling all required fields, and clicking Save creates the lead with "Referral" displayed in the Lead List Source column.
3. Verify that selecting "Caring.com / A place for mom" as Lead Source and saving displays "Caring.com / A place for mom" in both the Lead List Source column and the Lead Profile Header.
4. Verify that editing an existing lead and changing Lead Source from "Walk-in" to "Referral" updates the source in the Lead List and Lead Profile.
5. Verify that the Lead Source dropdown retains the previously selected value when reopening the Edit Lead Form.

Negative:
6. Verify that attempting to save a new lead without selecting a Lead Source displays a validation error "Lead Source is required".
7. Verify that the Lead Source dropdown does not allow free-text entry — only predefined options can be selected.
8. Verify that clearing the Lead Source field on an existing lead and saving shows a validation error.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify the Lead Source dropdown is positioned correctly within the Add/Edit Lead Form layout and is clearly labeled.
10. Verify the Source column is visible in the Lead List table between the Name and Service Type columns.
11. Verify the Lead Source value is displayed on the Lead Profile Header alongside name, status badge, and service type tags.
12. Verify the Lead Source dropdown has a placeholder text like "Select Source" when no value is selected.
13. Verify the dropdown options are readable and properly styled with consistent font and spacing.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify behavior when a lead is imported via bulk import with a source value of "Website" — it should display correctly in the Source column.
15. Verify behavior when a lead is imported without a source value — the Source column should show a default placeholder like "Unknown".
16. Verify that the Lead Source value persists correctly after multiple edits to the same lead.

Security:
17. Verify that only authenticated providers can view and modify the Lead Source field.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Lead Source dropdown loads all options within 1 second of clicking.
2. Verify the Lead List table with 500+ leads renders the Source column data within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to assign service type (ADH, ALF, etc.) to a lead
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to assign one or more service types to a lead within the Nemi Care Facility Portal, indicating which facility services the lead is interested in or eligible for. On the Add/Edit Lead Form, a Service Type dropdown allows the provider to select from options such as ADH (Adult Day Health), ALF (Assisted Living Facility), and other available service categories. Once assigned, the service type is displayed as tags on the Lead Profile Header — for example, colored tags showing "ADH" and "ALF" alongside the lead name and status badge. The Lead List table also includes a Service Type column showing the assigned types for each lead. This assignment is critical because it drives downstream workflows such as documentation steps (ADH Documentation Stepper vs ALF Documentation Stepper), rate card selection (Private Pay vs Medicaid rates), and bed/room management (ALF-specific). Without a service type assignment, the system cannot determine which onboarding pathway and documentation requirements apply to the lead.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider opens the Add Lead Form and clicks the Service Type dropdown, Then the system must display all available service type options (ADH, ALF, etc.).

2. If the provider selects one or more service types and saves, Then the system must store the selection and display the service type(s) as tags on the Lead Profile Header and in the Lead List Service Type column.
   Else if the provider does not select any service type and attempts to save, Then the system must display a validation error "Service Type is required".

3. If the provider assigns "ADH" as the service type, Then the system must route the lead through the ADH Documentation Stepper workflow.
   Else if the provider assigns "ALF" as the service type, Then the system must route the lead through the ALF Documentation Stepper workflow.

4. If the provider changes the service type on an existing lead from "ADH" to "ALF", Then the system must update the documentation workflow accordingly and reflect the change in the Lead Profile Header tags.

5. If the provider assigns multiple service types (e.g., both ADH and ALF), Then the system must display all assigned types as separate tags on the Lead Profile Header.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking the Service Type dropdown on the Add Lead Form shows options including "ADH", "ALF", and other available service types.
2. Verify that selecting "ADH" and saving the lead displays an "ADH" tag on the Lead Profile Header.
3. Verify that selecting both "ADH" and "ALF" displays two separate tags on the Lead Profile Header.
4. Verify that the Lead List Service Type column shows "ADH" for a lead assigned only ADH.
5. Verify that editing a lead and changing service type from "ALF" to "ADH" updates the tag on the Lead Profile Header.
6. Verify that assigning "ADH" triggers the ADH Documentation Stepper with steps: Case Agency Selection, Patient Required Doc, Case Manager Setup, Transportation Request, Billing Setup & Clearance.
7. Verify that assigning "ALF" triggers the ALF Documentation Stepper with steps: Case Agency Selection, Patient & CM Setup, Check Bed Availability, Billing Setup & Clearance, Assessment.

Negative:
8. Verify that saving a lead without selecting any service type displays a validation error "Service Type is required".
9. Verify that the Service Type dropdown does not allow free-text entry.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify service type tags on the Lead Profile Header are displayed as colored badges next to the lead name and status.
11. Verify the Service Type dropdown is clearly labeled and positioned within the Add/Edit Lead Form.
12. Verify the Service Type column in the Lead List table displays tags/badges rather than plain text for visual distinction.
13. Verify the dropdown has a placeholder text like "Select Service Type" when no value is selected.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify behavior when a provider assigns multiple service types and then removes one — the remaining type should be retained.
15. Verify behavior when the service type list is updated by the admin — new options should appear in the dropdown for new leads.

Security:
16. Verify that only authorized providers can assign or change service types on leads.
17. Verify that service type values cannot be tampered with via API calls outside the allowed options.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Service Type dropdown loads all options within 1 second.
2. Verify that changing the service type and saving reflects the update in the Lead List within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to add lead details
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to create a new lead record in the Nemi Care Facility Portal by entering comprehensive lead information through the Add Lead Form. The provider clicks the "Add  New Lead" button on the Lead List screen, which opens a form with fields for personal information.The form also includes a Lead Source dropdown to capture the origination channel, a Service Type dropdown (ADH, ALF, etc.) to assign the relevant service, and an Lead Owner dropdown to designate a sales team member. Upon successful submission, the new lead appears in the Lead List table with all entered details populated across columns (Name, Source, Service Type, Status etc.).  Adding lead details is the foundational step that initiates the entire lead management and conversion workflow.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider clicks "Add Lead" on the Lead List screen, Then the system must open the Add Lead Form with all required and optional fields.

2. If the provider fills all required fields (First Name, Last Name, Email, Phone, Service Type) and clicks Save, Then the system must create the lead and display it in the Lead List.
   Else if any required field is missing, Then the system must highlight the missing field(s) with validation errors and prevent form submission.

3. If the provider enters an invalid email format (e.g., "john.doe@"), Then the system must display "Enter a valid email address" error.
   Else if the provider enters a valid email, Then the field must be accepted.

4. If the provider enters a phone number with non-numeric characters (e.g., "abc-1234"), Then the system must display a validation error for the phone field.
   Else if the phone number is in a valid format, Then the field must be accepted.

5. If the provider clicks Cancel on the Add Lead Form, Then no lead must be created and the provider must return to the Lead List.

6. If a lead with the same email already exists, Then the system must display a warning "A lead with this email already exists" and allow the provider to decide whether to proceed.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify clicking "Add New Lead" on the Lead List opens the Add Lead Form with all fields
2. Verify entering all the details and clicking Save creates the lead.
3. Verify the newly created lead appears in the Lead List with Name "John Doe", the selected Source, Service Type "ADH", Status "New", and the selected Assignee.
4. Verify that selecting DOB "1955-06-15" using the date picker stores and displays the date correctly.
5. Verify that selecting Gender "Male" from the dropdown saves correctly.
6. Verify that entering Address "123 Main St, Suite 4, Orlando, FL 32801" saves the full address.

Negative:
7. Verify that clicking Save without entering First Name shows "First Name is required" validation error.
8. Verify that clicking Save without entering Last Name shows "Last Name is required".
9. Verify that entering an invalid email "john.doe@" shows "Enter a valid email address".
10. Verify that entering phone "abc-xyz" shows a phone validation error.
11. Verify that clicking Save without selecting Service Type shows "Service Type is required".
12. Verify that entering a DOB in the future (e.g., "2030-01-01") shows a validation error.

UI Scenarios:
13. Verify the Add Lead Form has a clean layout with labeled fields and proper spacing.
14. Verify required fields are marked with an asterisk (*) indicator.
15. Verify the Save and Cancel buttons are prominently displayed at the bottom of the form.
16. Verify dropdowns for Lead Source, Service Type, and Assigned To have placeholder text.

Edge Cases:
18. Verify that entering a very long name (100+ characters) is handled gracefully.
19. Verify behavior when a lead with the same email already exists — system should warn but allow creation.
20. Verify that special characters in the name field (e.g., "O'Brien", "Smith-Jones") are accepted.
21. Verify behavior when the form is submitted with only required fields and all optional fields empty.

Security:
22. Verify that lead data is transmitted over HTTPS when saving.
23. Verify that form fields are protected against XSS injection (e.g., entering "<script>alert(1)</script>" in the name field).
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Add Lead Form loads within 2 seconds after clicking "Add Lead".
2. Verify that saving a new lead completes and redirects within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to view the list of leads
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to view a comprehensive list of all leads in the Nemi Care Facility Portal through the Lead List screen. The Lead List displays leads in a table view with columns for Name, Source, Service Type, Status, and Added By, providing a quick overview of all leads and their current states. At the top of the screen, a search bar allows the provider to quickly find specific leads by name or other criteria, and filter buttons enable narrowing the list by various attributes. The blue navigation bar at the top includes tabs for Home, Co Leads, Residents, Scheduler, Documents, and other portal sections. An "Add New Lead" button is prominently placed for creating new leads directly from this screen. This is the primary landing page for lead management, giving the provider immediate visibility into their entire lead pipeline and enabling quick navigation to any individual lead for detailed actions.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider navigates to the Lead List screen, Then the system must display a table with all leads and columns: Lead ID, Lead Name, Source, Service Type, Status, Added By, Email, Lead Owner, Created on & Actions

2. If the provider types a search term in the search bar, Then the system must filter the list to show only leads matching the search term in real-time.
   Else if the search bar is cleared, Then the full list must be restored.

3. If the provider clicks on a lead row, Then the system must navigate to that lead's detail page.

4. If the lead list contains more items than the page limit, Then the system must display pagination controls at the bottom of the table.
   Else if all leads fit on one page, Then no pagination controls should be displayed.

5. If no leads exist in the system, Then the system must display an empty state message like "No leads found. Click Add Lead to create your first lead."
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify navigating to the Lead List screen displays a table with columns:  Lead ID, Lead Name, Source, Service Type, Status, Added By, Email, Lead Owner, Created on & Actions
2. Verify that each lead row shows populated values for all columns
3. Verify typing "John" in the search bar filters the list to show only leads with "John" in the name.
4. Verify clicking on a lead name navigates to the lead detail page with Lead Profile Header and Detail Tabs.
5. Verify the "Add New Lead" button is visible and functional on the Lead List screen.
6. Verify pagination works correctly when there are more than 20 leads (or the configured page size).

Negative:
7. Verify searching for a non-existent name (e.g., "XYZ123") shows "No results found" message.
8. Verify that the Lead List does not display leads that the provider does not have permission to view.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify the search bar is positioned at the top of the Lead List with a magnifying glass icon.
11. Verify the "Add New Lead" button is prominently displayed (e.g., blue button with "+" icon).
12. Verify the table has alternating row colors or hover effects for readability.
13. Verify column headers are bold and clearly labeled.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify behavior with a single lead in the system — the table should display one row correctly.
15. Verify behavior with zero leads — the system should show an empty state message with "Add Lead" guidance.
16. Verify that the search works with partial matches (e.g., typing "Jo" matches "John", "Joseph").
17. Verify that the search is case-insensitive (typing "john" matches "John").

Security:
18. Verify that unauthenticated users cannot access the Lead List screen.
19. Verify that the Lead List API endpoint does not expose sensitive lead data beyond what is displayed in the table.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Lead List loads and displays the first page of leads within 3 seconds for a dataset of 1000+ leads.
2. Verify that search filtering returns results within 1 second of typing.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to import & export list of leads.
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to bulk import leads from an external file (e.g., CSV, Excel) into the Nemi Care Facility Portal and export the current lead list to a downloadable file. The import functionality allows the provider to upload a file containing lead data with columns mapped to system fields, enabling migration from other systems or bulk data entry. The export functionality allows the provider to download the current Lead List (optionally filtered) as a CSV or Excel file for offline analysis, reporting, or sharing with stakeholders. Import and export buttons are accessible from the Lead List screen alongside the "Add Lead" button. During import, the system validates each row for required fields, duplicate detection (by email), and data format compliance, providing a summary report of successful and failed imports. This capability is essential for providers transitioning from manual or legacy systems and for generating periodic reports.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider clicks "Import" on the Lead List screen, Then the system must display a file upload dialog accepting CSV / Excel file formats.

2. If the uploaded file has valid headers and data, Then the system must process each row and import valid leads into the system.
   Else if the file has incorrect headers or unsupported format, Then the system must display "Invalid file format. Please use the provided template." and reject the file.

3. If a row in the import file has a duplicate email matching an existing lead, Then the system must flag that row as a duplicate and skip it, reporting it in the import summary.
   Else if the email is unique, Then the lead must be imported.

4. When the provider clicks "Export", Then the system must generate a downloadable file containing the currently displayed leads.
   If filters are active, Then only filtered leads must be included in the export.
   Else if no filters are active, Then all leads must be included.

5. If the import file is empty (no data rows), Then the system must display "The uploaded file contains no data" and not proceed with import.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify clicking "Import" on the Lead List opens a file upload dialog.
2. Verify uploading a valid CSV file with 10 leads imports all 10 leads successfully and shows "10 of 10 imported successfully" in the summary.
3. Verify imported leads appear in the Lead List with correct Name, Email, Phone, Source, and Service Type.
4. Verify clicking "Export" downloads a CSV/Excel file containing all leads from the Lead List.
5. Verify that applying a filter for Status "New" and clicking Export only includes "New" leads in the downloaded file.
6. Verify the export file contains headers matching the Lead List columns

Negative:
7. Verify uploading a file with 3 valid rows and 2 rows missing email shows "3 imported, 2 failed" with error details for the failed rows.
8. Verify uploading a file with duplicate emails (matching existing leads) skips those rows and reports them as duplicates.
9. Verify uploading an unsupported file format (e.g., .pdf, .doc) shows "Invalid file format" error.
10. Verify uploading an empty file shows "The uploaded file contains no data".
11. Verify uploading a file with incorrect column headers shows a mapping error.

UI Scenarios:
12. Verify "Import" and "Export" buttons are visible on the Lead List screen near the "Add Lead" button.
13. Verify the file upload dialog supports drag-and-drop functionality.
14. Verify the import summary report is displayed in a clear table format showing row number, status, and error message.
15. Verify a progress indicator is shown during file processing.

Edge Cases:
16. Verify importing a large file with 1000+ rows completes without timeout.
17. Verify that special characters in lead names (e.g., "O'Brien", "Garcia-Lopez") are preserved during import.
18. Verify that exporting an empty Lead List (no leads) generates a file with only headers.

Security:
19. Verify that the import file is scanned for malicious content before processing.
20. Verify that the export file does not include sensitive data fields not shown in the Lead List.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that importing a file with 500 leads completes processing within 30 seconds.
2. Verify that exporting 1000+ leads generates and starts downloading the file within 5 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to add filter on lead list
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to apply filters on the Lead List screen to narrow down the displayed leads based on specific criteria within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. Filter buttons are positioned near the search bar at the top of the Lead List table, allowing the provider to filter by attributes such as Status (New, Contacted, In-Progress, Qualified, Won, Lost), Lead Source, Service Type, Assigned To, and Date Range. When filters are applied, the Lead List table updates in real-time to show only leads matching the selected criteria. Multiple filters can be combined to create precise views (e.g., "All ADH leads assigned to John Smith with status In-Progress"). Active filters are visually indicated with badges or highlighted states so the provider knows which filters are in effect. A "Clear All Filters" option resets the list to show all leads, ensuring the provider can quickly return to the full view.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider clicks a filter button, Then the system must display filter options relevant to that attribute.

2. If the provider selects one or more filter values, Then the Lead List must update in real-time to show only matching leads.
   Else if no filter is selected, Then the full Lead List must be displayed.

3. If multiple filters are applied simultaneously, Then only leads matching all filter criteria must be shown.

4. If the applied filters return zero results, Then the system must display "No leads match the selected filters" instead of an empty table.

5. When the provider clicks "Clear All Filters", Then all active filters must be removed and the full Lead List must be restored.

6. If the provider selects a filter value and then deselects it, Then the filter must be removed and the list must update accordingly.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify clicking the Status filter button shows options: New, Contacted, In-Progress, Qualified, Won, Lost.
2. Verify selecting Status "In-Progress" filters the Lead List to show only leads with "In-Progress" status.
3. Verify combining Status "In-Progress" and Service Type "ADH" shows only leads matching both criteria.
4. Verify that adding Assigned To "John Smith" as a third filter further narrows the results.
5. Verify that active filters show visual indicators (badges/highlights).
6. Verify clicking "Clear All Filters" removes all filters and restores the full Lead List.

Negative:
7. Verify that applying a filter combination that matches no leads shows "No leads match the selected filters".
8. Verify that filters do not show leads the provider does not have permission to view.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify filter buttons are positioned near the search bar at the top of the Lead List.
10. Verify filter dropdowns display options in a clean, scrollable list.
11. Verify active filter badges show the selected value (e.g., "Status: In-Progress").
12. Verify the "Clear All Filters" button/link is visible when any filter is active.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that applying and removing filters repeatedly does not cause display issues.
14. Verify that browser back button after applying filters restores the previous filter state.
15. Verify that filters persist during the session (navigating away and back retains filters).

Security:
16. Verify that filter parameters cannot be manipulated via URL to bypass access controls.
17. Verify that the filter API endpoint validates all filter values against allowed options.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that applying a filter on 1000+ leads updates the list within 2 seconds.
2. Verify that clearing all filters and restoring the full list completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to edit lead details
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to modify existing lead information through the Edit Lead Form within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. From the Lead List or Lead Profile, the provider can access the Edit Lead Form, which pre-populates with the lead current data. The provider can modify any field and save the changes, which immediately reflect in the Lead List table and Lead Profile Header. The Lead Profile Header displays the updated information including name, status badge, service type tags (ADH/ALF), payment type tag (Medicaid/Private Pay), age, email, phone, gender, and address. The three-dot menu on the Lead Profile Header provides quick access to edit functionality. Edit capability is essential for keeping lead data current as new information is gathered through calls, visits, and follow-ups throughout the lead conversion process.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider opens the Edit Lead Form, Then the system must pre-populate all fields with the lead current data.

2. If the provider modifies one or more fields and clicks Save, Then the system must validate the changes and update the lead record.
   Else if validation fails (e.g., invalid email format), Then the system must display the specific validation error and prevent saving.

3. If the provider clicks Cancel on the Edit Lead Form, Then no changes must be saved and the lead data must remain unchanged.

4. If the provider clears a required field (e.g., First Name) and clicks Save, Then the system must display "First Name is required" and prevent saving.

5. If the provider changes the Service Type, Then the Lead Profile Header tags and the documentation workflow must update to reflect the new service type.

6. If the provider changes the email to one that already exists for another lead, Then the system must display a warning about the duplicate email.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that selecting "Edit" from the Lead List or three-dot menu opens the Edit Lead Form with all current data pre-populated.
2. Verify changing First Name from "John" to "Jonathan" and saving updates the name in the Lead List and Lead Profile Header.
3. Verify changing Phone from "555-123-4567" to "555-987-6543" and saving reflects the new number.
4. Verify changing Service Type from "ADH" to "ALF" updates the service type tags on the Lead Profile Header.
5. Verify changing Lead owner from "John Smith" to "Sarah Johnson" updates the assignment.
6. Verify that a success message appears after saving edits.

Negative:
7. Verify that clearing the First Name field and saving shows "First Name is required" validation error.
8. Verify that entering an invalid email format on edit shows a validation error.
9. Verify that clicking Cancel discards all changes and retains the original data.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify the Edit Lead Form has the same layout as the Add Lead Form with pre-populated values.
11. Verify the three-dot menu on the Lead Profile Header includes an "Edit" option.
12. Verify the Lead Profile Header shows updated photo, name, status badge, service type tags, payment type tag, age, email, phone, gender, and address after edit.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that editing a lead while another user is also editing the same lead handles concurrency (last save wins or conflict warning).
14. Verify that editing and saving with no actual changes does not create a false "updated" record.
15. Verify that the edit form retains unsaved changes if the provider accidentally navigates away and comes back.

Security:
16. Verify that only authorized providers can access the Edit Lead Form.
17. Verify that edit operations are logged in the audit trail.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Edit Lead Form loads with pre-populated data within 2 seconds.
2. Verify that saving edits reflects the updated data in the Lead List within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to view activity overview of Lead
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to view a comprehensive activity overview for a lead, showing all interactions and events in a chronological timeline within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. The Activity Timeline is displayed on the lead detail page and shows a vertical timeline of events including Lead Created, Visit Done, Phone Call, SMS, Visit Scheduled, status changes, and notes, each with timestamps. Each activity entry has a "View Summary" expandable link that the provider can click to see detailed information about that specific activity. The timeline is displayed in reverse chronological order with the most recent activity at the top. This overview gives the provider a complete picture of all touchpoints with the lead, enabling them to understand the full history of engagement before making their next outreach decision. The Activity tab is one of the tabs within the Lead Detail Tabs interface alongside Communication, Visits, Documentation, and Rate & Pricing.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider clicks the Activity tab on the lead detail page, Then the system must display the Activity Timeline with all activities in reverse chronological order.

2. If the lead has activities, Then each activity must display the type, timestamp, and a "View Summary" link.
   Else if the lead has no activities, Then the system must display "No activities recorded for this lead.".

3. If the provider clicks "View Summary" on an activity, Then the system must expand the entry inline to show full details.
   If the provider clicks "View Summary" again (or a collapse action), Then the details must collapse back.

4. If a new activity is logged for the lead, Then it must appear at the top of the timeline on the next load or refresh.

5. If the Activity Timeline has many entries (50+), Then the system must implement pagination or lazy loading to display entries efficiently.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking the Activity tab displays a vertical Activity Timeline with all lead activities.
2. Verify the timeline shows "Lead Created" as the earliest entry with the creation timestamp.
3. Verify that a Phone Call activity entry shows "Phone Call" type, call timestamp, and "View Summary" link.
4. Verify clicking "View Summary" on a Phone Call entry expands to show call type, duration, notes, and AI call summary.
5. Verify clicking "View Summary" on an SMS entry expands to show message content and AI SMS summary.
6. Verify clicking "View Summary" on a Visit entry expands to show visit type (Facility Tour/Consultation), date, time, and notes.
7. Verify that activities are displayed in reverse chronological order (newest first).

Negative:
8. Verify that a lead with no activities shows "No activities recorded for this lead.".
9. Verify that expanding a "View Summary" and immediately collapsing it works without errors.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify the Activity Timeline uses a vertical layout with connecting lines between entries.
11. Verify each activity type has a distinct icon (e.g., phone icon for calls, envelope icon for SMS, calendar icon for visits).
12. Verify timestamps are formatted consistently (e.g., "Mar 25, 2026 10:30 AM").
13. Verify the "View Summary" links are clickable and visually distinguishable from other text.
14. Verify the Activity tab is clearly labeled in the Lead Detail Tabs interface.

Edge Cases:
15. Verify behavior with 100+ activities — the timeline should handle pagination or lazy loading.
16. Verify that activities logged simultaneously (same timestamp) are displayed in a consistent order.
17. Verify that the timeline updates when a new activity is logged without requiring a full page reload.

Security:
18. Verify that the Activity Timeline only shows activities the provider is authorized to view.
19. Verify that Private notes do not appear in the Activity Timeline for unauthorized users.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Activity Timeline loads within 3 seconds for a lead with 50+ activities.
2. Verify that expanding "View Summary" loads the details within 1 second.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able Reject the lead with reason
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to reject a lead and document the specific reason for rejection within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. When the provider determines that a lead is not suitable for the facility services, they can use the three-dot menu on the Lead Profile Header or a reject action in the Lead List to initiate the rejection process. The system prompts the provider to select or enter a rejection reason (e.g., Does not meet criteria, No insurance coverage, Lead not responsive, Service area mismatch, Family decision) before the rejection is confirmed. Once rejected, the lead status changes to "Rejected" with the reason logged in the Activity Timeline. The rejected lead remains in the system for historical tracking and reporting purposes but is no longer active in the sales pipeline. This ensures proper documentation of why leads are rejected, enabling the provider to analyze patterns and potentially adjust their qualification criteria.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider selects "Reject" from the three-dot menu or Lead List action, Then the system must display a rejection reason dialog with predefined options and an optional free-text field.

2. If the provider selects a rejection reason and confirms, Then the system must update the lead status to "Rejected" and log the reason in the Activity Timeline.
   Else if the provider clicks Cancel, Then the rejection must be aborted and the lead must remain at its current status.

3. If the provider attempts to confirm rejection without selecting a reason, Then the system must display "Rejection reason is required" and prevent the action.

4. If the lead is rejected, Then it must remain in the system for historical tracking but be removed from the active sales pipeline view.

5. If the provider wants to re-activate a previously rejected lead, Then the system must allow status change from "Rejected" back to an active status with an appropriate log entry.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify clicking "Reject" from the three-dot menu on the Lead Profile Header opens a rejection reason dialog.
2. Verify selecting "Does not meet criteria" as the reason and confirming changes the lead status to "Rejected".
3. Verify the Activity Timeline shows a rejection entry with the reason "Does not meet criteria" and a timestamp.
4. Verify the Lead List Status column shows "Rejected" for the rejected lead.
5. Verify entering a custom free-text reason (e.g., "Patient relocated out of state") is accepted and saved.
6. Verify that a rejected lead can be re-activated by changing status back to "In-Progress".

Negative:
7. Verify that attempting to confirm rejection without selecting a reason shows "Rejection reason is required".
8. Verify that clicking Cancel on the rejection dialog aborts the rejection and retains the current status.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify the rejection reason dialog is a modal overlay with predefined reason options (radio buttons or dropdown) and an optional free-text area.
10. Verify the dialog has "Confirm" and "Cancel" buttons clearly styled.
11. Verify the rejected lead is visually distinguished in the Lead List (e.g., greyed out row or red status badge).

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that rejecting a lead that is currently assigned to a team member notifies the assignee.
13. Verify that rejecting a lead with active scheduled visits prompts the provider about pending visits.
14. Verify that multiple rejections (reject, re-activate, reject again) all log separate entries in the Activity Timeline.

Security:
15. Verify that only authorized providers can reject leads.
16. Verify that rejection actions are logged in the audit trail with the provider identity and timestamp.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the rejection reason dialog loads within 1 second.
2. Verify that confirming rejection updates the lead status within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to select call type, add pre-call notes, initiate a call with a lead and add in-call notes.
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to manage the complete call workflow with a lead through the Communication tab within the Lead Detail Tabs interface. The Communication - Call section provides a call type selector allowing the provider to choose the type of call, a pre-call notes text area where the provider can document talking points and objectives before making the call, and an initiate call button to start the call.
