During the call, an in-call notes area becomes available for the provider to document real-time conversation details, key points discussed, and action items. The call interface integrates with the lead profile, so the provider has access to the lead contact information, service type, and prior communication history while on the call. This structured call workflow ensures that every call is purposeful, well-documented, and contributes to the lead conversion process with complete records available for team review.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider navigates to the Call section, Then the system must display the call type selector, pre-call notes text area, and initiate call button.

2. If the provider selects a call type and clicks initiate call, Then the system must start the call and show the in-call notes area.
   Else if the provider clicks initiate call without selecting a call type, Then the system must display "Please select a call type before initiating the call".

3. If the provider enters pre-call notes before initiating the call, Then the notes must be saved and associated with the call record.
   Else if no pre-call notes are entered, Then the call must proceed without pre-call notes.

4. If the provider adds in-call notes during an active call, Then the notes must be saved in real-time.

5. When the call ends, Then the system must log the complete call record (type, pre-call notes, in-call notes, duration, timestamp) in the Activity Timeline.

6. If the call fails to connect, Then the system must display a connection error and allow the provider to retry or cancel.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify the Call section displays a call type selector with options
2. Verify selecting "Follow-up" as call type highlights it in the selector.
3. Verify typing "Discuss insurance options and tour schedule" in the pre-call notes text area saves the text.
4. Verify clicking the initiate call button starts the call and displays the in-call notes area.
5. Verify typing "Patient confirmed interest in ADH program, wants to schedule tour next week" in the in-call notes area during an active call saves the notes.
6. Verify that after ending the call, the call record appears in the Activity Timeline with type, pre-call notes, in-call notes, duration, and timestamp.

Negative:
7. Verify that clicking initiate call without selecting a call type shows "Please select a call type before initiating the call".
8. Verify that if the call fails to connect, an error message is displayed with retry and cancel options.
9. Verify that the in-call notes area is not accessible before the call is initiated.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify the call type selector is displayed as selectable buttons or radio options within the Call section.
11. Verify the pre-call notes text area is clearly labeled and supports multi-line input.
12. Verify the initiate call button is prominently styled (e.g., green phone icon).
13. Verify the in-call notes area appears during the active call with clear visual separation from pre-call notes.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify behavior when the provider navigates away during an active call — the call should continue and notes should be preserved.
15. Verify that very long pre-call notes (2000+ characters) are accepted and displayed correctly.
16. Verify that in-call notes are auto-saved periodically to prevent data loss on unexpected disconnection.

Security:
17. Verify that call records are accessible only to authorized team members.
18. Verify that call notes are transmitted over HTTPS.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Call section loads within 2 seconds when navigating to the Communication tab.
2. Verify that in-call notes are auto-saved within 1 second of typing.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to see the automatically generated AI call summary for each call.
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to view an AI-generated summary of each call made with a lead, providing a concise overview of key discussion points, decisions, and next steps. Within the Communication - Call section, after a call ends, an AI-generated call summary section automatically displays a summary that captures the main topics discussed, any commitments made, follow-up actions, and the overall sentiment of the conversation. The AI summary is also accessible from the Activity Timeline by clicking "View Summary" on a call entry. This AI-powered summarization saves providers significant time by eliminating the need to manually review lengthy call notes and ensures consistent, objective documentation of every call. The summary is generated from the combination of pre-call notes, in-call notes, and call metadata, providing a holistic view of the interaction. Providers can use these summaries to quickly prepare for follow-up calls and to share context with team members who may take over the lead.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When a call ends and notes are available, Then the system must automatically generate an AI call summary and display it in the call summary section.

2. If the call has both pre-call and in-call notes, Then the AI summary must incorporate context from both note sets.
   Else if only in-call notes exist, Then the summary must be generated from in-call notes alone.
   Else if no notes exist, Then the system must display "Insufficient data to generate call summary."

3. If the provider clicks "View Summary" on a call entry in the Activity Timeline, Then the AI summary must be shown inline as part of the expanded details.

4. If the AI summary generation fails due to a system error, Then the system must display "Summary generation failed. Please try again later." with a retry option.

5. If the provider views the summary immediately after the call ends, Then the system must show a loading indicator while the AI processes the summary.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that after ending a call with notes, an AI-generated call summary appears in the call summary section.
2. Verify the AI summary contains key discussion points extracted from the call notes.
3. Verify the AI summary lists follow-up actions identified from the conversation.
4. Verify that clicking "View Summary" on a call entry in the Activity Timeline shows the AI-generated summary.
5. Verify that each call with the same lead has its own independent AI summary.
6. Verify the AI summary is generated using both pre-call and in-call notes when both are available.

Negative:
7. Verify that a call with no notes shows "Insufficient data to generate call summary.".
8. Verify that if AI summary generation fails, "Summary generation failed. Please try again later." is displayed with a retry button.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify the AI call summary section is clearly labeled and positioned below the call notes.
10. Verify a loading indicator (spinner or skeleton) is shown while the AI summary is being generated.
11. Verify the summary text is formatted with clear sections (Key Points, Decisions, Follow-ups, Sentiment).
12. Verify the "View Summary" link in the Activity Timeline is clickable and expands smoothly.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that a very short call with minimal notes (e.g., "Left voicemail") still generates a meaningful summary.
14. Verify that a very long call with extensive notes (5000+ characters) generates a concise summary.
15. Verify that the AI summary is available even after navigating away and returning to the call record.

Security:
16. Verify that AI call summaries are accessible only to authorized team members.
17. Verify that sensitive lead information in the summary is handled in compliance with privacy policies.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the AI call summary is generated and displayed within 10 seconds of the call ending.
2. Verify that loading the summary from the Activity Timeline "View Summary" link takes less than 2 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to send SMS to lead
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to send SMS text messages to a lead directly from the Nemi Care Facility Portal  SMS section, the provider sees an SMS compose area where they can type a message, and a send button to deliver the message to the lead registered phone number. After sending, an AI-generated SMS summary section provides a brief overview of the communication. The sent SMS is logged in the Activity Timeline as an SMS entry with a timestamp and "View Summary" link. This functionality allows the provider to quickly reach out to leads who may prefer text communication, send appointment reminders, share brief updates, or follow up on previous conversations without leaving the portal. SMS communication is particularly effective for time-sensitive messages and for leads who may not respond to phone calls or emails.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider navigates to the SMS section , Then the system must display the SMS compose area and send button.

2. If the provider types a message and clicks Send, Then the system must deliver the SMS to the lead registered phone number and show a success confirmation.
   Else if the lead has no phone number on file, Then the system must display "Lead does not have a phone number on file" and prevent sending.

3. If the SMS is delivered successfully, Then the system must log it in the Activity Timeline with a timestamp.
   Else if the delivery fails (e.g., invalid number, network error), Then the system must display "SMS delivery failed" with the error reason.

4. If the provider attempts to send an empty SMS (no text), Then the system must display "Message cannot be empty" and prevent sending.

5. When an SMS is sent successfully, Then the system must generate an AI SMS summary and display it in the summary section.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify the SMS section displays an SMS compose area with a text input and a "Send Icon" button.
2. Verify typing "Hi John, following up on your interest in our ADH program" and clicking Send delivers the SMS.
3. Verify a success confirmation message appears after sending the SMS.
4. Verify the sent SMS appears in the Activity Timeline with a timestamp and "View Summary" link.
5. Verify the SMS record shows the full message text and delivery status.

Negative:
6. Verify that clicking Send with an empty message shows "Message cannot be empty".
7. Verify that sending SMS to a lead without a phone number shows "Lead does not have a phone number on file.".
8. Verify that if SMS delivery fails, "SMS delivery failed" is shown with the error reason.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify the SMS compose area supports multi-line text input.
10. Verify the Send button is clearly styled and positioned next to or below the compose area.
11. Verify the character count is displayed as the provider types the message.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify behavior when sending a very long SMS (500+ characters) — it should be sent as multiple parts if needed.
14. Verify behavior when sending SMS with special characters and emojis.
15. Verify behavior when the provider sends multiple SMS in rapid succession — each should be logged separately.

Security:
16. Verify that SMS content is transmitted securely.
17. Verify that SMS records are accessible only to authorized team members.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that the SMS is sent and delivery status is returned within 5 seconds.
2. Verify the SMS section loads within 2 seconds when navigating to the Communication tab.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to see the automatically generated AI SMS summary .
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to view an AI-generated summary for each SMS conversation with a lead, providing a quick overview of the communication content and context. Within the SMS summary section, after an SMS is sent, an AI-generated SMS summary section automatically displays a concise summary of the message. The AI summary is also accessible from the Activity Timeline by clicking "View Summary" on an SMS entry. This feature is particularly useful when multiple SMS exchanges have occurred, as the provider can quickly understand the overall thread without reading every individual message. The AI summarization leverages the message content to extract key information such as appointment confirmations, questions asked, and responses received. This ensures all team members have quick access to the gist of SMS communications with any lead.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When an SMS is sent, Then the system must automatically generate an AI SMS summary and display it in the summary section.

2. If the SMS content is substantial, Then the AI summary must capture key points, transcript, AI Summary, patient concerns and follow-ups.
   Else if the SMS content is very brief (e.g., "OK", "Yes"), Then the summary must provide contextual interpretation.

3. If the provider clicks "View Summary" on an SMS entry in the Activity Timeline, Then the AI summary must be shown inline.

4. If the AI summary generation fails, Then the system must display "Summary generation failed. Please try again later." with a retry option.

5. If the provider views the SMS section immediately after sending, Then a loading indicator must be shown while the AI processes the summary.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that after sending an SMS, an AI-generated SMS summary appears in the summary section.
2. Verify the summary captures the key pts (e.g., "Provider followed up regarding tour scheduling for ADH program").
3. Verify clicking "View Summary" on an SMS entry in the Activity Timeline shows the AI summary inline.
4. Verify that each SMS to the same lead has its own independent AI summary.
5. Verify the AI summary for a brief message (e.g., "Confirmed for 3 PM") provides contextual interpretation.

Negative:
6. Verify that if AI summary generation fails, "Summary generation failed. Please try again later." is displayed.
7. Verify that the retry button on a failed summary triggers regeneration.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify the AI SMS summary section is positioned below the sent message content.
9. Verify a loading indicator is shown while the summary is being generated.
10. Verify the summary text is concise and formatted for easy reading.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a single-word SMS (e.g., "Thanks") still generates a meaningful summary.
12. Verify that the summary is available after navigating away and returning to the SMS record.
13. Verify behavior when multiple SMS are sent rapidly — each should get its own summary.

Security:
14. Verify that AI SMS summaries are accessible only to authorized team members.
15. Verify that the AI processing does not expose SMS content to external systems.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the AI SMS summary is generated and displayed within 5 seconds of sending the message.
2. Verify that loading a summary from the Activity Timeline takes less than 2 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to add private notes to a lead
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to add private notes to a lead record that are visible only to the note creator within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. In the Communication - Notes section of the Lead Detail Tabs, the Private notes area allows the provider to type personal observations, reminders, or sensitive assessments about the lead that should not be shared with the broader team. The Private notes section includes a text area for composing the note and a Save button. Once saved, the private note is timestamped and associated with the lead record but remains invisible to all other team members, even those with access to the lead. This is important for providers to document personal impressions, internal assessments, or preliminary concerns that are not yet ready to be shared or that are relevant only to the individual provider managing the lead. Private notes appear in the creator Activity Timeline view but are hidden from other users Activity views.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider navigates to the Notes section, Then the system must display separate Private notes and Public notes areas.

2. If the provider types text in the Private notes area and clicks Save, Then the note must be saved with a timestamp and visible only to the creator.
   Else if the provider clicks Save with an empty text area, Then the system must display "Note content is required" and prevent saving.

3. If another team member views the same lead notes section, Then private notes created by other providers must not be visible.

4. If the provider edits an existing private note and saves, Then the updated text must be stored with an "edited" indicator and updated timestamp.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Notes section shows a Private notes area with a text area and Save button.
2. Verify typing "Lead seems hesitant about pricing — may need to offer payment plan options" and clicking Save stores the note with a timestamp.
3. Verify the saved private note is visible to the creator in the notes view.
4. Verify that another team member (Provider B) cannot see private notes created by Provider A when viewing the same lead.
5. Verify the private note entry displays the note text, timestamp, and a "Private" label.
6. Verify that editing a private note and saving updates the text and shows an "edited" indicator.

Negative:
7. Verify that clicking Save with an empty Private notes text area shows "Note content is required".
8. Verify that private notes are not visible in the Activity Timeline to other team members.
9. Verify that accessing the lead notes via API by an unauthorized user does not return private notes.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify the Private notes section is visually distinct from the Public notes section (e.g., labeled "Private" with a lock icon).
11. Verify the text area supports multi-line input with scrolling for long notes.
12. Verify the Save button is clearly positioned below the text area.
13. Verify the "Private" label or icon is visible on saved private note entries.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify saving a very long private note (5000+ characters) is accepted.
15. Verify behavior when the provider has 50+ private notes on a single lead — pagination or scrolling should work.
16. Verify that deleting a private note permanently removes it.

Security:
17. Verify that private notes are encrypted at rest and cannot be accessed by database queries from other users.
18. Verify that private notes are not included in lead export files.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that saving a private note completes within 2 seconds.
2. Verify that loading the Notes section with 50+ notes renders within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to add public notes to a lead
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to add public notes to a lead record that are visible to all team members with access to the lead within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. In the Communication - Notes section, the Public notes area allows the provider to document shared observations, updates, and follow-up information that the entire team should be aware of. The Public notes section includes a text area for composing the note and a Save button. Once saved, the public note is timestamped and visible to all team members who have access to the lead, facilitating team collaboration and ensuring everyone has the same information. Public notes appear in the Activity Timeline for all users, enabling transparent communication about lead progress and interactions. This is essential for team coordination, especially when leads are reassigned or when multiple team members interact with the same lead.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider navigates to the Notes section, Then the system must display the Public notes area with a text area and Save button.

2. If the provider types text in the Public notes area and clicks Save, Then the note must be saved with a timestamp and visible to all team members with lead access.
   Else if the provider clicks Save with an empty text area, Then the system must display "Note content is required" and prevent saving.

3. If a team member views the lead notes section, Then all public notes from all authors must be visible.

4. If the provider edits an existing public note, Then the updated text must be stored with an "edited" indicator, updated timestamp, and the original author attribution preserved.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify the Notes section shows a Public notes area with a text area and Save button.
2. Verify typing "Lead confirmed interest in ADH program, requesting tour next Tuesday" and clicking Save stores the note with a timestamp.
3. Verify the saved public note is visible to all team members with access to the lead.
4. Verify the public note appears in the Activity Timeline for all users.
5. Verify the note entry shows author name, note text, timestamp, and "Public" label.
6. Verify that multiple public notes from different team members are displayed in reverse chronological order.

Negative:
7. Verify that clicking Save with an empty Public notes text area shows "Note content is required".
8. Verify that a provider cannot edit or delete public notes authored by another team member.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify the Public notes section is visually distinct from Private notes (e.g., labeled "Public" with a team icon).
10. Verify each public note entry shows the author name and avatar/initials.
11. Verify the text area supports multi-line input.
12. Verify the "Public" label or icon is visible on saved public note entries.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that a public note saved by a team member who is later deactivated remains visible with their name.
14. Verify behavior when 100+ public notes exist on a single lead — pagination or lazy loading should work.
15. Verify that public notes are included in lead export if the export includes communication history.

Security:
16. Verify that public notes are only visible to authenticated team members with access to the lead.
17. Verify that public note content is sanitized to prevent XSS injection.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that saving a public note completes within 2 seconds.
2. Verify that loading public notes with 50+ entries renders within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to schedule a facility tour / Consultation visit with lead
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to schedule facility tours / consultation visit with leads through the Visit Management section of the Nemi Care Facility Portal. The Visit Management - Schedule Form provides fields for Lead Name, Visit Type (Facility Tour or Consultation), Date picker, Time picker, Location, and Notes. When the provider fills in the form and saves, the visit is created and appears on the Visit Management Calendar as a colored block and in the List View as a table row. The scheduled visit is also logged in the lead Activity Timeline as a "Visit Scheduled" entry with a timestamp. This capability ensures that tours / consultations are formally tracked, preventing double-booking and ensuring both the provider and the lead are prepared for the visit. Facility tours are often the decisive moment in lead conversion, making proper scheduling and preparation essential.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider clicks "Schedule Visit", Then the system must open the Schedule Form with all required fields.

2. If the provider fills all required fields (Lead Name, Visit Type, Date, Time) and clicks Save, Then the visit must be created and displayed on the calendar and list view.
   Else if any required field is missing, Then the system must highlight the missing field and prevent saving.

3. If the selected date and time conflict with an existing visit, Then the system must display a warning about the scheduling conflict.
   Else if no conflict exists, Then the visit must be saved normally.

4. If the provider selects "Facility Tour" as Visit Type, Then the calendar block should use the designated color for tours.
   Else if the provider selects "Consultation", Then the calendar block should use the designated color for consultations.

5. If the provider does not enter notes, Then the visit must be saved without notes (notes are optional).
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify clicking "Schedule Visit" opens the Schedule Form with fields: Lead Name, Visit Type, Date, Time, Location, Notes.
2. Verify selecting Lead Name "John Doe", Visit Type "Facility Tour", Date "2026-04-01", Time "10:00 AM", Location "Main Building" and clicking Save creates the visit.
3. Verify the visit appears on the calendar Day view as a colored block at 10:00 AM on April 1.
4. Verify the visit appears in List View with columns: Date "Apr 1, 2026", Time "10:00 AM", Lead Name "John Doe", Visit Type "Facility Tour", Status "Scheduled".
5. Verify a "Visit Scheduled" entry appears in the lead Activity Timeline.
6. Verify scheduling a "Consultation" visit type works correctly and appears with the appropriate color.

Negative:
7. Verify that saving without selecting a Visit Type shows a validation error.
8. Verify that saving without selecting a Date shows a validation error.
9. Verify that scheduling a visit at a time that conflicts with an existing visit shows a conflict warning.
10. Verify that selecting a past date shows a validation error or warning.

UI Scenarios:
11. Verify the Schedule Form has a clean layout with labeled fields and date/time pickers.
12. Verify the Visit Type dropdown shows "Facility Tour" and "Consultation" options.
13. Verify the calendar shows visits as colored blocks that are distinguishable by visit type.
14. Verify the List View table has properly aligned columns with sortable headers.

Edge Cases:
15. Verify scheduling multiple visits for the same lead on different dates works correctly.
16. Verify scheduling a visit at the end of business hours (e.g., 5:00 PM) is accepted.
17. Verify that the Notes field accepts long text (1000+ characters).

Security:
18. Verify that only authorized providers can schedule visits.
19. Verify that visit details are not accessible to unauthorized users.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Schedule Form loads within 2 seconds.
2. Verify saving a visit and reflecting it on the calendar takes less than 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to view scheduled visits in a calendar interface (Day, Week, and Month views), including both upcoming and past visits.
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to view all scheduled visits in a calendar interface with Day, Week, and Month view toggles within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. The Visit Management Calendar displays scheduled visits as colored blocks, with upcoming visits and past visits both visible for reference. The Day view shows hourly time slots with visits positioned at their scheduled times, the Week view shows a 7-day grid with visits distributed across days, and the Month view provides a high-level overview of all visits in the month. A toggle control at the top of the calendar allows switching between Day, Week, and Month views. Past visits are visually distinguished from upcoming visits (e.g., muted colors for past, vivid colors for upcoming). This calendar interface is essential for the provider to manage their schedule, avoid double-booking, and plan their time around lead visits effectively.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider navigates to the Visit Management calendar, Then the system must display the calendar in the default view with all scheduled visits.

2. If the provider clicks "Day" toggle, Then the calendar must switch to Day view with hourly slots.
   If the provider clicks "Week" toggle, Then the calendar must switch to Week view with a 7-day grid.
   If the provider clicks "Month" toggle, Then the calendar must switch to Month view.

3. If a visit is in the future, Then it must be displayed in vivid/active colors.
   Else if a visit is in the past, Then it must be displayed in muted/faded colors.

4. If the provider clicks on a visit block, Then the system must display the visit details in a popup or side panel.

5. If no visits are scheduled for the displayed period, Then the system must show an empty calendar with no blocks.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify the calendar loads in the default Week view showing scheduled visits as colored blocks.
2. Verify clicking "Day" toggle switches to Day view with hourly time slots.
3. Verify clicking "Week" toggle shows a 7-day grid with visits on respective days.
4. Verify clicking "Month" toggle shows a monthly overview with visit indicators on each day.
5. Verify upcoming visits are shown in vivid colors and past visits in muted colors.
6. Verify clicking on a visit block shows details: Lead Name "John Doe", Visit Type "Facility Tour", Time "10:00 AM", Status "Scheduled".

Negative:
7. Verify that a month with no scheduled visits shows an empty calendar.
8. Verify that clicking on an empty time slot does not cause errors.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify the Day/Week/Month toggle is prominently displayed at the top of the calendar.
10. Verify visit blocks are color-coded by visit type (e.g., blue for tours, green for consultations).
11. Verify the calendar has navigation arrows to move to previous/next day, week, or month.
12. Verify the current date is highlighted in the calendar.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify behavior when multiple visits are scheduled at overlapping times — they should be displayed side-by-side or stacked.
14. Verify that switching views rapidly (Day→Week→Month→Day) does not cause display issues.
15. Verify the calendar handles visits spanning different time zones correctly.

Security:
16. Verify that the calendar only shows visits the provider is authorized to view.
17. Verify that visit details in the calendar popup do not expose sensitive lead information to unauthorized users.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the calendar loads with 50+ visits within 3 seconds.
2. Verify switching between Day, Week, and Month views completes within 1 second.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to view Scheduled visits in List view.
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to view all scheduled visits in a tabular List View within the Nemi Care Facility Portal Visit Management section. The Visit Management List View displays visits in a table with columns for Date, Time, Lead Name, Visit Type, and Status. The list includes both upcoming and past visits, with clear sorting and visual distinction between them. The provider can sort by any column (e.g., by date, by lead name) and quickly scan multiple visits in a compact format that is more data-dense than the calendar view. This list view is particularly useful for administrative tasks like printing visit schedules, reviewing past visits, and getting a quick count of visits by type or status. The provider can switch between Calendar view and List view using a toggle or tab control.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider switches to List View, Then the system must display a table with all scheduled visits and columns

2. If the provider clicks a column header, Then the list must sort by that column.
   If clicked again, Then the sort order must toggle between ascending and descending.

3. If the provider clicks on a visit row, Then the system must open the visit details.

4. If the list has more visits than the page limit, Then pagination controls must be displayed.
   Else if all visits fit on one page, Then no pagination should be shown.

5. If no visits exist, Then the system must display "No scheduled visits" message.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify switching to List View displays a table with columns:
2. Verify each visit row shows data like Date "Apr 1, 2026", Time "10:00 AM", Lead Name "John Doe", Visit Type "Facility Tour", Status "Scheduled".
3. Verify clicking the Date column header sorts visits by date.
4. Verify clicking on a visit row opens the visit details.
5. Verify pagination works when there are 20+ visits.

Negative:
6. Verify that List View with no visits shows "No scheduled visits" message.
7. Verify that clicking on a column header for an unsortable column does not cause errors.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify the List View toggle is clearly accessible alongside the Calendar view toggle.
9. Verify the table has alternating row colors for readability.
10. Verify column headers are bold and clickable for sorting.
11. Verify upcoming visits and past visits are visually distinguishable (e.g., past visits in lighter text).

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that sorting by Lead Name handles names with special characters correctly.
13. Verify behavior with 500+ visits in the list — performance should remain acceptable.

Security:
14. Verify that the List View only shows visits the provider is authorized to see.
15. Verify that visit data cannot be scraped from the table via automated tools.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the List View loads with 100+ visits within 3 seconds.
2. Verify that sorting the list by any column completes within 1 second.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to edit the scheduled visit with Lead from both calender and list view.
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to edit an existing scheduled visit from either the Calendar view or the List View within the Nemi Care Facility Portal Visit Management section. When the provider clicks on a visit block in the Calendar or a visit row in the List View, the visit details open with an edit option. The edit form pre-populates with the current visit data and allows the provider to modify any field. After saving edits, the updated visit reflects immediately on both the Calendar and List View. This is important because visit schedules frequently change due to lead availability, provider conflicts, or rescheduling requests, and the system must support flexible updates. The edit action is also logged in the lead Activity Timeline to maintain a complete history of schedule changes.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider clicks edit on a visit from Calendar or List View, Then the system must open the edit form with all current visit data pre-populated.

2. If the provider modifies one or more fields and clicks Save, Then the system must validate and update the visit record.
   Else if validation fails, Then the system must show the specific error and prevent saving.

3. If the provider changes the date/time to a slot that conflicts with another visit, Then the system must show a scheduling conflict warning.
   Else if no conflict exists, Then the visit must be updated normally.

4. If the provider clicks Cancel, Then no changes must be saved.

5. When a visit is successfully edited, Then the change must be logged in the lead Activity Timeline.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify clicking a visit block in Calendar view and selecting "Edit" opens the edit form with pre-populated data.
2. Verify clicking a visit row in List View and selecting "Edit" opens the same edit form.
3. Verify changing Date from "Apr 1" to "Apr 5" and saving updates the visit on both Calendar and List View.
5. Verify the edit is logged in the lead Activity Timeline.
6. Verify clicking Cancel discards changes.

Negative:
7. Verify that clearing a required field (e.g., Date) and saving shows a validation error.
8. Verify that changing to a conflicting date/time shows a scheduling conflict warning.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify the edit form has the same layout as the schedule form with pre-populated values.
10. Verify Save and Cancel buttons are clearly positioned on the edit form.
11. Verify the edit option is accessible from both Calendar and List View interfaces.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify editing a past visit is either allowed with a warning or prevented with a message.
13. Verify that two providers editing the same visit simultaneously handles concurrency.
14. Verify editing a visit and saving with no actual changes does not create a false update log.

Security:
15. Verify that only authorized providers can edit visits.
16. Verify that edit actions are logged in the audit trail.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the visit edit form loads with pre-populated data within 2 seconds.
2. Verify saving an edited visit reflects the change on both views within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to apply filter on Scheduled visits
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to apply filters on scheduled visits in both the Calendar and List View within the Nemi Care Facility Portal Visit Management section. Filter options include Visit Type (Facility Tour, Consultation), Status (Scheduled, Completed, Cancelled), Lead Name, Date Range, and Assigned Staff. When filters are applied, only visits matching the criteria are displayed, helping the provider focus on specific subsets of their schedule. Active filters are indicated visually with badges or highlighted states, and a "Clear All Filters" option restores the full view. This is essential for providers managing a high volume of visits who need to quickly find tours scheduled for a specific week, view all completed consultations, or see visits assigned to a particular team member.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider clicks a filter control, Then the system must display relevant filter options.

2. If the provider selects one or more filter values, Then only visits matching all applied filters must be displayed.
   Else if no filters are applied, Then all visits must be shown.

3. If the applied filters return zero results, Then the system must display "No visits match the selected filters".

4. When the provider clicks "Clear All Filters", Then all filters must be removed and the full visit list must be restored.

5. If the provider applies filters in Calendar view, Then the same filters must carry over to List View and vice versa.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify clicking Visit Type filter shows options "Facility Tour" / "Consultation".
2. Verify selecting "Facility Tour" filters to show only tour visits.
3. Verify combining Visit Type "Facility Tour" and Status "Scheduled" shows only scheduled tours.
4. Verify active filters show visual indicators (badges/highlights).
5. Verify clicking "Clear All Filters" restores all visits.

Negative:
6. Verify applying filters that match no visits shows "No visits match the selected filters".

UI Scenarios:
7. Verify filter controls are accessible in both Calendar and List View.
8. Verify filter dropdowns are clearly labeled and easy to use.
9. Verify filter badges display the selected values.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify that filters applied in Calendar view persist when switching to List View.
11. Verify that applying and removing filters rapidly does not cause display glitches.

Security:
12. Verify filter parameters cannot be manipulated to view unauthorized visits.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify applying filters on 200+ visits updates the view within 2 seconds.
2. Verify clearing all filters restores the full view within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to add Scheduled visit notes
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to add notes to a scheduled visit, documenting observations, outcomes, and follow-up items from the visit within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. When viewing a visit detail (accessed from either the Calendar or List View), the provider can enter notes in the Notes field of the Visit Management Schedule Form. Notes can be added before the visit (preparation notes), during the visit (real-time observations), or after the visit (outcome and follow-up). Visit notes are saved with the visit record and visible to all team members with access to the lead. These notes also appear in the Activity Timeline when expanding the visit entry via "View Summary". This ensures comprehensive documentation of each visit interaction, which is critical for lead follow-up and for transferring context when a lead is reassigned to a different team member.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider opens a visit detail, Then the system must display the Notes field with any existing notes.

2. If the provider enters notes and clicks Save, Then the notes must be stored with the visit record.
   Else if the provider leaves the Notes field empty, Then the visit must be saved without notes.

3. If the provider updates existing notes, Then the system must save the updated version.

4. If visit notes exist, Then they must be displayed when expanding the visit in the Activity Timeline.

5. If the provider attempts to navigate away with unsaved notes, Then the system must warn about unsaved changes.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify the visit detail displays a Notes field for entering visit notes.
2. Verify typing "Lead was very interested in ADH program, asked about transportation" and saving stores the notes.
3. Verify saved notes are visible to other team members viewing the same visit.
4. Verify expanding the visit entry in the Activity Timeline via "View Summary" shows the notes.
5. Verify editing existing notes and saving updates the notes correctly.

Negative:
6. Verify that navigating away with unsaved notes triggers a warning dialog.

UI Scenarios:
7. Verify the Notes field is a multi-line text area with adequate size.
8. Verify the Notes field is clearly labeled within the visit detail view.
9. Verify the Save button is positioned near the Notes field.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify saving very long notes (3000+ characters) is accepted.
11. Verify that notes with special characters and line breaks display correctly.

Security:
12. Verify that visit notes are accessible only to authorized team members.
13. Verify that notes content is sanitized to prevent XSS.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify saving visit notes completes within 2 seconds.
2. Verify loading visit details with notes takes less than 2 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to view and send the the private pay rate card to Lead
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to view the Private Pay rate card and send it to a lead from within the Rate & Pricing tab of the Lead Detail Tabs interface in the Nemi Care Facility Portal. The Rate Card displays service rates in a table or card format showing the provider-set rates for Private Pay patients across different service categories. A "Send to Lead" button allows the provider to share the rate card with the lead via email or the portal communication channel. Private Pay rates are configurable by the provider and may vary based on the level of care and services included. This capability is essential during the lead conversion process when prospects need pricing information to make their decision, and it ensures that accurate, up-to-date rates are shared rather than outdated paper documents. The rate card send action is logged in the Activity Timeline for tracking.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider clicks the Send Rate Card tab, Then the system must display the Private Pay rate card with current provider-set rates.

2. If the provider clicks "Send to Lead" and the lead has a valid email, Then the system must send the rate card to the lead email and show a success confirmation.
   Else if the lead has no email on file, Then the system must display "Lead does not have an email address on file" and prevent sending.

3. If the rate card is sent successfully, Then the system must log the action in the Activity Timeline.

4. If the provider-set rates have been updated since the last view, Then the rate card must display the latest rates.

5. If the lead service type is "ADH", Then the rate card must show rates specific to ADH services.
   Else if the service type is "ALF", Then ALF-specific rates must be shown.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify clicking Rate & Pricing tab displays the Private Pay rate card with service rates in a table format.
2. Verify the rate card shows current provider-set rates for service categories.
3. Verify clicking "Send to Lead" sends the rate card to the lead email and shows a success message.
4. Verify a "Rate Card Sent" entry appears in the Activity Timeline with a timestamp.
5. Verify the rate card shows service-specific rates based on the lead service type.

Negative:
6. Verify that clicking "Send to Lead" for a lead without an email shows "Lead does not have an email address on file".
7. Verify that if the email delivery fails, an error message is displayed.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify the rate card is displayed in a clean table or card layout with service names and rates.
9. Verify the "Send to Lead" button is prominently displayed below or beside the rate card.
10. Verify the Rate & Pricing tab is accessible within the Lead Detail Tabs.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify sending the rate card multiple times to the same lead creates separate log entries.
12. Verify the rate card reflects updated rates if the provider changes prices between views.

Security:
13. Verify that rate card data is accessible only to authorized providers.
14. Verify that the sent rate card email uses HTTPS for any embedded links.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Rate & Pricing tab loads the rate card within 2 seconds.
2. Verify sending the rate card completes within 5 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to view and send the fixed, state-mandated Medicaid rates for Medicaid patients as configured by the super admin,
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to view the fixed, state-mandated Medicaid rates and send them to leads who are Medicaid patients within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. Unlike Private Pay rates which are set by the provider, Medicaid rates are fixed by the state and configured in the system by the super admin. The Rate Card for Medicaid patients displays these state-mandated rates in a table or card format with a "Send to Lead" button. The Medicaid rates are read-only for the provider — they can view and share them but cannot modify them. The payment type tag (Medicaid) on the Lead Profile Header indicates which rate card to display. This functionality ensures that Medicaid patients receive accurate, compliant rate information as mandated by the state, and that there is no risk of the provider accidentally sharing incorrect rates.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider views the Send rate card tab for a Medicaid lead, Then the system must display the state-mandated Medicaid rates configured by the super admin.

2. If the lead payment type is "Medicaid", Then the Medicaid rate card must be shown.
   Else if the payment type is "Private Pay", Then the Private Pay rate card must be shown.

3. If the provider clicks "Send to Lead" and the lead has a valid email, Then the Medicaid rate card must be sent and a success message displayed.
   Else if the lead has no email, Then the system must show an error.

4. If the provider attempts to modify Medicaid rates, Then the system must prevent editing and display the rates as read-only.

5. When the rate card is sent, Then the action must be logged in the Activity Timeline.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Send rate card tab for a Medicaid lead shows state-mandated Medicaid rates.
2. Verify the Medicaid rates match the values configured by the super admin.
3. Verify clicking "Send to Lead" sends the Medicaid rate card to the lead email.
4. Verify a "Medicaid Rate Card Sent" entry appears in the Activity Timeline.
5. Verify that the payment type tag "Medicaid" on the Lead Profile Header corresponds to displaying Medicaid rates.

Negative:
6. Verify that the provider cannot edit Medicaid rates — all rate fields are read-only.
7. Verify sending to a lead without email shows an error message.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify the Medicaid rate card is visually distinct from the Private Pay card (e.g., different header or label).
9. Verify rate fields are displayed as read-only (greyed out or non-editable).
10. Verify the "Send to Lead" button is functional on the Medicaid rate card.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that if the super admin updates Medicaid rates, the provider sees the updated rates on next load.
12. Verify behavior for a lead with both Medicaid and Private Pay — the system should show the appropriate card.

Security:
13. Verify that Medicaid rate modification is restricted to super admin only.
14. Verify that rate data transmitted to the lead is accurate and tamper-proof.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Medicaid rate card loads within 2 seconds.
2. Verify sending the Medicaid rate card completes within 5 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to select a Case Management Agency and request for the case manager to the agency
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to select a Case Management Agency and Request for the case manager to the case agency for a Medicaid lead as part of the documentation stepper workflow in the Nemi Care Facility Portal. The Case Agency Selection screen displays cards for available agencies, each showing Agency Name (e.g., "Binford Ltd."), Active status badge (green), email, phone number, and Response time (e.g., "4 Days"). This is Step 1 in both the ADH Documentation Stepper and the ALF Documentation Stepper for Medicaid patients. The provider reviews available agencies, selects the most appropriate one based on response time and availability, and sends the referral package containing the lead information and required documents. This step initiates the formal Medicaid qualification process and is the entry point into the case management workflow. Without selecting an agency and sending the referral, the lead cannot progress through the remaining documentation steps.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider reaches Step 1 of the documentation stepper for a Medicaid lead, Then the system must display available Case Management Agency cards with their details.

2. If the provider selects an agency and clicks "Request for case manager", Then the system must send the referral and mark Step 1 as complete.
   Else if no agency is selected and the provider tries to send, Then the system must display "Please select an agency before sending the referral".

3. If the referral is sent successfully, Then the system must allow progression to Step 2.
   Else if the send fails, Then the system must display an error and allow retry.

4. If only one agency is available, Then the system must still display it as a selectable card.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify Step 1 displays agency cards with Name "Binford Ltd.", Active badge (green), email, phone, and Response time "4 Days".
2. Verify clicking on an agency card highlights it as selected.
3. Verify clicking "Request for case manager" after selecting an agency sends the referral and shows a confirmation.
4. Verify Step 1 is marked as complete after sending the referral & requesting the case manager.
5. Verify the Activity Timeline shows "Request Sent to Binford Ltd." entry.
6. Verify the provider can proceed to Step 2 after completing Step 1.

Negative:
7. Verify that clicking "Request for case manager" without selecting an agency shows "Please select an agency".
8. Verify that if the send fails, an error message is displayed with a retry option.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify agency cards are displayed in a grid or list layout with consistent formatting.
10. Verify the Active status badge is green and clearly visible on each agency card.
11. Verify the 5-step progress bar is visible at the top showing Step 1 as the current step.
12. Verify the selected agency card has a distinct highlight (border, background color).

Edge Cases:
13. Verify behavior when no agencies are available — the system should display "No agencies available".
14. Verify that changing the selected agency before sending works correctly.
15. Verify behavior when the agency email is invalid — the system should handle gracefully.

Security:
16. Verify that referral packages are sent securely and contain only authorized lead information.
17. Verify that only authorized providers can send referral packages.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify agency cards load within 2 seconds.
2. Verify sending the requesting for case manager within 5 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to add generic notes to the referral form before sending to the case management agency.
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to add generic notes to the referral form before sending the referral package to the selected Case Management Agency within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. On the Case Agency Selection screen (Step 1 of the documentation stepper), after selecting an agency, the provider can enter free-text notes in a notes field. These notes provide additional context about the lead such as specific care needs, urgency level, insurance details, or any special considerations that the case management agency should be aware of.  This capability ensures that the agency receives complete, contextual information alongside the standard referral data, reducing the need for back-and-forth communication. The notes are also stored in the lead record for reference by the provider team.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider selects an agency  and request for case manager, Then the system must show a notes field for generic notes.

2. If the provider enters notes and request for case manager, Then the notes must be included in the request
   Else if no notes are entered, Then the request must be sent without notes.

3. If the provider enters notes, Then the notes must also be stored in the lead record for future reference.

4. If the notes contain more than the maximum allowed characters, Then the system must display a character limit warning.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify the system displays a notes field after requesting for case agency.
2. Verify typing "Patient requires wheelchair accessibility, primary language is Spanish" in the notes field captures the text.
4. Verify the referral notes are stored in the lead record and viewable after sending.
5. Verify sending the referral without notes works correctly (notes are optional).

Negative:
6. Verify that exceeding the maximum character limit shows a warning.

UI Scenarios:
7. Verify the notes field is a multi-line text area clearly labeled "Notes" or "Additional Notes".
9. Verify a character counter is shown if there is a character limit.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify entering notes with special characters, line breaks, and formatting is preserved.
11. Verify that editing notes after the referral is sent does not change the already-sent package.

Security:
12. Verify that notes content is sanitized before requesting for the case agency.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify saving notes completes within 1 second.
2. Verify sending the referral package with notes completes within 5 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to select a Case Manager and send the required documents for the lead
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to select a specific Case Manager from the assigned Case Management Agency and send the required documents for the lead within the Nemi Care Facility Portal documentation stepper. As part of the documentation workflow (Step 2 for ADH: Patient Required Doc / Step 2 for ALF: Patient & CM Setup), the provider needs to designate a case manager who will handle the lead case. The system displays a list of case managers associated with the selected agency, and the provider selects one. The provider then sends the required documents (as specified in the Document List: Mandatory Documents such as Intake Form, Contract Of Patient, State Specific Documents, TB Test, Adult Day Health Agreement) to the case manager. The 3-Column Doc Layout tracks the status of documents "Send To Patient", "Send To Case Manager", and "Upload Documents in System". This step is critical for advancing the Medicaid qualification process.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider reaches the Case Manager selection step, Then the system must display case managers associated with the selected agency.

2. If the provider selects a case manager and clicks "Send Documents", Then the system must send the required documents and update the tracking status.
   Else if no case manager is selected, Then the system must display "Please select a case manager" and prevent sending.

3. If documents are sent successfully, Then the step must be marked as complete.
   Else if sending fails, Then an error must be displayed with a retry option.

4. If the selected agency has no case managers, Then the system must display "No case managers available for this agency".

5. When documents are sent, Then the 3-Column Doc Layout must update the "Send To Case Manager" column status.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify the Case Manager selection step shows a list of case managers from the selected agency.
2. Verify selecting a case manager highlights the selection.
3. Verify clicking "Send Documents" sends required documents (Intake Form, Contract, State Specific Documents, TB Test, etc.) to the case manager.
4. Verify the "Send To Case Manager" column in the document tracking layout updates to "Sent" for each document.
5. Verify the step is marked as complete after successful document sending.

Negative:
6. Verify that clicking "Send Documents" without selecting a case manager shows "Please select a case manager".
7. Verify that if the agency has no case managers, "No case managers available for this agency" is displayed.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify case managers are displayed in a selectable list with names and contact details.
9. Verify the 3-Column Doc Layout shows "Send To Patient", "Send To Case Manager", "Upload Documents in System" columns.
10. Verify the stepper progress bar updates to show the current step.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify behavior when only one case manager is available — it should still be selectable.
12. Verify that sending documents to a case manager whose email is invalid shows an error.

Security:
13. Verify that documents are sent securely and contain only authorized patient information.
14. Verify that only authorized providers can send documents to case managers.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the case manager list loads within 2 seconds.
2. Verify sending documents completes within 5 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to View and Send patient required and optional documents to the patient. e.g; Intake form, Contract  etc.
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to view and send both mandatory and optional documents to a patient (lead) from the Documentation section of the Nemi Care Facility Portal. The Document List screen has a left panel showing "Mandatory Documents" (Intake Form, Contract Of Patient, State Specific Documents, TB Test, Adult Day Health Agreement) each with an eye icon for preview, and a right panel showing "Optional Documents" (Insurance Details, Document 1, Document 2) with checkboxes and eye icons. A "Send To Patient" button at the bottom allows the provider to send the selected documents to the patient. The 3-Column Doc Layout tracks document status across "Send To Patient", "Send To Case Manager", and "Upload Documents in System" columns. This ensures patients receive all necessary paperwork for onboarding, and the provider can track which documents have been sent, received, and completed.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider navigates to the Documentation tab, Then the system must display Mandatory Documents and Optional Documents in their respective panels.

2. If the provider clicks "Send To Patient", Then all mandatory documents must be included automatically.
   If optional documents are checked, Then they must also be included in the send.
   Else if no optional documents are checked, Then only mandatory documents are sent.

3. If the provider clicks an eye icon, Then the system must display a preview of that document.

4. If documents are sent successfully, Then the "Send To Patient" column in the tracking layout must update to "Sent".
   Else if sending fails, Then an error must be displayed with a retry option.

5. If the patient email is not on file, Then the system must display "Patient does not have an email on file" and prevent sending.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify the Documentation tab shows Mandatory Documents: Intake Form, Contract Of Patient, State Specific Documents, TB Test, Adult Day Health Agreement.
2. Verify Optional Documents are shown with checkboxes: Insurance Details, Document 1, Document 2.
3. Verify clicking an eye icon on "Intake Form" opens a preview of the document.
4. Verify checking "Insurance Details" checkbox and clicking "Send To Patient" sends mandatory + selected optional documents.
5. Verify the "Send To Patient" column updates to "Sent" after successful delivery.
6. Verify clicking "Send To Patient" without checking any optional documents sends only mandatory documents.

Negative:
7. Verify that sending documents to a patient without an email shows an error.
8. Verify that if document sending fails, an error with retry option is displayed.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify the left panel for Mandatory Documents and right panel for Optional Documents are clearly separated.
10. Verify eye icons are clickable and positioned next to each document name.
11. Verify checkboxes on optional documents are functional and visually clear.
12. Verify the "Send To Patient" button is prominently displayed at the bottom.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify resending documents that were already sent updates the tracking status.
14. Verify behavior when a document file is missing from the system — it should show an error indicator.

Security:
15. Verify that documents are sent securely via encrypted channels.
16. Verify that only authorized providers can view and send patient documents.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Document List loads within 2 seconds.
2. Verify document preview loads within 3 seconds.
3. Verify sending documents completes within 5 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to mark each mandatory document as received/not received
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to track the receipt status of each mandatory document from the patient within the Nemi Care Facility Portal Documentation section. For each mandatory document (Intake Form, Contract Of Patient, State Specific Documents, TB Test, Adult Day Health Agreement), the provider can mark it as "Received" or "Not Received". This status tracking is visible in the document tracking layout and provides a clear view of which documents are still pending. The provider can update the status as documents come in, maintaining an accurate and up-to-date picture of the documentation progress. This is essential for the onboarding workflow because all mandatory documents must be received before the lead can progress through the documentation stepper to completion. The receipt status helps the provider identify follow-up actions needed to collect outstanding documents.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider views mandatory documents, Then each document must display a status indicator ("Received" or "Not Received").

2. If the provider toggles a document to "Received", Then the status must update with a green indicator.
   Else if the provider toggles it to "Not Received", Then the status must show a red or grey indicator.

3. If all mandatory documents are marked "Received", Then the documentation step must be marked as complete.
   Else if any mandatory document is "Not Received", Then the step must remain incomplete.

4. When the provider changes a document status, Then the change must be logged with a timestamp.

5. If the provider refreshes the page, Then all document statuses must persist as previously set.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify each mandatory document has a "Received"/"Not Received" status indicator.
2. Verify marking "Intake Form" as "Received" updates the indicator to green.
3. Verify marking "TB Test" as "Not Received" shows a red or grey indicator.
4. Verify toggling a document from "Received" back to "Not Received" updates correctly.
5. Verify that marking all 5 mandatory documents as "Received" marks the documentation step as complete.

Negative:
6. Verify that the step cannot be completed if even one mandatory document is "Not Received".

UI Scenarios:
7. Verify the status indicators are visually clear (green checkmark for Received, red X or grey for Not Received).
8. Verify the toggle/button for changing status is intuitive and accessible.
9. Verify the document tracking layout shows accurate counts of received vs. pending.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify that rapidly toggling a document status does not cause data inconsistency.
11. Verify that status changes persist after page refresh.

Security:
12. Verify that only authorized providers can mark document receipt status.
13. Verify that status changes are logged in the audit trail.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify toggling a document status updates within 1 second.
2. Verify the document list with statuses loads within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to resend the documents to the patient if required.
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to resend documents to a patient when the original send was unsuccessful, the patient did not receive them, or the documents need to be sent again for any reason within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. In the Documentation section, for each document that has been previously sent, the provider can click a resend option to re-deliver the document to the patient email. This is essential for cases where the patient email had issues, the documents were lost, the patient accidentally deleted the email, or updated versions of documents need to be sent. Each resend action is logged in the Activity Timeline and the document tracking layout updates to show the latest send timestamp. The resend functionality is available for both mandatory and optional documents that were previously selected for sending.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider views a previously sent document, Then the system must show a "Resend" option.

2. If the provider clicks "Resend" and the patient has a valid email, Then the document must be re-delivered and a success message shown.
   Else if the patient has no valid email, Then the system must display an error and prevent resending.

3. When a document is resent, Then the tracking timestamp must update to the resend time.

4. When a document is resent, Then the action must be logged in the Activity Timeline.

5. If the resend fails due to a network error, Then the system must show "Resend failed. Please try again." with a retry option.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify a "Resend" option is available for previously sent documents.
2. Verify clicking "Resend" on "Intake Form" re-delivers it to the patient email and shows a success message.
3. Verify the document tracking timestamp updates to the latest resend time.
4. Verify a "Document Resent" entry appears in the Activity Timeline.
5. Verify resending multiple documents individually works correctly.

Negative:
6. Verify that resending to a patient without a valid email shows an error.
7. Verify that a "Resend" option is not available for documents that were never sent.
8. Verify that if resend fails, "Resend failed. Please try again." is shown.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify the "Resend" button/link is positioned next to each previously sent document.
10. Verify a loading indicator is shown during the resend process.
11. Verify the success message is clear and dismissible.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify resending the same document multiple times creates separate log entries.
13. Verify that resending works after the patient email has been updated.

Security:
14. Verify that only authorized providers can resend documents.
15. Verify documents are resent over secure channels.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify resending a document completes within 5 seconds.
2. Verify the resend action and tracking update occur without page reload.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to view and sign documents received from Patient
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to view documents that have been returned/uploaded by the patient and digitally sign them within the Nemi Care Facility Portal Documentation section. When the patient completes and submits documents (e.g., signed Intake Form, Contract Of Patient), they appear in the "Upload Documents in System" column of the 3-Column Doc Layout. The provider can click the eye icon to preview each received document, review its contents, and then apply their digital signature where required. The document signing workflow ensures that both parties (patient and provider) have signed the necessary documents before the onboarding process can proceed. Signed documents are stored securely in the system with an immutable audit trail showing who signed and when. This digital signing capability eliminates the need for paper-based signatures, speeding up the documentation process.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the patient submits documents, Then the system must display them in the "Upload Documents in System" column with preview icons.

2. If the provider clicks the eye icon, Then the system must display a full preview of the document.

3. If the provider clicks "Sign", Then the system must apply the digital signature and update the document status to "Signed by Provider".
   Else if the provider chooses not to sign, Then the document must remain in "Pending Signature" status.

4. If all required documents are signed, Then the documentation step must be completable.
   Else if any required document is unsigned, Then the step must remain incomplete.

5. When a document is signed, Then an immutable audit entry must be created with the signer identity and timestamp.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify received documents appear in the "Upload Documents in System" column.
2. Verify clicking the eye icon opens a full preview of the received document.
3. Verify clicking "Sign" applies the provider digital signature to the document.
4. Verify the document status updates to "Signed by Provider" with a timestamp.
5. Verify that all required signed documents allow the step to be marked complete.

Negative:
6. Verify that attempting to sign a corrupt or unreadable document shows an error.
7. Verify that the step cannot be completed if any required document lacks the provider signature.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify the document preview opens in a modal or full-screen viewer with zoom capability.
9. Verify the "Sign" button is clearly positioned on the document preview.
10. Verify signed documents show a visual indicator (e.g., green checkmark, "Signed" badge).

Edge Cases:
11. Verify signing a large document (50+ pages) works without timeout.
12. Verify that once signed, the document cannot be unsigned or modified.

Security:
13. Verify digital signatures use cryptographic methods ensuring non-repudiation.
14. Verify signed documents are stored with tamper-proof integrity checks.
15. Verify that only the authorized provider can apply their signature.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify document preview loads within 3 seconds.
2. Verify applying a digital signature completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to send signature reminders to pending signatories.
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to send signature reminders to patients or other parties who have pending document signatures within the Nemi Care Facility Portal Documentation section. When documents have been sent to a patient but signatures are still pending, the provider can identify which documents are awaiting signatures and send reminder notifications to prompt the patient to complete signing. The reminder can be sent via email or SMS based on the patient contact preferences. This is essential for maintaining momentum in the onboarding process, as documentation delays can stall lead conversion and admission. Each reminder sent is logged in the Activity Timeline, and the document tracking shows the last reminder sent date. The provider can send multiple reminders for the same document if needed.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When documents have pending signatures, Then the system must display a "Send Reminder" option for each pending document.

2. If the provider clicks "Send Reminder" and the patient has valid contact information, Then the system must send a reminder notification and show a success message.
   Else if the patient has no valid email or phone, Then the system must display "Unable to send reminder — patient contact information is missing".

3. When a reminder is sent, Then the system must log it in the Activity Timeline and update the last reminder date.

4. If the provider sends multiple reminders for the same document, Then each reminder must be logged separately.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify a "Send Reminder" option is available for documents with pending signatures.
2. Verify clicking "Send Reminder" sends a notification to the patient and shows a success message.
3. Verify a "Signature Reminder Sent" entry appears in the Activity Timeline.
4. Verify the document tracking shows the last reminder sent date.
5. Verify sending multiple reminders for the same document creates separate log entries.

Negative:
6. Verify that sending a reminder when patient has no contact info shows "Unable to send reminder — patient contact information is missing".

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify the "Send Reminder" button/link is positioned next to each pending document.
9. Verify pending documents are visually distinguished from signed documents.
10. Verify the success message for reminder sent is clear and dismissible.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify behavior when a reminder is sent and the patient signs before receiving it — the reminder should not cause issues.
12. Verify that sending reminders for multiple documents simultaneously works correctly.

Security:
13. Verify that only authorized providers can send signature reminders.
14. Verify that reminders do not expose document content in the notification — only a link to sign.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify sending a signature reminder completes within 3 seconds.
2. Verify the Documentation tab loads with pending signature indicators within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to view the Medicaid qualification probability score for a lead.
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to view a Medicaid qualification probability score for each lead within the Nemi Care Facility Portal, helping them assess the likelihood of a lead qualifying for Medicaid coverage. The score is calculated based on the lead information such as income, age, disability status, household size, and other Medicaid eligibility factors. The probability score is displayed on the Lead Profile or within the ADH Lead Management section as a percentage or rating (e.g., High, Medium, Low). This score helps providers prioritize their efforts — leads with higher probability of Medicaid qualification may be faster to convert through the Medicaid workflow, while low-probability leads may be better suited for Private Pay pathways. The score is a decision-support tool and does not replace the formal Medicaid qualification determination by the state or case management agency.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider views a lead profile, Then the system must display the Medicaid qualification probability score.

2. If the lead has sufficient data for scoring, Then the system must calculate and display the probability as a percentage or rating (High/Medium/Low).
   Else if the lead has insufficient data, Then the system must display "Insufficient data for scoring".

3. If the lead information is updated, Then the probability score must recalculate on the next view.

4. If the provider views the lead list, Then leads with high probability should be identifiable (e.g., via a score column or indicator).

5. If the Medicaid probability score calculation service is unavailable, Then the system must display "Score unavailable" with a retry option.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify the lead profile displays a Medicaid qualification probability score (e.g., "78%" or "High").
2. Verify the score reflects the lead current data (income, age, disability status).
3. Verify that updating lead income from "$15,000" to "$25,000" changes the probability score.
4. Verify high-probability leads are identifiable in the lead list.
5. Verify the score is displayed as a visual indicator (progress bar, badge, or percentage).

Negative:
6. Verify that a lead with insufficient data shows "Insufficient data for scoring" instead of a false score.
7. Verify that if the scoring service is down, "Score unavailable" with retry is displayed.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify the probability score is displayed prominently on the lead profile (e.g., colored badge — green for High, yellow for Medium, red for Low).
9. Verify the score section includes a label like "Medicaid Qualification Probability".
10. Verify hovering over the score shows a tooltip explaining the contributing factors.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify the score updates in real-time when lead data changes without requiring a manual refresh.
12. Verify scores at boundary values (e.g., exactly 50%) are categorized correctly.

Security:
13. Verify that Medicaid probability scores are accessible only to authorized providers.
14. Verify that the scoring algorithm cannot be reverse-engineered from the displayed score.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Medicaid probability score loads within 2 seconds on the lead profile.
2. Verify score recalculation after data update completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to send state transport request to Verida with details
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to submit a state transport request to Verida (a transportation service provider) with patient details as part of the ADH documentation stepper workflow in the Nemi Care Facility Portal. In the ADH Documentation Stepper Step 3  (Transportation Request), the provider fills out a transport request form with patient details including name, address, pickup location, facility address, scheduled service date. The form is submitted directly to Verida through the system integration. Transportation is a critical component of ADH (Adult Day Health) services, as many patients rely on provided transportation to attend daily health programs. Without this integration, the provider would need to separately contact Verida via phone or email, creating delays and potential errors in the transport setup process.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider reaches Step 3 of the ADH stepper, Then the system must display the Verida transport request form with all required fields.

2. If the provider fills all required fields and submits, Then the system must send the request to Verida and mark Step 4 as complete.
   Else if required fields are missing, Then the system must highlight the missing fields and prevent submission.

3. If the submission to Verida fails, Then the system must display an error message and allow retry.

4. When the request is submitted, Then the action must be logged in the Activity Timeline.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify Step 3 displays the transport request form with fields: patient name, address, pickup location, facility address, scheduled dates, special needs.
