21. Verify that uploaded files are scanned for malicious content (e.g., macro-enabled Excel files) before processing.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that importing a CSV file with 500 resident records completes within 10 seconds.
2. Verify that exporting the full resident list of 1000+ records generates the download file within 5 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to view resident basic details in header bar
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to view key resident information at a glance through a profile header bar displayed at the top of the resident detail page within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. The Resident Header Bar shows the resident's profile photo, full name, status badge (e.g., Active, In-progress, New Arrival, Discharged), and service type tags displayed as colored badges (e.g., ADH, ALF). Additionally, the header displays payment type information (e.g., Medicaid, Private Pay), the resident's age, email address, phone number, gender, and physical address. This header layout is similar to the Lead Profile Header from Lead Management, providing a consistent UI pattern across the application. The header bar remains visible as a persistent section at the top of the resident detail page, providing essential context regardless of which tab or section the provider is viewing below. This quick-reference header is vital for providers to confirm they are viewing the correct resident and to access fundamental contact and demographic information without navigating away from their current task.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider navigates to a resident's detail page, Then the system must load and display the Resident Header Bar with the resident's photo, name, status badge, service type tags, payment type, age, email, phone, gender, and address.

2. If the resident has a profile photo uploaded, Then the system must display the photo in the header.
   Else if no profile photo is available, Then the system must display a default avatar placeholder with the resident's initials.

3. If the resident is assigned service types ADH and ALF, Then both tags must be displayed as separate colored badges in the header.
   Else if the resident has only one service type, Then only that single tag must be shown.

4. If the resident's payment type is "Medicaid", Then the payment type must be displayed as "Medicaid" in the header.
   Else if the payment type is "Private Pay", Then it must be displayed as "Private Pay".

5. If any demographic field (email, phone, address) is not available for the resident, Then the header must display a dash or "N/A" for that field instead of leaving it blank.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that navigating to resident "Maria Garcia" detail page displays the Resident Header Bar with her profile photo, name "Maria Garcia", and status badge "Active".
2. Verify the header shows service type tags "ADH" and "ALF" as colored badges for a resident enrolled in both services.
3. Verify the header displays payment type "Medicaid" for a Medicaid-enrolled resident.
4. Verify the header displays payment type "Private Pay" for a privately paying resident.
5. Verify the header shows age "72", email "maria.garcia@email.com", phone "(555) 123-4567", gender "Female", and address "123 Oak Street, Miami, FL 33101".
6. Verify the Resident Header Bar remains visible when scrolling down the resident detail page.
7. Verify the header displays correctly for a resident with only one service type (e.g., "ADH" only).

Negative:
8. Verify that a resident with no profile photo uploaded displays a default avatar placeholder with initials (e.g., "MG" for Maria Garcia).
9. Verify that a resident with missing email shows "N/A" in the email field of the header.
10. Verify that a resident with missing phone number shows "N/A" in the phone field.

UI Scenarios:
11. Verify the profile photo is displayed on the left side of the header bar.
12. Verify the status badge uses color coding consistent with the Residents List (e.g., green for Active, yellow for In-progress).
13. Verify the service type tags are displayed as distinct colored badges that are visually differentiated (e.g., ADH in blue, ALF in green).
15. Verify the demographic details (age, email, phone, gender, address) are arranged in a readable layout within the header.

Edge Cases:
16. Verify the header displays correctly for a resident with a very long name (e.g., 50+ characters) without truncation issues or layout break.
17. Verify the header handles a resident with multiple service type tags (e.g., 3 or more) without layout overflow.
18. Verify the header renders correctly when the resident has no address on file — "N/A" should be shown.

Security:
19. Verify that only authenticated providers can view the Resident Header Bar and its data.
20. Verify that resident (email, phone, address) displayed in the header is not exposed in the page source or API responses to unauthorized users.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Resident Header Bar loads all data (photo, name, status, tags, demographics) within 1 seconds of navigating to the resident detail page.
2. Verify the profile photo thumbnail loads within 1 second without blocking the rendering of other header elements.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to view and edit frequency schedule
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to view and modify the frequency schedule for a resident within the Nemi Care Facility Portal, which determines the days of the week the resident is scheduled to attend or receive services. The Frequency Schedule section displays service days in a weekly calendar format with editable day slots representing each day of the week (Monday through Sunday). Each day slot can be toggled on or off to indicate whether the resident is scheduled to attend on that day, and the provider can edit the schedule by selecting or deselecting individual days. The schedule may also show the total number of scheduled days per week for quick reference. This scheduling information is critical for attendance tracking, transportation planning, staffing allocation, and billing calculations, as many facility services are billed based on the number of scheduled attendance days. Without the ability to view and edit frequency schedules, providers would lack visibility into each resident's expected attendance pattern, making it difficult to plan resources and verify attendance compliance.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider navigates to the Frequency Schedule section, Then the system must display a weekly calendar with day slots for Monday through Sunday, showing the current schedule for the resident.

2. If the provider clicks an inactive day slot, Then the system must toggle it to active and add the day to the schedule.
   Else if the provider clicks an active day slot, Then the system must toggle it to inactive and remove the day from the schedule.

3. If the provider saves changes after modifying the schedule, Then the system must persist the updated schedule and display a success confirmation.
   Else if the save fails due to a server error, Then the system must display an error message and retain the unsaved changes in the UI.

4. If the resident has no frequency schedule configured, Then the system must display all day slots as inactive/unselected with a message prompting the provider to set up the schedule.

5. If the provider modifies the schedule and navigates away without saving, Then the system must prompt the provider with a confirmation dialog asking whether to save or discard changes.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Frequency Schedule section displays a weekly calendar with day slots for Monday through Sunday.
2. Verify that a resident scheduled for Monday, Wednesday, and Friday shows those three days as active/selected and the remaining days as inactive.
3. Verify that clicking an inactive day slot (e.g., Tuesday) toggles it to active and adds it to the schedule.
4. Verify that clicking an active day slot (e.g., Friday) toggles it to inactive and removes it from the schedule.
5. Verify that saving the modified schedule persists the changes and the updated schedule appears on the next page load.
6. Verify that the schedule summary shows "5 days/week" when Monday through Friday are selected.
7. Verify that selecting all 7 days updates the summary to "7 days/week".

Negative:
8. Verify that saving a schedule with zero days selected shows a warning "At least one day must be scheduled" or saves the empty schedule based on business rules.
9. Verify that a save failure due to network error displays an error message and retains the unsaved changes.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify the weekly calendar format displays day slots in a horizontal row with day abbreviations (Mon, Tue, Wed, Thu, Fri, Sat, Sun).
11. Verify active day slots have a distinct visual appearance (e.g., filled/colored) compared to inactive slots (e.g., outlined/gray).
12. Verify that clicking a day slot provides immediate visual feedback (color change or animation) before saving.
13. Verify the total scheduled days count is displayed near the calendar.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify that rapidly clicking the same day slot multiple times toggles correctly without glitching.
15. Verify that navigating away from the Frequency Schedule with unsaved changes triggers a confirmation dialog.
16. Verify the schedule displays correctly when the resident has no previous schedule configured (all days inactive).

Security:
17. Verify that only authorized providers can edit the frequency schedule — read-only users should see the schedule but not be able to toggle days.
18. Verify that schedule changes are audit-logged with the provider who made the change and timestamp.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Frequency Schedule section loads and displays the weekly calendar within 1 second.
2. Verify that saving schedule changes completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to view transportation provider (VERIDA label), authorization expiration date, and usage progress bar (e.g., 30/90 days).
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to view transportation tracking information for a resident within the Nemi Care Facility Portal, displayed as a compact Transportation Tracking Card. The card shows the transportation provider name prominently (e.g., "VERIDA" label), the authorization expiration date indicating when the current transportation authorization period ends, and a usage progress bar showing how many days of authorized transportation have been used versus the total authorized days (e.g., "30/90 days"). The progress bar provides a visual indicator of utilization, making it easy for the provider to quickly assess whether a resident is on track with their transportation usage or approaching their authorization limit. This information is critical for ensuring residents have continuous access to transportation services, as expired authorizations or exhausted usage limits could leave residents without rides to the facility. The provider can use this card to proactively identify residents whose authorizations are nearing expiration or whose usage is approaching the maximum, enabling timely renewal requests or alternative arrangements. Without this visibility, providers would need to manually check external transportation systems to track authorization status and usage.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider navigates to the Transportation Tracking section, Then the system must display a Transportation Tracking Card with the provider name, authorization expiration date, and usage progress bar.

2. If the resident has an active transportation authorization, Then the card must display the provider name (e.g., "VERIDA"), expiration date, and current usage as "X/Y days" with a corresponding progress bar.
   Else if the resident has no transportation authorization on file, Then the card must display a message "No transportation authorization configured".

3. If the authorization expiration date is within 7 days from today, Then the expiration date must be highlighted in a warning color (e.g., orange or red).
   Else if the expiration date is more than 7 days away, Then it must be displayed in the default text color.

4. If the usage is at or above 90% of total authorized days (e.g., 81/90), Then the progress bar must change to a warning color (e.g., orange or red) to indicate approaching the limit.
   Else if usage is below 90%, Then the progress bar must display in the standard color (e.g., green or blue).

5. If the authorization has expired (expiration date is in the past), Then the card must display an "Expired" badge and the progress bar must be grayed out.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Transportation Tracking Card displays the provider name "VERIDA" prominently at the top of the card.
2. Verify the card displays the authorization expiration date (e.g., "Expires on: 06/30/2026").
3. Verify the usage progress bar shows "30/90 days" with the bar filled to approximately 33%.
4. Verify that for a resident with 60/90 days used, the progress bar shows approximately 67% fill.
5. Verify that an authorization expiring within 7 days (e.g., expires "03/31/2026" when today is "03/25/2026") displays the date in a warning color.
6. Verify that a resident with 85/90 days used shows the progress bar in a warning color (orange or red).

Negative:
7. Verify that a resident with no transportation authorization displays "No transportation authorization configured" instead of the card.
8. Verify that an expired authorization (e.g., expiration date "03/01/2026") shows an "Expired" badge and the progress bar is grayed out.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify the Transportation Tracking Card is displayed as a compact card layout consistent with the Figma design.
10. Verify the provider name "VERIDA" is displayed in a prominent label style at the top of the card.
11. Verify the progress bar is a horizontal bar with a fill that visually represents the usage ratio.
12. Verify the usage label "30/90 days" is displayed adjacent to or below the progress bar.
13. Verify the progress bar color transitions from green/blue (normal) to orange/red (high usage) as utilization increases.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify the card displays correctly when usage equals total authorized days (e.g., "90/90 days") with the bar at 100% and a warning indicator.
15. Verify the card displays correctly when usage is 0 days (e.g., "0/90 days") with an empty progress bar.
16. Verify behavior when the authorization expiration date is exactly today — the card should show an "Expiring Today" indicator.
17. Verify the card handles a non-standard authorization period (e.g., "45/120 days") correctly.

Security:
18. Verify that only authenticated providers can view the Transportation Tracking Card data.
19. Verify that transportation authorization data cannot be modified through the view-only card interface.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Transportation Tracking Card loads and displays all data (provider name, expiration date, progress bar) within 1 second.
2. Verify the progress bar renders correctly and smoothly without visual glitches on page load.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to view attendance card showing current month, attendance count (e.g., 06/31), and progress bar with attendance rate.
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to view attendance tracking information for a resident within the Nemi Care Facility Portal, displayed as a compact Attendance Card. The card prominently shows the current month name (e.g., "March"), the attendance count as a fraction of total days in the month (e.g., "06/31" indicating 6 days attended out of 31 days), and a progress bar that visually represents the attendance rate percentage. The progress bar fills proportionally based on the attendance rate, giving the provider an immediate visual understanding of how consistently the resident has been attending. The attendance rate percentage may also be displayed as a numeric value alongside the progress bar for precise reference. This attendance visibility is critical for billing accuracy (many services are billed per day of attendance), regulatory compliance (facilities must maintain minimum attendance records), and care coordination (identifying residents who may be declining or disengaging from services). Without this at-a-glance attendance view, the provider would need to manually count attendance records from daily logs to understand a resident's monthly attendance pattern.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the provider navigates to the Attendance Tracking section, Then the system must display an Attendance Card with the current month name, attendance count (days attended/total days), and a progress bar representing the attendance rate.

2. If the resident has attendance records for the current month, Then the card must display the count (e.g., "06/31") and fill the progress bar to the corresponding percentage.
   Else if the resident has no attendance records for the current month, Then the card must display "00/31" (or the appropriate day count for the month) with an empty progress bar.

3. If the current month is February in a non-leap year, Then the total days must show as 28.
   Else if the current month is February in a leap year, Then the total days must show as 29.
   Else if the current month has 30 days, Then the total must be 30.
   Else if the current month has 31 days, Then the total must be 31.

5. If the month rolls over, Then the system must automatically update the Attendance Card to reflect the new month and reset the attendance count.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Attendance Card displays the current month name "March" when viewed in March.
2. Verify the attendance count shows "06/31" for a resident who attended 6 out of 31 days in March.
3. Verify the progress bar is filled to approximately 19% for a resident with 6/31 attendance.
4. Verify the attendance rate percentage is displayed alongside the progress bar (e.g., "19%").
5. Verify a resident with 20/31 attendance shows the progress bar at approximately 65% with label "20/31".
6. Verify a resident with perfect attendance (31/31) shows a fully filled progress bar at 100%.
7. Verify that the card correctly shows "28" as the total for February in a non-leap year.

Negative:
8. Verify that a resident with zero attendance for the current month displays "00/31" with an empty progress bar.
9. Verify that the attendance count does not exceed the total days in the month (e.g., cannot show "32/31").

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify the Attendance Card is displayed as a compact card layout consistent with the Figma design.
11. Verify the current month name is displayed at the top of the card.
12. Verify the progress bar is a horizontal bar with a fill that visually represents the attendance rate.
13. Verify the attendance count label "06/31" is displayed adjacent to or below the progress bar.
14. Verify the progress bar color changes from green/blue (normal) to orange/red when attendance is below 50%.

Edge Cases:
15. Verify the card displays correctly on the first day of the month when attendance is "00/31".
16. Verify the card displays correctly for February showing "00/28" or "00/29" depending on the year.
17. Verify the card transitions correctly at month rollover (e.g., March 31 to April 1) with the new month and reset count.
18. Verify that attendance recorded on the last day of the month updates the card before the month rolls over.

Security:
19. Verify that only authenticated providers can view the Attendance Card data.
20. Verify that attendance data cannot be modified through the view-only Attendance Card interface.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Attendance Card loads and displays all data (month name, count, progress bar, percentage) within 1 second.
2. Verify that the attendance calculation is accurate and renders the progress bar correctly for all months without delay.

User Story: As staff, I should be able to send families a summary of daily participation
Description of User Story: The daily summary feature allows staff to compile and distribute a participation report to family members or authorized contacts for each participant. The summary includes attendance check-in and check-out times, vitals recorded during the day, activities participated in, meals consumed, and any notable observations from caregivers. Staff can review the auto-generated summary on screen, make edits or add personalized notes before sending. The delivery mechanism supports email and optional SMS notification with a link to the full summary. A "Send To Case Manager" button, similar to the one on the Absence Form, allows routing the summary to the assigned case manager for clinical review when needed.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the participant has no recorded activities for the day, Then the summary displays "No activities recorded" in the activities section rather than leaving it blank.
2. If the family contact has no email or phone on file, Then the system displays a warning that the summary cannot be delivered and prompts staff to update contact information.
3. If the staff edits the auto-generated summary content, Then the system marks the summary as "Modified by staff" in the audit trail.
4. If the participant was absent for the day, Then the summary reflects the absence reason and skips activity and vitals sections.
5. If the email delivery fails, Then the system retries once and notifies the staff member of the delivery failure with an option to resend.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that staff can select a participant with a full day of records and successfully send the auto-generated summary (attendance, vitals, activities, meals) to the family.
2. Verify that staff can add a personalized note (e.g., "Great day today!") and send the summary with the appended note to the family.
3. Verify that staff can select multiple participants (e.g., five) and batch-send summaries, ensuring each family receives the correct participant summary.

Negative:
4. Verify that the system displays an appropriate message when attempting to send a summary for a participant with no records for the day.
6. Verify that the system blocks sending summaries when the family has opted out of communications and displays the opt-out status.

UI Scenarios:
7. Verify that the summary preview screen displays sections for attendance, vitals, activities, meals, and notes in a clean and readable layout.
8. Verify that "Send" button clearly visible and distinguishable.

Edge Cases:
9. Verify that generating a summary at 11:59 PM for a participant still checked in includes a note indicating the participant is not yet checked out.
10. Verify that the system flags discrepancies when vitals exist but attendance is missing.

Security:
11. Verify that only users with "summary_send" permission can send summaries, while others have view-only access.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Auto-generation of a daily summary for a participant with a full day of records (attendance, vitals, activities, meals) completes within 2 seconds.
2. Batch sending of daily summaries for 50 participants completes email dispatch within 30 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to view Trip pickup / drop-off assignments for each driver/family
Description of User Story: The Trip Assignments screen displays a comprehensive view of all scheduled pickup and drop-off assignments organized by driver and family. Each assignment row shows the member name with photo, pickup address, scheduled pickup time, drop-off location (center or home), scheduled drop-off time, and the assigned driver name. The view supports filtering by driver to see a specific driver's route manifest, or by family to see all trips for a particular household. A map integration displays route pins for each pickup and drop-off point when a driver's route is selected. The Standing Order Form's weekday checkboxes reflect the recurring trip schedule, showing which days each member has standing transportation orders. A "Download" button allows exporting the trip manifest as a printable route sheet for drivers.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the provider filters by a driver who has no assignments for the day, Then the screen displays "No trips assigned to this driver for today".
3. If a trip assignment has no scheduled time (ad-hoc request), Then the time column displays "On Demand" and the trip is listed at the end of the route.
4. If the map cannot load due to connectivity issues, Then the system displays the text-based route list as a fallback.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
Verify that the provider can view trip assignments (e.g., 15 pickups and 15 drop-offs) across multiple drivers.
Verify that filtering by a specific driver displays only their assigned trips in route order with all details.
Verify that filtering by family groups members’ assignments together.
Verify that selecting a driver route displays map pins in sequence.
Verify that the provider can download a route sheet PDF containing all trip details.

Negative:
Verify that accessing archived trip data shows a message directing to reports.
Verify that searching for a non-existent driver returns a "Driver not found" message.

UI Scenarios:
Verify that each trip row displays member  name, pickup/drop-off details, time, and driver in a structured layout.
Verify that filter dropdowns are clearly labeled and include a "Clear Filters" option.
Verify that the map displays numbered route pins in sequence.

Edge Cases:
Verify that assignments with unavailable drivers are highlighted and marked as "Unassigned".
Verify that members at the same address with different times are listed separately.

Security:
Verify that only users with "transportation_view" permission can access the screen and address masking applies based on permissions.
Performance Scenarios:
1. The Trip Assignments screen loads within 2 seconds for a facility with 50 daily trip assignments, including map initialization.
2. Route sheet PDF generation and download completes within 3 seconds for a driver with 10 stops.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to assign / reassign drivers to Trip routes.
Description of User Story: The driver assignment feature allows providers to assign or reassign drivers to existing trip routes from the Trip Assignments management screen. A driver assignment dropdown or modal appears when the provider clicks the driver column for a specific route, displaying a list of available drivers with their current assignment count and vehicle capacity. When reassigning, the system shows the current driver and allows selection of a new driver, with an automatic notification sent to both the original and newly assigned driver. The Standing Order Form supports assigning a default driver to recurring routes via the Treatment Details section with weekday checkboxes. Reassignment logs maintain a complete audit trail of driver changes including the reason, timestamp, and the staff member who made the change.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the provider assigns a driver whose vehicle capacity would be exceeded by the route, Then the system displays a capacity warning and requires confirmation to proceed.
2. If the provider reassigns a driver without entering a reason, Then the system requires a reason to be provided before the reassignment can be saved.
3. If the selected replacement driver is already assigned to a conflicting route at the same time, Then the system displays a scheduling conflict warning with details of the overlap.
4. If the original driver has already departed for the route, Then the system blocks the reassignment and displays "Route in progress — cannot reassign".
5. If no drivers are available for assignment, Then the dropdown displays "No available drivers" and the provider can request dispatch support.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
Verify that a provider can assign a driver to an unassigned route and the assignment updates successfully.
Verify that route reassignment updates both drivers, triggers notifications, and logs the change in audit logs.
Verify that a provider can assign default drivers for recurring schedules via standing orders.
Verify that the audit log displays complete driver reassignment history.

Negative:
Verify that assigning a driver beyond vehicle capacity triggers a warning.
Verify that reassignment is blocked when the route is already in progress.
Verify that assigning a driver on leave is prevented with an appropriate message.

UI Scenarios:
Verify that the driver dropdown displays name, trip count, and vehicle type.
Verify that the reassignment modal includes current driver, reason field, and replacement selection.

Edge Cases:
Verify that multiple consecutive reassignments are captured in the audit trail.
Verify that one-time overrides do not impact standing order assignments.

Security:
Verify that only users with "transportation_manage" permission can assign/reassign drivers.
Performance Scenarios:
1. The driver assignment dropdown loads the list of available drivers with trip counts within 1 second.
2. Driver reassignment processing, including notification dispatch to both drivers, completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to view driver capacity and current trip assignments
Description of User Story: The Driver Capacity Dashboard provides providers with a real-time overview of all drivers, their vehicle capacity, current trip assignment counts, and availability status. Each driver card or row displays the driver name, vehicle type, total seat capacity, currently assigned member count, remaining capacity, and a list of assigned route names with scheduled times. A capacity utilization bar visually indicates how full each driver's schedule is (green for under 75%, yellow for 75-90%, red for over 90%). The dashboard supports filtering by date to view historical or future assignment loads, and by driver status (active, on leave, off duty). An aggregate summary at the top shows total fleet capacity, total assigned members, and overall utilization percentage for the selected date.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If a driver's capacity utilization exceeds 90%, Then the capacity bar displays in red and a warning icon appears next to the driver name.
2. If no drivers are active for the selected date, Then the dashboard displays "No active drivers for this date" with a suggestion to check scheduling.
3. If a driver has zero trip assignments, Then their capacity bar is empty and the status shows "Available" in blue.
4. If the provider hovers over a driver's route list, Then a tooltip displays the full route details including member names and pickup times.
5. If fleet utilization exceeds 85% overall, Then the aggregate summary banner changes to yellow with a "Near Capacity" alert.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
Verify that the dashboard displays active drivers with capacity, assigned counts, and summaries.
Verify that aggregate summary metrics (total fleet, assigned, utilization) are displayed correctly.
Verify that selecting future dates shows projected assignments.
Verify that filtering by active status displays only active drivers.
Verify that expanding driver rows shows detailed assignments.

Negative:
Verify that selecting a date with no trips shows 0% utilization and "No assignments".
Verify that unauthorized users are restricted from accessing the dashboard.

UI Scenarios:
Verify that capacity bars are color-coded (green/yellow/red) based on utilization.
Verify that summary cards display key metrics prominently.
Verify that driver rows are sortable by multiple parameters.

Edge Cases:
Verify that capacity recalculates when vehicle changes mid-day.
Verify that overbooked drivers show utilization above 100% with warning.

Security:
Verify that access is restricted based on permissions and sensitive details are masked.
Performance Scenarios:
1. The Driver Capacity Dashboard loads within 2 seconds for a fleet of 20 drivers with full assignment data.
2. Filtering by date or status refreshes the dashboard within 1 second.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to import trip attendance data from CSV file.
Description of User Story: The CSV Import feature allows providers to upload trip attendance data in bulk from an external source, such as a transportation vendor or GPS tracking system. The import interface presents a file upload area with drag-and-drop support and a browse button, accepting only .csv file format. After upload, the system displays a preview table showing the first 10 rows of parsed data mapped to system columns: Member Name/ID, Trip Date, Pickup Time, Drop-off Time, Driver, and Status (transported/no-show). A column mapping step allows providers to match CSV headers to system fields if the column names differ. Validation results are displayed inline, highlighting rows with errors (missing required fields, unrecognized member IDs, invalid date formats) in red. A "Confirm Import" button processes valid rows and generates a summary report.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the uploaded file is not in .csv format, Then the system rejects the file and displays "Invalid file format. Please upload a .csv file".
2. If the CSV file contains more than 1000 rows, Then the system processes the import in batches and displays a progress bar.
3. If a row references a Member ID not found in the system, Then the row is flagged as an error with "Member not found" and excluded from import.
4. If duplicate trip records exist (same member, date, and time), Then the system warns about duplicates and offers options to skip or overwrite.
5. If the CSV file is empty or contains only headers, Then the system displays "No data rows found in the uploaded file".
6. If the date format in the CSV does not match the expected format, Then the column mapping step highlights the date column and allows format specification (MM/DD/YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, etc.).
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
Verify that a valid CSV file is uploaded and all records are successfully imported.
Verify that column mapping allows import of non-standard CSV formats.
Verify that partial valid data is imported while invalid rows are skipped with error details.
Verify that preview functionality correctly displays parsed data before import.

Negative:
Verify that non-CSV formats (e.g., .xlsx) are rejected.
Verify that invalid data (e.g., incorrect IDs) results in zero imports with error reporting.
Verify that empty CSV files display a "No data rows found" message.

UI Scenarios:
Verify that the upload area supports drag-and-drop and browse options.
Verify that preview displays headers, sample rows, and row count.
Verify that validation errors are highlighted with tooltips.

Edge Cases:
Verify that large files are processed in batches with progress indication.
Verify that mixed date formats are detected and flagged.

Security:
Verify that only authorized users can access import functionality and files are scanned for security risks.
Performance Scenarios:
1. CSV file parsing and preview generation for a 500-row file completes within 3 seconds.
2. Full import processing of 500 validated records completes within 10 seconds with real-time progress indication.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to mark resident as transported or no-show for Trips
Description of User Story: The Trip Status Tracking feature allows providers to update the outcome of each scheduled trip by marking residents as either "Transported" or "No-Show" directly from the Trip Assignments screen. Each trip row includes a status action dropdown or toggle that presents the two options, and selecting one updates the trip record with a timestamp and the staff member who recorded the status. For "No-Show" selections, an optional reason dropdown appears (e.g., resident not ready, refused transport, not at pickup location, medical issue) to capture context for reporting. The Attendance Roster integrates with trip status so that a member marked as "Transported" auto-populates a pending check-in on arrival, while "No-Show" trips automatically generate an absence flag referencing the transportation no-show reason. A batch update option allows providers to mark multiple trips at once when a driver completes a route.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the provider marks a trip as "Transported" but the member does not arrive at the center within the expected window, Then the system flags the trip for follow-up after 30 minutes.
2. If the provider selects "No-Show" without selecting a reason, Then the system allows saving with a default "Reason not specified" tag but displays a reminder to add context.
3. If the provider attempts to update a trip status that has already been finalized, Then the system requires a supervisor override to modify the record.
4. If the batch update includes a mix of "Transported" and "No-Show" members, Then the system processes each trip individually with the appropriate status and reason.
5. If the provider marks a round-trip pickup as "No-Show", Then the system automatically cancels the corresponding return trip and notifies the driver.
6. If a trip is marked "Transported" and then the member is found to be absent at the center, Then the system allows status correction with an audit entry explaining the discrepancy.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
Verify that marking a trip as "Transported" updates status with timestamp and visual indicator.
Verify that marking a trip as "No-Show" captures the reason and updates records.
Verify that batch updates correctly update multiple trips simultaneously.
Verify that transported members appear in attendance system for check-in.
Verify that end-of-day reports display correct counts and summaries.

Negative:
Verify that updating cancelled trips is blocked.
Verify that batch updates are restricted for inactive routes.

UI Scenarios:
Verify that status dropdown clearly distinguishes "Transported" and "No-Show".
Verify that no-show reason appears inline upon selection.
Verify that batch update UI supports checklist and bulk actions.

Edge Cases:
Verify that re-pickup can be scheduled after a no-show and linked to the original record.
Verify that status overrides (e.g., transported by family) are recorded with notes.

Security:
Verify that only authorized users can update trip statuses and audit logs remain immutable.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Individual trip status update (mark as Transported or No-Show) saves and reflects on the screen within 1 second.
2. Batch status update for a route with 10 members completes processing within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to receive automated dashboard alert 60 days before Standing Order expiration.
Description of User Story: The Provider dashboard includes a notification bell icon and an alerts panel that surfaces time-sensitive reminders for upcoming Standing Order expirations. When a Standing Order is within 60 days of its expiration date, the system automatically generates a prominent alert card displayed in the dashboard alerts section with the resident name, Standing Order ID, and days remaining. The alert card uses a yellow warning indicator for 31-60 days and a red critical indicator for 30 days or fewer, ensuring the Provider can prioritize renewals. Clicking the alert card navigates the Provider directly to the Standing Order Form pre-populated with the existing order details for renewal. The alerts panel also shows a badge count on the notification bell reflecting the total number of pending expiration warnings. Dismissed alerts are moved to an archived alerts section accessible via a "View Archived" link at the bottom of the panel.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Standing Order expiration date is between 31 and 60 days from today, Then display the alert with a yellow warning severity indicator.
2. If the Standing Order expiration date is 30 days or fewer from today, Then display the alert with a red critical severity indicator.
3. If the Provider clicks on an expiration alert card, Then navigate to the Standing Order Form pre-populated with the current order data.
4. If the Provider dismisses an alert, Then move it to the archived alerts section and update the badge count.
5. If the Standing Order has already been renewed, Then remove the expiration alert from the active alerts panel automatically.
6. If no Standing Orders are expiring within 60 days, Then display no expiration alerts and show a zero badge count on the notification bell.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional :
1. Verify that an expiration alert is generated on the dashboard exactly 60 days prior to the Standing Order expiration date, displaying correct resident name, order ID, and days remaining.
2. Verify that clicking on an expiration alert card navigates to the Standing Order Form with all existing order details pre-populated.
3. Verify that the notification bell badge count accurately reflects the total number of active expiration alerts.
Verify that renewing a Standing Order before its expiration date automatically removes the corresponding alert from the dashboard.
4. Verify that dismissing an alert:
Moves the alert to the Archived Alerts section
Decrements the notification badge count accordingly

Negative Scenarios :
5. Verify that no alert is generated for a Standing Order with an expiration date greater than 60 days from the current date.
6. Verify that no alert is generated for a Standing Order that is already expired.
Verify that renewing a Standing Order does not result in duplicate expiration alerts.

UI Scenarios :
7. Verify that the alert card displays:
Yellow warning indicator for expiration between 31–60 days
Red critical indicator for expiration within ≤30 days
8. Verify that the alerts panel becomes scrollable when more than five alerts are displayed simultaneously.
9. Verify that the “View Archived” link:
Is visible at the bottom of the alerts panel
Navigates to the Archived/Dismissed Alerts screen

Edge Case :
10. Verify system behavior when the Standing Order expiration date is today (0 days remaining) and ensure correct alert classification.
11. Verify alert generation when multiple Standing Orders for the same resident have the same expiration date, ensuring each alert is displayed independently without data overlap.

Security :
12. Verify that expiration alerts are visible only to authenticated Providers with appropriate dashboard access permissions and are restricted for unauthorized users.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that the daily expiration check job processes 500 or more Standing Orders and generates all applicable alerts within 30 seconds.
2. Verify that the dashboard alerts panel loads within 2 seconds when displaying 50 or more active expiration alerts.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to modify Standing Order for changes in service days or times.
Description of User Story: The Standing Order Form allows the Provider to modify an existing Standing Order when changes to scheduled service days or times are required. The form displays pre-populated Submission Details, Member Information, and Medicaid Waiver sections from the original order, with the Treatment Details section containing weekday checkboxes (Monday through Friday) and time fields that are editable. The Provider can check or uncheck specific weekday checkboxes to add or remove service days and adjust the start and end time fields for each selected day. A "Reason for Modification" text area is required before the form can be submitted, ensuring an audit trail for all changes. The form includes a "Save Changes" button that validates all required fields and a "Cancel" button to discard edits and return to the previous view. Upon successful modification, a confirmation banner is displayed showing the updated service schedule summary.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider unchecks a weekday checkbox in Treatment Details, Then disable the corresponding time fields for that day and clear any previously entered times.
2. If the Provider checks a weekday checkbox, Then enable the start and end time fields for that day.
3. If the "Reason for Modification" field is empty when "Save Changes" is clicked, Then display an inline validation error "Modification reason is required" and prevent form submission.
4. If all required fields are valid and the Provider clicks "Save Changes", Then update the Standing Order record and display a success confirmation banner.
5. If the Provider clicks "Cancel" after making changes, Then discard all modifications and navigate back to the Standing Order list view.
6. If the Provider modifies the Standing Order to have zero service days selected, Then display a validation error "At least one service day must be selected" and prevent submission.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional: 
1. Verify that opening an existing Standing Order for editing pre-populates all form sections with the current order data.
2.  Verify that checking a new weekday checkbox adds the service day and enables the corresponding time fields.
3. Verify that unchecking a weekday checkbox removes the service day and disables the corresponding time fields.
4. Verify that modifying service times for an existing day and saving updates the Standing Order record correctly.
5. Verify the modification is recorded in the audit log with reason, changed fields, timestamp, and Provider identity.

Negative :
6. Verify that submitting the form without a modification reason displays a validation error and prevents saving.
7. Verify that submitting the form with zero weekday checkboxes selected displays a validation error.
8.  Verify that entering an end time earlier than the start time for a service day displays a time range validation error.

UI Scenarios :
9. Verify the Standing Order Form displays the weekday checkboxes (Monday through Friday) in the Treatment Details section with correct labels.
10. Verify the "Save Changes" and "Cancel" buttons are visible and appropriately positioned at the bottom of the form.
11. Verify a confirmation banner is displayed after a successful modification showing the updated schedule summary.

Edge Case: 
12. Verify behavior when the Provider modifies a Standing Order that is expiring within the next 24 hours.
13. Verify that simultaneous modifications to the same Standing Order by two Providers are handled with a concurrency conflict message.

Security Scenarios : 
14.Verify that only authorized Providers can modify Standing Orders and unauthorized users receive an access denied response.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that saving a Standing Order modification completes and displays the confirmation banner within 3 seconds under normal system load.
2. Verify that loading the Standing Order Form with pre-populated data renders within 2 seconds for orders with complex multi-day schedules.

User Story: As a staff member, I should be able to record full day, half-day or partial-day attendance by documenting actual arrival and departure times, with the system automatically calculating attendance duration and adjusting billable hours accordingly for members who attend only part of their scheduled day.
Description of User Story: The Attendance screen presents a table listing all residents with their photos, names, and day-of-week columns containing attendance checkboxes. When the Provider clicks on a checkbox cell for a specific resident and day, a dropdown or radio button selector appears offering three attendance options: Full Day, Partial, and Absent. Selecting "Full Day" marks the resident as present for the entire scheduled session with a green filled indicator in the cell. Selecting "Partial" marks partial attendance and prompts the Provider to enter arrival and departure times in a time picker popover. Selecting "Absent" marks the resident as absent for the day with a red indicator and may trigger the EDWP absence workflow. The "Mark Attendance" button at the top of the roster confirms and saves all attendance selections made for the displayed day.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider selects "Full Day" for a resident, Then display a green indicator in the attendance cell and record full day attendance.
2. If the Provider selects "Partial" for a resident, Then display the time picker popover requiring arrival and departure time entry.
3. If the Provider selects "Absent" for a resident, Then display a red indicator in the attendance cell and record the absence.
4. If the Provider selects "Partial" but does not enter valid arrival and departure times, Then display a validation error and do not save the partial attendance.
5. If the Provider clicks "Mark Attendance" with at least one attendance selection made, Then save all selections and display a success message.
6. If the Provider clicks "Mark Attendance" without making any attendance selections, Then display a warning message indicating no changes were made.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking a resident's attendance cell displays the attendance type selector with Full Day, Partial, and Absent options.
2.Verify that selecting "Full Day" records the attendance and shows a green indicator in the cell.
3. Verify that selecting "Partial" displays the time picker and correctly records arrival and departure times.
4. Verify that selecting "Absent" records the absence and shows a red indicator in the cell.
5. Verify that clicking "Mark Attendance" saves all attendance selections and displays a success confirmation.
6. Verify that the Provider can change a resident's attendance type from Full Day to Partial or Absent before clicking "Mark Attendance".

Negative: 
7. Verify that selecting "Partial" without entering arrival and departure times displays a validation error.
8. Verify that entering a departure time earlier than the arrival time for Partial attendance displays a validation error.
9. Verify that clicking "Mark Attendance" with no selections displays an appropriate warning.

UI Scenario: 
10. Verify the Attendance screen displays resident photos, names, and day-of-week columns with attendance checkboxes.
11. Verify the "Mark Attendance" button is prominently displayed at the top of the screen.
12. Verify the color coding: green for Full Day, a distinct indicator for Partial, and red for Absent.

Edge Case:
13.  Verify attendance behavior when the Provider sets the same resident's attendance multiple times before saving.
14. Verify behavior when attendance is set for a resident who was discharged or transferred on the same day.

Security: 
15. Verify that only authenticated Providers with attendance management permissions can set and modify resident attendance records.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that the attendance type selector renders within 1 second of clicking a resident attendance cell.
2. Verify that saving attendance for 50 or more residents via "Mark Attendance" completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a staff member, I should be able to mark members as absent and select the specific reason (hospitalization, scheduled vacation, illness, no-show, family emergency) with optional notes, enabling tracking of absence patterns and billing adjustments.
Description of User Story: The Absence Form (EDWP) provides a dedicated interface for recording member absences with structured reason tracking. The form opens with a resident header displaying the member name and ID, followed by a date picker for selecting the absence date. Below, radio buttons present the predefined absence reasons: Illness, Vacation, Hospitalization, No-Show, Family Emergency, and Other, allowing only one selection per absence record. A notes text area beneath the radio buttons allows staff to add optional free-text context about the absence. The "Send To Case Manager" button at the bottom routes the completed absence form to the assigned case manager for follow-up or billing review. The system uses absence data to generate pattern reports and automatically flags billing adjustments for days the member was not present.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the staff member selects "Other" as the absence reason, Then the notes field becomes mandatory to provide a custom explanation.
2. If the staff member attempts to save the absence form without selecting a reason, Then the system displays a validation error requiring a reason selection.
3. If the staff member selects a future date in the date picker, Then the system records it as a planned absence and tags it accordingly.
4. If the member already has an absence record for the selected date, Then the system warns about the duplicate and offers to update the existing record.
5. If the assigned case manager is unavailable or unassigned, Then the "Send To Case Manager" action routes to the supervisor queue with a flag for reassignment.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that staff can open the Absence Form, select the current date, select"Change" as the reason, add notes, and successfully send the record to the case manager.
2. Verify that the absence pattern report correctly identifies repeated absences (e.g., 5 illness-related absences in 30 days) and triggers an alert.

Negative:
5. Verify that the system displays a validation message when submitting the absence form without selecting a reason.
6. Verify that selecting "Other" without entering notes prevents submission and prompts the user to add notes.
7. Verify that the system rejects absence entries for dates before the member’s enrollment start date with an appropriate validation error.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the Absence Form displays resident details at the top, followed by date picker, absence reason options, notes field, and submission button in a structured layout.
9. Verify that absence reasons are displayed as radio buttons (Illness, Vacation, Hospitalization, No-Show, Family Emergency, Other) and allow only a single selection.
10. Verify that the date picker defaults to the current date and allows selection within the valid enrollment period.

Edge Cases:**
11. Verify that the system supports recording multiple absences for the same member on the same date (e.g., different sessions with different reasons).
12. Verify that the system generates an alert when a member has prolonged consecutive absences (e.g., 30 days).

Security:**
13. Verify that only users with "absence_manage" permission can create or edit absence records, while others have view-only access.
Performance Scenarios:
1. The Absence Form loads within 1 second, including member header and all radio button options.
2. Absence pattern report generation for a member with 12 months of absence history completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to complete Member Information Form (EDWP) with absence details.
Description of User Story: The Absence Form (EDWP) screen displays a structured layout with a resident header section showing the resident name and identification details at the top. Below the header, a date picker field allows the Provider to select the specific date of absence. The form includes radio buttons for selecting the absence reason such as medical appointment, hospitalization, personal leave, or other with a corresponding text field for custom explanations. A notes area provides a multi-line text field where the Provider can enter additional details or context about the absence. All required fields are marked with asterisks and inline validation messages appear when mandatory fields are left blank upon submission. The form footer contains a "Save" button to store the completed EDWP locally before it can be sent to the case manager.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider selects a valid absence date using the date picker, Then populate the date field and enable the remaining form sections.
2. If the Provider selects "Other" as the absence reason, Then enable the custom reason text field for entry.
3. If the Provider selects a predefined absence reason (medical appointment, hospitalization, personal leave), Then record the selection and keep the custom reason text field disabled.
4. If all required fields (date, absence reason) are completed, Then enable the "Save" button.
5. If the Provider clicks "Save" with missing required fields, Then display inline validation errors on each incomplete field and prevent saving.
6. If the Provider clicks "Save" with all required fields completed, Then save the EDWP data locally and display a confirmation message.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional: 
1. Verify the EDWP form loads with the correct resident name and identification in the header.
2.  Verify selecting a date in the date picker populates the absence date field correctly.
3.  Verify selecting each predefined absence reason radio button records the correct reason.
4. Verify selecting "Other" enables the custom reason text field and allows entry.
5. Verify entering notes in the notes area saves the text correctly with the form.
6. Verify clicking "Save" with all required fields completed saves the EDWP form and displays a success message.

Negative Scenarios :
7.  Verify clicking "Save" without selecting an absence date displays a validation error on the date field.
8.  Verify clicking "Save" without selecting an absence reason displays a validation error on the reason field.
9.  Verify selecting "Other" and leaving the custom reason text field blank displays a validation error.

UI Scenarios :
10.  Verify the resident header section displays prominently at the top of the EDWP form with the resident photo and name.
11.  Verify the date picker renders correctly and allows single date selection.
12.  Verify radio buttons for absence reasons are clearly labeled and only one can be selected at a time.
13.  Verify the notes area is a multi-line text field with adequate height for detailed entries.

Edge Case :
14. Verify behavior when the Provider selects a future date beyond the current week in the date picker.
15.  Verify form behavior when the Provider selects an absence date that already has a saved EDWP record for the same resident.

Security :
16. Security: Verify that only authorized Providers can access and complete the EDWP form for residents assigned to their facility.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the EDWP form loads with pre-populated resident header data within 2 seconds.
2. Verify saving the completed EDWP form persists data and displays the success message within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to send email completed EDWP form to state case manager for sign-off.
Description of User Story: The Absence Form (EDWP) screen includes a "Send To Case Manager" button positioned at the bottom of the completed form, enabling the Provider to email the finalized EDWP document to the assigned state case manager. When the Provider clicks this button, a confirmation dialog appears displaying the case manager's name and email address along with a preview summary of the EDWP form contents. The dialog contains "Confirm Send" and "Cancel" buttons allowing the Provider to verify the recipient and form data before dispatching. Upon confirmation, the system sends an email with the EDWP form attached as a PDF document to the case manager's registered email address. A status indicator on the form updates to "Sent - Awaiting Sign-off" after successful dispatch. If the email fails to send, an error notification is displayed with a "Retry" option.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the EDWP form is completed and saved, Then enable the "Send To Case Manager" button.
2. If the EDWP form has unsaved changes or incomplete required fields, Then disable the "Send To Case Manager" button and display a tooltip indicating the form must be saved first.
3. If the Provider clicks "Confirm Send" in the confirmation dialog, Then dispatch the email with the EDWP PDF attachment and update the form status to "Sent - Awaiting Sign-off".
4. If the Provider clicks "Cancel" in the confirmation dialog, Then close the dialog without sending any email.
5. If the email dispatch fails, Then display an error notification with a "Retry" option and keep the form status unchanged.
6. If the EDWP form has already been sent and is in "Sent - Awaiting Sign-off" status, Then display a "Resend" option instead of "Send To Case Manager".
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional
1. Verify that clicking "Send To Case Manager" on a completed EDWP form displays the confirmation dialog
2. Verify that clicking "Confirm Send" dispatches the email with the EDWP form as a PDF attachment to the case manager.
3. Verify the form status updates to "Sent - Awaiting Sign-off" after successful email dispatch.
4. Verify clicking "Cancel" in the confirmation dialog closes it without sending an email.
5.  Verify the "Retry" button reattempts email dispatch after a failed send.

Negative :
6. Verify the "Send To Case Manager" button is disabled when the EDWP form has unsaved changes.
7.  Verify the "Send To Case Manager" button is disabled when required form fields are incomplete.
8. Verify an error notification appears when the email fails to send due to an invalid case manager email address.

UI Scenarios :
9. Verify the "Send To Case Manager" button is positioned at the bottom of the EDWP form and styled as a primary action button.
10. Verify the confirmation dialog displays the case manager name, email, and form summary in a readable layout.
11. Verify the "Sent - Awaiting Sign-off" status indicator is displayed with a distinct color or icon on the form.

Edge Case :
12. Verify behavior when the case manager email address is not configured in the system for the assigned resident.
13. Verify behavior when the Provider attempts to send the same EDWP form to the case manager multiple times.

Security : 
14. Verify that the EDWP email is sent only to the registered case manager email and cannot be redirected to unauthorized recipients.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the confirmation dialog loads with case manager details and form summary within 2 seconds of clicking "Send To Case Manager".
2. Verify the email dispatch completes and the form status updates within 5 seconds of clicking "Confirm Send".

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to view daily attendance roster with attendance type for all residents.
Description of User Story: The Attendance Roster screen presents a comprehensive table listing all residents enrolled in the ADH program for the selected day. Each row in the table displays the resident's photo thumbnail, full name, and attendance status columns organized by day of the week with checkboxes or status indicators. The attendance type for each resident is visually distinguished using color-coded indicators: green for Full Day, amber for Partial, red for Absent, and gray for not yet recorded. A "Download" button in the toolbar allows the Provider to export the attendance roster as a PDF or CSV file. The roster header includes date navigation controls enabling the Provider to switch between days and a filter dropdown to view specific attendance types such as only absent or only partial residents.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the resident attendance is recorded as Full Day, Then display a green indicator in the attendance cell.
2. If the resident attendance is recorded as Partial, Then display an amber indicator in the attendance cell.
3. If the resident attendance is recorded as Absent, Then display a red indicator in the attendance cell.
4. If the resident attendance has not been recorded yet, Then display a gray or empty indicator in the attendance cell.
5. If the Provider selects a filter for a specific attendance type, Then display only residents matching that attendance type and hide others.
6. If the Provider clicks "Download" and selects PDF, Then generate and download the roster in PDF format. If the Provider selects CSV, Then generate and download the roster in CSV format.
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: Verify the Attendance Roster loads and displays all enrolled residents with their photos, names, and attendance statuses for the current day.
2. Positive Functional: Verify Full Day attendance is indicated with a green color-coded cell for the corresponding resident and day.
3. Positive Functional: Verify Partial attendance is indicated with an amber color-coded cell.
4. Positive Functional: Verify Absent attendance is indicated with a red color-coded cell.
5. Positive Functional: Verify using date navigation controls updates the roster to reflect attendance data for the newly selected day.
6. Positive Functional: Verify the "Download" button exports the roster in PDF format with correct data.
7. Positive Functional: Verify the "Download" button exports the roster in CSV format with correct data.
8. Positive Functional: Verify the attendance type filter dropdown filters the roster to show only residents with the selected attendance type.
9. Negative: Verify the roster displays a "No records found" message when no residents are enrolled for the selected day.
10. Negative: Verify the download fails gracefully with an error message if the export service is unavailable.
11. UI Scenario: Verify the roster table displays resident photo thumbnails in the correct column with proper sizing.
12. UI Scenario: Verify the "Download" and "Mark Attendance" buttons are visible in the toolbar above the roster.
13. UI Scenario: Verify the date navigation controls allow forward and backward day selection with appropriate date labels.
14. Edge Case: Verify roster behavior when a newly enrolled resident has no historical attendance data.
15. Edge Case: Verify the roster handles a day with all residents marked as Absent.
16. Security: Verify the attendance roster is only accessible to authenticated Providers and does not expose data to unauthorized roles.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Attendance Roster loads and renders all resident rows within 3 seconds for a facility with 100 or more enrolled residents.
2. Verify the "Download" export generates a PDF or CSV file within 5 seconds for a roster containing 100 or more residents.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to track EDWP electronic sign-off status from state case manager.
Description of User Story: The EDWP tracking view provides a status dashboard where the Provider can monitor the electronic sign-off progress of all submitted EDWP forms. Each row in the tracking table displays the resident name, absence date, date sent, case manager name, and a status column showing one of the following states: Pending, Signed Off, or Rejected. The status column uses color-coded badges with green for Signed Off, yellow for Pending, and red for Rejected to allow quick visual scanning. Clicking on a row expands a detail panel showing the full EDWP form content, any case manager comments, and the sign-off timestamp if applicable. A filter bar at the top allows the Provider to filter by status type, date range, or resident name to narrow down the list. An automatic email notification is sent to the Provider when a case manager completes a sign-off or rejects the form.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the EDWP form has been signed off by the case manager, Then display a green "Signed Off" badge in the status column.
2. If the EDWP form is still awaiting case manager action, Then display a yellow "Pending" badge in the status column.
3. If the EDWP form has been rejected by the case manager, Then display a red "Rejected" badge with the rejection reason in the detail panel.
4. If the Provider applies a status filter, Then display only EDWP entries matching the selected status.
5. If the Provider applies a date range filter, Then display only EDWP entries where the absence date falls within the selected range.
6. If the Provider applies a resident name filter, Then display only EDWP entries for the matching resident.
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: Verify the EDWP tracking view loads and displays all submitted forms with correct resident name, absence date, date sent, case manager, and status.
2. Positive Functional: Verify signed-off EDWP forms display a green "Signed Off" badge.
3. Positive Functional: Verify pending EDWP forms display a yellow "Pending" badge.
4. Positive Functional: Verify rejected EDWP forms display a red "Rejected" badge.
5. Positive Functional: Verify clicking a row expands the detail panel with full EDWP content, comments, and sign-off timestamp.
6. Positive Functional: Verify the Provider receives an email notification when a case manager signs off or rejects a form.
7. Positive Functional: Verify filtering by status shows only matching EDWP entries.
8. Positive Functional: Verify filtering by date range narrows results to the selected period.
9. Negative: Verify the tracking view displays a "No records found" message when no EDWP forms have been submitted.
10. Negative: Verify that filtering with a non-existent resident name returns no results with an appropriate message.
11. UI Scenario: Verify status badges use the correct color coding: green for Signed Off, yellow for Pending, red for Rejected.
12. UI Scenario: Verify the detail panel expansion animation is smooth and displays all fields in a readable layout.
13. UI Scenario: Verify the filter bar is positioned at the top and includes status, date range, and resident name filters.
14. Edge Case: Verify tracking behavior when a case manager signs off an EDWP form that was previously rejected and resubmitted.
15. Edge Case: Verify behavior when multiple EDWP forms for the same resident are in different statuses simultaneously.
16. Security: Verify that EDWP tracking data is accessible only to Providers associated with the respective facility and residents.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the EDWP tracking table loads within 3 seconds when displaying 200 or more submitted forms.
2. Verify that applying a filter refreshes the tracking table results within 2 seconds.

User Story:
As a Provider, I should be able to add resident participation in the activities :
 -Therapy activities
- Personal Care activities
- Participation in Meals
- Social Activities
Description of User Story: The Add Services/Activities Modal titled "Add Services - [Resident Name]" provides a structured interface for recording a resident's participation in daily activities. The modal contains a search bar at the top for quickly locating specific services, followed by a "Services List" section displaying categorized checkboxes for Laundry, Meals, Therapy, Personal Care, and Social Activities. When the Provider checks one or more activity checkboxes, the right panel dynamically updates a cost summary showing each selected service with its associated cost and a green Total bar at the bottom aggregating the total cost. The modal footer includes "Cancel" and "Save" buttons, where Cancel discards all selections and closes the modal, and Save records the selected activities against the resident for the current day. Each activity category may contain sub-items that expand when the parent category checkbox is selected.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider checks an activity category checkbox, Then add the activity to the right panel cost summary and update the green Total bar.
2. If the Provider unchecks a previously selected activity, Then remove it from the cost summary and recalculate the Total.
3. If the Provider types in the search bar, Then filter the Services List to show only matching activities.
4. If the Provider clicks "Save" with at least one activity selected, Then record the activities for the resident and close the modal.
5. If the Provider clicks "Save" with no activities selected, Then display a validation message indicating at least one activity must be selected.
6. If the Provider clicks "Cancel", Then discard all selections and close the modal without saving.
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: Verify that opening the Add Services modal displays the resident name in the title and all activity categories (Therapy, Personal Care, Meals, Social Activities) as checkboxes.
2. Positive Functional: Verify checking the Therapy checkbox adds Therapy to the cost summary with the correct cost.
3. Positive Functional: Verify checking the Personal Care checkbox adds Personal Care to the cost summary.
4. Positive Functional: Verify checking the Meals checkbox adds Meals participation to the cost summary.
5. Positive Functional: Verify checking the Social Activities checkbox adds Social Activities to the cost summary.
6. Positive Functional: Verify the green Total bar updates dynamically as activities are checked and unchecked.
7. Positive Functional: Verify clicking "Save" records all selected activities for the resident for the current day.
8. Positive Functional: Verify the search bar filters the Services List to show only matching activities.
9. Negative: Verify clicking "Save" with no activities selected shows a validation message.
10. Negative: Verify that searching for a non-existent activity shows a "No results found" message in the Services List.
11. UI Scenario: Verify the modal layout displays the Services List on the left and the cost summary on the right with a green Total bar at the bottom.
12. UI Scenario: Verify the "Cancel" and "Save" buttons are positioned in the modal footer.
13. UI Scenario: Verify the search bar is positioned at the top of the Services List area.
14. Edge Case: Verify behavior when all available activity categories are selected simultaneously.
15. Edge Case: Verify behavior when the Provider opens the modal for a resident who already has activities recorded for the same day.
16. Security: Verify that only authorized Providers can add activities for residents assigned to their facility.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Add Services/Activities modal loads with the full Services List within 2 seconds.
2. Verify the cost summary and green Total bar update within 500 milliseconds of checking or unchecking an activity checkbox.

User Story:
As a Provider, I should be able to edit resident participation in the activities :
 -Therapy activities
- Personal Care activities
- Participation in Meals
- Social Activities
Description of User Story: The Edit Services/Activities Modal allows the Provider to modify a resident's previously recorded activity participation for a given day. When opened, the modal pre-populates the Services List checkboxes based on the activities already recorded, with checked items reflecting the current participation and the right panel cost summary displaying the existing selections and their costs. The Provider can add new activities by checking additional checkboxes or remove existing activities by unchecking them, with the cost summary and green Total bar updating in real time. A search bar at the top enables quick filtering of the Services List when looking for specific activities to add or remove. The modal footer contains "Cancel" and "Save" buttons; Cancel reverts all changes and closes the modal, while Save persists the updated activity selections. An edit history log records each modification with a timestamp and the Provider who made the change.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the resident has existing recorded activities, Then pre-populate the corresponding checkboxes as checked and display them in the cost summary.
2. If the Provider checks an additional activity, Then add it to the cost summary and update the green Total bar.
3. If the Provider unchecks an existing activity, Then remove it from the cost summary and update the green Total bar.
4. If the Provider clicks "Save" with at least one activity selected, Then persist the updated selections and close the modal.
5. If the Provider clicks "Save" with all activities unchecked, Then display a validation warning that at least one activity must remain selected or confirm removal of all activities.
6. If the Provider clicks "Cancel", Then revert all changes to the original state and close the modal.
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: Verify that opening the edit modal pre-populates checkboxes for already recorded activities and displays them in the cost summary.
2. Positive Functional: Verify adding a new activity by checking its checkbox updates the cost summary and Total bar.
3. Positive Functional: Verify removing an existing activity by unchecking its checkbox removes it from the cost summary and updates the Total bar.
4. Positive Functional: Verify clicking "Save" persists the updated activity selections for the resident.
5. Positive Functional: Verify an edit history log entry is created with the timestamp and Provider identity after saving changes.
6. Positive Functional: Verify the search bar filters the Services List correctly while preserving the state of pre-checked items.
7. Negative: Verify clicking "Cancel" discards all changes and restores the original activity selections.
8. Negative: Verify that unchecking all activities and clicking "Save" displays a validation warning or confirmation.
9. Negative: Verify that saving with no actual changes from the original selection does not create a redundant edit history entry.
10. UI Scenario: Verify the modal title displays "Edit Services - [Resident Name]" and shows the correct date context.
11. UI Scenario: Verify pre-checked activities are visually distinguishable with filled checkboxes in the Services List.
12. UI Scenario: Verify the cost summary on the right panel reflects changes in real time as checkboxes are toggled.
13. Edge Case: Verify behavior when the Provider edits activities for a resident whose attendance was changed from Full Day to Absent after initial recording.
14. Edge Case: Verify behavior when two Providers attempt to edit the same resident's activities simultaneously.
15. Security: Verify that only the Provider who originally recorded the activities or a Provider with supervisor permissions can edit participation records.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Edit Services/Activities modal loads with pre-populated data within 2 seconds.
2. Verify that toggling activity checkboxes updates the cost summary and Total bar within 500 milliseconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to copy activities from master template to all present residents for the day.
Description of User Story: The daily operations screen includes an "Apply Master Template" button that allows the Provider to copy a predefined set of activities from the master activity template to all residents who are marked as present (Full Day or Partial) for the current day. When the Provider clicks the button, a confirmation dialog appears listing the activities defined in the master template along with the count of present residents who will receive the template activities. The dialog contains "Apply" and "Cancel" buttons for the Provider to confirm or abort the bulk operation. Upon confirmation, the system assigns the master template activities to each present resident, skipping any residents already marked as Absent. A progress indicator is displayed during the bulk assignment process, and a summary notification appears upon completion showing the number of residents updated and any skipped entries. Residents who already have individually recorded activities receive the template additions without overwriting their existing records.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider clicks "Apply Master Template", Then display the confirmation dialog with the template activity list and present resident count.
2. If the Provider clicks "Apply" in the confirmation dialog, Then assign template activities to all present residents and display a progress indicator.
