3. If the Provider clicks "Cancel" in the confirmation dialog, Then close the dialog without applying any template activities.
4. If a resident is marked as Absent, Then skip that resident during the bulk template application.
5. If a present resident already has individually recorded activities, Then add the template activities alongside existing records without overwriting.
6. If no residents are marked as present for the day, Then display a message indicating there are no eligible residents and disable the "Apply" button.
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: Verify clicking "Apply Master Template" displays the confirmation dialog with the correct template activities and present resident count.
2. Positive Functional: Verify clicking "Apply" assigns the master template activities to all Full Day residents.
3. Positive Functional: Verify clicking "Apply" assigns the master template activities to all Partial attendance residents.
4. Positive Functional: Verify absent residents are skipped during template application.
5. Positive Functional: Verify the summary notification displays the correct count of updated and skipped residents after completion.
6. Positive Functional: Verify that applying the template to residents with existing activities adds new activities without overwriting existing ones.
7. Negative: Verify clicking "Cancel" in the confirmation dialog does not apply any template activities.
8. Negative: Verify the "Apply" button is disabled when no residents are marked as present.
9. Negative: Verify appropriate error handling when the master template has no activities defined.
10. UI Scenario: Verify the "Apply Master Template" button is visible on the daily operations screen.
11. UI Scenario: Verify the confirmation dialog lists all template activities with clear labels.
12. UI Scenario: Verify a progress indicator is displayed during the bulk assignment process.
13. Edge Case: Verify behavior when the master template is applied twice for the same day without changes to attendance.
14. Edge Case: Verify behavior when a resident's attendance changes from Present to Absent after the template has already been applied.
15. Security: Verify that only Providers with template management permissions can execute the "Apply Master Template" operation.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that applying the master template to 100 or more present residents completes within 10 seconds.
2. Verify the confirmation dialog loads with the template activity list and resident count within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to view daily activity summary for each residents showing all recorded activities.
Description of User Story: The daily activity summary screen provides a consolidated view of all recorded activities for each resident on a selected day. The screen displays a table or card-based layout with each resident represented as a row or card showing their photo, name, attendance type, and a detailed list of all activities recorded for that day including Therapy, Personal Care, Meals, Social Activities, and any other services. Each activity entry includes the activity name, time recorded, and the Provider who logged the participation. A date selector at the top allows navigation between days, and a search or filter bar enables the Provider to find specific residents or filter by activity type. An "Export Summary" button allows the Provider to download the daily activity summary as a PDF or CSV report for record-keeping and compliance documentation.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If activities have been recorded for a resident on the selected day, Then display all activity entries with name, time, and recording Provider.
2. If no activities have been recorded for a resident, Then display a "No activities recorded" label for that resident.
3. If the Provider searches for a resident by name, Then filter the summary to display only matching results.
4. If the Provider selects a specific activity type filter, Then show only residents with the selected activity type.
5. If the Provider clicks "Export Summary" and selects PDF, Then generate and download the summary as a PDF document.
6. If the Provider clicks "Export Summary" and selects CSV, Then generate and download the summary as a CSV file.
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: Verify the daily activity summary loads and displays all residents with their recorded activities for the current day.
2. Positive Functional: Verify each activity entry shows the activity name, time recorded, and the Provider who logged it.
3. Positive Functional: Verify the date selector changes the displayed day and updates the summary accordingly.
4. Positive Functional: Verify the search bar filters the summary to show only the matching resident.
5. Positive Functional: Verify the activity type filter shows only residents with the selected activity type.
6. Positive Functional: Verify the "Export Summary" button generates a PDF with correct daily activity data.
7. Positive Functional: Verify the "Export Summary" button generates a CSV with correct daily activity data.
8. Negative: Verify residents with no recorded activities display a "No activities recorded" message.
9. Negative: Verify searching for a non-existent resident shows a "No results found" message.
10. Negative: Verify the export fails gracefully with an error message if the export service is unavailable.
11. UI Scenario: Verify each resident entry displays a photo thumbnail, name, and attendance type in the summary.
12. UI Scenario: Verify the date selector and filter bar are positioned at the top of the screen for easy access.
13. UI Scenario: Verify the "Export Summary" button is visible in the toolbar area.
14. Edge Case: Verify the summary for a day where all residents are marked as Absent and no activities are recorded.
15. Edge Case: Verify the summary correctly displays activities when a resident has more than 10 different activities recorded in a single day.
16. Security: Verify the daily activity summary is accessible only to Providers with view permissions for the respective facility.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the daily activity summary loads within 3 seconds for a facility with 100 or more residents and multiple activities each.
2. Verify exporting the daily summary as PDF or CSV completes within 5 seconds for 100 or more residents.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to generate and export Monthly Attendance Report for previous month's services.
Description of User Story: The Reports section includes a Monthly Attendance Report generator that allows the Provider to produce a comprehensive attendance report for the previous calendar month. The report configuration screen presents a month/year selector defaulting to the previous month, a facility selector for multi-facility Providers, and optional filters for specific residents or attendance types. Upon clicking "Generate Report", the system compiles attendance data including total days attended, Full Day counts, Partial counts, Absent counts, and total service hours for each resident into a structured report. The generated report is displayed in a preview pane with a tabular layout showing resident-level attendance breakdown, and the Provider can export it as PDF or Excel using "Export PDF" and "Export Excel" buttons in the report toolbar. A summary section at the top of the report shows aggregate facility-level statistics including total residents served, average daily attendance, and total billable service hours.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider selects the previous month and clicks "Generate Report", Then compile and display the attendance data for all residents for that month.
2. If the Provider applies a resident filter, Then generate the report for only the selected residents.
3. If the Provider applies an attendance type filter, Then include only records matching the selected attendance types in the report.
4. If the Provider clicks "Export PDF", Then download the report as a formatted PDF with the facility header and attendance breakdown.
5. If the Provider clicks "Export Excel", Then download the report as an Excel file with separate columns for each attendance metric.
6. If no attendance data exists for the selected month, Then display a "No data available" message and disable the export buttons.
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: Verify the Monthly Attendance Report screen loads with the month/year selector defaulting to the previous month.
2. Positive Functional: Verify clicking "Generate Report" compiles and displays attendance data for all residents for the previous month.
3. Positive Functional: Verify the report shows per-resident breakdown with total days attended, Full Day, Partial, Absent counts, and service hours.
4. Positive Functional: Verify the summary section displays total residents served, average daily attendance, and total billable service hours.
5. Positive Functional: Verify "Export PDF" downloads the report as a properly formatted PDF document.
6. Positive Functional: Verify "Export Excel" downloads the report as an Excel file with correct attendance data.
7. Positive Functional: Verify applying a resident filter generates the report only for the selected residents.
8. Positive Functional: Verify applying an attendance type filter includes only matching records.
9. Negative: Verify that selecting a month with no attendance data displays a "No data available" message.
10. Negative: Verify the export buttons are disabled when no report data is available.
11. Negative: Verify the report generation handles missing or incomplete attendance records gracefully without crashing.
12. UI Scenario: Verify the month/year selector, facility selector, and filter options are clearly labeled and easily accessible.
13. UI Scenario: Verify the report preview pane displays data in a tabular format with sortable column headers.
14. UI Scenario: Verify "Export PDF" and "Export Excel" buttons are prominently placed in the report toolbar.
15. Edge Case: Verify report accuracy when a resident was enrolled mid-month and has partial month data.
16. Edge Case: Verify report behavior for a month where every resident was absent every day.
17. Security: Verify that the Monthly Attendance Report is accessible only to Providers with reporting permissions and does not expose data from other facilities.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that generating the Monthly Attendance Report for a facility with 200 residents completes within 10 seconds.
2. Verify that exporting the report as PDF or Excel for 200 residents completes within 8 seconds.

User Story:
As a Provider, I should be able to select payment type
- Medicaid
- Private Pay
- Insurance
- Insurance + Medicaid
- Insurance + copay
- Medicaid + copay
Description of User Story: This feature allows a Provider to configure the payment type for a resident within the Payment Path Configuration module. The system presents a list of available payment types including Medicaid, Private Pay, Insurance, Insurance + Medicaid, Insurance + copay, and Medicaid + copay. The Provider selects the appropriate payment type based on the resident financial arrangement and payer agreements. Once a payment type is selected, the system dynamically adjusts the subsequent configuration screens to collect relevant information based on the chosen type. Only one primary payment type can be active at a time per resident, and changing the type triggers a confirmation prompt. The selected payment type drives downstream billing logic including invoice generation and payer split configurations.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider selects "Medicaid", Then Medicaid-specific fields (Prior-auth, frequency) must be displayed.
   Else if the Provider selects "Private Pay", Then private payment method fields (Card, Cash, Check) must be displayed.
   Else if the Provider selects "Insurance", Then insurance detail fields (carrier, policy, group) must be displayed.
   Else if the Provider selects "Insurance + Medicaid", Then both insurance and Medicaid configuration sections must be displayed.
   Else if the Provider selects "Insurance + copay", Then insurance fields along with copay amount configuration must be displayed.
   Else if the Provider selects "Medicaid + copay", Then Medicaid fields along with copay amount configuration must be displayed.

2. If the Provider changes an already saved payment type, Then the system must prompt a confirmation dialog before saving.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that a Provider selecting "Medicaid" as the payment type sees Medicaid-specific fields appear.
2. Verify that a Provider selecting "Private Pay" sees private payment method options appear.
3. Verify that a Provider selecting "Insurance" sees insurance detail fields appear.
4. Verify that a Provider selecting "Insurance + Medicaid" sees both Insurance and Medicaid sections displayed.
5. Verify that a Provider selecting "Insurance + copay" sees insurance and copay fields appear.
6. Verify that a Provider selecting "Medicaid + copay" sees Medicaid and copay fields appear.

Negative:
7. Verify that a Provider attempting to save without selecting any payment type sees a validation error.
8. Verify that a Provider changing the payment type after billing has been generated sees a warning about impact on existing invoices.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that all six payment types are listed clearly in the selection dropdown group.
10. Verify that the configuration form dynamically changes based on the selected payment type without page reload.
11. Verify that the confirmation dialog appears with clear messaging when changing an existing payment type.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that a Provider selecting a payment type, navigating away, and returning sees the selection persists.
13. Verify that a Provider rapidly switching between payment types results in the form fields updating correctly each time.

Security:
14. Verify that only authorized Provider roles can access and configure payment types.
15. Verify that payment type selection API calls are authenticated and cannot be manipulated via direct API access.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that payment type selection and dynamic form rendering should complete within 1 second.
Verify that saving the payment type configuration should complete within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add Billing details for Medicaid patient e.g Prior-auth, frequency etc.
Description of User Story: This feature enables a Provider to add Medicaid-specific billing details for a resident whose primary payment type is set to Medicaid. The Provider can enter prior authorization numbers, authorization start and end dates, service frequency, billing codes, and authorized units. The system validates that all required Medicaid billing fields are completed before allowing the configuration to be saved. These details are essential for generating compliant Medicaid claims and ensuring proper reimbursement. The billing details are stored against the resident record and linked to the service plan for accurate claim generation. The form supports adding multiple authorization entries for different service types if the resident receives varied Medicaid-covered services.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If all required Medicaid billing fields are filled with valid data, Then the "Save & next" button must be enabled.
   Else if any required field is empty or invalid, Then the "Save" button must be disabled and validation errors must be shown.

2. If the authorization end date is before the start date, Then the system must display a date validation error.

3. If the Provider adds multiple authorization entries, Then each entry must be saved independently and listed in the billing details.

4. If the Medicaid billing details are saved successfully, Then a confirmation message must appear.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that a Provider can add complete Medicaid billing details including prior-auth number (e.g., "PA-20260401"), dates, frequency, and billing codes.
2. Verify that saved billing details are displayed correctly on the resident payment configuration page.

Negative:
4. Verify that a Provider attempting to save with missing required fields (e.g., no prior-auth number) sees validation errors.
5. Verify that a Provider entering an authorization end date (e.g., "2026-03-01") before the start date (e.g., "2026-04-01") is blocked from saving.
6. Verify that a Provider entering an invalid billing code format sees a validation error.

UI Scenarios:
7. Verify that the Medicaid billing form has clearly labeled fields with appropriate input types (date pickers, text inputs, dropdowns).
9. Verify that validation error messages appear inline next to the respective fields.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a Provider entering a prior-auth number with special characters (e.g., "PA#2026/01") is handled appropriately.
12. Verify that a Provider can add billing details with authorization dates spanning multiple years.

Security:
13. Verify that only authorized Providers can add Medicaid billing details.
14. Verify that Medicaid billing data is encrypted in transit and at rest.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that medicaid billing form should load with all fields within 2 seconds.
Verify that saving Medicaid billing details with multiple authorizations should complete within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit Billing details for Medicaid patient e.g Prior-auth etc.
Description of User Story: This feature allows a Provider to edit previously saved Medicaid billing details for a resident. The Provider can navigate to the resident payment configuration and modify fields such as the prior authorization number, authorization dates, service frequency, billing codes, and authorized units. The system pre-populates the form with existing values so the Provider can easily identify and change specific fields. All edits undergo the same validation rules as the initial entry to maintain data integrity. The system records an audit trail of changes made to Medicaid billing details for compliance purposes. After editing, the updated details are reflected in any pending or future claims but do not alter already submitted claims.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider modifies a field with valid data, Then the "Save" button must be enabled and the change must be saved.
   Else if the Provider clears a required field, Then the system must show a validation error and prevent saving.

2. If the edit is saved successfully, Then a success notification must appear and the form must return to view mode.
   Else if the Provider cancels the edit, Then no changes must be saved and the original values must remain.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that a Provider can edit the prior-auth number (e.g., change "PA-001" to "PA-002") and the update is saved and displayed.
2. Verify that a Provider can edit authorization dates and frequency and the changes are confirmed.
3. Verify that a Provider can edit billing codes and the audit log records the change with previous and new values.
4. Verify that a Provider cancelling an edit results in no changes being applied.

Negative:
5. Verify that a Provider clearing a required field during edit sees a validation error.
6. Verify that a Provider entering an invalid date range (end before start) during edit is blocked from saving.
7. Verify that a Provider attempting to edit billing details for a discharged resident sees an appropriate message.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the edit form pre-populates all fields with current values correctly.
9. Verify that "Save" and "Cancel" buttons are clearly visible and functional during edit mode.
10. Verify that inline validation messages appear when edited values are invalid.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that editing billing details while another user is simultaneously viewing the same record is handled correctly.
12. Verify that editing a field back to its original value does not generate unnecessary audit entries.

Security:
13. Verify that only authorized Providers can edit Medicaid billing details.
14. Verify that the edit API endpoint validates authentication and authorization before processing.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that loading the edit form with pre-populated values should complete within 2 seconds.
Verify that saving edited Medicaid billing details should complete within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add single and multiple payers.
Description of User Story: This feature enables a Provider to add one or more payers to a resident payment configuration in the Assisted Living Facility system.  For each payer, the system collects payer name, payer type (individual, organization, insurance company), relationship to resident, and contact information. The system validates that at least one payer is designated and prevents duplicate payer entries for the same resident. When multiple payers are added, the system enables the split payment configuration feature. The payer list is displayed in a structured format showing all active payers with their roles and can be reordered by priority.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider adds the first payer, Then add more payers button should be visible

2. If the Provider adds additional payers, Then they must be shown in a list.
3. If all required payer fields are filled, Then the "Save" button must be enabled.
   Else if a required field is missing, Then validation errors must appear and saving must be blocked.

4. If the Provider tries to add a duplicate payer, Then the system must display a warning message.

5. If multiple payers are present, Then the split payment configuration option must be enabled automatically.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that a Provider can add a single payer (e.g., "Blue Cross Blue Shield") with complete information and it is saved.
2. Verify that a Provider can add a payer and then successfully add a second payer.
3. Verify that a Provider adding three payers sees all three listed with correct roles
4. Verify that the split payment option becomes available after a Provider adds multiple payers.

Negative:
5. Verify that a Provider attempting to save a payer with missing required fields (e.g., no payer name) sees validation errors.
6. Verify that a Provider attempting to add a duplicate payer entry (same name for same resident) sees a warning.
7. Verify that a Provider attempting to add a payer without any primary payer designated is blocked.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the "Add Payer" button is clearly visible and the payer form layout is intuitive.
9. Verify that the payer list displays payer name, type, relationship, and role in a table format.
10. Verify that the payer form closes or collapses after a successful save.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a Provider can add the maximum number of payers allowed by the system.
12. Verify that a Provider adding a payer with a very long name (e.g., 200 characters) and contact details does not cause UI overflow.

Security:
13. Verify that only authorized Providers can add payers to a resident configuration.
14. Verify that payer information is transmitted over encrypted channels and stored securely.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that adding a new payer and saving should complete within 2 seconds.
Verify that loading the payer list with 10+ payers should render within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit each payer's information
Description of User Story: This feature allows a Provider to edit the information of any payer associated with a resident in the Payment Path Configuration. The Provider can select a payer from the payer list and modify details such as payer name, payer type, relationship to resident, contact information, and payer role (primary, secondary). The system pre-populates the edit form with the current payer information, allowing the Provider to make targeted changes. All edits are validated against the same rules as initial entry to ensure data consistency. The system maintains an audit trail of all payer information changes. If the Provider changes the primary payer designation, the system updates the roles of other payers accordingly.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider edits a field with valid data, Then the changes must be saved upon clicking "Save".
   Else if the Provider clears a required field, Then a validation error must appear and saving must be blocked.

2. If the Provider changes a payer to "Primary", Then the existing primary payer must be demoted to "Secondary" automatically.

3. If the edit is successful, Then a success message must appear and the payer list must refresh.
   Else if the Provider cancels the edit, Then the original values must remain unchanged.

4. If the edited payer is linked to pending invoices, Then the system must notify the Provider of the potential impact.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that a Provider can edit a payer name (e.g., change "Aetna" to "Aetna Health Plan") and contact information and the update is confirmed.
2. Verify that a Provider changing a payer role from Secondary to Primary results in the previous Primary being demoted to Secondary.
3. Verify that a Provider can edit payer type and relationship and the changes are saved.
4. Verify that a Provider cancelling an edit results in no changes being applied.

Negative:
5. Verify that a Provider clearing the payer name field sees a validation error.
6. Verify that a Provider entering an invalid phone number format (e.g., "12345") for the payer contact is rejected.
7. Verify that a Provider attempting to edit a payer linked to finalized invoices sees a warning about the impact.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the edit form pre-populates all fields correctly with current payer data.
9. Verify that "Save" and "Cancel" buttons are functional and clearly labeled.
10. Verify that the payer list updates immediately after a successful edit.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that editing a payer while another user is viewing the same payer information does not cause conflicts.
12. Verify that editing the payer name to contain special characters (e.g., "O'Brien & Associates") is handled correctly.

Security:
13. Verify that only authorized Providers can edit payer information.
14. Verify that the payer edit API validates authorization and prevents unauthorized modifications.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that loading the payer edit form with pre-populated data should complete within 1 second.
Verify that saving payer edits should complete within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to select Card, Cash, Money order, Check payment option.
Description of User Story: This feature allows a Provider to select the preferred payment method for a resident payer within the Payment Path Configuration. The available payment options include Card, Cash, Money Order, and Check. The Provider can choose one or more payment methods applicable to the payer arrangement. When "Card" is selected, the system prompts for card details entry in a subsequent step. For Cash, Money Order, and Check, the system records the selection and may collect additional reference information such as check number or money order tracking number. The selected payment method drives the payment processing workflow and is reflected in the generated invoices. The system allows changing the payment method at any time, with the change taking effect on future transactions.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider selects "Card", Then the card details entry form must become visible and required.
   Else if the Provider selects "Cash", Then no additional details form must be displayed.
   Else if the Provider selects "Money Order", Then an optional tracking number field must appear.
   Else if the Provider selects "Check", Then an optional check number field must appear.

2. If no payment method is selected, Then the system must prevent proceeding to the next configuration step.

3. If the Provider changes the payment method, Then the change must apply to future transactions only.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that a Provider selecting "Card" as payment method sees the card details section appear.
2. Verify that a Provider selecting "Cash" sees the selection saved without additional prompts.
3. Verify that a Provider selecting "Money Order" and entering a tracking number (e.g., "MO-20260401") has the value saved.
4. Verify that a Provider selecting "Check" and entering a check number (e.g., "CHK-1001") has the value saved.
5. Verify that a Provider changing payment method from Cash to Card results in the card details form appearing.

Negative:
6. Verify that a Provider attempting to proceed without selecting any payment method sees an error.
7. Verify that a Provider selecting "Card" but skipping card details entry is blocked from saving.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that all four payment options (Card, Cash, Money Order, Check) are displayed clearly as radio buttons or selectable cards.
9. Verify that dynamic form sections appear smoothly when a payment method is selected.
10. Verify that the currently selected payment method is visually highlighted.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a Provider selecting a payment method, saving, and revisiting the page sees the selection persist.
12. Verify that a Provider selecting "Card" then switching to "Cash" results in the card details section being hidden.

Security:
13. Verify that only authorized Providers can select and configure payment methods.
14. Verify that payment method selection is transmitted securely and not exposed in URL parameters.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that payment method options should render within 1 second of navigating to the section.
Verify that saving the payment method selection should complete within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add card details.
Description of User Story: This feature enables a Provider to add credit or debit card details for a payer within the Payment Path Configuration when Card has been selected as the payment method. The form collects cardholder name, card number, expiration date, CVV, and billing address. The system performs real-time validation on the card number using the Luhn algorithm and verifies the card type (Visa, MasterCard, Amex, Discover) based on the number prefix. Card details are securely stored using tokenization so that the actual card number is never stored in plain text. The system masks the card number after entry, showing only the last four digits for display purposes. The Provider can add multiple cards for a single payer if needed.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If a valid card number is entered, Then the card type icon must be displayed and the field must show a green checkmark.
   Else if an invalid card number is entered, Then the field must show a red error indicator with a message "Invalid card number".

2. If the expiration date is in the past, Then the system must display "Card expired" error and prevent saving.

3. If all card fields are valid, Then the "Save" button must be enabled.

4. If the CVV length does not match the card type requirements, Then a validation error must appear.

5. If the card is saved successfully, Then the card number must be masked to show only the last four digits.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that a Provider can add complete card details with a valid Visa card number (e.g., "4111111111111111") and it is saved.
2. Verify that a Provider adding a MasterCard number sees the card type auto-detected as "MasterCard".
3. Verify that a Provider can add multiple cards for a single payer and all are listed.
4. Verify that after saving card details, the card number is masked showing only the last four digits (e.g., "****1111").

Negative:
5. Verify that a Provider entering an invalid card number (e.g., "1234567890123456") sees a validation error.
6. Verify that a Provider entering an expired expiration date (e.g., "01/20") is prevented from saving.
7. Verify that a Provider leaving required fields blank sees validation errors for each missing field.
8. Verify that a Provider entering a CVV with incorrect length (e.g., "12" for a Visa card) is rejected.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the card form has properly formatted input masks for card number, expiration (MM/YY), and CVV.
10. Verify that the card type icon updates dynamically as the card number is entered.
11. Verify that the billing address fields include street, city, state, and zip code with proper labels.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that a Provider entering a card number with spaces (e.g., "4111 1111 1111 1111") has them stripped before validation.
13. Verify that a Provider can add the maximum number of cards allowed for a single payer.

Security:
14. Verify that card details are tokenized and the actual card number is never stored in plain text in the database.
15. Verify that the card details form is served over HTTPS and CVV is not logged or cached.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that card number validation and type detection should occur within 500 milliseconds of input.
Verify that saving tokenized card details should complete within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit card details.
Description of User Story: This feature allows a Provider to edit previously saved card details for a payer within the Payment Path Configuration. The Provider can select a saved card from the payer card list and modify fields such as cardholder name, expiration date, and billing address. For security reasons, the full card number and CVV are not displayed during editing; only the last four digits and card type are shown. If the Provider needs to change the card number, the system requires entering the full new card number which then replaces the existing tokenized entry. All edits are validated with the same rules as initial entry including Luhn algorithm check for new card numbers. The system maintains an audit log of card detail modifications for compliance tracking.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider edits the cardholder name or billing address, Then the changes must be saved without requiring re-entry of the card number.

2. If the Provider changes the card number, Then the system must validate the new number and re-tokenize it.
   Else if the new card number is invalid, Then a validation error must appear and the change must not be saved.

3. If the updated expiration date is in the past, Then the system must display an error and prevent saving.

4. If the edit is saved successfully, Then a success message must appear.
   Else if the Provider cancels the edit, Then all original values must remain unchanged.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that a Provider can edit the cardholder name (e.g., from "John Smith" to "John A. Smith") and the update is saved.
2. Verify that a Provider can update the expiration date (e.g., from "12/26" to "12/28") and it is reflected.
3. Verify that a Provider can change the card number to a new valid number and the old token is replaced.
4. Verify that a Provider can update the billing address and the change is confirmed.

Negative:
5. Verify that a Provider entering an invalid new card number (e.g., "0000000000000000") sees a validation error.
6. Verify that a Provider setting the expiration date to a past date (e.g., "01/20") is prevented from saving.
7. Verify that a Provider cancelling the edit results in no changes being applied.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the edit form shows the masked card number (e.g., "****1111") and card type icon.
9. Verify that the "Change Card Number" option is clearly accessible and reveals the full card number input field.
10. Verify that validation messages appear inline during editing.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that editing card details while a payment is being processed using the same card is handled correctly.
12. Verify that a Provider editing the card and reverting all changes back to original values does not generate unnecessary audit entries.

Security:
13. Verify that the full card number and CVV are never displayed during editing.
14. Verify that all card edit operations are logged in the audit trail for PCI compliance.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that loading the card edit form with pre-populated data should complete within 1 second.
Verify that saving edited card details with re-tokenization should complete within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to perform split (Equal Split or Custom Split).
Description of User Story: This feature allows a Provider to configure payment split arrangements when a resident has multiple payers in the Payment Path Configuration. The Provider can choose between two split modes: Equal Split, which divides the total amount equally among all payers, or Custom Split, which allows the Provider to define specific percentages or fixed amounts for each payer. When Equal Split is selected, the system automatically calculates each payer share based on the number of payers. In Custom Split mode, the Provider manually enters the percentage or amount for each payer, and the system validates that the total equals 100% or the full billing amount. The split configuration is saved against the resident payment profile and is used during invoice generation to create payer-specific billing amounts.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider selects "Equal Split", Then the system must auto-calculate equal shares and disable manual percentage fields.
   Else if the Provider selects "Custom Split", Then manual percentage or amount fields must be enabled for each payer.

2. If the custom split percentages do not total 100%, Then the system must display a validation error and prevent saving.

3. If the custom split amounts do not total the billing amount, Then the system must show the remaining balance and prevent saving.

4. If only one payer exists, Then the split configuration section must be hidden or disabled.

5. If the split configuration is saved successfully, Then a summary of payer shares must be displayed.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that a Provider selecting Equal Split with 2 payers sees each payer assigned 50%.
2. Verify that a Provider selecting Equal Split with 3 payers sees the calculation handle the remainder correctly (e.g., 33.33%, 33.33%, 33.34%).
3. Verify that a Provider selecting Custom Split and assigning 70% to the primary payer and 30% to the secondary payer saves correctly.
4. Verify that the saved split configuration is reflected correctly in generated invoices.
5. Verify that a Provider switching from Equal Split to Custom Split can enter new percentages.

Negative:
6. Verify that a Provider entering custom split percentages that total more than 100% (e.g., 60% + 60%) sees an error.
7. Verify that a Provider entering custom split percentages that total less than 100% (e.g., 40% + 40%) is prevented from saving.
8. Verify that the split option is disabled when only one payer exists for a resident.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the Equal Split and Custom Split options are displayed as radio buttons or a toggle.
10. Verify that the split breakdown table displays payer names, percentages, and calculated amounts clearly.
11. Verify that a real-time running total is displayed when entering custom split values.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that Equal Split with an odd number of payers (e.g., 3) handles the non-round percentage and rounding correctly.
13. Verify that a Provider entering 0% for one payer in Custom Split mode is handled appropriately.

Security:
14. Verify that only authorized Providers can configure payment splits.
15. Verify that split configuration data cannot be tampered with via API manipulation.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that split calculation for Equal Split should complete and display within 1 second.
Verify that saving custom split configuration for 5+ payers should complete within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to generate separate invoices for each payer based on split configuration.
Description of User Story: This feature enables a Provider to generate individual invoices for each payer associated with a resident based on the configured payment split. When invoices are generated for a billing period, the system reads the split configuration (Equal or Custom) and creates separate invoice documents for each payer showing their respective share of the total charges. Each invoice includes the payer name, their percentage or amount share, detailed line items proportional to their split, applicable taxes, and the total amount due from that specific payer. The invoices are linked to the parent billing record for reconciliation purposes. Providers can preview all split invoices before finalizing them, and each finalized invoice can be independently exported as a PDF or sent to the respective payer.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the split configuration is "Equal Split", Then each payer invoice must show an equal share of the total amount.
   Else if the split configuration is "Custom Split", Then each payer invoice must reflect their specific configured percentage or amount.

2. If a payer invoice is finalized, Then it must become read-only and not editable.

3. If all payer invoices are finalized, Then the parent billing record must update its status to "Fully Invoiced".

4. If one payer invoice generation fails, Then the system must log the error and continue generating remaining invoices.

5. If the split configuration has changed since the last billing period, Then the new configuration must apply to the current invoices.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that a Provider can generate split invoices for 2 payers with Equal Split and each receives exactly 50% of the total.
2. Verify that a Provider generating split invoices with Custom Split (60%/40%) sees the correct amounts on each payer invoice.
3. Verify that previewing all split invoices shows a sum that matches the total billing amount.
4. Verify that a Provider can finalize and export each payer invoice as a PDF.
5. Verify that split invoices are linked to the parent billing record and visible in reconciliation.

Negative:
6. Verify that a Provider attempting to generate split invoices when no split configuration exists sees an error.
7. Verify that a Provider attempting to edit a finalized split invoice is blocked by the system.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the invoice list displays each payer invoice as a separate row with payer name, amount, and status.
9. Verify that the invoice detail view shows payer-specific line items with clear formatting.
10. Verify that the PDF export matches the on-screen invoice layout with correct payer details.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that generating split invoices for a resident with 5 payers results in all 5 invoices being created correctly.
12. Verify that generating invoices when one payer share results in fractional cents is handled with correct rounding.

Security:
13. Verify that only authorized Providers can generate and access split invoices.
14. Verify that payer-specific invoices are not accessible by other payers or unauthorized users.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that generating split invoices for a resident with 5 payers should complete within 5 seconds.
Verify that exporting a split invoice as PDF should complete within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add  Insurance details in Insurance tab.
Description of User Story: This feature allows a Provider to add insurance details for a resident within the Insurance tab of the Payment Path Configuration when Insurance is selected as a payment type. The Provider can enter insurance carrier name, policy number, group number, subscriber name, subscriber relationship to resident, effective date, and termination date. The system validates the insurance information including policy number format and date ranges. Once saved, the insurance details are linked to the resident payer profile and used for claim generation and billing. The form supports adding both primary and secondary insurance policies. The system checks for duplicate policy numbers to prevent errors in claim submissions.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If all required insurance fields are filled with valid data, Then the "Save" button must be enabled.
   Else if a required field is missing, Then validation errors must appear and saving must be blocked.

2. If the policy number already exists for another resident, Then the system must display a warning.


4. If the insurance details are saved successfully, Then a success notification must appear.

5. If the Provider adds secondary insurance, Then it must be clearly labeled as "Secondary" in the insurance list.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that a Provider can add primary insurance details (e.g., carrier "Aetna", policy "POL-123456") with all required fields and they are saved.
2. Verify that a Provider can add secondary insurance details and both primary and secondary are listed correctly.
3. Verify that a Provider entering subscriber details with a non-self relationship (e.g., "Spouse") has the data saved correctly.

Negative:
4. Verify that a Provider attempting to save insurance details with missing required fields (e.g., no policy number) receives validation errors.
6. Verify that a Provider entering a duplicate policy number (already used by another resident) sees a warning.
7. Verify that a Provider leaving the carrier name blank is prevented from saving.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the Insurance tab form is organized with clear sections for carrier info, subscriber info, and dates.
9. Verify that date fields use date pickers and policy number has an appropriate input mask.
10. Verify that the insurance list displays primary and secondary policies with labels and key details.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a Provider can add insurance with an effective date far in the future (e.g., 2 years ahead).
12. Verify that a Provider entering a very long policy number (e.g., 50 characters) does not cause UI overflow.

Security:
13. Verify that only authorized Providers can add insurance details.
14. Verify that insurance information is encrypted in transit and at rest.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that insurance form should load within 2 seconds when navigating to the Insurance tab.
Verify that saving insurance details should complete within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit Insurace details in Insurance tab.
Description of User Story: This feature allows a Provider to edit previously saved insurance details for a resident within the Insurance tab of the Payment Path Configuration. The Provider can modify fields such as insurance carrier name, policy number, group number, subscriber name, subscriber relationship, effective date, and termination date. The edit form pre-populates with the current insurance information so the Provider can easily identify and update specific fields. All edits are validated with the same rules as initial entry to maintain data accuracy. The system records an audit trail of all insurance detail modifications for compliance and claim reconciliation purposes. Updated insurance details are automatically reflected in pending or future claims but do not alter already submitted claims.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider edits a field with valid data, Then the change must be saved upon clicking "Save".
   Else if the Provider clears a required field, Then a validation error must appear and saving must be blocked.

2. If the new termination date is before the effective date, Then the system must prevent saving.

3. If the edit is saved successfully, Then a success notification must appear.
   Else if the Provider cancels the edit, Then original values must remain unchanged.

4. If submitted claims exist with the old insurance details, Then the system must not alter those claims.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that a Provider can edit the insurance carrier name (e.g., from "Aetna" to "Aetna Health") and the update is confirmed.
2. Verify that a Provider can update the policy number and group number and the changes are saved.
3. Verify that a Provider changing the termination date sees the audit log record the change with previous and new values.
4. Verify that a Provider cancelling an edit results in no changes being applied.

Negative:
5. Verify that a Provider clearing the carrier name field sees a validation error.
6. Verify that a Provider setting a termination date (e.g., "2025-01-01") before the effective date (e.g., "2026-01-01") is prevented from saving.
7. Verify that a Provider entering an invalid policy number format sees an appropriate error.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the edit form pre-populates all insurance fields with current values.
9. Verify that "Save" and "Cancel" buttons are visible and functional.
10. Verify that validation messages appear inline during editing.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that editing insurance details while a claim is being processed using those details is handled correctly.
12. Verify that a Provider changing both primary and secondary insurance details in the same session results in both being saved correctly.

Security:
13. Verify that only authorized Providers can edit insurance details.
14. Verify that insurance edit operations are logged for compliance auditing.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that loading the insurance edit form with pre-populated data should complete within 1 second.
Verify that saving edited insurance details should complete within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add billing details for Insurance + Medicaid payment.
Description of User Story: This feature enables a Provider to add billing details when a resident payment type is configured as Insurance + Medicaid within the Payment Path Configuration. The system presents a combined form that collects both insurance billing information and Medicaid billing information in a unified interface. For the insurance portion, the Provider enters carrier details, policy information, and authorized services. For the Medicaid portion, the Provider enters prior authorization numbers, authorization dates, frequency, and billing codes. The system validates that both insurance and Medicaid sections are completed before allowing the save. This dual-payer configuration ensures that claims are properly coordinated between the insurance carrier and Medicaid, with the system supporting coordination of benefits logic for claim processing.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If both Insurance and Medicaid sections have all required fields filled, Then the "Save" button must be enabled.
   Else if the Insurance section is incomplete, Then a validation error must appear on the Insurance section.
   Else if the Medicaid section is incomplete, Then a validation error must appear on the Medicaid section.

2. If the Provider saves only one section and leaves the other incomplete, Then the system must highlight the incomplete section.

3. If both sections are saved successfully, Then a combined success notification must appear.

4. If the coordination of benefits configuration conflicts, Then the system must display a warning about potential claim issues.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that a Provider can add complete Insurance + Medicaid billing details and both sections are saved.
2. Verify that a Provider filling in insurance carrier (e.g., "Cigna"), policy (e.g., "POL-789"), and authorized services has the data saved correctly.
3. Verify that a Provider filling in Medicaid prior-auth (e.g., "PA-20260401"), dates, and billing codes has the data saved correctly.
4. Verify that the saved configuration displays both Insurance and Medicaid details on the payment configuration page.

Negative:
5. Verify that a Provider attempting to save with the Insurance section incomplete sees validation errors on the Insurance section.
6. Verify that a Provider attempting to save with the Medicaid section incomplete sees validation errors on the Medicaid section.
7. Verify that a Provider entering conflicting dates between Insurance and Medicaid sections sees a warning.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the combined form clearly separates Insurance and Medicaid sections with headers or tabs.
9. Verify that both sections have properly labeled fields with appropriate input types.
10. Verify that the form allows scrolling or tabbed navigation between sections without data loss.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a Provider adding billing details with Insurance and Medicaid authorization dates that do not overlap is handled correctly.
12. Verify that a Provider filling both sections and navigating away without saving is prompted about unsaved changes.

Security:
13. Verify that only authorized Providers can add Insurance + Medicaid billing details.
14. Verify that all billing data is encrypted in transit and at rest.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the combined Insurance + Medicaid billing form should load within 2 seconds.
Verify that saving combined billing details should complete within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit billing details for Insurance + Medicaid payment.
Description of User Story: This feature allows a Provider to edit previously saved billing details for a resident with an Insurance + Medicaid payment configuration. The Provider can modify both the insurance billing information and the Medicaid billing information through a unified edit interface. The form pre-populates with existing values for both sections, allowing the Provider to make targeted changes. Insurance fields such as carrier, policy number, and authorized services can be updated independently of Medicaid fields such as prior-auth, authorization dates, and billing codes. All edits are validated with the same rules as initial entry for both sections. The system maintains an audit trail of all changes and ensures that updates are reflected in pending claims while not altering already submitted claims.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider edits Insurance fields with valid data, Then the changes must be saved without affecting Medicaid fields.
   Else if the Provider edits Medicaid fields with valid data, Then the changes must be saved without affecting Insurance fields.

2. If the Provider clears a required field in either section, Then a validation error must appear for that specific section.

3. If both sections are edited and saved successfully, Then a success notification must appear.
   Else if the Provider cancels the edit, Then all original values for both sections must remain unchanged.

4. If already submitted claims exist, Then they must not be modified by the billing detail edits.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that a Provider can edit the insurance carrier name (e.g., from "Cigna" to "Cigna Health") and the update does not affect Medicaid details.
2. Verify that a Provider can edit the Medicaid prior-auth number (e.g., from "PA-001" to "PA-002") and the update does not affect Insurance details.
3. Verify that a Provider editing both Insurance and Medicaid sections simultaneously has all changes saved correctly.
4. Verify that a Provider cancelling the edit results in no changes being applied to either section.

Negative:
5. Verify that a Provider clearing a required Insurance field (e.g., carrier name) sees a validation error on the Insurance section.
6. Verify that a Provider clearing a required Medicaid field (e.g., prior-auth number) sees a validation error on the Medicaid section.
7. Verify that a Provider entering conflicting authorization dates between sections sees a warning.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the edit form pre-populates both Insurance and Medicaid sections with current values.
9. Verify that both sections can be edited independently with their own "Save" or a combined "Save All" button.
10. Verify that inline validation messages appear for each section independently.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that editing billing details while a claim is being processed using the current details is handled correctly.
12. Verify that a Provider editing one section and leaving the other untouched results in only the edited section being updated.

Security:
13. Verify that only authorized Providers can edit Insurance + Medicaid billing details.
14. Verify that all edit operations are logged in the audit trail for compliance.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that loading the combined edit form with pre-populated data should complete within 2 seconds.
Verify that saving edits to both Insurance and Medicaid sections should complete within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to set billing split for both insurance and Medicaid portions.
Description of User Story: The Payment Path Configuration screen provides the Provider with a billing split interface that allows defining how charges are divided between insurance and Medicaid payers for a resident. The screen displays two clearly labeled payer sections, one for Insurance and one for Medicaid, each containing a percentage input field and a dollar amount field that auto-calculates based on the total charge. A toggle or radio group at the top allows the Provider to select "Split Billing" mode, which activates both payer sections simultaneously. The combined percentages must equal exactly 100%, and the system displays a real-time validation indicator showing whether the split is balanced. A "Save Configuration" button at the bottom persists the billing split settings to the resident payment profile, and a summary panel on the right displays the configured split breakdown for quick review.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider selects "Split Billing" mode, Then the system must enable both Insurance and Medicaid payer input sections.

2. If the Provider enters a valid Insurance percentage, Then the system must auto-calculate the Medicaid percentage as the remainder to total 100%.

3. If the combined percentages do not equal 100%, Then the system must display an inline validation error and disable the "Save " button.
   Else if the combined percentages equal exactly 100%, Then the system must display a green balanced indicator and enable the "Save " button.

4. If the Provider clicks "Save " with valid split values, Then the system must persist the billing split to the resident payment profile and display a success message.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Cancel", Then the system must discard all unsaved changes and return to the previous screen.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that selecting "Split Billing" mode enables both Insurance and Medicaid payer sections with percentage and dollar amount fields.
2. Verify that entering an Insurance percentage of 60% auto-calculates the Medicaid percentage as 40% to total 100%.
3. Verify that clicking "Save " with valid split values (e.g., 70% Insurance, 30% Medicaid) saves the billing configuration and displays a success message.
4. Verify that the summary panel displays the correct Insurance and Medicaid split percentages and dollar amounts after saving.
5. Verify that modifying the dollar amount for one payer recalculates the percentage fields accordingly.

Negative:
6. Verify that entering percentages not totaling 100% (e.g., 60% + 30%) displays a validation error and prevents saving.
7. Verify that entering a negative percentage value (e.g., -10%) displays a validation error.
8. Verify that entering a percentage greater than 100 for either payer displays a validation error.
9. Verify that leaving both percentage fields empty and attempting to save displays required field validation errors.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the real-time balanced indicator turns green when the split equals 100% and red otherwise.
11. Verify that the Insurance and Medicaid payer sections are clearly labeled with distinct visual separation.
12. Verify that the "Save " and "Cancel" buttons are properly positioned at the bottom of the form.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that entering decimal percentages such as 33.33% Insurance and 66.67% Medicaid totaling 100% is accepted.
14. Verify that behavior is correct when the total charge amount is zero and the Provider attempts to configure a billing split.

Security:
15. Verify that only authenticated Providers with billing configuration permissions can access and modify the billing split settings.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the Payment Path Configuration screen loads with existing billing split data within 2 seconds.
Verify saving the billing split configuration completes and displays the success confirmation within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to set mixed billing with insurance as primary and Medicaid as secondary.
Description of User Story: The Payment Path Configuration screen allows the Provider to establish a mixed billing arrangement where insurance serves as the primary payer and Medicaid acts as the secondary payer for a resident. The interface presents a payer priority section with two slots: a Primary Payer dropdown defaulting to Insurance and a Secondary Payer dropdown defaulting to Medicaid, both populated from the facility's configured payer list. When Insurance is set as primary, the system captures the insurance policy details including payer name, policy number, and coverage limits in the primary section. The Medicaid section below captures the Medicaid ID and any secondary coverage terms that apply after the insurance portion is billed. A coordination of benefits summary panel shows how charges flow from primary to secondary, including the estimated insurance responsibility and the remaining balance that Medicaid covers. The "Save Configuration" button persists the mixed billing hierarchy to the resident payment profile.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider selects Insurance as the Primary Payer, Then the system must display insurance-specific fields (policy number, payer name, coverage limits) in the primary payer section.

2. If the Provider selects Medicaid as the Secondary Payer, Then the system must display Medicaid-specific fields (Medicaid ID, secondary coverage terms) in the secondary payer section.

3. If both primary and secondary payer details are fully entered, Then the system must enable the "Save Configuration" button and display the coordination of benefits summary.
   Else if the Provider attempts to set the same payer as both primary and secondary, Then the system must display a validation error indicating primary and secondary payers must be different.

4. If the Provider clicks "Save Configuration" with incomplete required fields, Then the system must display inline validation errors and prevent saving.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Cancel", Then the system must discard unsaved changes and navigate back to the previous screen.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that selecting Insurance as primary and Medicaid as secondary displays the correct input fields for each payer section.
2. Verify that entering insurance policy number "POL-123456" in the primary section populates the field correctly.
3. Verify that entering Medicaid ID "MCD-789012" in the secondary section populates the field correctly.
4. Verify that the coordination of benefits summary displays the estimated insurance responsibility and Medicaid remaining balance.
5. Verify that clicking "Save Configuration" with all required fields saves the mixed billing hierarchy and displays a success message.

Negative:
6. Verify that setting the same payer type as both primary and secondary displays a validation error.
7. Verify that saving without entering the insurance policy number displays a required field validation error.
8. Verify that saving without entering the Medicaid ID displays a required field validation error.
9. Verify that entering an invalid insurance policy number format displays a format validation error.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the Primary Payer and Secondary Payer sections are visually distinct with clear labels indicating payer priority.
11. Verify that the coordination of benefits summary panel updates dynamically as payer details are entered.
12. Verify that the payer priority dropdowns display available payer options from the facility configuration.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that behavior is correct when the insurance coverage limit is lower than the total charge amount and the full remaining balance flows to Medicaid.
14. Verify that behavior is correct when the Provider changes the primary payer from Insurance to Medicaid after initial configuration.

Security:
15. Verify that only Providers with payment configuration permissions can access and modify the mixed billing settings.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the Payment Path Configuration screen loads payer dropdowns and existing configuration data within 2 seconds.
Verify saving the mixed billing configuration with coordination of benefits calculation completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add billing details for mixed Copay + Insurance.
Description of User Story: The Payment Path Configuration screen provides a form section for the Provider to add billing details when a resident uses a mixed payment arrangement combining Copay and Insurance. The form displays a Copay section with fields for copay amount, payment frequency (monthly, weekly, per service), and payment method selection. Below the Copay section, an Insurance section captures the insurance payer name, policy number, group number, and coverage effective dates. A billing summary panel on the right side calculates the total charge breakdown showing the copay portion and the insurance-covered portion in real time as fields are populated. The Provider must complete all required fields in both sections before the "Add Billing Details" button becomes active. Upon successful submission, the mixed Copay and Insurance billing configuration is stored against the resident profile and a confirmation toast notification is displayed.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider enters a valid copay amount and selects a payment frequency, Then the system must populate the copay portion in the billing summary panel.

2. If the Provider enters valid insurance details including payer name, policy number, and group number, Then the system must populate the insurance portion in the billing summary panel.

3. If all required fields in both Copay and Insurance sections are completed, Then the system must enable the "Add Billing Details" button.
   Else if any required field is missing or invalid, Then the system must keep the "Add Billing Details" button disabled and display inline validation hints.

4. If the Provider clicks "Add Billing Details" with valid data, Then the system must save the billing configuration and display a success toast notification.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Cancel", Then the system must discard all entered data and close the billing details form.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the add billing details form displays both Copay and Insurance sections with all required input fields.
2. Verify that entering a copay amount of $50 and selecting "Monthly" frequency populates the copay portion of the billing summary.
3. Verify that entering insurance payer name "Blue Cross", policy number "POL-001", group number "GRP-001", and coverage dates populates the insurance portion of the billing summary.
4. Verify that the billing summary panel calculates and displays the correct copay and insurance charge breakdown in real time.
5. Verify that clicking "Add Billing Details" with all required fields saves the configuration and displays a confirmation notification.

Negative:
6. Verify that leaving the copay amount field empty displays a required field validation error.
7. Verify that entering a negative copay amount (e.g., -$20) displays a validation error.
8. Verify that leaving the insurance policy number empty displays a required field validation error.
9. Verify that entering a coverage end date before the coverage start date displays a date range validation error.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the Copay and Insurance sections are visually separated with clear section headers.
11. Verify that the billing summary panel updates dynamically as the Provider enters values in both sections.
12. Verify that the "Add Billing Details" button is disabled until all required fields are completed.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that behavior is correct when the copay amount exceeds the total charge, leaving zero for the insurance portion.
14. Verify that behavior is correct when the Provider enters a copay amount of zero, making insurance responsible for the full amount.

Security:
15. Verify that insurance policy numbers and billing details are transmitted over encrypted connections and not exposed in browser logs.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the add billing details form loads with all sections and dropdowns populated within 2 seconds.
Verify saving the mixed Copay + Insurance billing configuration completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit billing details for mixed Copay + Insurance.
Description of User Story: The Payment Path Configuration screen allows the Provider to edit previously saved billing details for a mixed Copay + Insurance payment arrangement. When the Provider selects an existing mixed billing configuration and clicks the "Edit" button, the form opens pre-populated with the current copay amount, payment frequency, insurance payer name, policy number, group number, and coverage dates. All fields in both the Copay and Insurance sections become editable, allowing the Provider to modify any value as needed. The billing summary panel on the right recalculates in real time as the Provider makes changes to reflect the updated charge breakdown. A "Save Changes" button replaces the "Add Billing Details" button, and clicking it validates all fields before persisting the updated configuration. An audit trail entry is created recording the previous values and the new values along with the timestamp and Provider identity.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider clicks "Edit" on an existing mixed Copay + Insurance configuration, Then the system must open the form pre-populated with current billing values and enable all fields for editing.

2. If the Provider modifies the copay amount or payment frequency, Then the system must recalculate the billing summary panel to reflect the updated copay and insurance portions.

3. If the Provider modifies insurance details, Then the system must update the Insurance section and recalculate the billing summary accordingly.

4. If all modified fields pass validation, Then the system must enable the "Save Changes" button.
   Else if any modified field fails validation, Then the system must display inline validation errors and disable the "Save Changes" button.

5. If the Provider clicks "Cancel" after making edits, Then the system must discard all unsaved changes and revert the form to the last saved state.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking "Edit" on an existing mixed billing configuration opens the form pre-populated with the current copay and insurance details.
2. Verify that modifying the copay amount from $50 to $75 recalculates the billing summary panel in real time.
3. Verify that modifying the insurance policy number updates the Insurance section correctly.
4. Verify that clicking "Save Changes" with valid data persists the updated billing configuration and displays a success notification.
5. Verify that an audit trail entry is created recording previous values, new values, timestamp, and Provider identity after a successful edit.

Negative:
6. Verify that clearing the copay amount field and attempting to save displays a required field validation error.
7. Verify that entering an invalid insurance policy number format displays a validation error.
8. Verify that setting the coverage end date before the start date displays a date range validation error.
9. Verify that clicking "Save Changes" with no actual modifications displays an informational message indicating no changes were detected.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the form displays "Save Changes" and "Cancel" buttons instead of "Add Billing Details" when in edit mode.
11. Verify that the billing summary panel updates dynamically as the Provider edits values in both sections.
12. Verify that pre-populated fields display the current saved values accurately when the form opens in edit mode.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that behavior is correct when two Providers attempt to edit the same billing configuration simultaneously and a concurrency conflict is handled.
14. Verify that behavior is correct when the Provider changes the copay amount to a value that exceeds the total charge.

Security:
15. Verify that only Providers with billing edit permissions can access the edit functionality and unauthorized users see the configuration in read-only mode.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the edit form loads with pre-populated billing data within 2 seconds.
Verify saving edited billing configuration and generating the audit trail entry completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add billing details for mixed Medicaid + copay.
Description of User Story: The Payment Path Configuration screen provides a dedicated form for the Provider to add billing details when a resident uses a mixed Medicaid and copay payment arrangement. The Medicaid section at the top of the form captures the Medicaid ID, Medicaid plan name, eligibility start date, and any coverage notes specific to the Medicaid program. Below the Medicaid section, a Copay section allows the Provider to enter the copay amount, copay frequency (monthly, weekly, or per service), and the preferred copay collection method. A billing breakdown panel on the right calculates and displays the Medicaid-covered portion and the resident copay responsibility based on the entered values. All required fields are marked with asterisks and inline validation ensures data integrity before the form can be submitted. The "Add Billing Details" button at the form footer saves the mixed Medicaid and copay configuration to the resident profile upon successful validation.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider enters a valid Medicaid ID and plan name, Then the system must populate the Medicaid portion in the billing breakdown panel.

2. If the Provider enters a valid copay amount and selects a frequency, Then the system must populate the copay portion in the billing breakdown panel.

3. If all required fields in both Medicaid and Copay sections are completed, Then the system must enable the "Add Billing Details" button.
   Else if any required field is missing or invalid, Then the system must keep the "Add Billing Details" button disabled and display inline validation hints.

4. If the Provider clicks "Add Billing Details" with valid data, Then the system must save the billing configuration and display a success notification.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Cancel", Then the system must discard all entered data and close the form.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the add billing details form displays both Medicaid and Copay sections with all required input fields.
2. Verify that entering Medicaid ID "MCD-001", plan name "Medicaid Basic", and eligibility date "01/01/2026" populates the Medicaid portion of the billing breakdown.
3. Verify that entering copay amount $30, frequency "Monthly", and collection method "Check" populates the copay portion of the billing breakdown.
4. Verify that the billing breakdown panel calculates and displays the correct Medicaid and copay charge distribution.
5. Verify that clicking "Add Billing Details" with all required fields saves the configuration and displays a confirmation notification.

Negative:
6. Verify that leaving the Medicaid ID field empty displays a required field validation error.
7. Verify that entering an invalid Medicaid ID format displays a format validation error.
8. Verify that leaving the copay amount field empty displays a required field validation error.
9. Verify that entering a negative copay amount (e.g., -$15) displays a validation error.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the Medicaid and Copay sections are visually separated with clear section headers and labeled fields.
11. Verify that the billing breakdown panel updates dynamically as the Provider enters values in both sections.
12. Verify that the "Add Billing Details" button is disabled until all required fields are completed.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that behavior is correct when the copay amount is set to zero, making Medicaid responsible for the full charge.
14. Verify that behavior is correct when the Medicaid eligibility start date is set to a future date.

Security:
15. Verify that Medicaid ID and billing details are transmitted securely and not logged in plaintext in application logs.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the add billing details form loads with all sections and dropdown options populated within 2 seconds.
Verify saving the mixed Medicaid + Copay billing configuration completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit billing details for mixed Medicaid + copay.
Description of User Story: The Payment Path Configuration screen allows the Provider to edit previously saved billing details for a mixed Medicaid and copay payment arrangement. When the Provider selects an existing Medicaid + Copay billing configuration and clicks the "Edit" button, the form opens pre-populated with the current Medicaid ID, plan name, eligibility date, copay amount, copay frequency, and collection method. All fields in both sections become editable, enabling the Provider to update any value such as changing the copay amount due to a plan revision or updating the Medicaid plan name after a re-enrollment. The billing breakdown panel recalculates dynamically as changes are made. A "Save Changes" button validates all fields and persists the updated configuration upon successful submission. The system maintains an audit log capturing the previous and updated values along with the modification timestamp and Provider identity.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider clicks "Edit" on an existing Medicaid + Copay configuration, Then the system must open the form pre-populated with current values and enable all fields for editing.

2. If the Provider modifies the copay amount or frequency, Then the system must recalculate the billing breakdown panel to reflect the updated distribution.

3. If the Provider modifies Medicaid details such as ID or plan name, Then the system must update the Medicaid section and recalculate the billing breakdown.

4. If all modified fields pass validation, Then the system must enable the "Save Changes" button.
   Else if any modified field fails validation, Then the system must display inline validation errors and disable the "Save Changes" button.

5. If the Provider clicks "Cancel" after making edits, Then the system must discard all unsaved changes and revert the form to the last saved state.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking "Edit" on an existing Medicaid + Copay billing configuration opens the form pre-populated with current Medicaid and copay details.
2. Verify that modifying the copay amount from $30 to $45 recalculates the billing breakdown panel dynamically.
3. Verify that modifying the Medicaid plan name updates the Medicaid section correctly.
