4. Verify that clicking "Save Changes" with valid data saves the updated configuration and displays a success notification.
5. Verify that an audit log entry is created recording previous values, new values, timestamp, and Provider identity after a successful edit.

Negative:
6. Verify that clearing the Medicaid ID field and attempting to save displays a required field validation error.
7. Verify that entering an invalid Medicaid ID format during editing displays a format validation error.
8. Verify that setting the copay amount to a negative value (e.g., -$10) displays a validation error.
9. Verify that clicking "Save Changes" with no actual modifications displays an informational message indicating no changes were detected.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the form displays "Save Changes" and "Cancel" buttons when in edit mode.
11. Verify that the billing breakdown panel updates dynamically as the Provider edits values in both sections.
12. Verify that pre-populated fields display the current saved values accurately when the edit form opens.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that behavior is correct when two Providers attempt to edit the same billing configuration simultaneously and a concurrency conflict is handled gracefully.
14. Verify that behavior is correct when the Provider changes the Medicaid eligibility date to a past date that has already been billed.

Security:
15. Verify that only Providers with billing edit permissions can access the edit functionality and unauthorized users see the configuration in read-only mode.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the edit form loads with pre-populated Medicaid and copay data within 2 seconds.
Verify saving edited billing configuration and generating the audit log entry completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to select Card, Cash, Money order, Check payment option.
Description of User Story: The Payment Path Configuration screen includes a Payment Method Selection section that presents the Provider with four distinct payment options: Card, Cash, Money Order, and Check. Each option is displayed as a selectable tile or radio button with an associated icon and label for easy identification. When the Provider selects a payment method, the corresponding detail section expands below to capture method-specific information such as card details for Card, receipt acknowledgment for Cash, money order number for Money Order, or check number and bank name for Check. Only one payment method can be active at a time, and selecting a different method collapses the previous detail section and expands the new one. The selected payment method is highlighted with a distinct border or background color to confirm the active selection. A "Confirm Selection" button saves the chosen payment method to the resident payment profile.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider selects "Card", Then the system must expand the Card detail section and collapse any other open detail sections.
   Else if the Provider selects "Cash", Then the system must expand the Cash detail section and collapse any other open detail sections.
   Else if the Provider selects "Money Order", Then the system must expand the Money Order detail section and collapse any other open detail sections.
   Else if the Provider selects "Check", Then the system must expand the Check detail section and collapse any other open detail sections.

2. If the Provider clicks "Confirm Selection" with all required method-specific details completed, Then the system must save the payment method and display a success confirmation.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Confirm Selection" without completing required method-specific details, Then the system must display validation errors on the incomplete fields.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Payment Method Selection section displays four options: Card, Cash, Money Order, and Check with appropriate icons.
2. Verify that selecting "Card" expands the Card detail section with card-specific input fields.
3. Verify that selecting "Cash" expands the Cash detail section with receipt acknowledgment.
4. Verify that selecting "Money Order" expands the Money Order detail section with money order number field.
5. Verify that selecting "Check" expands the Check detail section with check number and bank name fields.
6. Verify that clicking "Confirm Selection" with complete details saves the payment method and displays a success message.

Negative:
7. Verify that clicking "Confirm Selection" without selecting any payment method displays a validation error.
8. Verify that selecting "Check" and leaving the check number field empty displays a required field validation error.
9. Verify that selecting "Money Order" and leaving the money order number empty displays a required field validation error.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the selected payment method tile is highlighted with a distinct border or background color.
11. Verify that selecting a new payment method collapses the previously expanded detail section and expands the new one.
12. Verify that each payment option tile displays an appropriate icon and label for easy identification.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that behavior is correct when the Provider rapidly switches between payment methods before completing any detail fields.
14. Verify that behavior is correct when the Provider selects a payment method, fills in details, switches to another method, and then switches back to check if previously entered details are retained.

Security:
15. Verify that payment method selections and details are not cached in the browser and are transmitted securely over encrypted connections.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the Payment Method Selection section loads with all four options displayed within 1 second.
Verify saving the selected payment method and details completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add card details.
Description of User Story: The Card Details form within the Payment Path Configuration allows the Provider to add credit or debit card information for a resident's payment profile. The form presents clearly labeled input fields for cardholder name, card number with input masking that displays only the last four digits after entry, expiration date with month and year selectors, and CVV with a masked input field. A card type indicator automatically detects and displays the card brand (Visa, MasterCard, Amex, Discover) based on the entered card number prefix. The form includes a billing address section with fields for street address, city, state, and ZIP code that can be auto-populated from the resident profile if available. An "Add Card" button validates all fields including Luhn algorithm verification for the card number and saves the card details in an encrypted format to the resident payment profile.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider enters a card number starting with 4 (Visa), Then the system must display the Visa brand icon next to the card number field.
   Else if the Provider enters a card number starting with 5 (MasterCard), Then the system must display the MasterCard brand icon.
   Else if the Provider enters a card number starting with 3 (Amex), Then the system must display the Amex brand icon.
   Else if the Provider enters a card number starting with 6 (Discover), Then the system must display the Discover brand icon.

2. If the Provider enters a card number that fails Luhn algorithm validation, Then the system must display an inline error "Invalid card number" and prevent form submission.

3. If the Provider selects an expiration date that is in the past, Then the system must display an inline error "Card is expired" and prevent form submission.

4. If all required fields are valid, Then the system must enable the "Add Card" button.
   Else if any required field is missing or invalid, Then the system must disable the "Add Card" button and display inline validation hints.

5. If the Provider clicks "Auto-fill from Profile" and a billing address exists on the resident profile, Then the system must populate the address fields automatically.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Card Details form displays all required fields: cardholder name, card number, expiration date, CVV, and billing address.
2. Verify that entering a valid Visa card number (starting with 4) auto-detects and displays the Visa brand icon.
3. Verify that entering a valid MasterCard number (starting with 5) auto-detects and displays the MasterCard brand icon.
4. Verify that the card number field masks the input to show only the last four digits after entry.
5. Verify that clicking "Add Card" with all valid details saves the card information and displays a success notification.
6. Verify that the "Auto-fill from Profile" button populates billing address fields from the resident profile.

Negative:
7. Verify that entering a card number that fails Luhn validation displays an "Invalid card number" error.
8. Verify that selecting a past expiration date displays a "Card is expired" error.
9. Verify that leaving the cardholder name empty displays a required field validation error.
10. Verify that entering a CVV with fewer digits than required displays a validation error.

UI Scenarios:
11. Verify that the card brand icon updates dynamically as the Provider types the card number.
12. Verify that the CVV field uses a masked input that hides the entered digits.
13. Verify that the expiration date uses separate month and year dropdown selectors.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify that behavior is correct when the Provider enters a card number for an unsupported card brand.
15. Verify that behavior is correct when the resident profile has no billing address and "Auto-fill from Profile" is clicked.

Security:
16. Verify that card details are encrypted before being stored and the full card number is never displayed after initial entry.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the Card Details form loads with all input fields and card brand detection within 1 second.
Verify saving encrypted card details to the resident payment profile completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit  card details.
Description of User Story: The Card Details edit form allows the Provider to modify previously saved card information in the resident payment profile. When the Provider clicks "Edit" on an existing card entry, the form opens with the cardholder name, masked card number showing only the last four digits, expiration date, and billing address pre-populated from the saved record. The CVV field is always blank for security reasons and must be re-entered for any update. The Provider can modify the cardholder name, enter a new card number which triggers re-validation and card brand detection, update the expiration date, or change the billing address. A "Save Changes" button validates all modified fields including Luhn verification for any new card number and persists the updated encrypted card details. The system logs an audit entry recording which fields were changed along with the timestamp and the Provider who made the modification.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider clicks "Edit" on a saved card, Then the system must open the edit form with pre-populated values and the CVV field blank.

2. If the Provider enters a new card number, Then the system must re-validate using the Luhn algorithm and update the card brand icon.

3. If the Provider modifies the expiration date to a past date, Then the system must display a "Card is expired" validation error.

4. If the Provider does not re-enter the CVV, Then the system must display a required field error on the CVV field when "Save Changes" is clicked.

5. If all fields pass validation, Then the system must enable the "Save Changes" button and save the encrypted card details upon click.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Cancel", Then the system must discard all changes and close the edit form.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking "Edit" on a saved card opens the form pre-populated with cardholder name, masked card number, expiration date, and billing address.
2. Verify that the CVV field is blank in edit mode and must be re-entered.
3. Verify that entering a new card number triggers card brand re-detection and Luhn validation.
4. Verify that modifying the cardholder name and saving updates the name correctly.
5. Verify that clicking "Save Changes" with valid data saves the updated encrypted card details and displays a success notification.
6. Verify that an audit entry is created after successful card detail modification.

Negative:
7. Verify that saving without re-entering the CVV displays a required field validation error.
8. Verify that entering a new card number failing Luhn validation displays an error and prevents saving.
9. Verify that changing the expiration date to a past date displays an expired card error.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the masked card number displays only the last four digits with asterisks for the remaining digits.
11. Verify that the card brand icon updates when a new card number is entered.
12. Verify that "Save Changes" and "Cancel" buttons are displayed in the edit form footer.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that behavior is correct when the Provider edits only the billing address without changing the card number or expiration date.
14. Verify that behavior is correct when concurrent edit attempts on the same card entry are handled with a conflict notification.

Security:
15. Verify that the full card number is never displayed in the edit form and card details are re-encrypted after modification.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the card edit form loads with pre-populated data within 2 seconds.
Verify saving updated encrypted card details completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to perform split (Equal Split or Custom Split).
Description of User Story: The Payment Path Configuration screen provides a Split Payment section that allows the Provider to divide a resident's total charge across multiple payers using either an Equal Split or a Custom Split method. A toggle or radio group at the top presents the two split modes: selecting "Equal Split" automatically divides the total charge equally among all configured payers, while selecting "Custom Split" enables manual percentage or dollar amount input fields for each payer. The Equal Split mode displays a read-only breakdown showing each payer with their equal share and the corresponding dollar amount. The Custom Split mode displays editable fields next to each payer with a running total indicator that turns green when the sum equals 100% and red when it does not. Both modes support two or more payers, and the Provider can add or remove payer rows in Custom Split mode. A "Apply Split" button saves the selected split configuration to the resident billing profile.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider selects "Equal Split", Then the system must calculate each payer's share by dividing the total charge equally and display the read-only breakdown.
   Else if the Provider selects "Custom Split", Then the system must enable editable percentage and dollar amount fields for each payer.

2. If the sum of Custom Split percentages equals 100%, Then the system must display a green running total indicator and enable the "Apply Split" button.
   Else if the sum of Custom Split percentages does not equal 100%, Then the system must display a red running total indicator and disable the "Apply Split" button.

3. If the Provider adds a payer row in Custom Split mode, Then the system must add a new editable row and recalculate the running total.
   Else if the Provider removes a payer row in Custom Split mode, Then the system must remove the row and recalculate the running total.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Split Payment section displays "Equal Split" and "Custom Split" options.
2. Verify that selecting "Equal Split" with two payers divides the total charge into two equal portions.
3. Verify that selecting "Equal Split" with three payers divides the total charge into three equal portions.
4. Verify that selecting "Custom Split" enables editable percentage fields for each payer.
5. Verify that the running total indicator turns green when custom percentages total 100%.
6. Verify that clicking "Apply Split" saves the configuration and displays a success message.
7. Verify that adding a new payer row in Custom Split mode displays a new editable row.

Negative:
8. Verify that custom percentages not totaling 100% display a red running total indicator and disable the "Apply Split" button.
9. Verify that removing all payer rows in Custom Split mode displays a validation error requiring at least two payers.
10. Verify that entering a negative percentage for any payer displays a validation error.

UI Scenarios:
11. Verify that the Equal Split mode displays a read-only breakdown with payer names and equal dollar amounts.
12. Verify that the Custom Split mode displays editable fields with a running total indicator.
13. Verify that the toggle between Equal Split and Custom Split modes switches the view correctly.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify that Equal Split behavior is correct when the total charge does not divide evenly among the number of payers, resulting in rounding differences.
15. Verify that behavior is correct when the Provider switches from Custom Split to Equal Split after entering partial custom values.

Security:
16. Verify that only authorized Providers with payment configuration permissions can access and modify the split payment settings.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the Split Payment section loads with payer data and split options within 2 seconds.
Verify applying the split configuration and saving completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to select facility service model (A La Carte or Blended Rate).
Description of User Story: The Service Model Configuration screen presents the Provider with two distinct service model options for the facility: A La Carte and Blended Rate. Each option is displayed as a selectable card with a descriptive title, a brief explanation of the billing approach, and an illustrative icon. The A La Carte card describes individual service tracking and pricing, while the Blended Rate card describes a single bundled price covering all services. When the Provider selects a model, the card is highlighted with a distinct border or background color to indicate the active selection. A description panel below the cards provides detailed billing implications for the selected model, including how charges will be calculated and what reporting will be available. The "Confirm Selection" button saves the chosen service model to the facility profile, and a warning dialog appears if the Provider attempts to switch models after initial configuration.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider selects "A La Carte", Then the system must highlight the A La Carte card and display individual service tracking billing implications in the description panel.
   Else if the Provider selects "Blended Rate", Then the system must highlight the Blended Rate card and display bundled pricing billing implications in the description panel.

2. If the Provider clicks "Confirm Selection" with a model selected, Then the system must save the service model to the facility profile.
   Else if no service model is selected when "Confirm Selection" is clicked, Then the system must display a validation error requesting model selection.

3. If the Provider attempts to switch from an already-configured model, Then the system must display a warning dialog explaining the implications before allowing the change.
   Else if the Provider cancels the model switch in the warning dialog, Then the system must retain the current model and close the dialog.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Service Model Configuration screen displays A La Carte and Blended Rate cards with titles, descriptions, and icons.
2. Verify that selecting "A La Carte" highlights the card and displays A La Carte billing implications.
3. Verify that selecting "Blended Rate" highlights the card and displays Blended Rate billing implications.
4. Verify that clicking "Confirm Selection" saves the chosen model and displays a success message.
5. Verify that switching models after initial configuration displays a warning dialog.
6. Verify that confirming the model switch in the warning dialog saves the new model and archives the previous configuration.

Negative:
7. Verify that clicking "Confirm Selection" without selecting any model displays a validation error.
8. Verify that canceling the model switch in the warning dialog retains the current model.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the selected service model card is highlighted with a distinct border or background color.
10. Verify that the description panel updates dynamically when switching between model selections.
11. Verify that both cards display clear icons and brief descriptions that differentiate the two models.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that behavior is correct when the Provider selects a model, navigates away without saving, and returns to the screen.
13. Verify that behavior is correct when the facility has active billing records and the Provider attempts to switch service models.

Security:
14. Verify that only facility administrators or Providers with configuration permissions can select or change the service model.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the Service Model Configuration screen loads with both model cards and descriptions within 2 seconds.
Verify saving the service model selection completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to configure A La Carte model to track individual services (Room, Board, Laundry, Transport).
Description of User Story: The A La Carte Configuration screen allows the Provider to set up individual service tracking for the facility by defining each billable service separately. The screen displays a services table with pre-defined service categories including Room, Board, Laundry, and Transport, each with columns for service name, unit of measure (per day, per week, per month), unit price, and an active/inactive toggle. The Provider can edit the unit price for each service and toggle services on or off based on the facility offerings. An "Add Custom Service" button at the top of the table allows the Provider to add additional service lines beyond the default categories with custom names and pricing. Each service row includes a description field where the Provider can enter details about what the service includes. The "Save Configuration" button at the bottom validates all price entries and saves the A La Carte service lineup to the facility billing profile.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider enters a valid unit price for a service, Then the system must display the price in the unit price column and include the service in billing calculations.

2. If the Provider toggles a service to inactive, Then the system must exclude the service from billing calculations and gray out the row.
   Else if the Provider toggles an inactive service back to active, Then the system must re-include the service in billing calculations and restore the row styling.

3. If the Provider clicks "Add Custom Service", Then the system must add a new editable row to the services table with blank fields for name, unit of measure, unit price, and description.

4. If the Provider clicks "Save Configuration" with all valid prices, Then the system must save the configuration and display a success message.
   Else if any active service has a missing or invalid price, Then the system must display a validation error and prevent saving.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the A La Carte Configuration screen displays pre-defined services (Room, Board, Laundry, Transport) with editable price fields.
2. Verify that entering a valid unit price of $120 per day for the Room service captures the value correctly.
3. Verify that toggling a service (e.g., Laundry) to inactive excludes it from billing calculations.
4. Verify that toggling an inactive service back to active re-includes it in billing calculations.
5. Verify that clicking "Add Custom Service" adds a new editable row with blank fields.
6. Verify that clicking "Save Configuration" with all valid prices saves the service lineup and displays a success message.

Negative:
7. Verify that leaving a unit price empty for an active service displays a required field validation error on save.
8. Verify that entering a negative unit price (e.g., -$50) displays a validation error.
9. Verify that entering non-numeric characters in the unit price field displays a format validation error.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that each service row displays columns for service name, unit of measure, unit price, description, and active/inactive toggle.
11. Verify that inactive service rows are visually grayed out to distinguish them from active services.
12. Verify that the "Add Custom Service" button is positioned at the top of the services table.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that behavior is correct when the Provider adds multiple custom services with the same name.
14. Verify that behavior is correct when all pre-defined services are toggled to inactive, leaving only custom services active.

Security:
15. Verify that only Providers with service configuration permissions can modify service prices and toggle service availability.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the A La Carte Configuration screen loads with all pre-defined services and prices within 2 seconds.
Verify saving the A La Carte configuration with 20 or more services completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to configure Blended Rate model with single bundle price and no individual service tracking.
Description of User Story: The Blended Rate Configuration screen allows the Provider to set a single bundled price that covers all facility services without tracking individual service usage. The screen displays a clean form with a "Bundle Price" input field where the Provider enters the all-inclusive monthly rate, a "Rate Effective Date" picker for specifying when the rate takes effect, and a description text area for documenting what services are included in the bundle. An information banner at the top of the screen clarifies that selecting Blended Rate means individual services such as Room, Board, Laundry, and Transport will not be tracked or billed separately. The form also includes a "Services Included" checklist showing all facility services that are covered under the blended rate, displayed as read-only checked items for documentation purposes. The "Save Configuration" button validates the bundle price and effective date before saving the Blended Rate settings to the facility billing profile.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider enters a valid bundle price and effective date, Then the system must enable the "Save Configuration" button.
   Else if the Provider leaves the Bundle Price field empty, Then the system must display a required field validation error and disable the "Save Configuration" button.

2. If the Provider selects an effective date in the past, Then the system must display a warning indicating the rate will be applied retroactively.

3. If the Provider clicks "Save Configuration" with valid data, Then the system must save the Blended Rate configuration and display a success message.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Cancel", Then the system must discard all entries and navigate back to the Service Model selection screen.

4. When the information banner is displayed, Then the system must clearly state that individual services will not be tracked separately under this model.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Blended Rate Configuration screen displays the Bundle Price field, Rate Effective Date picker, description area, and information banner.
2. Verify that entering a valid bundle price of $3,500 captures the value correctly.
3. Verify that selecting a Rate Effective Date of "01/01/2026" captures the date in the picker.
4. Verify that the "Services Included" checklist displays all covered services as read-only checked items.
5. Verify that clicking "Save Configuration" with valid data saves the configuration and displays a success message.

Negative:
6. Verify that leaving the Bundle Price field empty and saving displays a required field validation error.
7. Verify that entering a negative bundle price (e.g., -$100) displays a validation error.
8. Verify that entering non-numeric characters in the Bundle Price field displays a format validation error.
9. Verify that leaving the Rate Effective Date empty displays a required field validation error.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the information banner is prominently displayed at the top explaining that individual services are not tracked under this model.
11. Verify that the "Services Included" checklist items are read-only and cannot be unchecked by the Provider.
12. Verify that the "Save Configuration" and "Cancel" buttons are positioned at the bottom of the form.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that behavior is correct when the Provider enters a bundle price of zero.
14. Verify that behavior is correct when the Provider selects a past effective date and a warning about retroactive application is displayed.

Security:
15. Verify that only Providers with service configuration permissions can configure the Blended Rate model.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the Blended Rate Configuration screen loads with the form and services checklist within 2 seconds.
Verify saving the Blended Rate configuration completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to view service model description and billing implications before selection.
Description of User Story: The Service Model Selection screen includes a detailed preview panel that displays comprehensive descriptions and billing implications for each service model before the Provider commits to a selection. When the Provider hovers over or clicks on the A La Carte card, the preview panel populates with a detailed explanation of individual service tracking, per-service pricing, granular billing reports, and the ability to customize charges per resident. When the Provider hovers over or clicks on the Blended Rate card, the preview panel shows details about the single bundle approach, simplified billing, fixed monthly charges, and the absence of individual service tracking. The preview panel includes a comparison table highlighting key differences between the two models across dimensions such as billing complexity, reporting granularity, pricing flexibility, and resident customization. A "Learn More" link within each preview expands additional FAQs or examples relevant to the selected model.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider hovers over or clicks the A La Carte card, Then the system must display A La Carte description and billing implications in the preview panel.
   Else if the Provider hovers over or clicks the Blended Rate card, Then the system must display Blended Rate description and billing implications in the preview panel.

2. If the Provider clicks "Learn More" in the preview panel, Then the system must expand additional FAQs or examples for the displayed model.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Learn More" again when expanded, Then the system must collapse the additional content.

3. If the Provider selects a model after reviewing descriptions, Then the system must highlight the selected card and enable the "Confirm Selection" button.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Confirm Selection" without selecting a model, Then the system must display a validation message requesting model selection.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that hovering over or clicking the A La Carte card displays the A La Carte description and billing implications in the preview panel.
2. Verify that hovering over or clicking the Blended Rate card displays the Blended Rate description and billing implications in the preview panel.
3. Verify that the comparison table displays differences across billing complexity, reporting granularity, pricing flexibility, and customization.
4. Verify that clicking "Learn More" expands additional FAQs or examples for the selected model.
5. Verify that the Provider can select a model after reviewing the descriptions and save the selection.

Negative:
6. Verify that clicking "Confirm Selection" without selecting any model displays a validation message.
7. Verify that the preview panel does not display content from both models simultaneously when only one card is selected.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the preview panel updates smoothly when switching between A La Carte and Blended Rate cards.
9. Verify that the comparison table is formatted with clear column headers and row labels for easy readability.
10. Verify that the "Learn More" link is visible within the preview panel and toggles the expanded content.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that behavior is correct when the Provider rapidly switches between model cards to ensure the preview panel displays the correct content without flicker.
12. Verify that behavior is correct when the "Learn More" content is empty or not configured for a particular model.

Security:
13. Verify that service model descriptions and billing implications do not expose sensitive facility financial data to unauthorized roles.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the preview panel content loads within 1 second when the Provider selects a model card.
Verify the comparison table renders with all data within 1 second on screen load.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to generate rate card showing pricing for Private room or Semi-private room.
Description of User Story: The Rate Card Generation screen allows the Provider to create a detailed rate card that displays pricing for Private Room and Semi-Private Room options within the facility. The screen presents a room type selector with two options: Private Room and Semi-Private Room, and the Provider can select one or both to include on the rate card. For each selected room type, the system displays a pricing table with columns for service item, rate, frequency (daily, weekly, monthly), and any applicable notes. The Provider can enter or modify the base room rate, additional service charges, and any bundled amenities pricing for each room type. A "Generate Rate Card" button compiles the entered pricing data into a formatted rate card document that can be previewed on screen before finalizing. The generated rate card includes the facility name, effective date, room type headers, itemized pricing, and a total estimated monthly cost for each room type.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider selects "Private Room", Then the system must display the Private Room pricing table with editable rate fields.
   Else if the Provider selects "Semi-Private Room", Then the system must display the Semi-Private Room pricing table with editable rate fields.

2. If the Provider selects both room types, Then the system must display both pricing tables side by side or stacked.

3. If the Provider clicks "Generate Rate Card" with valid pricing data, Then the system must compile and display a formatted rate card preview.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Generate Rate Card" with missing base room rates, Then the system must display a validation error indicating the base rate is required.

4. If the Provider clicks "Cancel", Then the system must discard all entered pricing data and return to the previous screen.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Rate Card Generation screen displays a room type selector with Private Room and Semi-Private Room options.
2. Verify that selecting "Private Room" displays the pricing table with editable rate fields.
3. Verify that selecting "Semi-Private Room" displays the pricing table with editable rate fields.
4. Verify that selecting both room types displays both pricing tables.
5. Verify that entering pricing data and clicking "Generate Rate Card" produces a formatted preview with facility name, effective date, and itemized pricing.
6. Verify that the total estimated monthly cost is calculated correctly for each room type.

Negative:
7. Verify that clicking "Generate Rate Card" without entering a base room rate displays a validation error.
8. Verify that entering a negative rate value (e.g., -$200) displays a validation error.
9. Verify that clicking "Generate Rate Card" without selecting any room type displays a validation error.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the pricing tables display clear column headers for service item, rate, frequency, and notes.
11. Verify that the rate card preview includes the facility name and effective date prominently at the top.
12. Verify that the room type selector allows single or multiple selection with clear visual indication.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that behavior is correct when the Provider enters the same rate for both Private and Semi-Private room types.
14. Verify that behavior is correct when the Provider generates a rate card with only additional service charges and no base room rate filled.

Security:
15. Verify that rate card data and pricing information are accessible only to authorized Providers with pricing configuration permissions.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the Rate Card Generation screen loads with room type selector and empty pricing tables within 2 seconds.
Verify generating and displaying the rate card preview completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to view rate card interface with all applicable charges and services.
Description of User Story: The Rate Card View screen provides a comprehensive read-only interface displaying all applicable charges and services configured for the facility. The screen presents a structured layout with the facility name and rate card effective date at the top, followed by sections organized by room type (Private Room, Semi-Private Room) each listing all associated services and their pricing. Each section contains a detailed table with columns for service name, description, unit rate, billing frequency, and any applicable notes or conditions. A summary row at the bottom of each room type section displays the total estimated monthly cost aggregating all listed charges. The screen includes a toolbar with options to print the rate card, export it as PDF, or share it via email. Filter controls allow the Provider to view charges by specific categories such as room charges only, services only, or all charges combined.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider views the rate card, Then the system must display all room type sections with their associated charges in a read-only format.

2. If the Provider clicks "Print", Then the system must open the browser print dialog with the rate card formatted for printing.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Export as PDF", Then the system must generate and download the rate card as a PDF document.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Share via Email", Then the system must open an email composition dialog with the rate card attached.

3. If the Provider selects the "Room Charges Only" filter, Then the system must display only room-related charges and hide service charges.
   Else if the Provider selects "All Charges", Then the system must display all charges across all categories.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Rate Card View screen displays the facility name and rate card effective date at the top.
2. Verify that the Private Room section lists all associated services with correct service names, descriptions, unit rates, frequencies, and notes.
3. Verify that the Semi-Private Room section lists all associated services with the correct details.
4. Verify that the summary row for each room type displays the correct total estimated monthly cost.
5. Verify that clicking "Export as PDF" generates and downloads the rate card as a PDF.
6. Verify that clicking "Print" opens the print dialog with a properly formatted rate card.
7. Verify that filtering by "Room Charges Only" displays only room-related charges.

Negative:
8. Verify that the rate card view displays a "No rate card configured" message when no rate card data exists.
9. Verify that the export fails gracefully with an error message if the PDF generation service is unavailable.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the rate card is displayed in a clean, structured layout with clear section headers for each room type.
11. Verify that the toolbar with Print, Export as PDF, and Share via Email options is visible at the top of the screen.
12. Verify that the filter controls allow switching between room charges, services, and all charges views.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that behavior is correct when only one room type is configured and the other section is hidden.
14. Verify that the rate card displays correctly when a service has a zero unit rate.

Security:
15. Verify that the rate card view is accessible only to authenticated Providers and does not expose pricing data to unauthorized users.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the Rate Card View screen loads with all sections and data within 3 seconds for a rate card with 30 or more service items.
Verify exporting the rate card as PDF completes within 5 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit rate card
Description of User Story: The Rate Card Edit screen allows the Provider to modify an existing rate card by updating service charges, adding new service items, or removing outdated entries. When the Provider clicks "Edit Rate Card" from the rate card view, the screen transitions to an editable mode where all pricing fields, service descriptions, frequencies, and notes become modifiable. The Provider can update unit rates for existing services, change billing frequencies, add new service rows using an "Add Service" button, or remove service rows using a delete icon on each row. A revision history section at the bottom shows previous rate card versions with their effective dates, allowing the Provider to track changes over time. The "Save Changes" button validates all modified fields and saves the updated rate card with a new effective date. A "Preview Changes" button allows the Provider to compare the current and modified rate cards side by side before saving.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider clicks "Edit Rate Card", Then the system must transition the rate card view to editable mode with all fields enabled.

2. If the Provider modifies a unit rate, Then the system must recalculate the total estimated monthly cost for the affected room type section.

3. If the Provider clicks "Add Service", Then the system must add a new editable row with blank fields for service name, rate, frequency, and notes.
   Else if the Provider clicks the delete icon on a service row, Then the system must remove the row and recalculate the total cost.

4. If the Provider clicks "Save Changes" with valid data, Then the system must save the updated rate card with a new effective date and display a success message.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Cancel", Then the system must discard all modifications and revert to the read-only rate card view.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking "Edit Rate Card" transitions the view to editable mode with all pricing fields modifiable.
2. Verify that modifying a unit rate recalculates the total estimated monthly cost correctly.
3. Verify that clicking "Add Service" adds a new editable row to the services table.
4. Verify that clicking the delete icon removes the service row and recalculates the total cost.
5. Verify that clicking "Save Changes" saves the updated rate card with a new effective date and displays a success message.
6. Verify that clicking "Preview Changes" displays a side-by-side comparison of current and modified rate cards.
7. Verify that the revision history section shows previous rate card versions with effective dates.

Negative:
8. Verify that saving with an empty unit rate for any active service displays a validation error.
9. Verify that entering a negative unit rate (e.g., -$50) displays a validation error.
10. Verify that deleting all service rows and attempting to save displays a validation error requiring at least one service.

UI Scenarios:
11. Verify that the edit mode clearly distinguishes editable fields from read-only labels.
12. Verify that the "Add Service" button and delete icons are visible and accessible on each row.
13. Verify that the "Save Changes", "Preview Changes", and "Cancel" buttons are positioned at the bottom of the form.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify that behavior is correct when the Provider edits a rate card that is currently in use for active billing cycles.
15. Verify that behavior is correct when two Providers attempt to edit the same rate card simultaneously.

Security:
16. Verify that only Providers with rate card edit permissions can access the edit mode and unauthorized users see the read-only view.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the rate card edit screen loads with all editable fields within 2 seconds.
Verify saving the updated rate card and generating the revision history entry completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to apply discount to rate card if applicable.
Description of User Story: The Rate Card Discount screen allows the Provider to apply discounts to specific services or the overall rate card total for a resident or group of residents. The screen displays the current rate card with all services and their standard pricing, along with a "Apply Discount" section that provides options for percentage-based discount or fixed dollar amount discount. The Provider can select the discount type, enter the discount value, choose whether the discount applies to a specific service line or the entire rate card total, and optionally set a discount start date and end date for time-limited promotions. A discount preview panel shows the original price, discount amount, and final discounted price in real time as the Provider configures the discount. The "Apply Discount" button saves the discount configuration, and the discounted rate card is updated to reflect the new pricing with a visual indicator showing the discount applied on each affected line item.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider selects "Percentage" discount type, Then the system must enable the percentage input field and calculate the discount based on the percentage of the selected price.
   Else if the Provider selects "Fixed Amount" discount type, Then the system must enable the dollar amount input field and subtract the fixed amount from the selected price.

2. If the Provider applies the discount to a specific service line, Then the system must update only that service line pricing in the discount preview.
   Else if the Provider applies the discount to the entire rate card total, Then the system must update the overall total in the discount preview.

3. If the Provider sets both a start date and end date for the discount, Then the system must apply the discount only within the specified date range.

4. If the discount percentage exceeds 100% or the fixed amount exceeds the original price, Then the system must display a validation error indicating the discount cannot exceed the original amount.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Rate Card Discount screen displays the current rate card with standard pricing and the "Apply Discount" section.
2. Verify that selecting "Percentage" discount type and entering 10% displays the correct calculated discount (e.g., $350 off a $3,500 rate) in the preview panel.
3. Verify that selecting "Fixed Amount" discount type and entering $200 displays the correct discount in the preview panel.
4. Verify that applying a discount to a specific service line (e.g., Transport) updates only that line in the rate card.
5. Verify that applying a discount to the entire rate card total updates the overall pricing.
6. Verify that clicking "Apply Discount" saves the configuration and updates the rate card with discount indicators.
7. Verify that setting a discount end date causes automatic discount removal when the date is reached.

Negative:
8. Verify that entering a discount percentage greater than 100% displays a validation error.
9. Verify that entering a fixed discount amount greater than the service price displays a validation error.
10. Verify that entering a negative discount value (e.g., -5%) displays a validation error.
11. Verify that setting an end date before the start date displays a date range validation error.

UI Scenarios:
12. Verify that the discount preview panel updates in real time as the Provider enters discount values.
13. Verify that discounted line items display a visual indicator such as a strikethrough on the original price with the discounted price beside it.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify that behavior is correct when the Provider applies multiple discounts to the same service line.
15. Verify that behavior is correct when the Provider applies a 100% discount to a service line resulting in a zero price.

Security:
16. Verify that only Providers with discount management permissions can apply or modify rate card discounts.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the Rate Card Discount screen loads with current pricing and discount options within 2 seconds.
Verify applying and saving the discount configuration updates the rate card within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to send rate card and welcome email together to lead/family.
Description of User Story: This feature allows the Provider to send both the rate card and a welcome email simultaneously to the lead or family member. The system bundles the rate card document and a welcome message into a single outbound communication. The Provider selects the lead or family contact, attaches the appropriate rate card, and triggers the combined send action. The system validates that both components are present before dispatching. Upon successful delivery, the system logs the communication and updates the lead interaction history. This streamlines the onboarding communication process and ensures families receive pricing information alongside an introductory welcome.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens the lead/family detail page, Then the system must display the "Send Rate Card & Welcome Email" option when a rate card exists.

2. If the Provider selects a valid lead/family contact and a rate card is attached and clicks Send, Then the system must send the combined rate card and welcome email successfully and log the action.
   Else if no rate card is available for the service model, Then the system must prevent sending and display an appropriate error message.
   Else if the recipient email address is missing or invalid, Then the system must show a validation error and block the send action.

3. If the email is sent successfully, Then the system must update the lead interaction history with the sent date, recipient, and attachments.
   Else if email delivery fails due to a server error, Then the system must retry delivery and notify the Provider of the failure.

4. If the Provider cancels the send action before confirmation, Then no email must be sent and no log entry must be created.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Provider can send the rate card and welcome email together to a valid lead contact (e.g., lead "John Smith") and both are delivered successfully.
2. Verify that the system logs the sent email with correct date, recipient name, and attachment details in the interaction history.
3. Verify that the Provider can select from multiple available rate cards (e.g., "Standard Plan", "Premium Plan") before sending.
4. Verify that the welcome email content includes the correct facility branding and the lead's name.

Negative:
5. Verify that the system prevents sending when no rate card is available and displays an error message.
6. Verify that the system shows a validation error when the recipient email address is missing.
7. Verify that the system shows a validation error when the recipient email address format is invalid (e.g., "john@").
8. Verify that the system handles email server failure gracefully and notifies the Provider.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the "Send Rate Card & Welcome Email" button is visible and enabled when prerequisites are met.
10. Verify that a confirmation dialog appears before the email is sent.
11. Verify that a success toast notification is displayed after the email is sent.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that sending when the lead has multiple email addresses prompts the Provider to select one.
13. Verify that the system handles concurrent send attempts for the same lead without duplicate emails.
14. Verify that the system handles the scenario when the rate card attachment exceeds the maximum allowed file size.

Security:
15. Verify that only authenticated and authorized Providers can trigger the send action.
16. Verify that the rate card and welcome email content are transmitted over a secure channel (TLS/HTTPS).
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the combined rate card and welcome email is dispatched within 5 seconds of the Provider clicking send.
Verify the system can handle 50 concurrent email send requests without degradation or delivery failures.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to collect deposit through multiple payment methods.
Description of User Story: This feature enables the Provider to collect a security deposit from the responsible party using multiple payment methods. The system supports payment methods such as credit card, debit card, check, ACH transfer, and cash. The Provider selects the resident or lead, chooses the payment method, and enters the payment details to process the deposit. The system validates payment information before processing and generates a receipt upon successful collection. All payment transactions are recorded against the resident account for audit and reconciliation. This flexibility ensures families can use their preferred payment method for deposit collection.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens the deposit collection screen, Then the system must display all supported payment methods (credit card, debit card, check, ACH transfer, cash).

2. If the Provider selects a valid payment method and enters correct payment details and submits, Then the system must process the deposit and generate a receipt.
   Else if the payment is declined by the payment gateway, Then the system must display a declined message and allow the Provider to retry or choose a different method.
   Else if required payment fields are left blank, Then the system must highlight the missing fields and prevent submission.

3. If the Provider selects cash as the payment method, Then the system must record the deposit manually without gateway processing.

4. If the payment method is ACH transfer, Then the system must validate the routing and account numbers before processing.
   Else if the routing or account number is invalid, Then the system must show a field-level validation error and block submission.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Provider can collect a deposit via credit card (e.g., Visa ending in 4242) with valid card details.
2. Verify that the Provider can collect a deposit via debit card and the transaction is recorded.
3. Verify that the Provider can collect a deposit via check (e.g., check #10045) and the system records it as pending clearance.
4. Verify that the Provider can collect a deposit via ACH transfer with valid routing number (e.g., 021000021) and account number.
5. Verify that the Provider can collect a deposit via cash and the payment is recorded manually.
6. Verify that a receipt is generated upon successful deposit collection regardless of payment method.

Negative:
7. Verify that the system shows an error when credit card payment is declined.
8. Verify that the system prevents submission when required payment fields are empty.
9. Verify that the system rejects an invalid credit card number format (e.g., "1234").
10. Verify that the system rejects an expired credit card.
11. Verify that the system rejects invalid ACH routing or account numbers.

UI Scenarios:
12. Verify that all supported payment methods are displayed in a dropdown or selection list.
13. Verify that the form dynamically changes input fields based on selected payment method.
14. Verify that a success message and receipt preview appear after deposit collection.

Edge Cases:
15. Verify that the system handles gracefully when the payment gateway times out during processing.
16. Verify that the system prevents duplicate deposit collection for the same resident.
17. Verify that partial payment handling blocks submission when the deposit amount entered is less than the required amount.

Security:
18. Verify that credit card details are not stored in plain text and comply with PCI-DSS standards.
19. Verify that all payment transactions are logged with an audit trail including user, timestamp, and payment method.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify credit card payment processing completes within 10 seconds including gateway response.
Verify the deposit collection page loads all payment method options within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to record receipt of a security deposit, automatically update the resident status to "DEPOSIT RECEIVED", and send the deposit receipt to the responsible party.
Description of User Story: This feature allows the Provider to record the receipt of a security deposit and trigger automated downstream actions. When the Provider confirms deposit receipt, the system automatically updates the resident status to "DEPOSIT RECEIVED" in the resident record. Simultaneously, the system generates a deposit receipt document and sends it to the responsible party via email. The Provider enters deposit details including amount, date received, payment method, and reference number. The system validates all required fields before processing and ensures the status transition follows the correct workflow sequence. This automation reduces manual steps and ensures timely communication with the family.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens the resident deposit screen, Then the system must display the deposit recording form with all required fields.

2. If the Provider records the deposit with all required fields completed and submits, Then the system must update the resident status to "DEPOSIT RECEIVED" and send the receipt email to the responsible party.
   Else if required fields such as amount or date are missing, Then the system must highlight the missing fields and prevent saving.
   Else if the resident is already in "DEPOSIT RECEIVED" status, Then the system must prevent duplicate deposit recording and notify the Provider.

3. If the responsible party email is not on file, Then the system must record the deposit and update the status but must display a warning about the unsent email.

4. If the email delivery fails, Then the system must log the failure and allow the Provider to resend the receipt manually.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Provider can record deposit receipt and the resident status automatically updates to "DEPOSIT RECEIVED".
2. Verify that the system sends a deposit receipt email to the responsible party upon successful recording.
3. Verify that deposit details including amount (e.g., $1,500), date, method, and reference number are saved correctly.
4. Verify that the deposit receipt email contains the correct deposit amount, date, and resident name.
5. Verify that the resident status change to "DEPOSIT RECEIVED" is reflected immediately in the resident list and detail views.

Negative:
6. Verify that the system prevents saving when required deposit fields are left empty.
7. Verify that the system blocks deposit recording when the resident is not in the eligible status.
8. Verify that the system prevents duplicate deposit recording for the same resident.
9. Verify that the system handles email delivery failure and displays a warning to the Provider.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the deposit recording form displays all required fields with proper labels.
11. Verify that the resident status badge updates to "DEPOSIT RECEIVED" after recording.
12. Verify that a success notification is shown after deposit is recorded and email is sent.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that the system prompts the Provider to select one when the responsible party has multiple email addresses on file.
14. Verify that deposit recording with a zero or negative amount is rejected.
15. Verify that the system handles loss of connectivity during deposit recording gracefully.

Security:
16. Verify that only authorized Providers can record deposit receipts.
17. Verify that deposit receipt emails are sent over secure channels and do not expose sensitive financial details beyond what is necessary.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the resident status update and receipt email are triggered within 5 seconds of deposit recording.
Verify the deposit recording form loads within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to schedule RN assessment
Description of User Story: This feature enables the Provider to schedule a Registered Nurse (RN) assessment for a prospective resident as part of the pre-admission process. The Provider selects the resident or lead, chooses an available RN, and sets the assessment date, time, and location. The system validates that the selected RN is available at the chosen time slot and prevents double-booking. Upon successful scheduling, the system sends notifications to both the RN and the family or responsible party. The scheduled assessment appears on the facility calendar and the resident timeline for tracking purposes.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens the pre-admission assessment screen, Then the system must display the scheduling form with available RNs, date picker, time slots, and location fields.

2. If the Provider selects a valid RN, date, time, and location and confirms, Then the system must schedule the assessment and send notifications to the RN and responsible party.
   Else if the selected time slot conflicts with the RN's existing schedule, Then the system must block scheduling and show available alternative times.
   Else if the Provider does not select a required field such as date or RN, Then the system must highlight the missing field and prevent submission.

3. If the scheduled date is in the past, Then the system must reject the scheduling attempt with an error message.

4. If the notification to the RN or family fails to send, Then the system must log the failure and alert the Provider to resend.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Provider can schedule an RN assessment by selecting an RN (e.g., "RN Sarah Lee"), date, time (e.g., 10:00 AM), and location.
2. Verify that the system sends a notification to the assigned RN upon scheduling.
3. Verify that the system sends a notification to the responsible party upon scheduling.
4. Verify that the scheduled assessment appears on the facility calendar on the correct date.
5. Verify that the scheduled assessment appears on the resident timeline.

Negative:
6. Verify that the system prevents scheduling when the selected RN has a time conflict.
7. Verify that the system rejects scheduling with a past date (e.g., yesterday's date).
8. Verify that the system prevents submission when required fields such as RN or date are missing.
9. Verify that the system handles notification delivery failure gracefully.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the scheduling form displays available RNs in a dropdown.
11. Verify that the date and time picker restricts selection to future dates and valid time slots.
12. Verify that a confirmation dialog appears before finalizing the schedule.
13. Verify that a success message is displayed after scheduling is confirmed.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify that scheduling behavior is handled correctly when multiple Providers attempt to book the same RN at the same time.
15. Verify that the system shows an appropriate message when the RN list is empty or no RNs are available.
16. Verify that rescheduling an existing assessment updates the calendar and sends updated notifications.

Security:
17. Verify that only authenticated and authorized Providers can schedule RN assessments.
18. Verify that resident and RN personal information is not exposed in notification URLs or logs.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the scheduling form with available RNs and time slots loads within 3 seconds.
Verify notifications are dispatched within 5 seconds of scheduling confirmation.

User Story: As Provider, I should be able to add assessment details in the Assessment form.
Description of User Story: This feature allows the Provider to fill in the pre-admission assessment form with detailed clinical and personal information about the prospective resident. The assessment form includes sections for medical history, current medications, mobility status, cognitive assessment, dietary needs, and care requirements. The Provider enters data into the structured form fields and can save progress as a draft before final submission. The system validates mandatory fields and ensures data integrity across all sections. Upon completion, the assessment form is saved to the resident record for review by clinical staff. This ensures comprehensive data collection for informed admission decisions.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens the assessment form, Then the system must display all sections including medical history, medications, mobility, cognitive assessment, dietary needs, and care requirements.

2. If the Provider completes all mandatory fields and submits, Then the system must save the assessment and mark it as complete.
   Else if mandatory fields are left empty upon submission, Then the system must highlight the missing fields and block the submit action.
   Else if the Provider enters data in an invalid format (e.g., text in a numeric field), Then the system must show a field-level validation error.

3. If the Provider saves the form as a draft, Then the system must retain all entered data for future editing.

4. If the Provider navigates away without saving, Then the system must prompt a confirmation dialog to prevent data loss.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Provider can open the assessment form and enter details in all sections.
2. Verify that the Provider can save the assessment form as a draft and resume later with data intact.
3. Verify that the Provider can submit the completed assessment form successfully.
4. Verify that the saved assessment details are visible in the resident record.
5. Verify that all form sections including medical history, medications, mobility, cognitive, dietary, and care requirements are present.

Negative:
6. Verify that the system prevents submission when mandatory fields are empty.
7. Verify that the system shows validation errors for invalid data formats (e.g., entering "abc" in an age field).
8. Verify that the system prevents submission with only partial mandatory fields completed.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the assessment form displays all sections with proper labels and input types.
10. Verify that mandatory fields are marked with visual indicators (e.g., asterisks).
11. Verify that a confirmation dialog appears when navigating away from an unsaved form.
12. Verify that the draft status indicator is visible when the form is saved as a draft.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that the system handles the scenario when the Provider opens the same assessment form in multiple browser tabs.
14. Verify that the system handles very long text entries in free-text fields without truncation.
15. Verify that saving the form when the network connection is intermittent is handled gracefully.

Security:
16. Verify that only authorized Providers can access and edit the assessment form.
17. Verify that sensitive medical data in the assessment form is encrypted in transit and at rest.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the assessment form loads with all sections within 3 seconds.
Verify saving the form as a draft completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit pre-admission assessment.
Description of User Story: This feature allows the Provider to edit a previously saved or submitted pre-admission assessment. The Provider navigates to the resident record, opens the existing assessment, and modifies any section that needs updating. The system loads the previously saved data and allows changes to all editable fields. The system tracks changes with an audit trail capturing who made the edit and when. Upon saving the edits, the system re-validates all mandatory fields and updates the assessment record. This ensures assessment data remains accurate and current as new information becomes available before admission.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens an existing assessment for editing, Then the system must pre-populate all fields with the previously saved data.

2. If the Provider modifies assessment fields and saves, Then the system must update the record and log the changes in the audit trail with user identity and timestamp.
   Else if the Provider clears a mandatory field during editing, Then the system must prevent saving and show a validation error.

3. If the assessment is in draft status, Then the Provider must be able to edit freely and save as draft or submit.
   Else if the assessment has already been submitted, Then edits must be allowed but the system must record the modification with audit details.

4. If the Provider cancels edits without saving, Then the system must revert to the last saved version.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Provider can open an existing assessment and edit any field.
2. Verify that edited assessment data is saved correctly to the resident record.
3. Verify that the audit trail records the editor name, timestamp, and changed fields.
4. Verify that the Provider can edit a draft assessment and submit it.
5. Verify that the Provider can edit a previously submitted assessment.

Negative:
6. Verify that the system prevents saving when a mandatory field is cleared during editing.
7. Verify that the system rejects invalid data formats entered during editing (e.g., letters in a numeric field).
8. Verify that the system does not save changes if the Provider cancels the edit.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the edit button is visible and enabled on the assessment detail view.
10. Verify that the form pre-populates with existing assessment data when opened for editing.
11. Verify that a save confirmation message appears after successful edit.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that concurrent edits by two Providers on the same assessment are handled with conflict resolution.
13. Verify that editing an assessment does not affect linked notifications or scheduled RN assignments.
14. Verify that the system preserves the original submission date alongside edit dates.

Security:
15. Verify that only authorized Providers can edit assessments.
16. Verify that the audit trail for assessment edits cannot be tampered with or deleted.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the assessment form loads existing data for editing within 3 seconds.
Verify saving edits to the assessment completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to send assessment to RN  for electronic signature
Description of User Story: This feature allows the Provider to send a completed pre-admission assessment to the assigned Registered Nurse for electronic signature. The Provider reviews the completed assessment and triggers the send for signature action. The system generates a signing request and delivers it to the RN via email or in-app notification with a secure signing link. The RN can review the assessment document and apply their electronic signature directly. The system validates the signature and updates the assessment status to "SIGNED" upon completion. This ensures the clinical assessment is formally acknowledged by the RN before proceeding with the admission decision.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens the completed assessment, Then the system must display the "Send for Signature" button.

2. If the assessment is complete and the Provider clicks "Send for Signature", Then the system must deliver a signing request to the assigned RN via email or in-app notification.
   Else if the assessment is incomplete, Then the system must prevent sending for signature and display a completion error.

3. If the RN applies a valid electronic signature, Then the system must update the assessment status to "SIGNED" and record the signature timestamp.
   Else if the signing request delivery fails, Then the system must notify the Provider and allow resending.
   Else if the RN rejects or declines to sign, Then the system must update the status accordingly and notify the Provider.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Provider can send a completed assessment to the assigned RN (e.g., "RN Sarah Lee") for electronic signature.
2. Verify that the RN receives a signing request via email or in-app notification.
3. Verify that the RN can review the full assessment before signing.
4. Verify that the assessment status updates to "SIGNED" after the RN signs.
5. Verify that the system records the signature timestamp and RN identity.

Negative:
6. Verify that the system prevents sending an incomplete assessment for signature.
7. Verify that the system handles signing request delivery failure and allows resending.
8. Verify that the system handles the scenario where the RN declines to sign.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the "Send for Signature" button is visible only when the assessment is complete.
10. Verify that the assessment status badge updates to "PENDING SIGNATURE" after sending.
11. Verify that the RN signing interface displays the assessment in a readable format.
12. Verify that a confirmation is shown to the Provider after the signing request is sent.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that the system handles sending the assessment for signature to an RN who is no longer active.
14. Verify that the signing link expires after a configurable time period.
15. Verify that the system prevents the same assessment from being sent for signature multiple times simultaneously.

Security:
16. Verify that the electronic signature complies with applicable e-signature regulations.
17. Verify that the signing link is unique, secure, and cannot be reused or shared.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the signing request is delivered to the RN within 10 seconds of the Provider sending it.
Verify the assessment status update to "SIGNED" occurs within 3 seconds of signature submission.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to view completed assessment to determine if facility can manage care level.
Description of User Story: This feature allows the Provider to view a fully completed and signed pre-admission assessment to evaluate whether the facility can adequately manage the prospective resident's care needs. The system presents the assessment in a read-only format organized by sections such as medical history, mobility, cognitive status, and care requirements. The Provider reviews the care level indicators and compares them against the facility's capabilities. The view includes summary scores or flags that highlight areas requiring special attention. The Provider uses this information to make an informed admission or decline decision. This ensures patient safety by matching care needs to facility capacity.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the assessment is completed and signed, Then the Provider must be able to view the full assessment in read-only format with all sections visible.
   Else if the assessment is not yet signed by the RN, Then the system must show a pending status and limit decision actions.

2. If care requirements exceed facility capabilities, Then the system must flag those sections for Provider attention with a visual indicator.

3. If the Provider determines the facility can manage the care level, Then the Provider must be able to proceed to approve the admission.
   Else if the Provider identifies care concerns, Then the Provider must be able to add notes and proceed to decline or request additional evaluation.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Provider can view a completed and signed assessment in read-only format.
2. Verify that all assessment sections are displayed including medical history, mobility, cognitive, dietary, and care requirements.
3. Verify that the RN signature and signature date are visible on the assessment.
4. Verify that care level indicators and summary flags are displayed correctly.
5. Verify that the Provider can navigate from the assessment view to the admission decision action.

Negative:
6. Verify that the system indicates pending signature status if the assessment is not signed.
7. Verify that the system prevents admission decision actions on an unsigned assessment.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the assessment view is organized with clear section headers and a readable layout.
9. Verify that flagged care areas are visually distinct (e.g., highlighted in yellow or red).
10. Verify that the view supports scrolling or navigation between assessment sections.
11. Verify that a print option is available for the completed assessment.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that viewing an assessment with missing optional fields displays them as empty without errors.
13. Verify that the system handles viewing assessments with very large data entries gracefully.

Security:
14. Verify that only authorized Providers can view completed assessments.
15. Verify that the read-only view prevents any modification of assessment data.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the completed assessment loads within 3 seconds including all sections and care level indicators.
Verify the assessment view renders correctly for assessments with extensive medical history data.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to approve / reject admission
Description of User Story: This feature allows the Provider to make a final admission decision by approving or rejecting a prospective resident based on the completed pre-admission assessment. The Provider reviews the assessment and selects either "Approve" or "Reject" as the admission decision. When approving, the system updates the resident status to reflect admission approval and triggers downstream admission processing workflows. When rejecting, the system requires the Provider to enter a reason for the decline. The system records the decision with the Provider's identity, timestamp, and any associated notes. Notifications are sent to relevant stakeholders based on the decision outcome.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens the admission decision screen, Then the system must display both "Approve" and "Reject" action buttons.

2. If the Provider clicks "Approve" on a completed and signed assessment, Then the system must update the resident status to approved and initiate admission processing workflows.
   Else if the Provider attempts to decide without a completed and signed assessment, Then the system must block the decision and show an error.

3. If the Provider clicks "Reject", Then the system must require a rejection reason before accepting the submission.
