   Else if the Provider rejects but does not enter a reason, Then the system must prevent the rejection from being submitted.

4. If the Provider changes their decision after initial submission, Then the system must log the change with a full audit trail.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Provider can approve admission after reviewing the completed assessment.
2. Verify that the resident status updates to "Approved" upon admission approval.
3. Verify that the Provider can reject admission with a mandatory reason entered (e.g., "Care needs exceed facility capacity").
4. Verify that the system sends notifications to the responsible party and relevant staff upon approval.
5. Verify that the system sends a notification to relevant parties upon rejection.
6. Verify that the decision is logged with Provider identity, timestamp, and notes.

Negative:
7. Verify that the system blocks admission decision when the assessment is incomplete or unsigned.
8. Verify that the system prevents rejection submission without a reason.
9. Verify that the system prevents duplicate approve/reject actions on the same resident.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that "Approve" and "Reject" buttons are clearly visible on the assessment review screen.
11. Verify that a rejection reason text area appears when "Reject" is selected.
12. Verify that a confirmation dialog appears before finalizing the decision.
13. Verify that the resident status badge updates immediately after the decision.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify that the system handles two Providers attempting to make a decision on the same resident simultaneously.
15. Verify that the system allows revising a rejection to an approval if the assessment is updated.

Security:
16. Verify that only authorized Providers with decision-making privileges can approve or reject admissions.
17. Verify that the audit trail for admission decisions is immutable.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the admission decision processing and status update complete within 3 seconds.
Verify notifications are dispatched within 5 seconds of the decision.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to notify family/responsible party of decline decision
Description of User Story: This feature enables the Provider to send a formal notification to the family or responsible party when an admission has been declined. After the rejection decision is recorded, the Provider triggers the notification which includes the decline reason and any relevant next steps or recommendations. The system uses the responsible party contact information on file to deliver the notification via email. The Provider can review and optionally customize the notification content before sending. The system logs the notification in the resident communication history. This ensures transparent and professional communication of the admission outcome to families.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens the declined resident record, Then the system must display the "Notify Decline" button.

2. If the admission is declined and the Provider triggers the notification, Then the system must send the decline notification to the responsible party with the decline reason.
   Else if the admission status is not "Declined", Then the system must prevent sending a decline notification.
   Else if the responsible party email is missing, Then the system must alert the Provider and prevent email delivery.

3. If the Provider customizes the notification content before sending, Then the system must use the customized message instead of the default template.

4. If the notification delivery fails, Then the system must log the failure and allow the Provider to retry.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Provider can send a decline notification to the responsible party after admission rejection.
2. Verify that the notification includes the decline reason (e.g., "Care needs exceed facility capacity").
3. Verify that the Provider can customize the notification content before sending.
4. Verify that the sent notification is logged in the resident communication history.
5. Verify that the default notification template includes appropriate language and next steps.

Negative:
6. Verify that the system warns when the responsible party email is not on file.
7. Verify that the system prevents sending a decline notification for an approved admission.
8. Verify that the system handles email delivery failure gracefully.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the "Notify Decline" button appears only for declined admissions.
10. Verify that a notification preview screen is shown before sending.
11. Verify that a success message is displayed after the notification is sent.
12. Verify that the notification editor allows text formatting.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that the system handles the scenario when the Provider sends the decline notification multiple times.
14. Verify that the notification content handles special characters in the decline reason correctly.

Security:
15. Verify that only authorized Providers can send decline notifications.
16. Verify that the notification does not expose internal assessment details beyond the decline reason.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the decline notification is delivered within 10 seconds of the Provider sending it.
Verify the notification preview loads within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to release room hold and make room available again upon decline.
Description of User Story: This feature allows the Provider to release a room that was previously held for a prospective resident whose admission has been declined. When the admission is declined, the Provider triggers the room release action which changes the room status from "HOLD" back to "AVAILABLE". The system updates the room inventory and census data to reflect the newly available room. The release action is only available for rooms associated with declined admissions to prevent accidental release. The system logs the room status change with the Provider identity and timestamp. This ensures room availability is accurately maintained and rooms are not unnecessarily blocked after a decline.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens a declined admission record with a room on hold, Then the system must display the "Release Room Hold" button.

2. If the admission is declined and the Provider confirms the release, Then the system must change the room status from "HOLD" to "AVAILABLE" and update the room inventory and census report.
   Else if the admission is not in declined status, Then the system must prevent room hold release.
   Else if the room is already in "AVAILABLE" status, Then the system must notify the Provider that no hold exists to release.

3. If the release action fails due to a system error, Then the room must remain on hold and the Provider must be notified to retry.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Provider can release the room hold for a declined admission (e.g., room "101-A").
2. Verify that the room status changes from "HOLD" to "AVAILABLE" after release.
3. Verify that the room inventory is updated to reflect the available room.
4. Verify that the census report reflects the updated available beds count after release.
5. Verify that the room status change is logged with Provider identity and timestamp.

Negative:
6. Verify that the system prevents room release when the admission is not declined.
7. Verify that the system shows a message when the room is already available.
8. Verify that the system handles release failure due to a system error gracefully.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the "Release Room Hold" button is visible only for declined admissions with held rooms.
10. Verify that a confirmation dialog appears before releasing the room hold.
11. Verify that the room status is updated in the room management view immediately after release.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that the system handles two Providers attempting to release the same room hold simultaneously.
13. Verify that the system handles releasing a hold for a room that was reassigned to another resident.
14. Verify that releasing the room hold does not affect other rooms or residents.

Security:
15. Verify that only authorized Providers can release room holds.
16. Verify that the audit log for room status changes is tamper-proof.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the room status change from "HOLD" to "AVAILABLE" completes within 2 seconds.
Verify the census report updates reflect the room release within 5 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to assign specific room/suite to approved resident after resident admission approves.
Description of User Story: This feature enables the Provider to assign a specific room or suite to a resident whose admission has been approved. The Provider navigates to the approved resident record and selects from the list of available rooms or suites. The system displays room details including room number, type, floor, capacity, and current availability status. Upon selection and confirmation, the system associates the room with the resident and updates the room status to indicate assignment. The system validates that the selected room is available and not already assigned to another resident. This ensures approved residents are placed in appropriate accommodations as part of the admission workflow.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens the approved resident record, Then the system must display the available rooms list with details including room number, type, floor, and status.

2. If the resident is approved and the Provider selects an available room and confirms, Then the system must assign the room and update the room status.
   Else if the selected room is not available, Then the system must prevent assignment and display an error.
   Else if the resident is not in approved status, Then the system must block room assignment.

3. If the Provider changes the room assignment before move-in, Then the system must release the previous room and assign the new one.

4. If no rooms are available, Then the system must notify the Provider that no rooms are currently available for assignment.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Provider can assign an available room (e.g., room "201-B") to an approved resident.
2. Verify that the room status updates to "Assigned" after assignment.
3. Verify that the room details (number, type, floor) are displayed correctly in the selection list.
4. Verify that the assigned room appears on the resident record.
5. Verify that the Provider can change the room assignment before move-in.

Negative:
6. Verify that the system prevents assigning an already-occupied room.
7. Verify that the system prevents room assignment for non-approved residents.
8. Verify that the system handles the scenario when no rooms are available.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the available rooms list is sortable and filterable by type, floor, and status.
10. Verify that a confirmation dialog appears before finalizing room assignment.
11. Verify that the room assignment is reflected immediately in the room management view.
12. Verify that a success message appears after room assignment.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that concurrent room assignment attempts for the same room by different Providers are handled correctly.
14. Verify that assigning a wheelchair-accessible room to a resident with mobility needs is supported.
15. Verify that room reassignment does not result in data loss.

Security:
16. Verify that only authorized Providers can assign rooms to residents.
17. Verify that room assignment actions are logged in the audit trail.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the available rooms list loads within 3 seconds.
Verify room assignment processing completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to change room status from HOLD to OCCUPIED.
Description of User Story: This feature allows the Provider to change a room status from "HOLD" to "OCCUPIED" when a resident moves into the assigned room. The Provider navigates to the room management screen or the resident record and triggers the status change action. The system validates that the room is currently in "HOLD" status and that a resident is assigned to it before allowing the transition. Upon confirmation, the room status updates to "OCCUPIED" and the census data is adjusted accordingly. The system records the status change with the Provider identity, timestamp, and associated resident. This status transition is a critical step in the admission workflow to reflect actual room occupancy.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens the room management screen, Then the system must display the current status of each room and available transitions.

2. If the room is in "HOLD" status and a resident is assigned, Then the Provider must be able to change the status to "OCCUPIED" and the census data must be updated.
   Else if the room is not in "HOLD" status, Then the system must prevent the status change and show an error.
   Else if no resident is assigned to the room, Then the system must block the "OCCUPIED" status change.

3. If the status change is confirmed, Then the system must log the change with Provider identity, timestamp, and resident association.

4. If a system error occurs during the status change, Then the room must remain in "HOLD" and the Provider must be notified.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Provider can change room status from "HOLD" to "OCCUPIED" for room "101-A" with an assigned resident.
2. Verify that the census report updates the occupied beds count after the status change.
3. Verify that the status change is logged with Provider identity and timestamp.
4. Verify that the resident record reflects the occupied room.

Negative:
5. Verify that the system blocks the status change when the room is not in "HOLD" status.
6. Verify that the system prevents marking a room as "OCCUPIED" without an assigned resident.
7. Verify that the system handles a system error during status change gracefully.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the room status is visually updated in the room management view after the change.
9. Verify that a confirmation dialog appears before changing the status.
10. Verify that the room status dropdown or action button shows only valid transitions.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that concurrent status change attempts on the same room by different Providers are handled correctly.
12. Verify that changing status back from "OCCUPIED" to "HOLD" is controlled by appropriate business rules.
13. Verify that the status change for rooms with multiple beds tracks individual bed occupancy correctly.

Security:
14. Verify that only authorized Providers can change room status.
15. Verify that the room status change audit log is immutable.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the room status change from "HOLD" to "OCCUPIED" completes within 2 seconds.
Verify the census data update reflects the change within 5 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add move-in date for resident.
Description of User Story: This feature allows the Provider to set the move-in date for an approved resident as part of the admission processing workflow. The Provider navigates to the resident record and enters the planned move-in date using a date picker. The system validates that the date is in the future or today and that the resident has an approved admission and an assigned room. The move-in date is recorded on the resident profile and used for scheduling admission-related tasks and notifications. The system allows updating the move-in date before the actual move-in occurs. This ensures proper planning and coordination for the resident's arrival at the facility.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens the approved resident record, Then the system must display the move-in date field with a date picker.

2. If the resident has an approved admission and assigned room and the Provider enters a valid date (today or future), Then the system must save the move-in date.
   Else if the date entered is in the past, Then the system must reject it with a validation error.
   Else if the resident does not have an approved admission, Then the system must prevent adding a move-in date.

3. If the resident does not have an assigned room, Then the system must warn the Provider to assign a room first.

4. If the Provider updates an existing move-in date, Then the system must save the new date, log the change, and adjust any linked schedules.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Provider can add a move-in date (e.g., 2026-05-01) for an approved resident with an assigned room.
2. Verify that the move-in date is saved on the resident record.
3. Verify that the Provider can update an existing move-in date to a new valid date.
4. Verify that the system accepts today's date as a valid move-in date.

Negative:
5. Verify that the system rejects a past date (e.g., yesterday) for move-in.
6. Verify that the system blocks adding a move-in date for a non-approved resident.
7. Verify that the system warns when adding a move-in date without a room assignment.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the move-in date field uses a date picker with appropriate restrictions.
9. Verify that the date picker restricts past dates from selection.
10. Verify that a save confirmation is shown after the move-in date is set.
11. Verify that the move-in date is displayed on the resident profile.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that setting a move-in date far in the future (e.g., more than 1 year ahead) is accepted.
13. Verify that updating the move-in date multiple times logs each change in the audit trail.
14. Verify that the system handles date format differences across locales correctly.

Security:
15. Verify that only authorized Providers can add or modify the move-in date.
16. Verify that move-in date changes are recorded in the audit trail.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify saving the move-in date completes within 2 seconds.
Verify the date picker loads available dates within 1 second.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to update resident status to ACTIVE upon admission approval.
Description of User Story: This feature allows the Provider to update the resident status to "ACTIVE" once all admission processing steps are complete and the resident has been officially admitted. The Provider triggers the status update from the resident record after verifying that the room is assigned, the move-in date is set, and all required documentation is in order. The system validates all prerequisites before allowing the status change to "ACTIVE". Upon activation, the resident becomes visible in the active resident roster and eligible for care planning and service delivery. The status change is recorded with an audit trail. This marks the official completion of the admission process.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens the resident record, Then the system must display a prerequisites checklist showing room assignment, move-in date, and required documentation status.

2. If all prerequisites are met and the Provider activates the resident, Then the system must change the status to "ACTIVE" and the resident must appear in the active roster.
   Else if any prerequisite is missing, Then the system must block activation and display the missing requirements.
   Else if the resident is already in "ACTIVE" status, Then the system must inform the Provider that no change is needed.

3. If the activation succeeds, Then the system must record the change in the audit trail with Provider identity, timestamp, and previous status.

4. If a system error occurs during activation, Then the status must remain unchanged and the Provider must be notified.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Provider can update resident status to "ACTIVE" when all prerequisites (room, move-in date, documentation) are met.
2. Verify that the resident appears in the active resident roster after activation.
3. Verify that the audit trail records the status change with Provider identity and timestamp.
4. Verify that the resident becomes eligible for care planning after activation.

Negative:
5. Verify that the system blocks activation when the room is not assigned.
6. Verify that the system blocks activation when the move-in date is not set.
7. Verify that the system blocks activation when required documentation is missing.
8. Verify that the system notifies the Provider if the resident is already "ACTIVE".

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the "Activate Resident" button is visible on the resident record when all prerequisites are met.
10. Verify that a checklist of prerequisites is shown with status indicators.
11. Verify that a confirmation dialog appears before activation.
12. Verify that the resident status badge updates to "ACTIVE" immediately after confirmation.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that activation fails gracefully if a system error occurs mid-process.
14. Verify that concurrent activation attempts by multiple Providers for the same resident are handled correctly.

Security:
15. Verify that only authorized Providers can activate residents.
16. Verify that the activation audit trail cannot be modified or deleted.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the resident status update to "ACTIVE" completes within 3 seconds.
Verify the active resident roster reflects the new resident within 5 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to automatically update census report with Occupied Beds +1, Available Beds -1., Deposit Received -1.
Description of User Story: This feature ensures that the census report is automatically updated when a resident is admitted and their status changes to active. The system increments the Occupied Beds count by 1, decrements the Available Beds count by 1, and decrements the Deposit Received count by 1 to reflect the transition from deposit stage to actual occupancy. These updates happen automatically as part of the admission processing workflow without requiring manual intervention. The census report reflects real-time facility occupancy data used for operational planning and regulatory compliance. The system validates that the census numbers remain consistent and non-negative after the update.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When a resident status changes to "ACTIVE", Then the system must automatically update the census report: Occupied Beds +1, Available Beds -1, Deposit Received -1, without requiring manual input.

2. If the census update would result in a negative Available Beds count, Then the system must flag the inconsistency and alert the administrator.

3. If multiple admissions are processed simultaneously, Then the system must ensure each admission correctly and independently adjusts the census counts without race conditions.

4. If the admission is reversed after activation, Then the system must revert the census changes (Occupied Beds -1, Available Beds +1, Deposit Received +1).

5. If a system error prevents the census update, Then the system must log the error and queue the update for retry.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the census report automatically increments Occupied Beds by 1 when a resident status changes to "ACTIVE".
2. Verify that the census report automatically decrements Available Beds by 1 upon admission activation.
3. Verify that the census report automatically decrements Deposit Received by 1 upon admission activation.
4. Verify that the updated census counts are visible immediately in the report without manual refresh.
5. Verify that the census is accurate after multiple sequential admissions (e.g., 5 admissions in a row).

Negative:
6. Verify that the system flags a negative Available Beds count as a data inconsistency.
7. Verify that the system handles census update failure and retries automatically.
8. Verify that the system prevents manual tampering with automatic census counts.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the census report dashboard displays Occupied Beds, Available Beds, and Deposit Received counts clearly.
10. Verify that the census report updates in real-time without requiring a manual page refresh.
11. Verify that historical census data is accessible for past dates.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that concurrent admissions update the census correctly without race conditions.
13. Verify that census accuracy is maintained when an admission is reversed immediately after processing.
14. Verify that census counts handle the boundary case when the facility reaches full occupancy.

Security:
15. Verify that only the system process can automatically update census data, not manual users.
16. Verify that census update logs are maintained in an immutable audit trail.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the census report update completes within 3 seconds of admission activation.
Verify the census report loads with current data within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to click on the "Discharge" action from the resident list.
Description of User Story: This feature provides the Provider with a "Discharge" action button directly accessible from the resident list view. The Provider can identify the resident to be discharged and initiate the discharge process by clicking the "Discharge" action associated with that resident. The action is available only for residents with an "ACTIVE" status to prevent discharging non-admitted residents. Clicking the action navigates the Provider to the discharge details form where the discharge workflow begins. The system ensures the action is contextually appropriate and visible only for eligible residents. This streamlines the discharge initiation by providing quick access from the main resident list.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider views the resident list, Then the system must display the "Discharge" action only for residents with "ACTIVE" status.

2. If the Provider clicks the "Discharge" action for an active resident, Then the system must navigate to the discharge details form with pre-populated resident data.
   Else if the resident does not have "ACTIVE" status, Then the "Discharge" action must be hidden or disabled.
   Else if the Provider does not have discharge privileges, Then the "Discharge" action must not be displayed.

3. If the system fails to load the discharge form, Then the Provider must be notified with an error message.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the "Discharge" action is visible for active residents (e.g., resident "Mary Johnson") in the resident list.
2. Verify that clicking "Discharge" navigates to the discharge details form.
3. Verify that the discharge form pre-populates with the correct resident details.
4. Verify that each active resident has their own individual "Discharge" action.

Negative:
5. Verify that the "Discharge" action is not available for non-active residents.
6. Verify that the "Discharge" action is not available for already-discharged residents.
7. Verify that the system handles navigation failure to the discharge form gracefully.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the "Discharge" action is clearly labeled and positioned in the action menu or row.
9. Verify that the "Discharge" action has a distinct icon or color to differentiate it from other actions.
10. Verify that the action is accessible via the actions dropdown or context menu for each resident row.
11. Verify that the resident list is searchable and filterable to locate the resident for discharge.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that clicking "Discharge" for a resident whose status changes between list load and click is handled correctly.
13. Verify that the discharge action performs correctly when the resident list contains hundreds of records.

Security:
14. Verify that only Providers with discharge privileges can see and use the "Discharge" action.
15. Verify that the discharge action does not expose sensitive resident data in the URL or browser history.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the discharge details form loads within 3 seconds after clicking the "Discharge" action.
Verify the resident list with discharge actions renders within 3 seconds for lists up to 500 residents.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to fill all the discharge details with mandatory discharge reason from predefined categories (Cannot Skip).
Description of User Story: This feature requires the Provider to complete all discharge details including selecting a mandatory discharge reason from predefined categories before a discharge can be processed. The discharge form includes fields for discharge date, discharge reason from a dropdown of predefined categories, destination after discharge, and any additional notes. The discharge reason field cannot be skipped or left empty as it is required for regulatory reporting and record keeping. The predefined categories ensure consistent data collection across all discharges. The system validates that all mandatory fields are completed before allowing submission. This ensures comprehensive and standardized documentation of every discharge event.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens the discharge details form, Then the system must display all mandatory fields including discharge date, discharge reason dropdown, and destination.

2. If the Provider fills all mandatory fields including the discharge reason and submits, Then the system must accept the discharge submission and record it.
   Else if the discharge reason is not selected, Then the system must block submission and show a validation error for the required field.
   Else if any other mandatory field is empty, Then the system must highlight the missing field and prevent submission.

3. If the Provider navigates away without submitting, Then the system must prompt to save or discard unsaved data.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Provider can fill all discharge details and submit the form successfully.
2. Verify that the discharge reason dropdown displays all predefined categories (e.g., "Voluntary", "Medical", "Financial", "Deceased", "Transfer").
3. Verify that the discharge is recorded with the correct reason category in the resident record.
4. Verify that the discharge date, destination, and notes are saved along with the reason.
5. Verify that each predefined category (Voluntary, Medical, Financial, Deceased, Transfer) is selectable and saves correctly.

Negative:
6. Verify that the system blocks submission when the discharge reason is not selected.
7. Verify that the system blocks submission when the discharge date is missing.
8. Verify that the system shows validation errors for all empty mandatory fields at once.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the discharge reason field is marked as mandatory with a visual indicator (e.g., asterisk).
10. Verify that the predefined categories are displayed in a dropdown with clear labels.
11. Verify that the form layout groups related fields logically.
12. Verify that a confirmation dialog appears before final submission.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that the system handles discharge submission gracefully when the predefined categories list is empty due to a configuration error.
14. Verify that the Provider cannot type free text in the discharge reason field when it is restricted to predefined options.
15. Verify that the system prompts correctly when navigating away from the form with unsaved data.

Security:
16. Verify that only authorized Providers can access and submit the discharge form.
17. Verify that discharge records cannot be modified after submission without an audit trail.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the discharge form with predefined categories loads within 3 seconds.
Verify discharge submission processing completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add specific events leading to discharge decision.
Description of User Story: This feature allows the Provider to document specific events or incidents that led to the discharge decision as part of the discharge process. The Provider can add one or more events with details including event date, description, category, and any involved parties. Each event entry captures the chronological sequence of occurrences that contributed to the discharge. The system supports adding multiple events to build a comprehensive timeline. The event entries are saved alongside the discharge record for regulatory compliance, family communication, and internal documentation. This ensures a clear and auditable record of the circumstances leading to a discharge decision.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens the discharge details form, Then the system must display an "Add Event" button to allow documenting events leading to the discharge.

2. If the Provider adds one or more events with all required fields (date, description, category) and saves, Then the events must be saved and linked to the discharge record.
   Else if the Provider submits an event without a required field such as description, Then the system must show a validation error.

3. If the Provider adds multiple events, Then the system must display them in chronological order by event date.

4. If the Provider removes an event before discharge submission, Then the event must be deleted from the discharge record.

5. If the Provider submits the discharge without any events, Then the system must allow submission as events are optional supplementary data.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Provider can add a specific event with date (e.g., 2026-03-15), description, and category.
2. Verify that the Provider can add multiple events (e.g., 3 events) to a single discharge record.
3. Verify that events are displayed in chronological order by event date.
4. Verify that all events are linked to the discharge record upon submission.
5. Verify that the Provider can remove an event before submitting the discharge.
6. Verify that discharge can be submitted without adding any events.

Negative:
7. Verify that the system shows a validation error when an event is added without a description.
8. Verify that the system shows a validation error when an event is added without a date.
9. Verify that the system rejects an event with a future date beyond the discharge date.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that an "Add Event" button is clearly visible on the discharge form.
11. Verify that each event entry displays date, description, and category in a readable format.
12. Verify that a remove or delete option is available for each event entry.
13. Verify that the event timeline is visually organized for easy review.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify that adding a large number of events (e.g., 20+) does not break the form layout.
15. Verify that event descriptions with special characters or very long text (e.g., 1000+ characters) are handled correctly.
16. Verify that editing an existing event before submission updates the record correctly.

Security:
17. Verify that only authorized Providers can add events to the discharge record.
18. Verify that discharge event records are included in the immutable audit trail after submission.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify adding an event to the discharge form saves within 2 seconds.
Verify the discharge form with 20 events renders within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to fill mandatory discharge survey/comment
Description of User Story: The Discharge Survey screen presents the Provider with a structured form containing mandatory fields that must be completed as part of the resident discharge workflow. The form includes a reason for discharge dropdown with predefined options such as transfer to another facility, return to family, medical escalation, voluntary departure, and other with a free-text explanation field. A multi-line comments text area allows the Provider to document clinical observations, behavioral notes, and any relevant discharge circumstances in detail. Required fields are marked with asterisks and inline validation messages appear when the Provider attempts to submit without completing all mandatory entries. A character counter is displayed beneath the comments field indicating the minimum required length of 50 characters to ensure substantive documentation. The form footer contains "Save Draft" and "Submit Survey" buttons, where submission locks the survey from further edits and advances the discharge workflow to the next step.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider selects a predefined reason for discharge from the dropdown, Then the system must record the selection and keep the free-text explanation field disabled.

2. If the Provider selects "Other" as the reason for discharge, Then the system must enable the free-text explanation field and mark it as required.
   Else if the Provider selects any other predefined reason, Then the system must keep the free-text explanation field hidden.

3. If the comments text area contains fewer than 50 characters, Then the system must display the character counter in red and prevent form submission.
   Else if the comments text area contains 50 or more characters, Then the system must display the character counter in green and allow submission.

4. If all mandatory fields are completed and valid, Then the system must enable the "Submit Survey" button for final submission.
   Else if any mandatory field is incomplete, Then the system must disable submission and highlight the incomplete fields.

5. When the Provider clicks "Save Draft", Then the system must persist all current entries without validation enforcement and allow future editing.

6. If the Provider clicks "Submit Survey" with all fields valid, Then the system must lock the survey from edits and advance the discharge workflow.
   Else if any field is invalid, Then the system must display inline validation errors and prevent submission.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the discharge survey form loads with mandatory fields marked by asterisks and pre-populated resident name and discharge date.
2. Verify that selecting "Transfer to Another Facility" from the discharge reason dropdown records the correct selection.
3. Verify that selecting "Other" as the discharge reason enables the free-text explanation field for custom entry.
4. Verify that the character counter turns green when comments reach or exceed 50 characters.
5. Verify that clicking "Submit Survey" with all mandatory fields completed locks the form and advances the workflow.
6. Verify that clicking "Save Draft" saves partial entries and allows the Provider to return later.

Negative:
7. Verify that clicking "Submit Survey" without selecting a discharge reason displays an inline validation error on the dropdown field.
8. Verify that clicking "Submit Survey" with comments fewer than 50 characters displays a character minimum validation error.
9. Verify that selecting "Other" and leaving the explanation field blank prevents submission with a validation error on the explanation field.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the discharge reason dropdown displays all predefined options including "Transfer to Another Facility", "Return to Family", "Medical Escalation", "Voluntary Departure", and "Other" in the correct order.
11. Verify that the comments text area displays a character counter beneath it showing current character count and the 50-character minimum.
12. Verify that "Save Draft" and "Submit Survey" buttons are positioned in the form footer with appropriate styling.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that the Provider cannot edit the survey form after it has been submitted and locked.
14. Verify that pasting text exceeding the maximum character limit into the comments field is handled without breaking the counter or the form.

Security:
15. Verify that only authorized Providers assigned to the resident can access and submit the discharge survey.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the discharge survey form loads with all fields and dropdown options within 2 seconds.
Verify that submitting the completed survey saves and locks the form within 3 seconds under normal system load.

User Story:
As a Privider, I should be able to select and send notification to 
- Parent/Guardian
- Primary Caregiver
- Care team members
- Case Manager with EDWP  mail
Description of User Story: The Discharge Notification screen allows the Provider to select one or more notification recipients from a categorized contact list and send discharge-related notifications via email. The screen displays four recipient categories presented as expandable sections: Parent/Guardian, Primary Caregiver, Care Team Members, and Case Manager, each showing the associated contact names and email addresses with individual checkboxes. The Provider can select any combination of recipients across categories using the checkboxes, and a "Select All" option is available for each category. For the Case Manager category, selecting the recipient automatically attaches the EDWP form as a PDF document to the notification email. A notification preview panel on the right side displays the email subject line, body content, and list of attachments before sending. The "Send Notification" button dispatches the emails to all selected recipients and logs the notification event in the discharge workflow audit trail.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider selects one or more recipients from any category, Then the system must enable the "Send Notification" button and update the preview panel.
   Else if no recipients are selected, Then the system must disable the "Send Notification" button and display a prompt to select at least one recipient.

2. If the Case Manager checkbox is selected, Then the system must automatically attach the EDWP form PDF to the notification email.
   Else if the Case Manager checkbox is not selected, Then the system must not attach the EDWP form to the notification.

3. If the Provider clicks "Send Notification" and all emails are dispatched successfully, Then the system must display a success confirmation and log the event in the audit trail.
   Else if one or more email dispatches fail, Then the system must display a partial failure notification identifying the failed recipients and offer a "Retry" option for those recipients.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Discharge Notification screen loads with four recipient categories displaying correct contact names and email addresses.
2. Verify that selecting individual checkboxes adds the corresponding recipients to the notification list.
3. Verify that "Select All" within a category selects all contacts in that category simultaneously.
4. Verify that selecting the Case Manager automatically attaches the EDWP form PDF to the notification.
5. Verify that the notification preview panel displays the correct email subject, body, and attachments.
6. Verify that clicking "Send Notification" dispatches emails to all selected recipients and logs the event in the audit trail.

Negative:
7. Verify that "Send Notification" is disabled when no recipients are selected.
8. Verify that an error message is displayed when an email dispatch fails for one or more recipients.
9. Verify that a selected recipient with an invalid or missing email address is flagged before sending.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the four recipient categories are displayed as expandable sections with checkboxes for each contact.
11. Verify that the notification preview panel is displayed on the right side with subject, body, and attachment sections.
12. Verify that the "Send Notification" button is prominently styled and positioned below the preview panel.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that the screen handles the scenario where the resident has no Parent/Guardian or Primary Caregiver contacts configured.
14. Verify that deselecting the Case Manager after initially selecting them removes the EDWP attachment from the preview.

Security:
15. Verify that notification emails are sent only to verified contacts associated with the resident and cannot be redirected to external addresses.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Discharge Notification screen loads all recipient categories with contact details within 2 seconds.
Verify that sending notifications to 10 or more recipients completes within 5 seconds with confirmation displayed.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to view the EDWP form once it has been signed and returned by the Case Manager, and the signed EDWP should be stored under a dedicated sub-tab within the Documents section.
Description of User Story: The Documents section of the resident profile includes a dedicated "Signed EDWP" sub-tab that stores all electronically signed EDWP forms returned by the Case Manager. When a Case Manager signs and returns an EDWP form, the system automatically files the signed document under this sub-tab with the signature date, case manager name, and document status displayed in a list view. The Provider can click on any signed EDWP entry to open a read-only viewer displaying the complete form with the electronic signature, timestamp, and any case manager comments. The sub-tab displays a badge count indicating the total number of signed EDWP documents available. A download button on each entry allows the Provider to save the signed EDWP as a PDF file locally. The list is sorted by signature date in descending order so the most recently signed documents appear first.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Case Manager has signed and returned one or more EDWP forms, Then the system must display the "Signed EDWP" sub-tab with a badge count matching the number of signed documents.
   Else if no signed EDWP forms exist for the resident, Then the system must display the "Signed EDWP" sub-tab with a zero badge count and an empty state message.

2. If the Provider clicks on a signed EDWP entry, Then the system must open the read-only viewer with the complete form, signature, and case manager comments.

3. If the Provider clicks the download button on an entry, Then the system must generate and download the signed EDWP as a PDF file.

4. If a newly signed EDWP is returned while the Provider is viewing the sub-tab, Then the system must refresh the list and update the badge count.
   Else if no new documents arrive, Then the system must retain the current list without change.

5. If the Provider attempts to edit a signed EDWP form, Then the system must prevent editing and display a message that signed documents are read-only.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the "Signed EDWP" sub-tab appears in the Documents section with the correct badge count matching the number of signed forms.
2. Verify that the signed EDWP list displays the signature date, case manager name, and status for each entry.
3. Verify that clicking an entry opens a read-only viewer with the complete form, electronic signature, timestamp, and case manager comments.
4. Verify that the download button generates and downloads the signed EDWP as a PDF file to the local machine.
5. Verify that the list is sorted by signature date in descending order with the most recently signed document appearing first.
6. Verify that the badge count increments by one when a new signed EDWP is filed in the system.

Negative:
7. Verify that the sub-tab displays an empty state message when no signed EDWP forms exist for the resident.
8. Verify that the Provider cannot edit or modify a signed EDWP document.
9. Verify that a download failure due to a corrupted document file displays an appropriate error message.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the "Signed EDWP" sub-tab is visually distinct within the Documents section tab list.
11. Verify that the read-only viewer displays the electronic signature prominently with the signer name and timestamp.
12. Verify that the download button icon and label are clearly visible on each list entry row.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that the system handles duplicate entries when a Case Manager signs the same EDWP form twice.
14. Verify that the list handles 100 or more signed EDWP entries using pagination or infinite scroll.

Security:
15. Verify that only authorized Providers with document access permissions can view and download signed EDWP forms.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the "Signed EDWP" sub-tab loads and displays the list within 2 seconds for residents with 50 or more signed documents.
Verify that the read-only viewer renders the complete signed EDWP form within 2 seconds of clicking an entry.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to fill staff discharge survey  (Final Evaluation) form.
Description of User Story: The Staff Discharge Survey (Final Evaluation) form enables the Provider to document a comprehensive staff assessment of the resident at the time of discharge. The form is divided into sections covering functional status evaluation with rating scales for activities of daily living such as bathing, dressing, mobility, and eating. A behavioral assessment section includes checkboxes and rating fields for cognitive function, social engagement, and behavioral incidents observed during the stay. A clinical summary section provides multi-line text areas for documenting the resident overall health status at discharge, medications effectiveness, and recommended follow-up care. Each section includes a staff evaluator name field auto-populated with the logged-in Provider name and an evaluation date field defaulting to the current date. The form footer contains "Save Draft" and "Submit Evaluation" buttons where submission finalizes the evaluation and attaches it to the discharge record.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider completes all required rating fields in the functional status section, Then the system must mark the section as complete with a green checkmark.
   Else if any required rating field in the functional status section is left blank, Then the system must display an inline validation error when submission is attempted.

2. If the Provider clicks "Submit Evaluation" with all sections completed, Then the system must finalize the evaluation, attach it to the discharge record, and lock the form.
   Else if any section is incomplete, Then the system must display validation errors on each incomplete section and prevent submission.

3. When the Provider clicks "Save Draft", Then the system must save all current entries without enforcing validation and allow future editing.

4. If the evaluation has already been submitted, Then the system must display the form in read-only mode with a "Submitted" status indicator.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Staff Discharge Survey form loads with all sections and auto-populated evaluator name and current date.
2. Verify that selecting rating values for ADL activities (bathing, dressing, mobility, eating) records the correct ratings.
3. Verify that checking behavioral assessment checkboxes and entering ratings records the data correctly.
4. Verify that entering text in clinical summary fields (health status, medication effectiveness, follow-up care) saves the entries as part of the evaluation.
5. Verify that clicking "Submit Evaluation" with all sections completed finalizes and locks the form.
6. Verify that clicking "Save Draft" saves partial entries for later completion.

Negative:
7. Verify that clicking "Submit Evaluation" with incomplete functional status ratings displays inline validation errors on each unanswered field.
8. Verify that clicking "Submit Evaluation" with empty required clinical summary fields displays validation errors.
9. Verify that the Provider cannot edit a submitted evaluation form.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the form sections are clearly separated with headers for functional status, behavioral assessment, and clinical summary.
11. Verify that rating scales display correct labels and selectable values for each activity of daily living.
12. Verify that "Save Draft" and "Submit Evaluation" buttons are positioned in the form footer.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that the Provider can change the evaluation date to a date different from the discharge date without causing validation errors.
14. Verify that simultaneous access by two staff members to the same evaluation form is handled without data corruption.

Security:
15. Verify that only authenticated Providers with discharge evaluation permissions can access and submit the staff survey form.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Staff Discharge Survey form loads with all sections and pre-populated fields within 2 seconds.
Verify that submitting the completed evaluation saves and locks the form within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add resident/family discharge survey capturing their feedback.
Description of User Story: The Resident/Family Discharge Survey form enables the Provider to capture feedback from the resident or their family members regarding their experience during the stay at the facility. The form includes a respondent identification section where the Provider selects whether the feedback is from the resident, a family member, or both using radio buttons, along with the respondent name and relationship fields. A satisfaction rating section presents multiple aspects of care such as quality of care, staff responsiveness, facility cleanliness, meal quality, and activity programs, each with a five-point Likert scale ranging from Very Dissatisfied to Very Satisfied. An open-ended feedback section provides text areas for what the respondent liked most, areas for improvement, and any additional comments. The form includes a consent checkbox confirming the respondent authorized the feedback to be recorded. The "Submit Feedback" button saves the completed survey and associates it with the resident discharge record.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider selects "Resident" as the respondent, Then the system must require only the resident name field and hide the relationship field.
   Else if the Provider selects "Family Member" as the respondent, Then the system must require both the respondent name and relationship fields.
   Else if the Provider selects "Both" as the respondent type, Then the system must require the respondent name and relationship fields for the family member.

2. If all satisfaction rating fields are completed and the consent checkbox is checked, Then the system must enable the "Submit Feedback" button.
   Else if the consent checkbox is not checked, Then the system must disable the "Submit Feedback" button and display a message that consent is required.

3. If the Provider clicks "Submit Feedback" with incomplete satisfaction ratings, Then the system must display validation errors on each unanswered rating field and prevent submission.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the survey loads with respondent identification radio buttons, name field, and relationship field.
2. Verify that selecting "Resident" as the respondent hides the relationship field and requires only the resident name.
3. Verify that selecting "Family Member" enables and requires the relationship field.
4. Verify that satisfaction rating Likert scales are displayed for all care aspects and allow a single selection per aspect.
5. Verify that open-ended feedback text areas accept and save entered text for the "liked most", "areas for improvement", and "additional comments" fields.
6. Verify that clicking "Submit Feedback" with all fields completed and consent checked saves the survey to the discharge record.

Negative:
7. Verify that clicking "Submit Feedback" without checking the consent checkbox displays a validation error requiring consent.
8. Verify that clicking "Submit Feedback" with incomplete satisfaction ratings displays validation errors on the unanswered items.
9. Verify that selecting "Family Member" without entering the relationship field prevents submission with a validation error.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the five-point Likert scale labels (Very Dissatisfied to Very Satisfied) are clearly displayed for each care aspect.
11. Verify that the consent checkbox is positioned before the "Submit Feedback" button with clear authorization text.
12. Verify that the open-ended feedback text areas have adequate height and placeholder text guiding the respondent.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that submitting a second survey for the same resident displays an appropriate warning or prevents duplicate submission.
14. Verify that the respondent name field accepts and stores names containing special characters such as hyphens and accented letters.

Security:
15. Verify that only authorized Providers can access and submit the resident/family discharge survey for residents assigned to them.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Resident/Family Discharge Survey form loads within 2 seconds with all sections rendered.
Verify that submitting the completed survey saves and associates it with the discharge record within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add quality of care rating from resident/family.
Description of User Story: The Quality of Care Rating form allows the Provider to record an overall quality rating provided by the resident or their family as part of the discharge feedback process. The form presents a star-based rating component with a one-to-five star scale where the Provider clicks on the appropriate star to record the overall quality of care rating. Below the star rating, a breakdown section lists specific quality dimensions such as medical care quality, personal care assistance, communication effectiveness, safety and security, and emotional support, each with an individual one-to-five star rating. A comments text area beneath the ratings allows the respondent to provide qualitative feedback supporting their rating. The form displays the respondent name from the linked discharge survey and includes a date stamp for when the rating was captured. A "Save Rating" button persists the data and links it to the resident discharge record for reporting and analytics purposes.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider clicks on a star in the overall rating component, Then the system must highlight all stars up to and including the selected star and record the rating value.

2. When the Provider clicks on a star in a quality dimension row, Then the system must highlight the selected stars for that dimension and record the individual rating.

3. If the overall rating is not selected and the Provider clicks "Save Rating", Then the system must display a validation error and prevent saving.
   Else if the overall rating is selected, Then the system must enable saving.

4. If all quality dimension ratings are completed along with the overall rating, Then the system must enable the "Save Rating" button for submission.
   Else if any quality dimension rating is left blank, Then the system must display a validation warning but allow saving with the overall rating as the minimum requirement.

5. If the rating has already been saved for this resident, Then the system must display the form in read-only mode with the previously saved ratings.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Quality of Care Rating form loads with the star-based overall rating component and all quality dimension rating rows displayed.
2. Verify that clicking a star (e.g., 4 out of 5) in the overall rating highlights the correct number of stars and records the value as 4.
3. Verify that selecting individual star ratings for each quality dimension (medical care, personal care, communication, safety, emotional support) records the correct values.
4. Verify that entering qualitative comments in the text area saves the feedback alongside the star ratings.
5. Verify that clicking "Save Rating" persists all ratings and links them to the resident discharge record.
6. Verify that the respondent name and date stamp are correctly displayed on the form.

Negative:
7. Verify that clicking "Save Rating" without selecting the overall star rating displays a validation error preventing save.
8. Verify that the form displays in read-only mode when a rating has already been saved for the resident.
9. Verify that saving with no quality dimension ratings selected displays the expected validation warning while still allowing save.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the star-based rating component displays five clickable stars with hover highlighting on mouse-over.
11. Verify that quality dimension labels are clearly listed with individual star rating components aligned horizontally.
12. Verify that the comments text area is positioned below the ratings with adequate space for detailed feedback.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that clicking a different star after an initial selection correctly updates the recorded rating value.
14. Verify that the form behaves correctly when accessed for a resident who does not have a linked discharge survey.

Security:
15. Verify that only authorized Providers can access and save quality of care ratings for residents assigned to their facility.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Quality of Care Rating form loads with all star components rendered within 2 seconds.
Verify that saving the completed rating persists data and displays confirmation within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to preview discharge confirmation details.
Description of User Story: The Discharge Confirmation Preview screen provides a consolidated read-only summary of all discharge-related information collected throughout the discharge workflow. The preview is organized into clearly labeled sections including resident demographics, discharge reason and date, discharge survey results, staff final evaluation summary, resident/family feedback ratings, notification recipients and status, medication list summary, and financial settlement details. Each section displays the key data points in a structured format with labels and values aligned for easy scanning. A "Print" button in the toolbar generates a printable version of the confirmation details formatted for standard paper size. An "Edit" link next to each section header allows the Provider to navigate back to the corresponding form to make corrections before final confirmation. The "Confirm Discharge" button at the bottom of the preview finalizes the discharge process and triggers all downstream system updates.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If all discharge workflow steps are completed, Then the system must display all sections with their data and enable the "Confirm Discharge" button.
   Else if one or more required workflow steps are incomplete, Then the system must display warning indicators on the incomplete sections and disable the "Confirm Discharge" button.

2. When the Provider clicks the "Edit" link for a section, Then the system must navigate to the corresponding discharge form pre-populated with current data.

3. When the Provider clicks "Print", Then the system must generate a printable PDF version of the discharge confirmation details.

4. If the Provider clicks "Confirm Discharge", Then the system must finalize the discharge, trigger all system updates, and display a success confirmation.
   Else if the Provider attempts to navigate away from the preview without confirming, Then the system must display a prompt asking whether to continue or return to the preview.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Discharge Confirmation Preview displays all sections with correct data from completed workflow steps.
2. Verify that the resident demographics section shows the correct name, date of birth, admission date, and discharge date.
3. Verify that clicking the "Edit" link next to a section navigates to the corresponding form pre-populated with current data.
4. Verify that the "Print" button generates a formatted printable version of the confirmation details.
5. Verify that clicking "Confirm Discharge" finalizes the process and triggers all downstream system updates.
6. Verify that the financial settlement section displays accurate billing and refund details.

Negative:
7. Verify that incomplete sections display warning indicators and the "Confirm Discharge" button remains disabled.
8. Verify that "Confirm Discharge" is disabled when required workflow steps have not been completed.
9. Verify that the system prompts the Provider when attempting to navigate away from the preview without confirming discharge.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that all sections are clearly labeled with headers and data displayed in a structured label-value format.
11. Verify that the "Print" button and "Confirm Discharge" button are prominently positioned in the toolbar and footer respectively.
12. Verify that "Edit" links are visible next to each section header and styled as actionable links.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that the preview handles a section with no data correctly when an optional workflow step was skipped.
14. Verify that a downstream system update failure after "Confirm Discharge" is clicked displays an appropriate error and does not leave the discharge in an inconsistent state.

Security:
15. Verify that only authorized Providers with discharge confirmation permissions can access the preview and confirm discharge.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Discharge Confirmation Preview loads all sections within 3 seconds even when all workflow steps are completed with full data.
Verify that clicking "Confirm Discharge" completes the finalization and triggers all downstream updates within 5 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to create final medication list showing all medications at discharge.
Description of User Story: The Final Medication List screen enables the Provider to generate a comprehensive list of all medications the resident is currently prescribed at the time of discharge. The screen displays a table with columns for medication name, dosage, frequency, route of administration, prescribing physician, start date, and status indicating whether the medication should be continued, discontinued, or modified post-discharge. The table is pre-populated from the resident active medication records in the system, and the Provider can update the post-discharge status for each medication using a dropdown selector. An "Add Medication" button allows the Provider to add any new medications prescribed specifically for the discharge transition. A notes column provides a text field for each medication row where the Provider can add special instructions or tapering details. The "Generate Final List" button compiles the table into a formatted document that is attached to the discharge record and can be printed for the resident or family.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the resident has active medication records, Then the system must pre-populate the medication table with all current prescriptions and default status to "Continue".
   Else if the resident has no active medication records, Then the system must display an empty table with a message indicating no active medications and allow manual entry.

2. If the Provider selects "Discontinue" for a medication, Then the system must visually strike through the medication row and record the discontinuation.
   Else if the Provider selects "Modify" for a medication, Then the system must enable the dosage and frequency fields for editing.
   Else if the Provider selects "Continue", Then the system must keep the row in its default display state.

3. If the Provider clicks "Generate Final List" with one or more medications missing a status selection, Then the system must display a validation error requiring all statuses to be set.

4. If the Provider clicks "Add Medication" and enters a duplicate medication name, Then the system must display a warning about the potential duplicate entry.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the medication table loads pre-populated with all active medications and correct column data including name, dosage, frequency, route, and prescribing physician.
2. Verify that selecting "Continue" status for a medication (e.g., Metformin 500mg) records it for post-discharge continuation.
3. Verify that selecting "Discontinue" for a medication visually strikes through the row and records the discontinuation.
4. Verify that selecting "Modify" for a medication enables the dosage and frequency fields for inline editing.
5. Verify that "Add Medication" allows entry of a new medication with all required fields filled.
6. Verify that "Generate Final List" compiles the table into a formatted document attached to the discharge record.
7. Verify that notes entered in the notes column are saved alongside the corresponding medication record.

Negative:
8. Verify that "Generate Final List" displays a validation error when one or more medications are missing a status selection.
9. Verify that a warning is displayed when adding a duplicate medication name already present in the table.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the medication table columns are clearly labeled and the status dropdown options (Continue, Discontinue, Modify) are fully visible.
11. Verify that the "Add Medication" button and "Generate Final List" button are prominently positioned on the screen.
12. Verify that the print output is formatted for standard paper size with a legible font and organized layout.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that the table handles a resident with 50 or more active medications without display issues.
14. Verify that adding a new medication after the final list has already been generated prompts the Provider to regenerate the list.

Security:
15. Verify that only authorized Providers with medication management permissions can create and modify the final medication list.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Final Medication List screen loads with pre-populated data within 2 seconds for residents with 30 or more active medications.
Verify that "Generate Final List" compiles and attaches the document within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to include any outstanding balances on final invoice.
Description of User Story: The Final Invoice screen includes an Outstanding Balances section that consolidates all unpaid charges associated with the resident account into the discharge billing statement. The section displays a table listing each outstanding charge with columns for service date, description, category (room and board, medical services, ancillary charges, pharmacy), original amount, payments received, and remaining balance. The system automatically retrieves all unpaid invoices and unbilled services from the resident financial ledger and presents them in chronological order. A subtotal row at the bottom of the table calculates the total outstanding balance across all categories. The Provider can add manual adjustment entries using an "Add Adjustment" button for charges not captured in the automated system, such as property damage fees or late payment penalties. Each adjustment entry requires a description and amount field with an optional notes field for justification. The outstanding balance total is automatically carried forward to the final invoice summary.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the resident has unpaid invoices or unbilled services, Then the system must display all charges in the Outstanding Balances table with correct amounts.
   Else if the resident has no outstanding balances, Then the system must display an empty table with a message indicating no outstanding charges exist.

2. When the Provider clicks "Add Adjustment", Then the system must add a new row with description, amount, and notes fields for manual entry.

3. If the Provider enters a negative amount in an adjustment entry, Then the system must treat it as a credit and subtract it from the total outstanding balance.
   Else if the amount is positive, Then the system must add it to the total outstanding balance.

4. If the Provider submits an adjustment without a description, Then the system must display a validation error requiring a description before saving.

5. If outstanding balances exist, Then the system must carry forward the total to the final invoice summary automatically.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Outstanding Balances table loads with all unpaid charges retrieved from the resident financial ledger.
2. Verify that charges are displayed in chronological order by service date.
3. Verify that the subtotal row correctly calculates the total outstanding balance across all categories.
4. Verify that "Add Adjustment" allows manual entry of a description (e.g., "Late Payment Penalty"), amount, and notes.
5. Verify that the subtotal recalculates to include all manual adjustments after they are added.
6. Verify that the total outstanding balance is automatically carried forward to the final invoice summary section.

Negative:
7. Verify that an empty state message is displayed when the resident has no outstanding balances.
8. Verify that a validation error is shown when an adjustment is submitted without a description.
9. Verify that entering a non-numeric value in the adjustment amount field displays a validation error.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the Outstanding Balances table columns are clearly labeled with monetary values right-aligned.
11. Verify that the subtotal row is visually distinct from the charge rows using bold formatting.
12. Verify that the "Add Adjustment" button is positioned below the table with clear labeling.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that the table correctly displays partially paid invoices with mixed payment statuses.
14. Verify that the subtotal calculation correctly handles negative adjustment amounts as credits.

Security:
15. Verify that only authorized Providers with financial management permissions can view and modify outstanding balance entries.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Outstanding Balances table loads all unpaid charges within 2 seconds for residents with 50 or more charge entries.
Verify that adding a manual adjustment and recalculating the subtotal completes within 1 second.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to apply any applicable deductions (unpaid balances, damages) to security deposit.
Description of User Story: The Security Deposit Deductions screen allows the Provider to apply itemized deductions against the resident security deposit as part of the discharge financial settlement. The screen displays the original security deposit amount at the top, followed by a deductions table with columns for deduction type, description, reference number, amount, and supporting documentation upload. Predefined deduction categories include unpaid balances, property damage, cleaning fees, key replacement, and other with a free-text option. The Provider can add deduction entries by clicking "Add Deduction" and selecting the appropriate category, entering the amount, and optionally uploading photographic evidence or supporting documents. A running balance section below the table shows the original deposit, total deductions, and remaining balance in real time as deductions are added or removed. The Provider can remove a deduction entry by clicking the delete icon on the row. A "Save Deductions" button persists all entries and updates the financial settlement record.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider selects a predefined deduction category (e.g., "Property Damage"), Then the system must auto-populate the description field with the category name and allow editing.
   Else if the Provider selects "Other" as the deduction category, Then the system must require a custom description to be entered.

2. If the total deductions exceed the original security deposit amount, Then the system must display a warning indicating the deductions exceed the deposit and show a negative remaining balance.
   Else if total deductions are within the deposit amount, Then the system must show a positive remaining balance.

3. If the Provider uploads supporting documentation, Then the system must attach the file to the deduction entry and display a document icon.

4. If the Provider clicks "Save Deductions" without adding any entries, Then the system must save an empty deductions record indicating no deductions applied.

5. If the Provider removes a deduction entry, Then the system must recalculate the running balance and update the display immediately.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the screen displays the original security deposit amount and an empty deductions table on load.
2. Verify that "Add Deduction" allows entry of category (e.g., "Property Damage"), description, amount ($150.00), reference number, and document upload.
3. Verify that the running balance calculates correctly as each deduction is added (e.g., $1000 deposit minus $150 deduction = $850 remaining).
4. Verify that removing a deduction entry recalculates the running balance immediately.
5. Verify that uploading supporting documentation attaches the file to the corresponding deduction entry with a document icon.
6. Verify that "Save Deductions" persists all entries and updates the financial settlement record.

Negative:
7. Verify that a warning is displayed when total deductions exceed the original deposit amount.
8. Verify that selecting "Other" category without entering a custom description displays a validation error.
9. Verify that entering a negative deduction amount displays a validation error.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the deductions table columns are clearly labeled and monetary amounts are right-aligned.
11. Verify that the running balance section shows original deposit, total deductions, and remaining balance with clear formatting.
12. Verify that the delete icon is visible on each deduction row and triggers removal on click.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that the screen displays an appropriate message when the resident has no security deposit on file.
14. Verify that the table handles 20 or more deduction entries without display or performance issues.

Security:
15. Verify that only authorized Providers with financial management permissions can apply and modify security deposit deductions.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Security Deposit Deductions screen loads within 2 seconds with the original deposit amount displayed.
Verify that adding a deduction and recalculating the running balance completes within 1 second.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to calculate final refund amount after all deductions.
Description of User Story: The Final Refund Calculation screen presents a comprehensive financial summary that calculates the net refund amount owed to the resident or responsible party after all applicable deductions have been applied. The screen displays a breakdown starting with the original security deposit amount, followed by an itemized list of all deductions grouped by category with subtotals for each category. Below the deductions, any prepaid service credits or overpayments are added back as credit line items. The net refund amount is prominently displayed in a highlighted section calculated as the original deposit minus total deductions plus any credits. A calculation details panel shows each arithmetic step for transparency and audit purposes. The Provider can click "Recalculate" to refresh the computation if any upstream financial data has changed. A "Lock Calculation" button freezes the final refund amount, preventing further changes, and records the locked amount with a timestamp in the discharge financial record.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the total deductions are less than the original deposit, Then the system must display a positive net refund amount as the amount owed to the resident.
   Else if the total deductions exceed the original deposit and credits combined, Then the system must display a negative balance indicating the amount owed by the resident.
