2. If prepaid credits or overpayments exist, Then the system must add them as positive line items in the refund calculation.
   Else if no credits exist, Then the system must calculate the refund as original deposit minus total deductions only.

3. If the Provider clicks "Lock Calculation", Then the system must freeze the refund amount and disable all editing of financial data.

4. If upstream financial data has changed since the last calculation, Then the system must display a notification prompting the Provider to click "Recalculate".
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the screen displays the original deposit, itemized deductions, credits, and the calculated net refund amount.
2. Verify that deduction categories display correct subtotals and individual line items (e.g., "Property Damage: $150.00").
3. Verify that prepaid credits (e.g., overpayment of $50.00) are added to the refund calculation correctly.
4. Verify that the net refund amount equals original deposit minus total deductions plus total credits.
5. Verify that "Recalculate" refreshes the computation with updated upstream financial data.
6. Verify that "Lock Calculation" freezes the refund amount and records it with a timestamp.

Negative:
7. Verify that the system displays a negative balance when total deductions exceed the deposit and credits combined.
8. Verify that the Provider cannot modify financial data after the calculation is locked.
9. Verify that when no deductions or credits exist the system correctly shows the full deposit as the refund amount.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the net refund amount is prominently highlighted and clearly distinguishable from other line items.
11. Verify that the calculation details panel shows each arithmetic step transparently for audit review.
12. Verify that the "Recalculate" and "Lock Calculation" buttons are clearly labeled and positioned on the screen.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify calculation accuracy when both large deductions and multiple small credits exist simultaneously.
14. Verify that clicking "Recalculate" multiple times in rapid succession does not produce inconsistent results.

Security:
15. Verify that only authorized Providers with financial settlement permissions can lock the final refund calculation.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Final Refund Calculation screen loads and computes the net refund within 2 seconds.
Verify that "Recalculate" refreshes the computation and updates the display within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to generate final billing invoice showing all services through discharge date.
Description of User Story: The Final Billing Invoice screen enables the Provider to generate a comprehensive invoice document that itemizes all services rendered to the resident from the admission date through the discharge date. The invoice header includes the facility name, address, tax identification number, resident name, account number, admission date, and discharge date. The invoice body displays a detailed line-item table with columns for service date, service description, category, quantity, unit price, and line total. Services are grouped by category including room and board, medical services, pharmacy, therapy, ancillary charges, and any applicable taxes or surcharges. A summary section at the bottom shows the subtotal, applicable discounts or insurance adjustments, total payments received, outstanding balance, and final amount due. The Provider can click "Generate Invoice" to create a PDF document with a unique invoice number, and a "Send Invoice" button allows the Provider to email the invoice to the responsible party.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the resident has services rendered across multiple categories, Then the system must group the line items by category with category subtotals.

2. If insurance adjustments or discounts apply, Then the system must display them as separate line items in the summary section with negative amounts.
   Else if no adjustments apply, Then the system must display the subtotal as the pre-payment total.

3. If the total payments received equal or exceed the total charges, Then the system must display the final amount due as zero or a credit balance.
   Else if payments are less than total charges, Then the system must display the outstanding amount due.

4. When the Provider clicks "Generate Invoice", Then the system must create a PDF with a unique invoice number and timestamp.

5. If the Provider clicks "Send Invoice" without a responsible party email on file, Then the system must display an error message prompting the Provider to add a contact email.
   Else if an email is on file, Then the system must send the PDF to that address.

6. If the invoice has already been generated, Then the system must allow regeneration with a new version number and mark the previous version as superseded.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the invoice header displays correct facility name, address, tax ID, resident name, account number, admission date, and discharge date.
2. Verify that all services from admission to discharge are listed as line items with correct date, description, category, quantity, unit price, and line total.
3. Verify that services are grouped by category (room and board, medical, pharmacy, therapy, ancillary) with accurate category subtotals.
4. Verify that the summary section correctly calculates subtotal, discounts, insurance adjustments, payments received, and final amount due.
5. Verify that "Generate Invoice" creates a PDF with a unique invoice number (e.g., INV-2026-00457).
6. Verify that "Send Invoice" emails the PDF to the responsible party email address on file.

Negative:
7. Verify that an error is displayed when "Send Invoice" is clicked and no responsible party email is on file.
8. Verify that the system handles a resident with no billable services between admission and discharge by displaying an empty line-item table.
9. Verify that regenerating an invoice assigns a new version number and marks the previous version as superseded.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the line-item table displays columns with correct headers and monetary values are right-aligned.
11. Verify that the summary section is visually distinct with the final amount due prominently displayed.
12. Verify that "Generate Invoice" and "Send Invoice" buttons are clearly labeled and positioned in the toolbar.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that invoice generation handles a resident with a same-day admission and discharge date correctly.
14. Verify that calculation accuracy is maintained when the resident has 200 or more line-item services.

Security:
15. Verify that only authorized Providers with billing permissions can generate and send final invoices.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Final Billing Invoice screen loads all line items within 3 seconds for residents with 100 or more services.
Verify that "Generate Invoice" creates the PDF document within 5 seconds for invoices with 200 or more line items.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to initiate a refund request that goes through a three-level approval workflow
Description of User Story: The Refund Request screen enables the Provider to initiate a formal refund request that enters a three-level approval workflow before the refund can be processed. The request form includes fields for the resident name, refund amount pre-populated from the locked final refund calculation, refund reason dropdown, payment method selection for the refund disbursement, and a justification text area. Upon submission, the refund request is routed to a Level 1 approver (Facility Manager) who can approve, reject, or request modifications. If approved at Level 1, the request escalates to Level 2 (Regional Director) for review. Level 2 approval escalates the request to Level 3 (Finance Director) for final authorization. At each level, the approver can view the complete refund details, add comments, and take action. The Provider can track the approval status in real time through a workflow status tracker displaying the current level, approver name, and action taken at each completed level.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider submits a refund request with all required fields completed, Then the system must route the request to the Level 1 approver and update the status to "Pending Level 1 Approval".

2. If an approver at any level rejects the request, Then the system must update the status to "Rejected at Level [N]" and notify the Provider with the rejection reason.
   Else if an approver requests modifications, Then the system must return the request to the Provider with modification comments and update the status to "Modifications Requested".

3. If the Level 1 approver approves, Then the system must escalate to Level 2 and update the status to "Pending Level 2 Approval".
   Else if the Level 2 approver approves, Then the system must escalate to Level 3 and update the status to "Pending Level 3 Approval".
   Else if the Level 3 approver approves, Then the system must mark the refund as "Approved" and authorize it for processing.

4. If the Provider submits the request without entering a justification, Then the system must display a validation error and prevent submission.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the refund request form loads with pre-populated resident name and refund amount from the locked calculation.
2. Verify that submitting the request routes it to the Level 1 approver (Facility Manager) and updates the status to "Pending Level 1 Approval".
3. Verify that Level 1 approval escalates the request to the Level 2 approver (Regional Director).
4. Verify that Level 2 approval escalates the request to the Level 3 approver (Finance Director).
5. Verify that Level 3 approval marks the request status as "Approved" and authorizes processing.
6. Verify that the workflow status tracker displays the current level, approver name, and actions taken at each completed level.

Negative:
7. Verify that rejection at any level updates the status to "Rejected at Level [N]" and notifies the Provider with the rejection reason.
8. Verify that "Modifications Requested" status returns the request to the Provider for changes with the approver comments visible.
9. Verify that submitting the request without a justification displays a validation error and prevents submission.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the workflow status tracker visually represents three approval levels with progress indicators.
11. Verify that each approval level on the tracker displays the approver name, action taken, and timestamp.
12. Verify that the refund reason dropdown and payment method selection are clearly labeled and required.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that a refund request pending approval beyond a configurable threshold triggers an escalation notification.
14. Verify that resubmitting a modified request after modifications were requested routes correctly back into the approval workflow at Level 1.

Security:
15. Verify that only designated approvers at each level can approve, reject, or request modifications for refund requests.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that submitting the refund request and routing it to Level 1 completes within 3 seconds.
Verify that the workflow status tracker loads the current approval status within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to send refund check to responsible party
Description of User Story: The Refund Disbursement screen allows the Provider to process and send the approved refund to the designated responsible party after the three-level approval workflow is completed. The screen displays the approved refund amount, responsible party name, mailing address, and preferred payment method retrieved from the resident financial profile. The Provider can select the disbursement method from options including physical check, electronic fund transfer, or direct deposit, with corresponding fields for check number, bank account details, or routing information. A "Generate Check" button creates a printable check document with the payee name, amount, date, and facility details pre-populated. The Provider enters the check number and mailing tracking number in designated fields for record keeping. A "Mark as Sent" button updates the refund status to "Disbursed" and records the sent date, method, and tracking details in the discharge financial record. An automated email notification is sent to the responsible party confirming the refund has been dispatched.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider selects "Physical Check" as the disbursement method, Then the system must enable the check number, mailing tracking number fields, and the "Generate Check" button.
   Else if the Provider selects "Electronic Fund Transfer", Then the system must enable the bank account and routing number fields.
   Else if the Provider selects "Direct Deposit", Then the system must enable the bank account details field pre-populated from the resident financial profile if available.

2. If the Provider clicks "Mark as Sent" without entering the required tracking or check number, Then the system must display a validation error and prevent the status update.

3. If the refund is successfully marked as sent, Then the system must update the status to "Disbursed" and send an automated email notification to the responsible party.

4. If the refund request has not been fully approved through all three levels, Then the system must disable the disbursement screen and display a message that approval is pending.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the screen displays the approved refund amount, responsible party name, address, and preferred payment method.
2. Verify that selecting "Physical Check" enables the check number, tracking number fields, and the "Generate Check" button.
3. Verify that "Generate Check" creates a printable check document with the correct payee name, refund amount, date, and facility details.
4. Verify that "Mark as Sent" updates the refund status to "Disbursed" and records the sent date, method, and tracking number.
5. Verify that an automated email notification is sent to the responsible party upon the disbursement being marked as sent.
6. Verify that selecting "Electronic Fund Transfer" enables the bank account and routing number fields.

Negative:
7. Verify that the disbursement screen is disabled and shows a pending approval message when the three-level approval workflow is not complete.
8. Verify that clicking "Mark as Sent" without entering the required check number or tracking number displays a validation error.
9. Verify that a missing responsible party email address is flagged with an error message when the system attempts to send the confirmation notification.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that disbursement method options are displayed as clearly labeled radio buttons or a dropdown.
11. Verify that the "Generate Check" button and "Mark as Sent" button are clearly styled and positioned.
12. Verify that the printable check document layout matches standard check formatting with payee, amount, and date fields.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that attempting to disburse a refund amount of $0.00 displays a validation warning.
14. Verify that changing the disbursement method after initially selecting one clears the previously entered method-specific fields.

Security:
15. Verify that only authorized Providers with financial disbursement permissions can process and send refund checks.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Refund Disbursement screen loads with all pre-populated details within 2 seconds.
Verify that "Generate Check" creates the printable document within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to release discharged resident's room and mark as AVAILABLE.
Description of User Story: The Room Release screen allows the Provider to officially release the room assigned to a discharged resident and update its availability status in the facility room management system. The screen displays the current room details including room number, floor, wing, room type, and the discharged resident name with discharge date. A room condition checklist is presented with items such as furniture intact, walls undamaged, bathroom cleaned, linens removed, and keys returned, each with a checkbox that the Provider must complete. A notes field allows the Provider to document any room condition issues or maintenance requirements before releasing the room. The "Release Room" button changes the room status from "Occupied" to "Available" in the system and removes the resident assignment from the room record. A confirmation dialog appears before the status change is applied, requiring the Provider to verify the action. Upon release, the room appears in the available rooms inventory for new resident assignments.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If all room condition checklist items are checked, Then the system must enable the "Release Room" button.
   Else if one or more checklist items are unchecked, Then the system must disable the "Release Room" button and display a message that all items must be verified.

2. If the Provider clicks "Release Room" and confirms the dialog, Then the system must update the room status to "Available" and remove the resident assignment.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Cancel" in the confirmation dialog, Then the system must close the dialog and keep the room in its current status.

3. If the room has maintenance issues noted, Then the system must update the room status to "Maintenance Required" instead of "Available" and flag it for the facilities team.

4. If the room has already been released, Then the system must display the screen in read-only mode with the release details and timestamp.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Room Release screen loads with the correct room details (number, floor, wing, type) and discharged resident information.
2. Verify that completing all checklist items (furniture intact, walls undamaged, bathroom cleaned, linens removed, keys returned) enables the "Release Room" button.
3. Verify that the confirmation dialog appears when "Release Room" is clicked.
4. Verify that confirming the dialog updates the room status from "Occupied" to "Available" and removes the resident assignment.
5. Verify that the released room appears in the available rooms inventory after release.
6. Verify that room condition notes are saved in the release record.

Negative:
7. Verify that "Release Room" is disabled when one or more checklist items are incomplete.
8. Verify that clicking "Cancel" in the confirmation dialog does not change the room status.
9. Verify that the screen is in read-only mode when the room has already been released.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the room condition checklist displays all items with checkboxes and descriptive labels.
11. Verify that the confirmation dialog clearly states the action and includes "Confirm" and "Cancel" buttons.
12. Verify that the room status indicator changes visually from "Occupied" to "Available" after release.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that noting a maintenance issue in the room condition notes correctly sets the room status to "Maintenance Required" instead of "Available".
14. Verify that a concurrent release attempt by two Providers for the same room is handled without data conflict.

Security:
15. Verify that only authorized Providers with room management permissions can release rooms and update availability.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Room Release screen loads with room details and checklist within 2 seconds.
Verify that the room status update to "Available" completes within 2 seconds after confirmation.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to automatically update census report with Occupied Beds -1, Available Beds +1.
Description of User Story: The Census Report Auto-Update feature automatically adjusts the facility census report whenever a resident room is released during the discharge process. When the room status changes from "Occupied" to "Available", the system decrements the Occupied Beds count by one and increments the Available Beds count by one in the real-time census dashboard. The census report displays the total bed capacity, current occupied beds, available beds, beds under maintenance, and occupancy percentage, all updated in real time. An audit log entry is created recording the census change event with the resident name, room number, previous and new counts, timestamp, and the Provider who initiated the discharge. The census dashboard is accessible from the facility management section and includes date-based historical views allowing the Provider to review census trends over time. A notification banner appears on the dashboard when a census change occurs within the current session alerting other Providers to the updated availability.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If a room status changes from "Occupied" to "Available", Then the system must decrement Occupied Beds by 1 and increment Available Beds by 1 in the census report.
   Else if a room status changes from "Occupied" to "Maintenance Required", Then the system must decrement Occupied Beds by 1 and increment Maintenance Beds by 1.

2. When the census report is viewed after an update, Then the system must display the latest bed counts and occupancy percentage.

3. When a census change event occurs, Then the system must create an audit log entry with the resident name, room number, previous counts, new counts, timestamp, and Provider name.

4. If the Provider selects a historical date range, Then the system must display the census data for that period.
   Else if no census changes have occurred today, Then the system must display the current counts with no notification banner.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the census report decrements Occupied Beds by 1 when a room is released during discharge.
2. Verify that the census report increments Available Beds by 1 when a room is released during discharge.
3. Verify that the occupancy percentage recalculates correctly after each census update.
4. Verify that an audit log entry is created with the correct resident name, room number, previous and new counts, timestamp, and Provider name.
5. Verify that the notification banner appears on the census dashboard when a census change occurs in the current session.
6. Verify that historical date-based views display accurate census trends for the selected period.

Negative:
7. Verify that the census report does not change when a room release is cancelled before the confirmation dialog is confirmed.
8. Verify that a census update failure due to a system error leaves the counts consistent and surfaces an error message.
9. Verify that the system prevents the Occupied Beds count from going below zero.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the census dashboard displays total capacity, occupied beds, available beds, maintenance beds, and occupancy percentage clearly.
11. Verify that the notification banner is visually prominent and can be dismissed by the Provider.
12. Verify that historical date range selectors allow easy navigation of census data by month or custom range.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify census accuracy when multiple rooms are released simultaneously by different Providers.
14. Verify census behavior when the last occupied room is released bringing Occupied Beds to zero.

Security:
15. Verify that only authorized Providers can view the census report and the associated audit log entries.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the census report updates within 2 seconds of a room status change.
Verify that the census dashboard loads historical data for a 12-month range within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to archive complete resident record with 7-year retention for compliance.
Description of User Story: The Record Archival screen enables the Provider to initiate the archival of a discharged resident complete record with a mandatory seven-year retention period to comply with healthcare regulatory requirements. The screen displays a summary of all record categories included in the archive: clinical records, medication history, financial records, discharge documentation, care plans, incident reports, and correspondence. Each category shows a document count and total file size to provide transparency about the archive contents. A retention policy section displays the archival start date, calculated expiration date seven years from the discharge date, and the applicable compliance regulation reference. The Provider reviews the archive summary and clicks "Initiate Archive" to begin the archival process, which moves the records to a secure long-term storage system while maintaining an index reference in the active system. A confirmation screen displays the archive reference number and estimated completion time. The archived records remain searchable and retrievable through the archive search interface throughout the retention period.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the resident has records across all categories, Then the system must display all categories with accurate document counts and file sizes.
   Else if a record category has no documents, Then the system must display the category with a zero count and a note indicating no records exist.

2. When the Provider clicks "Initiate Archive", Then the system must move all records to secure long-term storage and generate a unique archive reference number.

3. If the archival process encounters an error, Then the system must display an error message with details and allow the Provider to retry.

4. If the seven-year retention period expires, Then the system must flag the archive for review and potential deletion per compliance policy.

5. If the Provider attempts to archive a resident who is not yet discharged, Then the system must display an error message indicating archival is only available for discharged residents.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the archive summary displays all record categories (clinical, medication, financial, discharge, care plans, incidents, correspondence) with correct document counts and file sizes.
2. Verify that the retention policy section shows the correct archival start date, seven-year expiration date (e.g., 2026-04-01 to 2033-04-01), and regulation reference.
3. Verify that "Initiate Archive" moves records to long-term storage and generates a unique archive reference number.
4. Verify that the confirmation screen displays the archive reference number and estimated completion time.
5. Verify that archived records are searchable and retrievable through the archive search interface within the retention period.
6. Verify that an index reference to the archived record is maintained in the active system after archival.

Negative:
7. Verify that an error message is displayed when archival is attempted for a resident who has not yet been discharged.
8. Verify that an archival process failure due to insufficient storage displays an error with a retry option.
9. Verify that the system prevents duplicate archival of the same resident record.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that each record category is displayed with a clear label, document count badge, and file size indicator.
11. Verify that the retention policy section is formatted with clear date labels and regulation references.
12. Verify that the "Initiate Archive" button is prominently positioned with a confirmation prompt before processing begins.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that archival handles a resident record containing files totaling over 10 GB without timeout or data loss.
14. Verify that the archive is correctly flagged for review when the seven-year retention period is reached.

Security:
15. Verify that only authorized Providers with records management permissions can initiate archival and that archived records maintain encryption at rest.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Record Archival screen loads with category summaries within 3 seconds for residents with extensive records.
Verify that the archival process initiates and returns a confirmation with reference number within 5 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to preserve all historical data including clinical, financial, and administrative records.
Description of User Story: The Historical Data Preservation screen provides the Provider with a comprehensive view and verification interface ensuring all resident data across clinical, financial, and administrative domains is preserved intact following discharge. The screen is organized into three main tabs: Clinical Records showing medical history, care plans, assessments, medication records, lab results, and incident reports; Financial Records showing billing history, payment records, insurance claims, refund transactions, and deposit details; and Administrative Records showing admission documentation, consent forms, correspondence, discharge documents, and staff evaluations. Each tab displays a data integrity checklist with verification status indicators confirming that all records are complete, uncorrupted, and properly linked. A "Verify Preservation" button runs an automated integrity check across all three domains, comparing record counts and checksums against the source system. The verification results are displayed in a summary panel with pass or fail indicators for each record category. A "Generate Preservation Certificate" button creates a signed document certifying the completeness and integrity of the preserved records for compliance and audit purposes.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If all record categories pass the integrity check, Then the system must display green pass indicators for all categories and enable the "Generate Preservation Certificate" button.
   Else if one or more record categories fail the integrity check, Then the system must display red fail indicators on the failing categories and disable the "Generate Preservation Certificate" button.

2. If the Provider clicks on a failing category, Then the system must display detailed error information including missing record identifiers and suggested corrective actions.

3. If the Provider clicks "Generate Preservation Certificate" after all checks pass, Then the system must create a signed PDF document with record counts, checksums, and certification timestamp.

4. If the Provider attempts to access the preservation screen for a non-discharged resident, Then the system must display an error that preservation verification is only available post-discharge.

5. If the integrity check identifies corrupted records, Then the system must flag them for immediate review and notify the system administrator.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the screen displays three tabs (Clinical, Financial, Administrative) with correct record categories and document counts.
2. Verify that the Clinical Records tab lists all clinical data categories (medical history, care plans, assessments, medication records, lab results, incident reports) with accurate counts.
3. Verify that the Financial Records tab lists all financial data categories (billing history, payment records, insurance claims, refund transactions, deposit details) with accurate counts.
4. Verify that the Administrative Records tab lists all administrative data categories (admission docs, consent forms, correspondence, discharge documents, staff evaluations) with accurate counts.
5. Verify that "Verify Preservation" runs the integrity check and displays pass or fail indicators for each record category.
6. Verify that "Generate Preservation Certificate" creates a signed PDF with record counts, checksums, and certification timestamp.

Negative:
7. Verify that failing integrity check categories display red indicators and that "Generate Preservation Certificate" is disabled until all categories pass.
8. Verify that clicking on a failing category displays detailed error information including missing record identifiers and suggested corrective actions.
9. Verify that the screen displays an appropriate error when accessed for a resident who has not yet been discharged.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the three tabs are clearly labeled, switchable, and display the correct content in each.
11. Verify that pass indicators are displayed in green and fail indicators in red with clear iconography.
12. Verify that the summary panel is displayed below the tabs after the integrity check completes with all categories listed.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that the integrity check correctly handles a record category that has zero documents without marking it as a failure.
14. Verify that preservation verification works correctly for a resident with records spanning more than five years of stay.

Security:
15. Verify that only authorized Providers with records preservation permissions can run verification and generate preservation certificates, and that integrity checks validate the encryption status of stored records.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the "Verify Preservation" integrity check completes within 10 seconds for residents with 1000 or more records across all domains.
Verify that "Generate Preservation Certificate" creates the PDF document within 3 seconds after all checks pass.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to create new visit notes from patients' charts.
Description of User Story: The Patient Charting screen displays a resident header bar at the top showing patient demographics, followed by a tabbed interface for clinical data. Within the patient overview, providers can initiate new visit notes directly from the chart by clicking a "Create Visit Note" action button. The visit note creation form opens as a modal or inline panel with fields for visit date, visit type, subjective findings, objective observations, assessment, and plan (SOAP format). Providers can attach relevant clinical data from the chart tabs (vitals, medications, allergies) to the visit note for comprehensive documentation. The completed visit note is saved to the Progress Notes tab and becomes part of the patient permanent medical record. An auto-save mechanism preserves draft notes in case the provider navigates away or loses connectivity.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the provider clicks "Create Visit Note" and a draft visit note already exists for the same date, Then the system must prompt the provider to resume the draft or create a new note.
   Else if the provider attempts to save a visit note with all clinical fields empty, Then the system must display a validation error requiring at least one clinical section to be completed.
   Else if the provider selects a visit date in the future, Then the system must display a warning that future-dated notes may require supervisor approval.
   Else if the provider clicks "Cancel" after entering data into the visit note form, Then the system must prompt a confirmation dialog to discard or save as draft.

2. If the patient chart is locked by another provider, Then the system must allow creating the visit note in read-only chart mode but warn that attached data may not reflect the latest updates.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that a provider can open a patient chart for "John Doe", click "Create Visit Note", fill in all SOAP fields (Subjective: "Headache", Objective: "BP 120/80", Assessment: "Tension headache", Plan: "Rest and ibuprofen"), attach recent vitals, and save successfully; the note appears in the Progress Notes tab with the correct date "2026-04-01" and provider name.
 2. Verify that a provider can resume a previously auto-saved draft visit note for patient "Jane Smith" and complete it; the system saves the final version and removes the draft indicator.
 3. Verify that a provider can create a visit note of type "SOAP" and attach both vitals and active medications; all attached data is accurately reflected in the saved note.

Negative:
4. Verify that a provider attempting to save a visit note with no content entered in any clinical field receives a validation message requiring at least one section.
5. Verify that a provider entering a visit date "2026-03-01" that falls before the patient "John Doe" admission date "2026-03-15" receives a validation error rejecting the date.
6. Verify that a provider trying to create a visit note for a discharged patient is blocked and sees a discharge status warning.

UI Scenarios:
7. Verify that the "Create Visit Note" button is prominently positioned in the patient overview section and is styled as a primary action button.
 8. Verify that the visit note form displays SOAP section headers (Subjective, Objective, Assessment, Plan) as clearly labeled expandable text areas.
9. Verify that the draft indicator badge is visible on the patient chart header when an unsaved draft exists for patient "Jane Smith".

Edge Cases:
10. Verify that a provider can create two visit notes for the same patient "John Doe" on "2026-04-01" with different visit types "SOAP" and "Progress Note"; the system saves both as separate entries.
11. Verify that when a provider loses internet connectivity mid-note, the auto-save preserves the draft locally and syncs when connectivity is restored.

Security:
12. Verify that only providers with "visit_note_create" permission can access the Create Visit Note action; clinical staff without this permission see the button as disabled.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the visit note form opens within 1 second of clicking "Create Visit Note", with all auto-populated fields rendered.
Verify that saving a visit note with attached vitals and medications completes within 2 seconds, and the Progress Notes tab reflects the new entry immediately.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to add, update, or delete flags for patients.
Description of User Story: The patient flag management feature allows providers to assign, modify, and remove visual flag indicators on patient charts to communicate critical information across care team members. Flags appear as colored badges on the resident header bar, providing immediate visual cues about patient conditions such as fall risk, allergy alerts, isolation precautions, or behavioral notes. Providers can add a new flag by selecting from a predefined list of flag types and optionally entering a custom note describing the reason for the flag. Updating a flag allows changing the flag type, severity level, or associated notes without removing the original audit trail. Deleting a flag requires a confirmation step and records the removal in the audit log with the provider name and timestamp. Multiple flags can coexist on a single patient chart, each displayed with its respective color coding and priority ordering.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the provider adds a flag type that already exists on the patient chart for "John Doe", Then the system must warn about the duplicate and offer to update the existing flag instead.

2. If the provider attempts to delete a critical-severity flag, Then the system must require an additional confirmation with a mandatory reason field.
   Else if the flag is non-critical severity, Then the system must display a standard single-step confirmation dialog.

3. If the provider edits a flag and changes its severity from low to critical, Then the system must send a notification to the care team about the escalated flag.

4. If the provider tries to add more than the maximum allowed flags per patient (e.g., 10), Then the system must display a limit warning and suggest reviewing existing flags.

5. If the provider adds a flag with an expiration date, Then the system must automatically remove the flag on the expiration date and log the auto-removal.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that a provider can add a "Fall Risk" flag with high severity and note "History of 2 falls in past month" for patient "John Doe"; the flag appears as a red badge on the patient header.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that a provider can update an existing "Allergy Alert" flag for patient "Jane Smith" from medium to critical severity; the badge color changes and the care team is notified.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that a provider can delete a "Temporary Isolation" flag after the isolation period ends for patient "John Doe"; the flag is removed and the deletion is logged with timestamp "2026-04-01".
4. Verify that 4. Verify that a provider can add three flags (Fall Risk, Allergy, Behavioral) to patient "Jane Smith" and all appear on the header in priority order.

Negative:
5. Verify that 5. Verify that a provider attempting to add a flag without selecting a flag type receives a validation error requiring type selection.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that a provider without supervisor permission trying to delete a flag added by another provider receives an authorization error.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that a provider attempting to add a flag for a discharged patient "John Doe" receives a discharge status error.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that 8. Verify that flag badges on the resident header bar are color-coded (red for critical, orange for high, yellow for medium, blue for low) and display the flag type as a tooltip on hover.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that the "Add Flag" button is positioned near the resident header and opens a dropdown or modal with the predefined flag type list.
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the flag edit form displays current values pre-populated with the existing flag type, severity, and notes for patient "Jane Smith".

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that 11. Verify that a provider adding a flag with an expiration date set to today "2026-04-01" sees the system add the flag and remove it at end of day, logging both events.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that when two providers add different flags ("Fall Risk" and "Allergy Alert") to patient "John Doe" simultaneously, both flags are saved without conflict.

Security:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that only providers with "flag_manage" permission can add, edit, or delete flags; other users see flags in read-only mode on the patient header.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that adding or updating a flag completes within 1 second, and the updated badge appears on the patient header immediately across all connected sessions.
Verify that loading a patient chart with 10 active flags renders all badges in the header within 500 milliseconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to add note for other staff member.
Description of User Story: The staff note feature enables providers to create internal notes directed at specific staff members within the patient chart context. These notes appear in a dedicated staff communication panel accessible from the patient overview, separate from clinical progress notes to maintain clear documentation boundaries. When adding a note, the provider selects one or more recipient staff members from a searchable dropdown, enters the note content, and optionally sets a priority level (Normal, Urgent). The recipient staff members receive a notification badge on their dashboard indicating a new note requires attention. Each note displays the author, timestamp, recipient names, and read/unread status. Notes can be marked as resolved by the recipient to indicate the communicated task or information has been acknowledged.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the provider attempts to send a staff note without selecting a recipient, Then the system must display a validation error requiring at least one recipient.

2. If the provider sends a note and the recipient is currently offline, Then the system must queue the notification and deliver it when the recipient next logs in.

3. If the provider enters a note exceeding the maximum character limit, Then the system must display a character count warning and prevent submission until the note is shortened.

4. If the recipient marks a note as resolved, Then the system must show the sender the resolution timestamp and the resolver name.
   Else if the note is not yet marked resolved, Then the system must show the note as pending in the sender's communication panel.

5. If the selected recipient staff member has been deactivated, Then the system must warn that the recipient is inactive and suggest selecting an alternative.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that a provider can add a staff note for nurse "Sarah Lee" with content "Please check vitals at 2 PM" with Normal priority; the nurse receives a notification and the note appears in the communication panel for patient "John Doe".
2. Verify that 2. Verify that a provider can send an Urgent priority note to two staff members "Sarah Lee" and "Dr. Brown"; both receive highlighted urgent notifications on their dashboards.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that nurse "Sarah Lee" can view a staff note from the provider and mark it as resolved; the provider sees the resolved status with timestamp "2026-04-01 14:30".

Negative:
4. Verify that 4. Verify that a provider attempting to send a staff note with empty content receives a validation error requiring note text.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that a provider attempting to send a note to deactivated staff member "Tom White" receives a warning about the inactive status and is blocked from sending.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that a provider trying to add a staff note for a patient they do not have chart access to is blocked with an authorization error.

UI Scenarios:
7. Verify that 7. Verify that the "Add Staff Note" button is visible in the patient overview panel and opens a form with recipient search dropdown, text area, and priority selector.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that staff notes in the communication panel for patient "John Doe" display sender avatar, name, timestamp, priority badge, and read/unread indicator.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that Urgent notes are visually distinguished with a red priority badge and appear at the top of the notes list.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that when a provider sends a staff note and then their account is deleted, the note persists with the original author name for audit purposes for patient "John Doe".
11. Verify that 11. Verify that when a provider sends the same note content to "Sarah Lee" twice within one minute for patient "John Doe", the system saves both as separate note entries.

Security:
12. Verify that 12. Verify that staff notes are visible only to the sender and designated recipients; other staff members with chart access to patient "John Doe" cannot view notes not addressed to them.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that sending a staff note and delivering the notification to the recipient completes within 2 seconds.
Verify that loading the staff communication panel with 100 historical notes renders within 2 seconds with pagination support.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to send message to patients or providers.
Description of User Story: The messaging feature within the patient chart allows providers to compose and send secure messages to patients or other providers directly from the patient overview context. The message composition form includes a recipient search field that supports selecting patients by name or MRN and providers by name or specialty, a subject line, and a rich-text message body. Messages sent to patients are delivered through the patient portal or secure messaging channel, while provider-to-provider messages appear in the internal messaging inbox. The system maintains a threaded conversation view showing all messages exchanged about a specific patient, enabling continuous communication context. Sent messages are logged in the patient chart communication history for compliance and audit purposes. Read receipts indicate when the recipient has opened the message.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the provider sends a message to a patient who has not activated their patient portal account, Then the system must display a warning that the message may not be received and suggest alternative contact methods.

2. If the provider leaves the subject line empty, Then the system must auto-fill the subject with "Message regarding [Patient Name] - [Date]".
   Else if the subject is provided, Then the system must use the entered subject as-is without modification.

3. If the provider attempts to send a message with an empty body, Then the system must display a validation error requiring message content.

4. If the recipient provider is out-of-office, Then the system must display an out-of-office auto-response and suggest contacting the covering provider.

5. If the message attachment exceeds the maximum file size, Then the system must reject the attachment and display the size limit.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that a provider can send a message to patient "John Doe" through the portal with subject "Follow-up Instructions" and detailed body text; the patient receives the message and the chart logs the communication with date "2026-04-01".
2. Verify that 2. Verify that a provider can send a message to specialist "Dr. Brown" regarding a referral for patient "Jane Smith"; the specialist sees the message in their inbox with the patient context.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that a provider can view the threaded conversation history for patient "John Doe" showing three prior exchanges with timestamps and read receipts.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that a provider can attach a lab report "Lab-2026-04-01.pdf" to a message sent to "Dr. Brown"; the recipient downloads the attachment successfully.

Negative:
5. Verify that 5. Verify that a provider attempting to send a message with no recipient selected receives a validation error requiring at least one recipient.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that a provider sending a message to patient "John Doe" whose portal is deactivated receives a delivery failure warning, and the attempt is logged.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that a provider attempting to attach a file exceeding 25 MB receives a rejection with a file size error.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that 8. Verify that the "Send Message" action opens a compose form with recipient search, subject input, rich-text editor, and attachment button clearly laid out.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that the threaded conversation view for patient "John Doe" displays messages with alternating sender alignment (left for received, right for sent) and timestamps.
10. Verify that 10. Verify that read receipt indicators show a checkmark icon next to sent messages that have been opened by the recipient.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that 11. Verify that a provider can send a message to both patient "John Doe" and provider "Dr. Brown" in the same compose action; the system delivers to both channels appropriately.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that when patient "John Doe" reads a message exactly at midnight "2026-04-01 00:00", the read receipt correctly logs the date.

Security:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that all messages are encrypted in transit and at rest; message access to patient "John Doe" communication history is restricted to the sender, recipient, and authorized administrators for compliance review.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that sending a message with a 10 MB attachment completes delivery within 3 seconds.
Verify that loading a threaded conversation history with 200 messages renders within 2 seconds with pagination.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add Medication of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Add Medication feature allows providers to record new medication entries for a resident through the Medications tab within the patient charting interface. The tabbed interface displays Medications as one of the primary tabs, and clicking the "Add" button opens a medication entry form with fields for medication name (searchable drug database), dosage, frequency, route of administration, start date, and prescribing provider. The form includes an "Add Note" link for supplementary instructions such as special administration requirements or clinical rationale. Upon saving, the medication is added to the resident medication list and becomes visible in the Medications tab table view with all entered details. The system performs drug interaction checks against the resident current medication list and active allergies before finalizing the save, alerting the provider to potential conflicts.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. f the caregiver clicks the Add Medication button, then the Add Medication modal should open displaying the resident's name, allergies, and intolerances at the top.
2.If the modal opens, then all required fields (Medicine Name, Sig, Quantity, Refill, Duration, Start Date, End Date, Diagnosis Code, and Notes) should be visible and fillable.
3.If the caregiver selects a medicine from the dropdown, then the selected medicine should reflect correctly in the Medicine Name field.
4.If the caregiver selects Permanent, then the Duration, Start Date, and End Date fields should not be required.
5.If the caregiver selects Temporary, then the Duration, Start Date, and End Date fields should be required before saving.
6.If the caregiver fills in all required fields and clicks Save, then the medication should be successfully added to the resident's medication list.
7.If the caregiver leaves any required field empty and clicks Save, then a validation error should be displayed for the missing field.
8.If the selected medicine conflicts with the resident's listed allergies or intolerances, then a warning should be displayed before saving.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Add Medication modal opens displaying the resident's name, allergies, and intolerances when the caregiver clicks the Add Medication button.
2. Verify that all required fields (Medicine Name, Sig, Quantity, Refill, Duration, Start Date, End Date, Diagnosis Code, and Notes) are visible and fillable when the modal opens.
3. Verify that the selected medicine reflects correctly in the Medicine Name field when the caregiver selects a medicine from the dropdown.
4. Verify that the medication is successfully added to the resident's medication list when the caregiver fills in all required fields and clicks Save.
5. Verify that Duration, Start Date, and End Date fields are not required when the caregiver selects Permanent.
6. Verify that Duration, Start Date, and End Date fields are required when the caregiver selects Temporary.

Negative:
7. Verify that a validation error is displayed for the missing field when the caregiver leaves any required field empty and clicks Save.
8. Verify that a warning is displayed before saving when the selected medicine conflicts with the resident's listed allergies or intolerances.
9. Verify that a validation error is displayed when the End Date is set before the Start Date.
10. Verify that the modal closes without saving any data when the caregiver clicks Cancel.

UI:
11. Verify that the resident's allergies and intolerances are clearly displayed at the top of the modal when it opens.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit Medication of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Edit Medication feature allows providers to modify existing medication entries in a resident chart through the Medications tab. Clicking on a medication row in the list view or selecting an "Edit" action opens the medication form pre-populated with current values for medication name, dosage, frequency, route, start date, and notes. Providers can update any field including dosage adjustments, frequency changes, or adding discontinuation dates to reflect current treatment plans. The system maintains a version history of all changes, recording the previous values, new values, editing provider, and timestamp for each modification. Drug interaction and allergy checks are re-executed upon saving edits to ensure ongoing safety validation. An "End Date" field allows providers to discontinue a medication by setting a stop date rather than deleting the record entirely.
Acceptance Criteria:
1.If the Provider clicks the Edit button on an existing medication, then the Edit Medication modal should open with all previously saved fields pre-populated.
2.If the modal opens, then all fields (Medicine Name, Sig, Quantity, Refill, Duration, Start Date, End Date, Diagnosis Code, and Notes) should be editable.
3.If the Provider updates any field and clicks Save, then the medication record should be updated with the new details.
4.If the Provider changes the medication type from Permanent to Temporary, then the Duration, Start Date, and End Date fields should become required.
5.If the Provider changes the medication type from Temporary to Permanent, then the Duration, Start Date, and End Date fields should no longer be required.
6.If the updated medicine conflicts with the resident's listed allergies or intolerances, then a warning should be displayed before saving.
7.If the Provider sets the End Date before the Start Date, then a validation error should be displayed.
8.If the Provider leaves any required field empty and clicks Save, then a validation error should be displayed.
9.If the Provider clicks Cancel, then the modal should close without saving any changes
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that a provider can edit a medication dosage from 5mg to 10mg for resident "John Doe", save successfully, and see the updated dosage reflected in the Medications tab list.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that a provider can discontinue "Lisinopril" by setting end date "2026-04-01"; the medication moves to the inactive list with the end date displayed.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that a provider can view version history of an edited medication for resident "John Doe" and see two entries showing the original and updated values with timestamps.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that a provider can edit the frequency of "Lisinopril" from "once daily" to "twice daily" and add a note explaining the reason; both changes are saved.

Negative:
5. Verify that 5. Verify that a provider setting an end date "2026-03-01" before the start date "2026-04-01" for medication "Lisinopril" receives a date validation error.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that a provider attempting to edit a medication on a locked chart for resident "Jane Smith" sees a chart lock message and is blocked.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that a provider modifying a medication dosage that triggers a critical drug interaction for resident "John Doe" is blocked from saving until the interaction is acknowledged.

UI Scenarios:

9. Verify that 9. Verify that version history is accessible via a "History" link on the medication detail view, displayed as a timeline of changes.
10. Verify that 10. Verify that discontinued medications appear in a separate "Inactive" section of the Medications tab with a visual strikethrough or greyed styling.

Performance Scenarios:

Verify that saving an edited medication with drug interaction re-check completes within 2 seconds.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to view list of Medications for a resident.
Description of User Story: The Medications tab within the patient charting tabbed interface presents a comprehensive list view of all medications associated with a resident, organized in a table format. The table displays columns for medication name, dosage, frequency, route of administration, start date, end date, prescribing provider, and status (Active/Inactive/Discontinued). Active medications are displayed by default at the top of the list, with discontinued and inactive medications available through a toggle or filter option. Each medication row is clickable to expand and reveal additional details including administration notes, version history link, and last modified information. The list supports sorting by any column header and includes a total count of active medications displayed above the table. A print-friendly view allows providers to generate a medication summary report for the resident.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the resident has no medications on record, Then the Medications tab must display a "No medications recorded" message with an option to add the first medication.

2. If the resident has more than 20 active medications, Then the system must paginate the list with navigation controls at the bottom.

3. If a medication has an expiring refill (within 7 days), Then the system must display a refill warning icon next to the medication name.
   Else if the refill is not expiring within 7 days, Then the system must display the medication row without any refill warning indicator.

4. If the provider clicks "Print" and the resident has both active and inactive medications, Then the system must include only active medications by default with an option to include inactive.

5. If a medication was recently added (within 24 hours), Then the system must display a "New" badge on the medication row for easy identification.

7. Verify that 7. Verify that a provider clicking "Print" while the medication list for resident "Jane Smith" is still loading sees the system wait for full load before generating the print view.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider, I should be able search and filter medications of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Medications tab includes a search bar and filter options positioned above the medication list table, enabling providers to quickly locate specific medications within a resident chart. The search bar supports real-time text matching against medication names, allowing providers to type partial names and see filtered results instantly. Filter options include status (Active, Discontinued, Inactive), medication category (e.g., Cardiac, Pain Management, Antibiotics), prescribing provider, and date range for start date. Multiple filters can be applied simultaneously, and the active filter count is displayed as a badge on the filter button. Clearing all filters restores the default view showing all active medications. The search and filter state persists during the session so providers can navigate between tabs and return to their filtered medication view.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the search text matches no medications in the list, Then the system must display "No medications found matching your search" with a suggestion to adjust the search criteria.
   Else if the provider applies a date range filter with the end date before the start date, Then the system must display a validation error for the date range.
   Else if the provider searches for a medication using a brand name, Then the system must also match against generic name equivalents.

2. If all filters result in zero medications, Then the system must show an empty state with the applied filter summary and a "Clear Filters" action.
   Else if the provider types fewer than two characters in the search bar, Then the system must wait until at least two characters are entered before filtering.

3. Verify that 3. Verify that a provider applying a date range filter for medications started between "2026-03-01" and "2026-04-01" sees only medications started in that period for resident "John Doe".
4. Verify that 4. Verify that a provider clearing all filters sees the full active medication list for resident "Jane Smith" restored.

7. Verify that 7. Verify that a provider applying filters that result in zero matching medications for resident "Jane Smith" sees an empty state with the applied filter summary.

9. Verify that 9. Verify that filter options are accessible via a "Filter" button that opens a dropdown panel with status, category, provider, and date range options.
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the active filter count badge appears on the filter button (e.g., a circled "2") when two filters are applied for resident "John Doe".

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider , I should be able to add Vitals of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Add Vitals feature is accessible from the Vitals tab within the patient charting tabbed interface. Clicking the "Add" button opens the Add Vitals modal titled "Add Vitals - [Resident Name]" which provides structured fields for Date, Time, Next Due, and Added By at the top, auto-populated with current values. Below, the modal lists individual vital measurement fields: Temperature (°F), Blood Pressure (systolic/diastolic mmHg), Heart Rate (bpm), Respiration Rate (bpm), Oxygen Saturation (%), Weight (kg), and Height (ft/in), each accompanied by an "Add Note" link for supplementary observations. The modal footer contains "Cancel" and "Save" buttons where Cancel discards entries and Save validates and persists the vitals record to the resident profile. Auto-populated fields reduce manual entry and ensure accurate audit trails for clinical documentation.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the provider enters a Temperature value outside the clinically valid range (e.g., below 85°F or above 110°F), Then the system must highlight the field in red and display a warning about the abnormal value.

2. If the provider leaves all vital fields empty and clicks "Save", Then the system must display a validation error requiring at least one vital measurement to be recorded.

3. If the Blood Pressure systolic value is lower than the diastolic value, Then the system must flag the entry as clinically implausible and require correction before saving.

4. If the provider clicks "Cancel" after entering partial data, Then the system must prompt a confirmation dialog asking if they want to discard the unsaved entries.
   Else if no data has been entered, Then the system must close the modal immediately without a confirmation prompt.

5. If the "Next Due" field is left blank, Then the system must default to the facility-configured vitals schedule interval.

6. If the provider enters a Heart Rate value of zero, Then the system must reject the value with a validation error indicating it is outside the acceptable range.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that a provider can open the Add Vitals modal for resident "John Doe", enter Temperature 98.6°F, Blood Pressure 120/80 mmHg, Heart Rate 72 bpm, and click Save; all values are stored correctly and appear in the Vitals tab list.
2. Verify that a provider can record Oxygen Saturation as 97% for resident "Jane Smith" and add a note "Measured at rest" using the Add Note link; the note is stored alongside the vital reading.
3. Verify that a provider can enter only Weight 70kg and Height 5ft 8in for resident "John Doe", click Save, and the system accepts the partial vitals record.
4. Verify that a provider opening the Add Vitals modal for resident "Jane Smith" on "2026-04-01" sees Date, Time, and Added By fields auto-populated correctly with current values.

Negative:
5. Verify that a provider entering a Heart Rate of 0 bpm for resident "John Doe" and clicking Save receives a validation error.
6. Verify that a provider entering non-numeric characters "abc" in the Temperature field for resident "Jane Smith" is prevented from proceeding with a format validation error.
7. Verify that a provider entering Blood Pressure as 80/130 (systolic lower than diastolic) for resident "John Doe" sees the entry flagged and is blocked from saving.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the Add Vitals modal displays the title "Add Vitals - John Doe" with Date, Time, Next Due, and Added By fields at the top, and seven vital measurement fields below with correct units and "Add Note" links.
9. Verify that the "Cancel" and "Save" buttons are positioned in the modal footer, with Save as the primary action button.
10. Verify that each vital field displays the appropriate unit label (°F, mmHg, bpm, %, kg, ft/in) next to the input field.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a provider recording vitals for resident "John Doe" exactly at midnight "2026-04-01 00:00" sees the system correctly assign the record to the correct calendar date.
12. Verify that a provider entering a Temperature of 98.6666°F for resident "Jane Smith" sees the system round to the configured precision.

Security:
13. Verify that only providers with "vitals_record" permission can access the Add Vitals modal; unauthorized roles see the vitals history in read-only mode without the add action.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Add Vitals modal opens within 1 second after clicking the Add button, with all fields and auto-populated values rendered.
Verify that saving a complete set of seven vitals with notes completes within 2 seconds, and the updated vitals list reflects the new record immediately.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider , I should be able to edit Vitals of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Edit Vitals feature allows providers to modify previously recorded vital signs for a resident through the Vitals tab in the patient charting interface. Clicking an "Edit" action on a vitals record row opens the edit modal pre-populated with the existing values for all vital fields including Temperature, Blood Pressure, Heart Rate, Respiration Rate, Oxygen Saturation, Weight, and Height. Providers can update individual vital values, modify associated notes, or correct data entry errors while the system preserves the original record in a version history. The edit form retains the same layout as the Add Vitals modal with Date, Time, and Added By fields showing the original recording details and a "Modified By" field capturing the editing provider. Clinical range validation is re-executed upon saving to ensure updated values remain within acceptable parameters. A mandatory reason field requires providers to document why the vital record is being modified.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the provider attempts to save the edit without entering a modification reason, Then the system must display a validation error requiring the reason field.

2. If the provider edits a vital value to a clinically implausible number (e.g., Heart Rate 500 bpm), Then the system must reject the value with a range validation error.

3. If another provider is simultaneously editing the same vitals record, Then the system must display a concurrent edit warning and prevent data overwriting.

4. If the provider reverts all values to the original without any changes and clicks "Save", Then the system must detect no modifications and must not create a new version history entry.

5. If the vitals record being edited is more than 30 days old, Then the system must require supervisor approval for the modification.
   Else if the vitals record is 30 days old or less, Then the system must allow the edit with only the standard mandatory reason field.
Test Scenarios:

3. Verify that 3. Verify that a provider can view version history for resident "John Doe" and see the original entry by "Nurse A" and the modification by "Dr. B" with timestamps "2026-04-01".

7. Verify that 7. Verify that a provider trying to edit a vitals record for resident "John Doe" that is locked due to billing finalization is blocked with a record lock message.

Verify that saving an edited vitals record with clinical re-validation completes within 2 seconds.

Records are sorted by date and time in descending order by default, with the most recent vitals appearing at the top. Each row is expandable to reveal full details including notes, Next Due information, and a link to version history if the record has been modified.
