Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider , I should be able to add Allergies of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Add Allergy feature is accessible from the Allergies tab within the patient charting tabbed interface. Clicking the "Add" button opens the Add Allergy screen with fields for allergen name (searchable database lookup), reaction type (e.g., Rash, Anaphylaxis, Nausea, Swelling), severity level (Mild, Moderate, Severe, Life-Threatening), onset date, and status (Active/Inactive). The allergen search field supports lookup against a standardized allergen database including medications, foods, and environmental substances. A notes text area allows providers to document additional details about the allergic reaction history or specific triggers. Upon saving, the allergy is added to the resident allergy list and triggers cross-referencing against the active medication list to flag potential contraindications. The allergy record is prominently displayed across all chart tabs as a safety alert banner.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the provider searches for an allergen not found in the database, Then the system must allow manual entry of the allergen name with a "Custom Entry" flag.

2. If the provider attempts to save the allergy form without selecting an allergen name, Then the system must display a validation error requiring the allergen field.

3. If the provider adds an allergy that matches a currently prescribed medication, Then the system must display a critical medication contraindication alert requiring acknowledgment.

4. If the provider selects severity as "Life-Threatening", Then the system must flag the allergy with a red banner across all chart tabs and notify the care team.
   Else if the provider selects severity as "Severe", Then the system must flag the allergy with an orange banner and display a care team alert.
   Else if the provider selects severity as "Moderate" or "Mild", Then the system must display the allergy in the list without a full-page banner but with appropriate color coding.

5. If the provider adds a duplicate allergen that already exists in the allergy list, Then the system must warn about the duplicate and offer to update the existing record.

6. If the provider leaves severity unselected, Then the system must default severity to "Moderate" and allow saving with a reminder to confirm the severity level.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that a provider can open the Add Allergy form for resident "John Doe", search for "Penicillin", select "Rash" as reaction type and "Moderate" severity, and save; the allergy appears in the Allergies tab list and a safety banner is displayed.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that a provider can add a "Peanut" food allergy with "Anaphylaxis" reaction and "Life-Threatening" severity for resident "Jane Smith"; the allergy is flagged with a red banner and the care team is notified.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that a provider adding an allergy for resident "John Doe" sees the system detect a contraindication with an active medication and the alert displays the conflicting medication details.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that a provider can add an allergy for resident "Jane Smith" with notes "Patient experienced hives within 30 minutes of exposure"; the notes are saved and visible in the allergy details.

Negative:
5. Verify that 5. Verify that a provider attempting to save the allergy form for resident "John Doe" without entering an allergen name receives a validation error.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that a provider trying to add "Penicillin" allergy for resident "Jane Smith" when it already exists in the allergy list receives a duplicate warning.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that a provider entering a non-existent allergen with special characters for resident "John Doe" sees the system allow custom entry but sanitize the input.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that 8. Verify that the Add Allergy form displays fields for allergen (with search), reaction type dropdown, severity dropdown, onset date picker, status toggle, and notes text area in a structured layout.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that the allergen search field for resident "Jane Smith" shows a dropdown of matching results as the provider types, with allergen category labels (Medication, Food, Environmental).
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the safety alert banner appears at the top of all chart tabs for resident "John Doe" in a prominent red or orange bar with the allergy name and severity.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that 11. Verify that a provider adding an allergy for resident "Jane Smith" with onset date "2016-04-01" (10 years ago) sees the system accept the entry and record it with the historical date.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that a provider adding 15 allergies for resident "John Doe" sees all displayed in the allergy list and the safety banner shows a scrollable or summarized view.

Security:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that only providers with "allergy_manage" permission can add allergy records; other clinical staff see the Allergies tab in read-only mode.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the allergen database search returns matching results within 1 second of typing three or more characters.
Verify that medication contraindication cross-reference check completes within 2 seconds after saving the allergy record.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider , I should be able to edit Allergies of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Edit Allergy feature allows providers to modify existing allergy records for a resident through the Allergies tab in the patient charting interface. Clicking "Edit" on an allergy record row opens the edit form pre-populated with the current allergen name, reaction type, severity level, onset date, status, and notes. Providers can update any field including changing the reaction type if new symptoms are identified, adjusting severity based on clinical reassessment, or marking the allergy as inactive if it has been resolved or disproven. The system maintains a version history of all allergy modifications, capturing the previous and updated values along with the editing provider and timestamp. Upon saving changes, the medication contraindication cross-reference is re-executed to ensure ongoing safety checks reflect the updated allergy information. A mandatory reason field documents the clinical justification for modifying the allergy record.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the provider attempts to save the edit without entering a modification reason, Then the system must display a validation error requiring the reason field.

2. If the provider changes the allergen name to a different substance, Then the system must treat it as a new allergy and prompt to deactivate the original record.

3. If the provider marks a "Life-Threatening" allergy as inactive, Then the system must require supervisor approval before the status change is finalized.
   Else if the provider marks a non-life-threatening allergy as inactive, Then the system must allow the status change with only the standard mandatory reason field.

4. If another provider is simultaneously editing the same allergy record, Then the system must display a concurrent edit warning.

5. If the provider changes the severity to "Life-Threatening" from a lower level, Then the system must update the safety banner to red and notify the care team.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that a provider can edit a Penicillin allergy reaction type from "Rash" to "Anaphylaxis" for resident "John Doe" with reason "Updated based on recent exposure incident"; the change is saved and version history is updated.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that a provider can mark a food allergy as inactive for resident "Jane Smith" with reason "Allergy disproven by testing" on "2026-04-01"; the allergy moves to inactive and the safety banner is updated.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that a provider can view version history for resident "John Doe" and see two entries showing the original record and the modification with details.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that a provider changing severity from Moderate to Severe for resident "Jane Smith" sees the medication contraindication check re-run and the safety banner update accordingly.

Negative:
5. Verify that 5. Verify that a provider attempting to save an edit for resident "John Doe" without entering a modification reason receives a validation error.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that a provider trying to edit an allergy record for resident "Jane Smith" while the chart is locked by another provider is blocked with a chart lock message.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that a provider changing a Life-Threatening allergy to inactive for resident "John Doe" without supervisor approval is blocked by the system.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that 8. Verify that the edit form for resident "Jane Smith" mirrors the Add Allergy layout with all fields pre-populated, plus a "Modified By" field and a "Reason for Modification" text area.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that the version history is accessible via a "History" link for resident "John Doe" showing a timeline of changes with diff highlights.
10. Verify that 10. Verify that when status is changed to "Inactive" for resident "Jane Smith", the allergy row visually changes to a greyed-out style in the list.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that 11. Verify that a provider editing an allergy record for resident "John Doe" that was added less than one minute ago sees the system save the edit and create a separate version history entry.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that a provider reverting all fields to original values for resident "Jane Smith" and clicking Save sees the system detect no changes and not create a new version history entry.

Security:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that only the original recording provider or a provider with "allergy_edit" permission can modify allergy records; others see the edit action as disabled.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that opening the allergy edit form with pre-populated values loads within 1 second.
Verify that saving an edited allergy with medication contraindication re-check completes within 2 seconds.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider , I should be able to view list of Allergies for a resident.
Description of User Story: The Allergies tab within the patient charting tabbed interface presents a comprehensive list of all allergy records for a resident in a table format. The table displays columns for allergen name, reaction type, severity level, onset date, status (Active/Inactive), recorded by provider, and a notes indicator icon. Active allergies are shown at the top by default with a clear visual distinction from inactive records which appear in a separate section below. Each allergy row is expandable to reveal full details including notes, modification history link, and last updated information. A summary count of active allergies is displayed above the table, and a prominent safety alert banner at the top of the chart reflects all active allergies for clinical awareness. The list supports sorting by any column and includes a print or export option for generating allergy summary reports.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the resident has no allergy records on file, Then the Allergies tab must display "No allergies recorded" with an option to add the first allergy and a reminder to confirm "No Known Allergies" status.

2. If the resident has active life-threatening allergies, Then those records must be highlighted in red at the very top of the list with a critical icon.
   Else if the resident has active severe allergies but no life-threatening ones, Then those records must be highlighted in orange at the top of the active section.

3. If the allergy list exceeds 30 records, Then the system must paginate the list with navigation controls.

4. If an allergy record has been modified, Then a "Modified" indicator icon must appear on the row.
   Else if the allergy record has not been modified, Then the row must display without a "Modified" indicator icon.

5. If the provider clicks "Print" and both active and inactive allergies exist, Then the print view must include active allergies by default with an option to include inactive.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that a provider opening the Allergies tab for resident "John Doe" sees 5 active allergies listed with all columns (allergen, reaction, severity, onset date, status, provider) correctly populated.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that a provider sees active allergies for resident "Jane Smith" at the top and 2 inactive allergies in a separate section below with "Inactive" status labels.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that a provider expanding a Penicillin allergy row for resident "John Doe" sees the notes "Causes hives within 1 hour" and a link to version history.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that a provider sorting the list by severity for resident "Jane Smith" sees Life-Threatening allergies appear at the top.

Negative:
5. Verify that 5. Verify that a provider opening the Allergies tab for newly admitted resident "John Doe" with no records sees the "No allergies recorded" message with a prompt to confirm NKA status.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that a provider attempting to access the Allergies tab for patient "Jane Smith" they are not authorized to view sees an access denied message.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that a provider clicking "Export" for resident "John Doe" while the allergy list is still loading sees the system complete the load before generating the export.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that 8. Verify that the Allergies tab table for resident "John Doe" has color-coded severity indicators (red for Life-Threatening, orange for Severe, yellow for Moderate, green for Mild) in each row.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that the active allergy count for resident "Jane Smith" is displayed above the table header (e.g., "5 Active Allergies") and the safety alert banner is visible at the top.
10. Verify that 10. Verify that inactive allergies for resident "John Doe" are visually differentiated with greyed-out text or strikethrough styling.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that 11. Verify that resident "Jane Smith" with 40 allergy records across active and inactive statuses sees the table paginate correctly and the summary count accurately reflect only active allergies.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that when two providers add different allergies "Penicillin" and "Sulfa" to resident "John Doe" simultaneously, both records appear in the list upon page refresh.

Security:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that the Allergies tab is viewable by all authorized clinical staff; add and edit actions are restricted based on role-specific permissions.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Allergies tab list loads and renders within 2 seconds for a resident with up to 50 allergy records.
Verify that sorting by any column completes within 500 milliseconds for an allergy list of up to 50 entries.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider, I should be able search and filter Allergies of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Allergies tab includes a search bar and filter options positioned above the allergy list table, enabling providers to quickly locate specific allergy records within a resident chart. The search bar supports real-time text matching against allergen names and notes content, allowing providers to type partial names and see filtered results instantly. Filter options include status (Active, Inactive), severity level (Mild, Moderate, Severe, Life-Threatening), reaction type, allergen category (Medication, Food, Environmental), and date range for onset date. Multiple filters can be applied simultaneously and the active filter count is displayed as a badge on the filter button. Clearing all filters restores the default view showing all active allergies at the top. The search and filter state persists during the session when navigating between chart tabs.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the search text matches no allergy records, Then the system must display "No allergies found matching your search" with a suggestion to adjust the criteria.
   Else if the provider applies a date range filter with end date before start date, Then the system must display a validation error.
   Else if the provider searches by allergen name, Then the system must also match against generic and brand name equivalents for medication allergens.

2. If all applied filters result in zero matching records, Then the system must show an empty state with the active filter summary and a "Clear Filters" action.
   Else if the provider types fewer than two characters in the search bar, Then the system must wait until at least two characters before filtering.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that a provider typing "Peni" in the search bar for resident "John Doe" sees the list filter to show "Penicillin" and "Penicillin V" as matching allergy records.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that a provider filtering by severity "Severe" and category "Food" for resident "Jane Smith" sees only severe food allergies displayed.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that a provider applying a date range filter for allergies with onset between "2025-04-01" and "2026-04-01" for resident "John Doe" sees only recently onset allergies shown.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that a provider clearing all filters for resident "Jane Smith" sees the full active allergy list restored.

Negative:
5. Verify that 5. Verify that a provider searching for "XYZ" which matches no allergies for resident "John Doe" sees the "No allergies found" message.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that a provider setting a date range filter with end date "2026-03-01" before start date "2026-04-01" for resident "Jane Smith" receives a date validation error.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that a provider applying filters that result in zero records for resident "John Doe" sees an empty state with the applied filter summary.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that 8. Verify that the search bar is positioned above the allergy table with a magnifying glass icon and placeholder text "Search allergies...".
9. Verify that 9. Verify that filter options are accessible via a "Filter" button that opens a dropdown panel with status, severity, reaction type, category, and date range options.
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the active filter count badge on the filter button for resident "Jane Smith" updates dynamically as filters are added or removed.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that 11. Verify that a provider searching with leading and trailing whitespace "  Penicillin  " for resident "John Doe" sees the system trim the input and perform the search on the cleaned text.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that a provider applying a severity filter "Severe" and a search query "Peni" simultaneously for resident "Jane Smith" sees results reflecting the intersection of both criteria.

Security:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that search and filter functionality respects chart access permissions; providers without access to a resident chart cannot search their allergy records.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that real-time search filtering returns results within 300 milliseconds of the provider pausing typing for a list of up to 50 allergy records.
Verify that applying or removing filters updates the allergy list within 500 milliseconds.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider , I should be able to add Progress Notes of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Patient Charting screen provides a Progress Notes tab where the Provider can add new progress notes for a resident. The tab displays a list of existing progress notes with an "Add" button prominently placed at the top right of the panel. Clicking the "Add" button opens a form or modal with fields for note date, note type or category, subject line, and a rich text body area for entering the clinical narrative. The Provider can attach relevant tags or link the note to a specific encounter or visit as part of the charting workflow. Upon completing all required fields and clicking "Save", the progress note is persisted to the resident chart and the new entry appears at the top of the progress notes list with the creation timestamp and author name.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens the Progress Notes tab and clicks the "Add" button, Then the system must display a new progress note form with all required fields empty and ready for input.

2. If the Provider completes all required fields (date, note type, subject, and body) and clicks Save, Then the system must persist the progress note to the resident chart and display a success notification.
   Else if any required field is left empty or contains invalid data, Then the system must display inline validation errors on the incomplete fields and block saving.

3. If the Provider clicks Save with valid data, Then the system must add the new progress note to the top of the progress notes list with the correct creation timestamp and author name.
   Else if the system encounters an error while saving, Then the system must retain the entered data and display an error message with a retry option.

4. If the Provider clicks Cancel while entering a note, Then the system must discard all unsaved data and close the form, returning to the progress notes list.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking the "Add" button on the Progress Notes tab opens a new progress note form with fields for date, note type, subject, and body.
2. Verify that entering all required fields (date, note type "Clinical Update", subject "Follow-up Visit", body text) and clicking "Save" successfully saves the progress note and displays a success message.
3. Verify that the newly added progress note appears at the top of the progress notes list with the correct date, subject, and author name.
4. Verify that the Provider can select different note types (e.g., "Clinical Update", "Discharge Summary") from the note type dropdown.
5. Verify that the Provider can enter and format rich text content in the note body field.

Negative:
6. Verify that clicking "Save" without entering the note date displays a required field validation error.
7. Verify that clicking "Save" without entering a subject displays a required field validation error.
8. Verify that clicking "Save" with an empty note body displays a required field validation error.
9. Verify that selecting a future date (e.g., tomorrow's date) for the note date displays a date validation error.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the "Add" button is prominently displayed at the top right of the Progress Notes tab.
11. Verify that the progress note form displays clearly labeled fields with appropriate placeholders.
12. Verify that the "Save" and "Cancel" buttons are properly positioned at the bottom of the form.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that entering the maximum allowed character count (e.g., 5000 characters) in the note body field saves correctly without truncation.
14. Verify that attempting to add a progress note while another session has the same resident chart open is handled without data loss.

Security:
15. Verify that only authenticated Providers with charting permissions can add progress notes to a resident chart.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the progress note form loads within 2 seconds after clicking the "Add" button.
Verify saving a new progress note completes and the list refreshes within 3 seconds.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider , I should be able to edit Progress Notes of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Patient Charting screen allows the Provider to edit existing progress notes for a resident from the Progress Notes tab. When the Provider selects a progress note from the list and clicks the "Edit" action, the form opens pre-populated with the existing note date, note type, subject, and body content. All fields become editable, allowing the Provider to modify any value such as correcting the clinical narrative or updating the note type. The system tracks the modification by recording the original values, updated values, the editing Provider identity, and the timestamp of the change. Upon clicking "Save Changes", validation runs on all fields before persisting the update, and the progress notes list reflects the edited entry with an "Edited" indicator. The Provider can also click "Cancel" to discard changes and return to the list view.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider clicks "Edit" on an existing progress note, Then the system must open the form pre-populated with the current note date, type, subject, and body, with all fields enabled for editing.

2. If the Provider modifies any field and all fields pass validation, Then the system must enable the "Save Changes" button.
   Else if any field fails validation after modification, Then the system must display inline validation errors and disable the "Save Changes" button.

3. If the Provider clicks "Save Changes" with valid data, Then the system must persist the updated note, record an audit trail entry with original values, updated values, Provider identity, and timestamp, and display a success notification.
   Else if the system fails to save the update, Then the system must retain the edited data and display an error message with a retry option.

4. If the Provider clicks "Cancel" after making edits, Then the system must discard all unsaved changes and close the edit form, returning to the progress notes list.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking "Edit" on a progress note opens the form pre-populated with the existing note date, type, subject, and body.
2. Verify that modifying the note body (e.g., correcting a clinical narrative) and clicking "Save Changes" updates the progress note successfully.
3. Verify that the edited note displays with an "Edited" indicator in the progress notes list.
4. Verify that the audit trail records the original values, updated values, Provider identity, and modification timestamp after a successful edit.
5. Verify that the Provider can change the note type (e.g., from "Clinical Update" to "Discharge Summary") from the dropdown during editing.

Negative:
6. Verify that clearing the subject field and attempting to save displays a required field validation error.
7. Verify that clearing the note body and attempting to save displays a required field validation error.
8. Verify that changing the note date to an invalid format (e.g., "13/32/2025") displays a date validation error.
9. Verify that clicking "Save Changes" with no actual modifications displays an informational message indicating no changes were detected.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the form displays "Save Changes" and "Cancel" buttons when in edit mode.
11. Verify that pre-populated fields display the current saved values accurately when the edit form opens.
12. Verify that the "Edited" indicator is visually distinct from original note entries in the list.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that when two Providers attempt to edit the same progress note simultaneously, the concurrency conflict is handled gracefully without data loss.
14. Verify that a note created on a date prior to the system date boundary can be edited without errors.

Security:
15. Verify that only the original author or a Provider with edit permissions can modify a progress note.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the edit form loads with pre-populated progress note data within 2 seconds.
Verify saving edited progress note and generating the audit trail entry completes within 3 seconds.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider , I should be able to view list of Progress Notes for a resident.
Description of User Story: The Patient Charting screen includes a Progress Notes tab that displays a comprehensive list of all progress notes recorded for a resident. The list is presented in a table or card layout showing key columns such as note date, note type, subject, author name, and creation or last modified timestamp. Notes are sorted by date in descending order so the most recent entries appear at the top of the list. Each row or card provides a quick preview of the note content and action buttons for editing or viewing the full note detail. The list supports pagination or infinite scroll for residents with a large volume of progress notes. A count indicator at the top shows the total number of progress notes available for the resident.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider selects the Progress Notes tab, Then the system must load and display all progress notes for the selected resident in a table or card layout, sorted by date in descending order.

2. If the resident has no progress notes, Then the system must display an empty state message indicating no progress notes have been recorded.
   Else if the number of progress notes exceeds the page size, Then the system must enable pagination or infinite scroll to load additional entries.

3. If the Provider clicks on a note entry, Then the system must expand or navigate to the full note detail view, including the complete note body.

4. If the progress notes list fails to load due to a system error, Then the system must display an error message with a retry option.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that selecting the Progress Notes tab displays a list of all progress notes for the resident.
2. Verify that each progress note entry shows the note date, note type, subject, author name, and timestamp.
3. Verify that progress notes are sorted by date in descending order with the most recent note at the top.
4. Verify that clicking on a note entry (e.g., a note dated 2025-03-15) displays the full note detail including the complete body.
5. Verify that the count indicator displays the correct total number of progress notes for the resident.

Negative:
6. Verify that navigating to the Progress Notes tab for a resident with no notes displays an appropriate empty state message.
7. Verify that when the progress notes API returns an error, an error message with a retry option is displayed.
8. Verify that accessing the Progress Notes tab for a non-existent resident ID displays an appropriate error message.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the progress notes list uses a consistent table or card layout with clearly labeled columns.
10. Verify that pagination controls or infinite scroll are visible and functional when the note count exceeds the page limit.
11. Verify that the Progress Notes tab is highlighted or active when selected among other charting tabs.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that when the resident has exactly one progress note, the list displays it correctly without pagination controls.
13. Verify that when the resident has hundreds of progress notes (e.g., 500+), the list handles the large dataset without UI lag.

Security:
14. Verify that only authenticated Providers can view the progress notes list for a resident.
15. Verify that a Provider cannot view progress notes for a resident outside their authorized facility.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the progress notes list loads and displays the first page of entries within 2 seconds.
Verify pagination or scroll loading of additional progress notes completes within 2 seconds per page.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider, I should be able search and filter Progress Notes of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Patient Charting screen provides search and filter capabilities on the Progress Notes tab to help the Provider quickly locate specific notes within a resident chart. A search bar at the top of the progress notes list allows the Provider to search by keywords that match against the note subject, body content, or author name. Adjacent to the search bar, filter options are available for note type, date range, and author, enabling the Provider to narrow down the displayed notes based on specific criteria. The search and filter results update the list dynamically, showing only matching progress notes with the search term highlighted in the results. A clear filters button resets all applied filters and restores the full progress notes list. The total count indicator updates to reflect the number of filtered results.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider enters a keyword (e.g., "hypertension") in the search bar, Then the system must filter the progress notes list to display only entries matching the keyword in the subject, body, or author name, with the keyword highlighted in results.

2. If the Provider selects a note type from the filter dropdown (e.g., "Clinical Update"), Then the system must display only progress notes matching the selected type.
   Else if the Provider selects a date range using the date filter, Then the system must display only progress notes within the specified start and end dates.

3. If the Provider applies multiple filters simultaneously, Then the system must display only progress notes matching all applied filter criteria.
   Else if no progress notes match the search or filter criteria, Then the system must display a "No results found" message.

4. If the Provider clicks the clear filters button, Then the system must remove all applied filters and restore the complete progress notes list with the full count.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that entering a keyword (e.g., "hypertension") in the search bar filters the progress notes list to show matching entries.
2. Verify that the search keyword is highlighted in the matching results.
3. Verify that selecting a note type filter (e.g., "Clinical Update") displays only notes of that type.
4. Verify that selecting a date range filter (e.g., 2025-01-01 to 2025-03-31) displays only notes within that range.
5. Verify that clicking the clear filters button resets all filters and restores the full list.
6. Verify that the count indicator updates to reflect the number of filtered results.

Negative:
7. Verify that searching with a keyword that matches no notes (e.g., "xyzunknown") displays a "No results found" message.
8. Verify that selecting a date range where the start date is after the end date displays a validation error.
9. Verify that entering only special characters (e.g., "###") in the search bar returns no results or handles input gracefully.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the search bar and filter options are prominently displayed at the top of the Progress Notes tab.
11. Verify that filter dropdowns display available options correctly and are easy to interact with.
12. Verify that the clear filters button is visible when any filter is active.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that applying a filter that matches exactly one progress note displays that single note correctly.
14. Verify that search behavior with a very long keyword string (e.g., 500+ characters) handles the input without crashing.

Security:
15. Verify that search and filter operations do not expose progress notes from other residents in the results.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify search results are displayed within 2 seconds after the Provider enters a keyword.
Verify applying a date range filter returns filtered results within 2 seconds.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider , I should be able to add Activities of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Patient Charting screen provides an Activities tab where the Provider can add new activities for a resident. The tab displays a list of existing activities with an "Add" button at the top right of the panel. Clicking the "Add" button opens a modal dialog presenting available services as checkboxes, allowing the Provider to select one or more activity types for the resident. Each service checkbox displays the activity name and its associated cost, and a cost summary section at the bottom of the modal calculates the total cost of all selected services in real time. The Provider can also specify the activity date, add optional notes, and confirm the selection by clicking "Save". Upon successful submission, the selected activities are recorded in the resident chart and appear in the activities list with the date, service names, and total cost.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider clicks the "Add" button on the Activities tab, Then the system must open the Add Activities modal with available service checkboxes, each displaying the activity name and associated cost, and a cost summary section.

2. If the Provider selects one or more service checkboxes, Then the system must update the cost summary in real time to display the cumulative total cost of all selected services.
   Else if the Provider deselects a previously selected service, Then the system must recalculate and update the cost summary to reflect the reduced total.

3. If the Provider clicks Save with at least one service selected and a valid activity date, Then the system must save the activities to the resident chart and display a success notification.
   Else if the Provider clicks Save without selecting any services, Then the system must display a validation message requiring at least one service selection.
   Else if the Provider clicks Save without entering an activity date, Then the system must display a required field validation error for the date field.

4. If the Provider clicks Cancel or closes the modal, Then the system must discard all selections and close the modal without saving.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking the "Add" button opens a modal with available services displayed as checkboxes with names and costs.
2. Verify that selecting one service checkbox (e.g., "Physical Therapy - $50") updates the cost summary to show $50.
3. Verify that selecting multiple service checkboxes (e.g., "Physical Therapy - $50" and "Occupational Therapy - $75") updates the cost summary to show the cumulative total ($125).
4. Verify that deselecting a service recalculates and reduces the cost summary accordingly.
5. Verify that entering an activity date (e.g., 2025-04-01), selecting services, and clicking "Save" successfully saves the activities to the resident chart.
6. Verify that the newly added activities appear in the activities list with the correct date, service names, and total cost.

Negative:
7. Verify that clicking "Save" without selecting any service displays a validation message requiring at least one service.
8. Verify that clicking "Save" without entering an activity date displays a required field validation error.
9. Verify that selecting a future date beyond the allowed range for the activity date displays a validation error.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the modal displays service checkboxes in an organized layout with clear labels for name and cost.
11. Verify that the cost summary section is prominently displayed at the bottom of the modal and updates in real time.
12. Verify that the "Save" and "Cancel" buttons are clearly visible within the modal footer.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that selecting all available services results in the cost summary reflecting the correct cumulative total.
14. Verify that when only one service is available and the Provider selects it, the modal and cost summary behave correctly.

Security:
15. Verify that only authenticated Providers with charting permissions can add activities to a resident chart.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the Add Activities modal loads with all available services within 2 seconds after clicking "Add".
Verify saving selected activities completes and the activities list refreshes within 3 seconds.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider , I should be able to edit Activities of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Patient Charting screen allows the Provider to edit previously recorded activities for a resident from the Activities tab. When the Provider selects an existing activity entry and clicks the "Edit" action, the modal opens pre-populated with the previously selected service checkboxes, activity date, notes, and the cost summary reflecting the original selections. The Provider can modify the selections by checking or unchecking service checkboxes, changing the activity date, or updating the notes. The cost summary recalculates dynamically as the Provider modifies service selections. Upon clicking "Save Changes", the system validates all fields and persists the updated activity record. An audit trail is maintained that captures the original and updated values along with the editing Provider identity and modification timestamp.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider clicks "Edit" on an existing activity, Then the system must open the modal pre-populated with the current service selections, activity date, notes, and cost summary, with all fields enabled for editing.

2. If the Provider modifies service checkboxes, Then the system must recalculate the cost summary in real time to reflect the updated total.
   Else if the Provider modifies the activity date or notes, Then the system must update the respective fields in the modal.

3. If at least one service is selected and all required fields are valid, Then the system must enable the "Save Changes" button.
   Else if no services are selected, Then the system must display a validation message and disable the "Save Changes" button.

4. If the Provider clicks "Save Changes" with valid data, Then the system must persist the updated activity record, create an audit trail entry with original values, updated values, Provider identity, and timestamp, and display a success notification.

5. If the Provider clicks "Cancel", Then the system must discard all unsaved changes and close the modal.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking "Edit" on an activity entry opens the modal pre-populated with existing service selections, activity date, notes, and cost summary.
2. Verify that checking an additional service checkbox (e.g., adding "Speech Therapy - $60") recalculates the cost summary to include the new service cost.
3. Verify that unchecking a previously selected service (e.g., removing "Physical Therapy - $50") recalculates the cost summary to exclude that cost.
4. Verify that modifying the activity date (e.g., changing from 2025-03-01 to 2025-03-15) and clicking "Save Changes" updates the record successfully.
5. Verify that an audit trail entry is created after a successful edit with original and updated values, Provider identity, and timestamp.

Negative:
6. Verify that unchecking all services and attempting to save displays a validation message requiring at least one service.
7. Verify that changing the activity date to an invalid format (e.g., "2025-13-40") displays a date validation error.
8. Verify that clicking "Save Changes" with no actual modifications displays an informational message indicating no changes were detected.
9. Verify that clearing the activity date field and attempting to save displays a required field validation error.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the modal displays "Save Changes" and "Cancel" buttons when in edit mode.
11. Verify that the cost summary updates in real time as the Provider modifies service selections in the edit modal.
12. Verify that pre-selected service checkboxes are clearly marked when the edit modal opens.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that when two Providers attempt to edit the same activity entry simultaneously, the concurrency conflict is handled gracefully without data loss.
14. Verify that when the Provider edits an activity and selects a service that has been deactivated since the original entry, an appropriate warning or restriction is displayed.

Security:
15. Verify that only Providers with activity edit permissions can access the edit functionality on the Activities tab.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the edit modal loads with pre-populated activity data within 2 seconds.
Verify saving edited activity and generating the audit trail entry completes within 3 seconds.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider , I should be able to view list of Activities for a resident.
Description of User Story: The Patient Charting screen includes an Activities tab that displays a comprehensive list of all activities recorded for a resident. The list is presented in a table format showing columns for activity date, service names, total cost, notes, author name, and creation timestamp. Activities are sorted by date in descending order so the most recent entries appear at the top of the list. Each row provides a summary of the activity including the services performed and their combined cost, along with action buttons for editing or viewing full details. The list supports pagination or infinite scroll for residents with extensive activity histories. A total count indicator at the top shows the number of activity entries available for the resident.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider selects the Activities tab, Then the system must load and display all activities for the selected resident in a table format, sorted by date in descending order.

2. If the resident has no activities recorded, Then the system must display an empty state message indicating no activities have been recorded.
   Else if the number of activities exceeds the page size, Then the system must enable pagination or infinite scroll to load additional entries.

3. If the Provider clicks on an activity entry, Then the system must expand or navigate to the full activity detail view, including all selected services and their individual costs.

4. If the activities list fails to load due to a system error, Then the system must display an error message with a retry option.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that selecting the Activities tab displays a list of all activities for the resident.
2. Verify that each activity entry shows the activity date, service names, total cost, notes, author name, and timestamp.
3. Verify that activities are sorted by date in descending order with the most recent activity at the top.
4. Verify that clicking on an activity entry displays the full detail including individual service costs (e.g., "Physical Therapy - $50", "Occupational Therapy - $75").
5. Verify that the count indicator displays the correct total number of activity entries for the resident.

Negative:
6. Verify that navigating to the Activities tab for a resident with no activities displays an appropriate empty state message.
7. Verify that when the activities API returns an error, an error message with a retry option is displayed.
8. Verify that accessing the Activities tab for a non-existent resident ID displays an appropriate error message.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the activities list uses a consistent table layout with clearly labeled columns.
10. Verify that pagination controls or infinite scroll are visible and functional when the activity count exceeds the page limit.
11. Verify that the Activities tab is highlighted or active when selected among other charting tabs.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that when the resident has exactly one activity entry, the list displays it correctly without pagination controls.
13. Verify that when the resident has hundreds of activity entries (e.g., 500+), the list handles the large dataset without UI lag.

Security:
14. Verify that only authenticated Providers can view the activities list for a resident.
15. Verify that a Provider cannot view activities for a resident outside their authorized facility.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the activities list loads and displays the first page of entries within 2 seconds.
Verify pagination or scroll loading of additional activities completes within 2 seconds per page.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to search and filter Activies of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Patient Charting screen provides search and filter capabilities on the Activities tab to help the Provider quickly locate specific activity records within a resident chart. A search bar at the top of the activities list allows the Provider to search by keywords that match against the service names, notes content, or author name. Adjacent to the search bar, filter options are available for service type, date range, and cost range, enabling the Provider to narrow down the displayed activities based on specific criteria. The search and filter results update the list dynamically, showing only matching activity entries with the search term highlighted in the results. A clear filters button resets all applied filters and restores the full activities list. The total count indicator updates to reflect the number of filtered results.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider enters a keyword (e.g., "Physical Therapy") in the search bar, Then the system must filter the activities list to display only entries matching the keyword in service names, notes, or author name, with the keyword highlighted in results.

2. If the Provider selects a service type from the filter dropdown (e.g., "Occupational Therapy"), Then the system must display only activities matching the selected service type.
   Else if the Provider selects a date range using the date filter (e.g., 2025-01-01 to 2025-03-31), Then the system must display only activities within the specified start and end dates.

3. If the Provider applies multiple filters simultaneously, Then the system must display only activities matching all applied filter criteria.
   Else if no activities match the search or filter criteria, Then the system must display a "No results found" message.

4. If the Provider clicks the clear filters button, Then the system must remove all applied filters and restore the complete activities list with the full count.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that entering a keyword (e.g., "Physical Therapy") in the search bar filters the activities list to show matching entries.
2. Verify that the search keyword is highlighted in the matching results.
3. Verify that selecting a service type filter (e.g., "Occupational Therapy") displays only activities of that service type.
4. Verify that selecting a date range filter (e.g., 2025-01-01 to 2025-03-31) displays only activities within that date range.
5. Verify that clicking the clear filters button resets all filters and restores the full list.
6. Verify that the count indicator updates to reflect the number of filtered results.

Negative:
7. Verify that searching with a keyword that matches no activities (e.g., "xyzunknown") displays a "No results found" message.
8. Verify that selecting a date range where the start date is after the end date displays a validation error.
9. Verify that entering only special characters (e.g., "###") in the search bar returns no results or handles input gracefully.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the search bar and filter options are prominently displayed at the top of the Activities tab.
11. Verify that filter dropdowns display available options correctly and are easy to interact with.
12. Verify that the clear filters button is visible when any filter is active.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that applying a filter that matches exactly one activity entry displays that single entry correctly.
14. Verify that search behavior with a very long keyword string (e.g., 500+ characters) handles the input without crashing.

Security:
15. Verify that search and filter operations do not expose activity records from other residents in the results.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify search results are displayed within 2 seconds after the Provider enters a keyword.
Verify applying a date range filter returns filtered results within 2 seconds.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add Documents of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Patient Charting screen provides a Documents tab where the Provider can add new documents to a resident chart. The tab displays a list of existing documents with an "Add" button at the top right of the panel. Clicking the "Add" button opens a form or modal with fields for document title, document type or category, upload date, and a file upload area that accepts common document formats such as PDF, DOCX, JPG, and PNG. The Provider can also add optional notes or a description for the document to provide context. A file size limit and accepted format validation are enforced during the upload process, and a progress indicator shows the upload status. Upon clicking "Save", the document metadata and file are persisted to the resident chart and the new entry appears in the documents list with the title, type, upload date, and file size.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider clicks the "Add" button on the Documents tab, Then the system must open the document upload form with all required fields empty and a file upload area ready for input.

2. If the Provider uploads a file within the accepted format (PDF, DOCX, JPG, PNG) and within the size limit, Then the system must display the file name and size in the upload area and show a progress indicator.
   Else if the Provider uploads a file exceeding the size limit, Then the system must display a validation error and prevent the file from being attached.
   Else if the Provider uploads a file in an unsupported format (e.g., EXE, ZIP), Then the system must display a format validation error and prevent the file from being attached.

3. If the Provider clicks Save with all required fields completed and a valid file attached, Then the system must save the document to the resident chart and display a success notification.
   Else if any required field is missing, Then the system must display inline validation errors on the incomplete fields and block saving.

4. If the Provider clicks Cancel or closes the form, Then the system must discard all entered data and the uploaded file without saving.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking the "Add" button opens a document upload form with fields for title, type, date, file upload area, and optional notes.
2. Verify that uploading a PDF file within the size limit (e.g., a 2 MB file) attaches the file and displays the file name and size.
3. Verify that uploading a DOCX file within the size limit attaches the file successfully.
4. Verify that uploading a JPG or PNG image within the size limit attaches the file successfully.
5. Verify that entering all required fields and clicking "Save" saves the document and displays a success notification.
6. Verify that the newly added document appears in the documents list with the correct title, type, upload date, and file size.

Negative:
7. Verify that uploading a file exceeding the maximum size limit (e.g., a 25 MB file when the limit is 10 MB) displays a validation error.
8. Verify that uploading a file in an unsupported format (e.g., EXE) displays a format validation error.
9. Verify that clicking "Save" without uploading a file displays a required field validation error.
10. Verify that clicking "Save" without entering a document title displays a required field validation error.

UI Scenarios:
11. Verify that the file upload area displays a drag-and-drop zone or browse button for file selection.
12. Verify that a progress indicator is shown during file upload.
13. Verify that the "Save" and "Cancel" buttons are properly positioned in the form footer.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify that uploading a file with a very long file name (e.g., 200+ characters) is handled without display overflow or errors.

Security:
15. Verify that uploaded files are scanned for malware before being attached to the resident chart.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the document upload form loads within 2 seconds after clicking the "Add" button.
Verify uploading and saving a document of up to 10 MB completes within 5 seconds.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit Documents of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Patient Charting screen allows the Provider to edit previously uploaded documents for a resident from the Documents tab. When the Provider selects an existing document entry and clicks the "Edit" action, the form opens pre-populated with the current document title, document type, upload date, notes, and a reference to the attached file. The Provider can modify the document title, change the document type, update the notes, or replace the attached file by uploading a new one. If a new file is uploaded, it replaces the existing file while the original is archived for audit purposes. The "Save Changes" button validates all fields and persists the updated document metadata and file. An audit trail records the previous and updated values along with the modification timestamp and Provider identity.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider clicks "Edit" on an existing document, Then the system must open the form pre-populated with the current document title, type, date, notes, and file reference, with all fields enabled for editing.

2. If the Provider modifies the document title, type, or notes, Then the system must update the respective fields in the form in real time.
   Else if the Provider uploads a new replacement file, Then the system must replace the current file reference with the new file and archive the original for audit purposes.

3. If all modified fields pass validation, Then the system must enable the "Save Changes" button.
   Else if any modified field fails validation, Then the system must display inline validation errors and disable the "Save Changes" button.

4. If the Provider clicks "Save Changes" with valid data, Then the system must persist the updated document metadata and file, record an audit trail entry with previous values, updated values, Provider identity, and timestamp, and display a success notification.

5. If the Provider clicks "Cancel", Then the system must discard all unsaved changes and close the edit form.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking "Edit" on a document entry opens the form pre-populated with the existing title, type, date, notes, and attached file reference.
2. Verify that modifying the document title (e.g., from "Lab Results Jan" to "Lab Results Jan 2025") and clicking "Save Changes" updates the record successfully.
3. Verify that uploading a new replacement file replaces the existing file and the updated file name is displayed.
4. Verify that the audit trail records original values, updated values, Provider identity, and modification timestamp after a successful edit.
5. Verify that changing the document type from the dropdown (e.g., from "Lab Report" to "Discharge Summary") updates the document category correctly.

Negative:
6. Verify that clearing the document title field and attempting to save displays a required field validation error.
7. Verify that uploading a replacement file exceeding the size limit (e.g., 25 MB) displays a size validation error.
8. Verify that uploading a replacement file in an unsupported format (e.g., EXE) displays a format validation error.
9. Verify that clicking "Save Changes" with no actual modifications displays an informational message indicating no changes were detected.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the form displays "Save Changes" and "Cancel" buttons when in edit mode.
11. Verify that the current attached file name and size are displayed in the edit form.
12. Verify that a file upload area is available in the edit form for replacing the attached file.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that when two Providers attempt to edit the same document simultaneously, the concurrency conflict is handled gracefully without data loss.
14. Verify that when the Provider replaces a file, the original file is correctly archived and retrievable for audit purposes.

Security:
15. Verify that only Providers with document edit permissions can access the edit functionality and replacement files are scanned for malware.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the edit form loads with pre-populated document data within 2 seconds.
Verify saving edited document metadata and replacement file completes within 5 seconds.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add minor incident with its details
Description of User Story: The Patient Charting screen provides an Incidents tab where the Provider can add minor incident records for a resident. Clicking the "Add" button on the Incidents section opens a form with fields specific to minor incident documentation including incident date, incident time, location within the facility, brief incident description, immediate actions taken, and any witnesses present. The form also includes a severity classification that defaults to "Minor" and allows the Provider to select the type of minor incident such as a slip, verbal altercation, or minor property damage. A notification preference section allows the Provider to indicate whether the family or guardian has been informed. Upon clicking "Save", the minor incident record is validated, persisted to the resident chart, and the new entry appears in the incidents list.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider clicks the "Add" button on the Incidents tab, Then the system must open the minor incident form with severity defaulting to "Minor" and all required fields empty and ready for input.

2. If the Provider completes all required fields (date, time, location, and description) and clicks Save, Then the system must persist the minor incident to the resident chart and display a success notification.
   Else if any required field is left empty, Then the system must display inline validation errors on the incomplete fields and block saving.

3. If the Provider indicates that the family or guardian has been informed, Then the system must record the notification status in the incident record.

4. If the minor incident is saved successfully, Then the system must display the new entry in the incidents list with the correct date, incident type, and description.

5. If the Provider clicks Cancel, Then the system must discard all entered data and close the form.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking the "Add" button opens the minor incident form with severity defaulting to "Minor".

3. Verify that selecting a minor incident type (e.g., "Slip") records the selection in the form.
4. Verify that entering immediate actions taken and witness details captures the information in the form.
5. Verify that clicking "Save" with all required fields saves the minor incident and displays a success notification.
6. Verify that the newly added minor incident appears in the incidents list with the correct date, type, and description.

8. Verify that clicking "Save" without entering the incident description displays a required field validation error.
9. Verify that entering an incident time in an invalid format (e.g., "25:99") displays a time validation error.
10. Verify that entering a future date (e.g., tomorrow's date) for the incident date displays a validation error.

UI Scenarios:
11. Verify that the minor incident form displays clearly labeled fields with appropriate input types for date, time, and text.
12. Verify that the severity classification field defaults to "Minor" and is visually indicated.
13. Verify that the "Save" and "Cancel" buttons are properly positioned at the bottom of the form.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify that entering the maximum allowed character count (e.g., 1000 characters) in the incident description field saves correctly without truncation.

Security:
15. Verify that only authenticated Providers with charting permissions can add minor incident records to a resident chart.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit minor incident with its details
Description of User Story: The Patient Charting screen allows the Provider to edit previously recorded minor incident details for a resident from the Incidents tab. When the Provider selects an existing minor incident entry and clicks the "Edit" action, the form opens pre-populated with the current incident date, time, location, description, actions taken, witnesses, incident type, and notification status. All fields become editable, allowing the Provider to update any value such as correcting the description or adding newly identified witnesses. The system tracks modifications by recording the original and updated values, the editing Provider identity, and the modification timestamp in an audit trail. Upon clicking "Save Changes", validation runs on all fields before persisting the update, and the incidents list reflects the edited entry with an "Edited" indicator.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider clicks "Edit" on an existing minor incident, Then the system must open the form pre-populated with the current incident date, time, location, description, actions taken, witnesses, type, and notification status, with all fields enabled for editing.

2. If the Provider modifies any field and all fields pass validation, Then the system must enable the "Save Changes" button.
   Else if any field fails validation after modification, Then the system must display inline validation errors and disable the "Save Changes" button.

3. If the Provider clicks "Save Changes" with valid data, Then the system must persist the updated incident, create an audit trail entry with previous values, updated values, Provider identity, and timestamp, and display a success notification.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Save Changes" with no actual modifications, Then the system must display an informational message indicating no changes were detected.

4. If the Provider clicks "Cancel" after making edits, Then the system must discard all unsaved changes and close the edit form.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking "Edit" on a minor incident opens the form pre-populated with the existing date, time, location, description, actions, witnesses, type, and notification status.
2. Verify that modifying the incident description (e.g., adding clarifying details) and clicking "Save Changes" updates the record successfully.
3. Verify that adding additional witness details during editing saves the updated information.
4. Verify that the edited minor incident displays with an "Edited" indicator in the incidents list.
5. Verify that the audit trail records original values, updated values, Provider identity, and modification timestamp after a successful edit.

Negative:
6. Verify that clearing the incident description and attempting to save displays a required field validation error.
7. Verify that changing the incident date to an invalid format (e.g., "13/32/2025") displays a date validation error.
8. Verify that changing the incident time to an invalid format (e.g., "25:99") displays a time validation error.
9. Verify that clicking "Save Changes" with no actual modifications displays an informational message indicating no changes were detected.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the form displays "Save Changes" and "Cancel" buttons when in edit mode.
11. Verify that pre-populated fields display the current saved values accurately when the edit form opens.
12. Verify that the "Edited" indicator is visually distinct from original entries in the incidents list.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that when two Providers attempt to edit the same minor incident simultaneously, the concurrency conflict is handled gracefully without data loss.
14. Verify that editing a minor incident that was created prior to the current reporting period boundary completes without errors.

Security:
15. Verify that only Providers with incident edit permissions can modify minor incident records.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the edit form loads with pre-populated minor incident data within 2 seconds.
Verify saving edited minor incident and generating the audit trail entry completes within 3 seconds.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to view list of minor incidents for a resident.
Description of User Story: The Patient Charting screen includes an Incidents tab that displays a list of all minor incidents recorded for a resident. The list is presented in a table format showing columns for incident date, incident time, incident type, location, brief description, severity classification, and the author who recorded the incident. Minor incidents are sorted by date in descending order so the most recent entries appear at the top. Each row provides a summary of the incident and action buttons for editing or viewing full details. The list supports filtering by severity to show only minor incidents when both minor and major incidents are present. A count indicator at the top shows the total number of minor incident entries for the resident.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider selects the Incidents tab, Then the system must load and display all incidents for the selected resident, sorted by date in descending order.

2. If the Provider applies the "Minor" severity filter, Then the system must display only minor incident entries in the list.
   Else if the resident has no minor incidents recorded, Then the system must display an empty state message indicating no minor incidents have been recorded.

3. If the Provider clicks on a minor incident entry, Then the system must expand or navigate to the full incident detail view, displaying all recorded fields.

4. If the incidents list fails to load due to a system error, Then the system must display an error message with a retry option.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that selecting the Incidents tab displays the incidents list for the resident.
2. Verify that filtering by "Minor" severity shows only minor incident entries.
3. Verify that each minor incident entry shows the date, time, type (e.g., "Slip"), location (e.g., "Hallway"), description, severity ("Minor"), and author.
4. Verify that minor incidents are sorted by date in descending order with the most recent at the top.
5. Verify that clicking on a minor incident entry displays the full incident detail including all recorded fields.
6. Verify that the count indicator displays the correct total number of minor incidents for the resident.

Negative:
7. Verify that filtering by "Minor" severity when no minor incidents exist displays an empty state message.
8. Verify that when the incidents API returns an error, an error message with a retry option is displayed.
9. Verify that accessing the Incidents tab for a non-existent resident ID displays an appropriate error message.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the incidents list uses a consistent table layout with clearly labeled columns.
11. Verify that the severity filter clearly distinguishes "Minor" from "Major" incident categories.
12. Verify that the Incidents tab is highlighted or active when selected among other charting tabs.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that when the resident has exactly one minor incident, the list displays it correctly without pagination controls.
14. Verify that when the resident has hundreds of minor incidents (e.g., 300+), the list handles the large dataset without UI lag.

Security:
15. Verify that only authenticated Providers can view the minor incidents list for a resident within their authorized facility.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the minor incidents list loads and displays the first page of entries within 2 seconds.
Verify pagination or scroll loading of additional minor incidents completes within 2 seconds per page.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add major incident details
Description of User Story: The Patient Charting screen provides the ability for a Provider to add major incident records for a resident from the Incidents tab. Clicking the "Add" button and selecting "Major" severity opens an expanded incident form with comprehensive fields including incident date, incident time, location, detailed incident description, injury or harm assessment, emergency services contacted, actions taken, witnesses present, and supervisor notification status. The form requires more detailed documentation compared to minor incidents due to the elevated severity classification. A mandatory narrative field captures the full incident account, and the Provider must indicate whether regulatory reporting is required based on the incident nature. Upon clicking "Save", the major incident is validated, persisted to the resident chart, and triggers any configured notification workflows for supervisory review.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider clicks "Add" and selects "Major" severity, Then the system must open the expanded major incident form with all required fields empty and severity set to "Major".

2. If the Provider completes all required fields (date, time, location, detailed description, and mandatory narrative) and clicks Save, Then the system must persist the major incident to the resident chart and trigger any configured notification workflows for supervisory review.
   Else if any required field is left empty, Then the system must display inline validation errors on the incomplete fields and block saving.

3. If the Provider indicates that regulatory reporting is required, Then the system must flag the incident for regulatory follow-up in the system.

4. If the major incident is saved successfully, Then the system must display the new entry in the incidents list with the correct date, incident type, and "Major" severity badge.

5. If the Provider clicks Cancel, Then the system must discard all entered data and close the form.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking "Add" and selecting "Major" severity opens the expanded major incident form with all required fields.
2. Verify that entering incident date (e.g., 2025-04-01), time (e.g., 14:00), location (e.g., "Room 205"), and detailed description captures the values correctly.
3. Verify that completing the injury assessment and indicating emergency services were contacted (e.g., "Yes - EMS called") records the information.
4. Verify that entering the mandatory narrative and indicating regulatory reporting is required captures the data and flags the incident for follow-up.
5. Verify that clicking "Save" with all required fields saves the major incident and triggers notification workflows.
6. Verify that the newly added major incident appears in the incidents list with the correct date, type, and "Major" severity.

Negative:
7. Verify that clicking "Save" without entering the incident date displays a required field validation error.
8. Verify that clicking "Save" without entering the detailed description displays a required field validation error.
9. Verify that clicking "Save" without completing the mandatory narrative displays a required field validation error.
10. Verify that entering a future date (e.g., tomorrow's date) for the incident date displays a validation error.

UI Scenarios:
11. Verify that the major incident form displays clearly labeled sections for incident details, injury assessment, and notifications.
12. Verify that the severity classification field displays "Major" and is visually distinct from "Minor".
13. Verify that the "Save" and "Cancel" buttons are properly positioned at the bottom of the expanded form.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify that entering the maximum allowed character count (e.g., 5000 characters) in the mandatory narrative field saves correctly without truncation.

Security:
15. Verify that only authenticated Providers with elevated charting permissions can add major incident records.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the major incident form loads within 2 seconds after selecting "Major" severity.
Verify saving a new major incident and triggering notification workflows completes within 3 seconds.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit major incident details
Description of User Story: The Patient Charting screen allows the Provider to edit previously recorded major incident details for a resident from the Incidents tab. When the Provider selects an existing major incident entry and clicks the "Edit" action, the expanded form opens pre-populated with the current incident date, time, location, detailed description, injury assessment, emergency services status, actions taken, witnesses, supervisor notification status, narrative, and regulatory reporting flag. All fields become editable, allowing the Provider to update critical details such as adding supplemental witness statements or correcting the injury assessment. The system enforces strict audit trail requirements for major incident edits, recording the original values, updated values, reason for modification, editing Provider identity, and the exact modification timestamp. Upon clicking "Save Changes", all fields are validated before persisting the update.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider clicks "Edit" on an existing major incident, Then the system must open the expanded form pre-populated with all current field values (date, time, location, description, injury assessment, emergency services status, narrative, regulatory flag) and enable all fields for editing.

2. If the Provider modifies any field and all fields pass validation, Then the system must enable the "Save Changes" button.
   Else if any field fails validation after modification, Then the system must display inline validation errors and disable the "Save Changes" button.

3. If the Provider clicks "Save Changes" with valid data, Then the system must persist the updated major incident, create a strict audit trail entry with original values, updated values, reason for modification, Provider identity, and exact modification timestamp, and display a success notification.
   Else if the Provider modifies the regulatory reporting flag, Then the system must update the incident flagging status accordingly.

4. If the Provider clicks "Cancel" after making edits, Then the system must discard all unsaved changes and close the edit form.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
