2. Verify that modifying the detailed description (e.g., adding supplemental context) and clicking "Save Changes" updates the record successfully.
3. Verify that updating the injury assessment (e.g., changing from "Minor bruise" to "Laceration requiring stitches") saves the corrected information.
4. Verify that adding supplemental witness information during editing saves the updated details.
5. Verify that the audit trail records original values, updated values, reason for modification, Provider identity, and exact modification timestamp after a successful edit.

Negative:
6. Verify that clearing the detailed description and attempting to save displays a required field validation error.
7. Verify that clearing the mandatory narrative and attempting to save displays a required field validation error.
8. Verify that changing the incident date to an invalid format (e.g., "13/32/2025") displays a date validation error.
9. Verify that clicking "Save Changes" with no actual modifications displays an informational message indicating no changes were detected.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the form displays "Save Changes" and "Cancel" buttons when in edit mode for major incidents.
11. Verify that pre-populated fields display the current saved values accurately when the edit form opens.
12. Verify that the "Major" severity indicator remains visible and visually distinct during editing.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that when two Providers attempt to edit the same major incident simultaneously, the concurrency conflict is handled gracefully without data loss.
14. Verify that editing a major incident that has already been submitted for regulatory review displays an appropriate warning or restriction.

Security:
15. Verify that only Providers with elevated incident edit permissions can modify major incident records and all edits are logged.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the edit form loads with pre-populated major incident data within 2 seconds.
Verify saving edited major incident and generating the audit trail entry completes within 3 seconds.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider , I should be able to add Progress Notes of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Add Progress Note feature allows providers to create new clinical progress notes for a resident within the Patient Charting module. The form presents structured fields including note date, note type, subject, and a rich-text body area for documenting clinical observations, treatment progress, and care plan updates. The provider can attach relevant tags or categories to the note for easier retrieval and filtering later. Upon clicking Save, the system validates all required fields, persists the note to the resident record, and displays a success confirmation. The note is immediately visible in the resident's progress notes list with the author name and creation timestamp.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens the Add Progress Note form, Then the system must display the form with the current date and provider name pre-populated and the Save button disabled until required fields are filled.

2. If the Provider clicks Save with the subject field filled and body content entered, Then the system must validate the entries, persist the progress note, and display a success confirmation.
   Else if the subject field is empty when Save is clicked, Then the system must highlight the subject field and display a validation error indicating it is required.
   Else if the body field is empty when Save is clicked, Then the system must highlight the body field and display a validation error requiring note content.

3. If the Provider selects a note type from the dropdown, Then the system must associate the selected type with the progress note record.

4. If the Provider clicks Cancel after entering partial data, Then the system must prompt a confirmation dialog asking whether to discard unsaved changes.
   Else if the Provider confirms discard, Then the system must discard unsaved data and return to the progress notes list.

5. If the session expires while the Provider is composing a note, Then the system must preserve the draft locally and prompt re-authentication before saving.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that a Provider can open the Add Progress Note form and see the current date and provider name auto-populated.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that a Provider can enter subject "Follow-up Assessment", select note type "Clinical", type observations in the body, and save; the note is stored with correct metadata.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that a Provider can create a progress note with tags "Cardiology" and "Follow-up"; the tags are saved and displayed on the note record.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that a newly added progress note immediately appears at the top of the resident's notes list with the correct timestamp and author name.

Negative:
5. Verify that 5. Verify that the system displays a required field validation error and prevents submission when the Provider attempts to save with the subject field empty.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that the system displays a validation error requiring note content when the Provider attempts to save with an empty body field.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that the system displays a character limit validation error when the Provider enters a subject exceeding the maximum character limit.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that 8. Verify that the Add Progress Note form displays fields for date, note type dropdown, subject input, body rich-text editor, and tags in a clean vertical layout.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that the Save and Cancel buttons are positioned at the bottom of the form with Save as the primary action button.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that a progress note added at exactly midnight is correctly assigned to the current calendar date.
11. Verify that 11. Verify that the system accepts and saves content without truncation when the Provider pastes a large block of text (5000+ characters) into the body field.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that when two providers simultaneously create progress notes for the same resident, both notes are saved independently without conflict.

Security:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that only users with the "progress_notes_create" permission can access the Add Progress Note form, and unauthorized roles see progress notes in read-only mode.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Add Progress Note form loads within 1 second after clicking the add button, with all fields and dropdowns rendered.
Verify that saving a progress note with a full body and tags completes within 2 seconds, and the notes list reflects the new entry immediately.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider , I should be able to edit Progress Notes of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Edit Progress Note feature allows providers to modify existing progress notes within a resident's charting record. When the provider selects a note and clicks the edit action, the system opens the note in an editable form pre-populated with the existing subject, note type, body content, and tags. The provider can update any field including the subject, body text, note type, and associated tags. Upon saving, the system validates the changes, updates the record, and maintains an audit trail showing the original content, modification timestamp, and the editing provider's identity. The updated note is reflected immediately in the progress notes list.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider clicks the edit action on an existing progress note, Then the system must open the note in edit mode with all fields pre-populated with the current values.

2. If the Provider modifies the note body and clicks Save, Then the system must record the previous version in the audit trail and persist the updated content.
   Else if the Provider clears the subject field and clicks Save, Then the system must display a validation error indicating the subject is required and prevent submission.

3. If another Provider has edited the same note since it was opened, Then the system must display a conflict warning and offer options to overwrite or reload the latest version.

4. If the Provider clicks Cancel after making changes, Then the system must prompt a confirmation dialog to discard unsaved edits.
   Else if the Provider confirms discard, Then the system must revert to the original note content and return to the notes list.

5. If the Provider attempts to edit a note that has been locked by an administrator, Then the system must display a message indicating the note is locked and cannot be modified.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that a Provider can open an existing progress note in edit mode with all fields pre-populated with current values.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that a Provider can change the subject from "Initial Assessment" to "Updated Assessment", modify the body, and save; the updated note is displayed with the new content.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that the audit trail records the original content, the modification timestamp, and the editing provider name when a note is edited.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that a Provider can update the note type from "Clinical" to "Administrative" and save; the updated type is reflected in the notes list.

Negative:
5. Verify that 5. Verify that the system prevents submission with a required field error when the Provider clears the subject field and attempts to save.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that the system displays a validation error requiring note content when the Provider clears the body content entirely and attempts to save.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that the system blocks editing with a locked note notification when the Provider attempts to edit a note created more than 30 days ago that is in a locked state.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that 8. Verify that the edit form displays the same layout as the add form with all fields pre-populated and editable.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that a "Last Modified" indicator showing date, time, and editor name is visible on the edit form.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the system displays a "No changes detected" message when the Provider opens a note for editing, makes no changes, and clicks Save.
11. Verify that 11. Verify that a Provider viewing a note sees the updated content upon refreshing when another Provider edits and saves the same note simultaneously.

Security:
12. Verify that 12. Verify that only the original author or users with "progress_notes_edit" permission can modify progress notes, and other providers see the edit action disabled.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the progress note edit form loads with pre-populated data within 1 second after clicking the edit action.
Verify that saving an edited progress note with audit trail update completes within 2 seconds.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider , I should be able to view list of Progress Notes for a resident.
Description of User Story: The Progress Notes List view provides a comprehensive listing of all clinical progress notes associated with a specific resident. Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider, I should be able search and filter Progress Notes of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Search and Filter functionality for Progress Notes enables providers to quickly locate specific notes within a resident's charting history. A search bar at the top of the progress notes list allows free-text keyword search across note subjects and body content, with results updating in real time as the provider types. Filter dropdowns are available for note type, author, and date range, allowing providers to narrow down the list based on specific criteria. Multiple filters can be combined simultaneously for precise results. A "Clear Filters" button resets all applied filters and restores the full notes list. The total count updates dynamically to reflect the number of matching results.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider enters a search keyword that matches no notes, Then the system must display "No results found" with a suggestion to adjust the search criteria.
   Else if the Provider selects a note type filter and a date range filter simultaneously, Then the system must apply both filters as an AND condition, showing only notes matching both criteria.
   Else if the Provider clears the search bar text, Then the system must remove the keyword filter and apply only the remaining active filters.
   Else if the Provider selects a date range where the start date is after the end date, Then the system must display a validation error requesting a valid date range.
   Else if the Provider filters by an author who has no notes for the resident, Then the system must display "No notes found for the selected author".
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that typing "medication" in the search bar filters the list to show only notes containing that keyword in the subject or body, e.g., three matching notes.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that selecting note type "Clinical" and date range "Last 7 days" narrows the list to show only matching notes, e.g., two results.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that filtering by author "Dr. Smith" shows only notes authored by Dr. Smith.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that clicking "Clear Filters" after applying multiple filters restores the full list of notes with the correct total count.

Negative:
5. Verify that 5. Verify that the system displays "No results found" when the Provider searches for a keyword like "xyznonexistent".
6. Verify that 6. Verify that the system displays a date range validation error when the Provider sets start date as March 20 and end date as March 10.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that the system sanitizes input containing only special characters and returns no results without crashing.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that 8. Verify that the search bar is positioned at the top of the progress notes list with a search icon and placeholder text "Search progress notes...".
9. Verify that 9. Verify that filter dropdowns for note type, author, and date range are aligned horizontally below the search bar with clear labels.
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the "Clear Filters" button is visible when any filter is active and hidden when no filters are applied.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that 11. Verify that searching for a single character "a" returns all notes containing that letter without performance degradation.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that applying a date range filter spanning an entire year with hundreds of notes returns paginated results correctly.

Security:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that search and filter functionality respects permission boundaries and providers can only search within notes they are authorized to view.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that real-time search filtering returns results within 500 milliseconds for a resident with 300 progress notes.
Verify that applying combined filters (note type + author + date range) refreshes the list within 1 second.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider , I should be able to add Activities of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Add Activity feature allows providers to record new activity entries for a resident within the Patient Charting module. The form presents fields for activity date, activity type (e.g., physical therapy, recreational, social, educational), duration, participation level, and a notes field for additional observations. The provider can specify whether the resident participated independently, with assistance, or declined the activity. Upon saving, the system validates required fields, persists the activity record, and displays it in the resident's activity list with the recording provider's name and timestamp. The feature supports documenting both scheduled and ad-hoc activities throughout the day.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens the Add Activity form, Then the system must display the form with the current date and provider name pre-populated.

2. If the Provider selects an activity type, enters a valid duration, and clicks Save, Then the system must validate the entries and persist the activity record.
   Else if the Provider clicks Save without selecting an activity type, Then the system must display a validation error indicating the activity type is required.
   Else if the Provider enters a duration of zero or a negative number, Then the system must display a validation error requiring a valid positive duration.

3. If the Provider selects participation level "Declined", Then the system must make the notes field mandatory to document the reason for declining.
   Else if the Provider selects "Independent" or "With Assistance", Then the notes field must remain optional.

4. If the Provider clicks Cancel after entering partial data, Then the system must prompt a confirmation dialog before discarding changes.

5. If the Provider selects an activity date in the future, Then the system must allow saving as a scheduled activity and tag it as "Planned".
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that a Provider can open the Add Activity form and see the current date and provider name auto-populated.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that a Provider can select "Physical Therapy" type, enter 30-minute duration, mark participation as "Independent", and save; the activity record is stored correctly.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that a Provider can record a recreational activity with participation level "With Assistance" and a note about the resident's engagement; both the level and note are saved.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that an ad-hoc social activity added outside the schedule appears in the activities list with the correct timestamp.

Negative:
5. Verify that 5. Verify that the system displays a required field validation error when the Provider attempts to save without selecting an activity type.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that the system rejects a duration of -15 minutes with a validation error for invalid duration.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that the system requires notes explaining the reason when the Provider selects "Declined" participation but leaves the notes field empty.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that 8. Verify that the Add Activity form displays fields for date, activity type dropdown, duration input with unit label, participation level radio buttons, and a notes text area.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that the Save and Cancel buttons are positioned at the bottom of the form with Save as the primary action.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the system accepts and records a duration of exactly 1 minute.
11. Verify that 11. Verify that the system accepts all 20 entries without limit restrictions when a Provider records 20 activities for the same resident on the same day.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that an activity added at exactly midnight is correctly assigned to the current calendar date.

Security:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that only users with the "activities_create" permission can access the Add Activity form, and unauthorized roles see activity records in read-only mode.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Add Activity form loads within 1 second after clicking the add button, with all dropdowns and fields rendered.
Verify that saving an activity record with notes completes within 2 seconds and the activities list reflects the new entry immediately.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider , I should be able to edit Activities of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Edit Activity feature allows providers to modify existing activity records within a resident's charting profile. When the provider clicks the edit action on an activity entry, the system opens the form pre-populated with the current activity type, date, duration, participation level, and notes. The provider can update any field and save the changes, which triggers validation before persisting the updates. The system maintains an audit trail recording the original values, modification timestamp, and the identity of the editing provider. The updated activity is immediately reflected in the activities list with a modification indicator.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider clicks the edit action on an existing activity, Then the system must open the activity form pre-populated with all current values.

2. If the Provider modifies the duration and clicks Save, Then the system must record the previous duration in the audit trail and persist the updated value.
   Else if the Provider clears the activity type and clicks Save, Then the system must display a validation error indicating the activity type is required.
   Else if the Provider changes the duration to zero and clicks Save, Then the system must display a validation error for invalid duration.

3. If another Provider is currently editing the same activity record, Then the system must display a conflict notification and prevent simultaneous edits.

4. If the Provider clicks Cancel after making changes, Then the system must prompt a confirmation dialog to discard unsaved edits.
   Else if the Provider confirms discard, Then the system must revert to the original activity values and return to the activities list.

5. If the Provider attempts to edit an activity that has been finalized or locked, Then the system must display a message that the record cannot be modified.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that a Provider can open an existing activity in edit mode with all fields pre-populated with current values.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that a Provider can change the participation level from "With Assistance" to "Independent" and save; the updated level is displayed in the activities list.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that the audit trail records the change with the previous value when the Provider edits a physical therapy activity duration from 30 to 45 minutes.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that the Provider can update the notes field with additional observations and save; the updated notes are persisted and visible on the record.

Negative:
5. Verify that 5. Verify that the system prevents submission with a required field error when the Provider clears the activity type field and attempts to save.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that the system displays a validation error for invalid duration when the Provider changes the duration to zero and attempts to save.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that the system blocks editing with a locked record notification when the Provider attempts to edit an activity from a previous billing period that is locked.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that 8. Verify that the edit form displays the same layout as the add form with all fields pre-populated and editable.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that a "Last Modified" indicator showing date, time, and editor name is visible on the edited activity record.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the system displays "No changes detected" when the Provider opens an activity for editing, makes no changes, and clicks Save.
11. Verify that 11. Verify that a Provider viewing an activity sees the updated content upon refreshing when another Provider edits and saves the same activity simultaneously.

Security:
12. Verify that 12. Verify that only the original recorder or users with "activities_edit" permission can modify activity records, and other providers see the edit action disabled.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the activity edit form loads with pre-populated data within 1 second after clicking the edit action.
Verify that saving an edited activity record with audit trail update completes within 2 seconds.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider , I should be able to view list of Activities for a resident.
Description of User Story: The Activities List view provides a comprehensive listing of all activity records associated with a specific resident within the Patient Charting module. The list is displayed in a tabular format with columns for activity date, activity type, duration, participation level, recorded by, and a status indicator. Activities are sorted by date in descending order so the most recent entries appear first. Each row supports a click or expand action to view full details including notes and metadata. Pagination controls at the bottom allow navigation through large volumes of activity records. A summary section at the top shows the total number of activities and a breakdown by activity type.

   Else if the Provider clicks on an activity row to view details, Then the system must display the full details in an inline detail panel without navigating away from the list.
   Else if the list contains activities from multiple providers, Then the system must display the recording provider name on each activity row.

2. If the Provider navigates to page 2 and returns to page 1, Then the system must preserve the list state and display correctly.
   Else if an activity has been recently edited, Then the system must display a "Modified" badge next to the activity date.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to search and filter Activies of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Search and Filter functionality for Activities enables providers to quickly locate specific activity records within a resident's charting history. A search bar at the top of the activities list allows free-text keyword search across activity types and notes, with results updating in real time. Filter dropdowns are available for activity type, participation level, recorded by, and date range, allowing providers to narrow down results based on specific criteria. Multiple filters can be combined simultaneously for precise results. A "Clear Filters" button resets all applied filters and restores the full activities list. The result count updates dynamically to reflect the number of matching records.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider enters a search keyword that matches no activities, Then the system must display "No results found" with a suggestion to adjust the search criteria.
   Else if the Provider selects an activity type filter and a date range filter simultaneously, Then the system must apply both filters as an AND condition, showing only activities matching both criteria.
   Else if the Provider clears the search bar text, Then the system must remove the keyword filter and apply only the remaining active filters.
   Else if the Provider selects a date range where the start date is after the end date, Then the system must display a validation error requesting a valid date range.
   Else if the Provider filters by participation level "Declined", Then the system must display only activities where the resident declined.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that typing "physical" in the search bar filters the list to show only activities matching that keyword, e.g., four matching activities.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that selecting activity type "Social" and date range "Last 30 days" narrows the list to only matching records.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that filtering by participation level "With Assistance" shows only assisted activities.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that clicking "Clear Filters" after applying multiple filters restores the full list with the correct total count.

Negative:
5. Verify that 5. Verify that the system displays "No results found" when the Provider searches for a keyword like "nonexistentkeyword".
6. Verify that 6. Verify that the system displays a validation error when the Provider sets a start date after the end date in the date range filter.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that the system sanitizes input containing only special characters and returns no results without errors.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that 8. Verify that the search bar is positioned at the top of the activities list with a search icon and placeholder text "Search activities...".
9. Verify that 9. Verify that filter dropdowns for activity type, participation level, recorded by, and date range are aligned horizontally below the search bar.
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the "Clear Filters" button is visible when any filter is active and hidden when no filters are applied.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that 11. Verify that searching for a single character returns all matching activities without performance degradation.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that applying a date range filter spanning an entire year with hundreds of records returns paginated results correctly.

Security:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that search and filter functionality respects permission boundaries and providers can only search within activities they are authorized to view.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that real-time search filtering returns results within 500 milliseconds for a resident with 300 activity records.
Verify that applying combined filters (type + participation level + date range) refreshes the list within 1 second.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add Documents of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Add Document feature allows providers to upload and attach documents to a resident's charting record within the Patient Charting module. The upload form presents fields for document title, document category (e.g., lab report, imaging, consent form, referral letter, insurance), upload date, and a file upload area supporting drag-and-drop and browse functionality. Supported file formats include PDF, DOCX, JPG, PNG, and TIFF with a configurable maximum file size. Upon upload, the system validates the file type, size, and required metadata fields before persisting the document to the resident's record. A success confirmation is displayed and the new document appears immediately in the resident's documents list.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens the Add Document form, Then the system must display the form with the current date auto-populated and the upload area active for drag-and-drop and browse.

2. If the Provider enters a valid title, selects a category, and uploads a supported file, Then the system must validate the metadata and file, persist the document, and display a success confirmation.
   Else if the Provider clicks Save without entering a document title, Then the system must display a validation error indicating the title is required.
   Else if the Provider uploads a file exceeding the maximum allowed size, Then the system must reject the upload and display a file size limit error.
   Else if the Provider uploads a file with an unsupported format such as .exe, Then the system must reject the upload and display a message listing supported formats.

3. If the Provider clicks Cancel after selecting a file, Then the system must discard the upload and return to the documents list.

4. If the network connection is interrupted during upload, Then the system must display an upload failure message and allow the Provider to retry.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that a Provider can open the Add Document form and see the current date auto-populated.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that a Provider can enter title "Blood Work Results", select category "Lab Report", upload a PDF file, and save; the document is stored and displayed in the list.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that a Provider can use drag-and-drop to upload a JPG imaging scan; the system accepts the file and displays the preview thumbnail.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that a Provider can upload a DOCX consent form with category "Consent Form" and the document is stored with correct metadata.

Negative:
5. Verify that 5. Verify that the system displays a required field validation error when the Provider attempts to save without a document title.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that the system rejects with a file size error when the Provider uploads a 50MB file exceeding the 10MB limit.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that the system rejects with an unsupported file format error listing allowed formats when the Provider uploads an .exe file.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that 8. Verify that the upload form displays fields for title, category dropdown, date, and a drag-and-drop file upload area with a browse button.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that the file upload area shows file name, size, and a progress bar during upload.
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the Save and Cancel buttons are positioned at the bottom of the form.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that 11. Verify that the system truncates the display name for a file with a 255+ character filename but preserves the full filename in storage.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that the system accepts both documents as separate records with unique identifiers when two documents with the same title and category are uploaded for the same resident.

Security:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that only users with "documents_upload" permission can access the Add Document form, and unauthorized roles see documents in read-only mode.
14. Verify that 14. Verify that the system scans uploaded files for malware before persisting them to the resident record.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Add Document form loads within 1 second after clicking the add button.
Verify that uploading a 5MB PDF file completes within 5 seconds, and the documents list reflects the new entry immediately.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit Documents of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Edit Document feature allows providers to modify the metadata and replace the file of an existing document within a resident's charting record. When the provider selects the edit action on a document, the system opens the form pre-populated with the current title, category, date, and a reference to the attached file. The provider can update the title, category, and notes, or replace the attached file with a new upload. Upon saving, the system validates the changes, updates the document record, and maintains an audit trail with the previous metadata and file version. The updated document is reflected immediately in the documents list with a modification indicator.
Acceptance Criteria:

2. If the Provider updates the title and category and clicks Save, Then the system must validate the changes and persist the updated metadata.
   Else if the Provider clears the title field and clicks Save, Then the system must display a validation error indicating the title is required.

3. If the Provider uploads a replacement file within the size and format limits, Then the system must replace the previous file and retain the old version in the audit trail.
   Else if the Provider uploads a replacement file exceeding the maximum size, Then the system must reject the new file and retain the original.
   Else if the Provider uploads a replacement file in an unsupported format, Then the system must reject the upload and display the supported formats message.

4. If another Provider has edited the same document since it was opened, Then the system must display a conflict warning and offer options to overwrite or reload.

5. If the Provider attempts to edit a document that has been locked or archived, Then the system must display a message that the document cannot be modified.

9. Verify that 9. Verify that a "Last Modified" indicator showing date, time, and editor name is visible on the edit form.

Edge Cases:

11. Verify that 11. Verify that a provider simultaneously viewing a document receives a notification that the document has been updated when another Provider replaces the file.

Security:
12. Verify that 12. Verify that only the original uploader or users with "documents_edit" permission can modify document records, and other providers see the edit action disabled.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the document edit form loads with pre-populated metadata within 1 second after clicking the edit action.
Verify that saving an edited document with a replacement file of 5MB completes within 5 seconds.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit minor incident with its details
Description of User Story: The Edit Minor Incident feature allows providers to modify existing minor incident records within a resident's charting profile. When the provider clicks the edit action on an incident, the system opens the form pre-populated with the current incident type, date and time, location, description, witness names, and immediate actions taken. The provider can update any field to correct details or add information discovered after the initial report. Upon saving, the system validates the changes, updates the record, and maintains a complete audit trail showing the original values, modification timestamp, and editing provider identity. The updated incident is reflected immediately in the incident log with a modification indicator.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider clicks the edit action on a minor incident, Then the system must open the incident form pre-populated with all current values including incident type, date, location, description, witnesses, and actions taken.

2. If the Provider updates the description and clicks Save, Then the system must validate the changes and persist the updated content with an audit trail entry.
   Else if the Provider clears the description field and clicks Save, Then the system must display a validation error indicating the description is required.

3. If the Provider modifies the incident type and saves, Then the system must record the previous type in the audit trail before applying the change.

4. If another Provider has edited the same incident since it was opened, Then the system must display a conflict warning with options to overwrite or reload.

5. If the Provider attempts to edit an incident that has been reviewed and closed by a supervisor, Then the system must display a message that the record is finalized and cannot be modified.
   Else if the Provider clicks Cancel after making changes, Then the system must prompt a confirmation dialog to discard unsaved edits.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that a Provider can open a minor incident in edit mode with all fields pre-populated with current values.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that a Provider can add a witness name that was initially missed and save; the updated witness list is displayed.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that correcting the incident location from "Hallway" to "Room 204" is saved and the audit trail records the change.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that updating the immediate actions taken with follow-up details is saved and visible on the record.

Negative:
5. Verify that 5. Verify that the system prevents submission with a required field error when the Provider clears the description and attempts to save.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that the system displays a validation error when the Provider clears the incident type and attempts to save.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that the system blocks editing with a finalized record notification when the Provider attempts to edit an incident that has been closed by a supervisor.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that 8. Verify that the edit form displays the same layout as the add form with all fields pre-populated and editable.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that a "Last Modified" indicator with date, time, and editor name is visible on the edited incident.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the system displays "No changes detected" when the Provider opens an incident for editing, makes no changes, and clicks Save.
11. Verify that 11. Verify that each successive edit creates a separate audit trail entry when the Provider edits an incident multiple times in succession.

Security:
12. Verify that 12. Verify that only the original reporter or users with "incidents_edit" permission can modify incident records, and other providers see the edit action disabled.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the incident edit form loads with pre-populated data within 1 second after clicking the edit action.
Verify that saving an edited incident with audit trail update completes within 2 seconds.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add major incident details
Description of User Story: The Add Major Incident feature allows providers to record serious incidents involving a resident that require elevated documentation and reporting. The form includes fields for incident date and time, incident type (e.g., serious fall with injury, fracture, hospitalization, elopement, abuse allegation, medication adverse event), location, detailed description, witness names, immediate actions and interventions, injuries sustained, and emergency services involvement. The provider must classify the severity as "Major" and indicate whether the incident is reportable to regulatory authorities. Upon saving, the system validates all required fields, persists the record, and triggers immediate notifications to the facility administrator, attending physician, and designated family contact. The incident is flagged prominently in the resident's charting record.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens the Add Major Incident form, Then the system must display the form with the current date, time, and provider name auto-populated and the severity pre-set to "Major".

2. If the Provider completes all required fields and clicks Save, Then the system must validate the entries, persist the incident, and trigger immediate notifications to the administrator, attending physician, and designated family contact.
   Else if the Provider clicks Save without entering a description, Then the system must display a validation error indicating the description is required.
   Else if the Provider clicks Save without selecting an incident type, Then the system must display a validation error requiring an incident type.

3. If the Provider selects "Abuse Allegation" as the incident type, Then the system must display additional mandatory fields for alleged perpetrator and reporting agency.
   Else if the Provider selects any other incident type, Then those additional fields must remain hidden.

4. If the Provider indicates emergency services were involved, Then the system must require fields for EMS agency, response time, and transport destination.
   Else if emergency services were not involved, Then those fields must remain optional and hidden.

5. If the Provider clicks Cancel after entering partial data, Then the system must prompt a confirmation dialog warning that major incidents must be documented promptly.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that a Provider can open the Add Major Incident form and see the current date, time, and provider name auto-populated with severity pre-set to "Major".
2. Verify that 2. Verify that a Provider can select "Serious Fall with Injury", document a hip fracture in the dining area, record EMS transport to the hospital, and save; the incident is stored with all details and notifications are sent to the administrator, physician, and family.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that a Provider can record an elopement incident with location "Main Entrance", witness names, and immediate search actions; all details are saved and the administrator is notified.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that marking an incident as reportable to regulatory authorities causes the system to generate a regulatory report template.

Negative:
5. Verify that 5. Verify that the system displays a required field validation error when the Provider attempts to save without a description.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that the system displays a validation error when the Provider attempts to save without selecting an incident type.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that the system requires the alleged perpetrator field before allowing save when the Provider selects "Abuse Allegation" but leaves that field empty.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that 8. Verify that the major incident form displays prominently labeled sections for incident details, injuries, emergency services, witnesses, and regulatory reporting.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that the form uses visual severity indicators such as a red border or header to distinguish it from the minor incident form.
10. Verify that 10. Verify that conditional fields such as EMS details and regulatory fields appear dynamically based on Provider selections.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that 11. Verify that a major incident recorded at 11:59 PM is correctly assigned to the current calendar date.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that the system accepts the record with the witness field empty but logs it when the Provider records a major incident with no witnesses present.

Security:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that only users with "major_incidents_create" permission can access the Add Major Incident form, and unauthorized roles cannot create major incident records.
14. Verify that 14. Verify that major incident records are encrypted at rest and access is logged for compliance auditing.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Add Major Incident form loads within 1 second after clicking the add button with all conditional field logic active.
Verify that saving a major incident with full details triggers all three notifications (administrator, physician, family) within 5 seconds.

Sub-Module: Patient Charting (Common for ADH and ALF)
User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit major incident details
Description of User Story: The Edit Major Incident feature allows providers to modify existing major incident records within a resident's charting profile. When the provider clicks the edit action on a major incident, the system opens the form pre-populated with all current fields including incident type, date and time, location, description, injuries, witness names, emergency services details, and regulatory reporting status. The provider can update any field to correct details, add follow-up information, or append investigation findings. Upon saving, the system validates the changes, updates the record, and maintains a comprehensive audit trail recording every modification with timestamps and editor identity. The updated incident triggers a re-notification to the facility administrator if critical fields such as injuries or incident type are changed.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider clicks the edit action on a major incident, Then the system must open the form pre-populated with all current values including the comprehensive audit history section.

2. If the Provider updates the description with investigation findings and clicks Save, Then the system must validate the updates and persist the changes with a new audit trail entry.
   Else if the Provider clears the description field and clicks Save, Then the system must display a validation error indicating the description is required.

3. If the Provider modifies a critical field such as incident type or injuries sustained, Then the system must record the change in the audit trail and re-notify the facility administrator.
   Else if only non-critical fields such as witness names are changed, Then the system must update the record without triggering a re-notification.

4. If another Provider has edited the same incident since it was opened, Then the system must display a conflict warning with options to overwrite or reload.

5. If the Provider attempts to edit a major incident that has been submitted to regulatory authorities, Then the system must display a warning that modifications will be tracked and may require an amended regulatory submission.
   Else if the Provider clicks Cancel after making changes, Then the system must prompt a confirmation dialog to discard unsaved edits.

9. Verify that 9. Verify that a comprehensive audit history section is visible below the form showing all previous modifications with timestamps.

User Story:
As a Provider, I should be able to view  and add Face Sheet section records in respective card for resident.
- Care Plans
- Allergies
- Medication
- Vitals
- Progress Notes
- Events
- Activities
Description of User Story: The Face Sheet screen presents a comprehensive overview of a resident's clinical and personal information organized into distinct card-based sections including Care Plans, Allergies, Medication, Vitals, Progress Notes, Events, and Activities. Each card displays a summary header with the section name, a record count badge, and a collapsible content area listing the most recent entries for that category. The Provider can expand any card to view all historical records within that section, with each entry showing the creation date, author, and a brief summary line. An "Add" button is positioned in the header area of each card, allowing the Provider to open the respective data entry form directly from the Face Sheet without navigating away. The Face Sheet layout uses a responsive grid so that cards rearrange appropriately on different screen sizes while maintaining readability. A global search bar at the top of the Face Sheet allows the Provider to search across all sections simultaneously to quickly locate specific records.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider navigates to the Face Sheet for a resident, Then the system must display all seven section cards (Care Plans, Allergies, Medication, Vitals, Progress Notes, Events, Activities) with their record count badges.

2. If the Provider clicks on a collapsed section card header, Then the system must expand the card to display all records in that section with creation date, author, and summary.
   Else if the Provider clicks on an expanded section card header, Then the system must collapse the card to show only the summary view.

3. When the Provider clicks the "Add" button on a section card, Then the system must open the corresponding data entry form for that section.

4. If a section has zero records, Then the system must display the card with a "No records found" message and keep the "Add" button visible and functional.

5. If the Provider enters a search term in the global search bar, Then the system must filter and highlight matching records across all section cards.
   Else if the search term yields no results in any section, Then the system must display a "No matching records found" message below the search bar.
Test Scenarios:

2. Verify that 2. Verify that expanding a section card displays all records with creation date, author, and summary for each entry.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that collapsing an expanded card returns it to the summary view with only the header and record count badge visible.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that clicking the "Add" button on the Care Plans card opens the Care Plan data entry form.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that clicking the "Add" button on each of the remaining six section cards opens the correct respective data entry form.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that the global search bar returns matching records across multiple sections simultaneously when a keyword like "insulin" is entered.

Negative:

8. Verify that 8. Verify that entering a search term with no matches displays a "No matching records found" message.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that the Face Sheet displays an appropriate error message when resident data fails to load due to a service error.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the Face Sheet layout uses a responsive card grid that rearranges properly on tablet and desktop screen sizes.
11. Verify that 11. Verify that each section card header displays the section name, record count badge, and "Add" button in a consistent layout.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that the global search bar is positioned at the top of the Face Sheet and is clearly visible.

Edge Cases:

Performance Scenarios:

User Story: As a Provider , I should be able to add Care Plan of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Care Plan creation form allows the Provider to create a new care plan record for a resident within the ADH Patient Charting module. The form includes fields for care plan title, care plan type dropdown selection, start date and target end date pickers, goals text area for documenting specific clinical or behavioral goals, interventions text area for detailing the planned clinical actions, and a responsible staff member dropdown. A priority selector allows the Provider to classify the care plan as High, Medium, or Low priority using radio buttons or a dropdown. The form validates that all required fields including title, type, start date, and at least one goal are populated before allowing submission. Upon successful save, the system records the creation timestamp and the authoring Provider identity, and the new care plan appears in the resident Face Sheet Care Plans section.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider fills in all required fields (title, type, start date, at least one goal) and clicks "Save", Then the system must create the care plan record and display a success confirmation message.

2. If the Provider clicks "Save" without entering a care plan title, Then the system must display an inline validation error "Care plan title is required" on the title field.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Save" without selecting a care plan type, Then the system must display an inline validation error "Care plan type is required" on the type dropdown.

3. If the Provider clicks "Save" without setting a start date, Then the system must display an inline validation error "Start date is required" on the date picker.

4. If the Provider selects a target end date that is before the start date, Then the system must display a validation error "Target end date must be after start date".

5. If the Provider clicks "Cancel" on the form, Then the system must discard all entered data and navigate back to the previous view without saving.

3. Verify that 3. Verify that the newly created care plan appears in the resident Face Sheet Care Plans section card after saving.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that the Provider can select High, Medium, or Low priority and the selection is saved correctly with the care plan.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that the responsible staff dropdown lists available staff members and allows selection.

7. Verify that 7. Verify that clicking "Save" without selecting a care plan type displays an inline validation error "Care plan type is required".
8. Verify that 8. Verify that clicking "Save" without a start date displays an inline validation error "Start date is required".
9. Verify that 9. Verify that selecting a target end date earlier than the start date displays a validation error "Target end date must be after start date".

10. Verify that 10. Verify that the care plan type dropdown displays all available care plan categories in a selectable list.
11. Verify that 11. Verify that the start date and target end date fields use date pickers with proper calendar rendering.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that the "Save" and "Cancel" buttons are clearly visible at the bottom of the form.

User Story: As a Provider , I should be able to view list of Care Plan for the resident
Description of User Story: The Care Plan list view displays all care plans associated with a specific resident in a tabular format within the ADH Patient Charting module. Each row in the table shows the care plan title, type, priority level with a color-coded badge, start date, target end date, status indicator (Active, Completed, or Discontinued), and the authoring Provider name. The list is sorted by default in descending order by creation date so the most recent care plans appear at the top. Pagination controls at the bottom of the table allow the Provider to navigate through large sets of care plan records. Clicking on any row in the list navigates the Provider to the detailed care plan view showing full goals, interventions, modification history, and all associated metadata. A total record count is displayed above the table header indicating how many care plans exist for the resident.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the resident has one or more care plans, Then the system must display them in the table sorted by creation date descending.
   Else if the resident has no care plans, Then the system must display a "No care plans found" message in the table area.

2. When the Provider clicks on a care plan row, Then the system must navigate to the detailed care plan view for the selected record.

3. If the total number of care plans exceeds the page size, Then the system must display pagination controls at the bottom of the table.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able search and filter care plan of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Care Plan list view includes a search bar and filter controls that enable the Provider to locate specific care plan records efficiently for a resident in the ADH Patient Charting module. The search bar accepts free-text input and performs a keyword match against the care plan title, goals, and interventions fields, displaying matching results as the Provider types. Filter dropdown controls allow the Provider to narrow results by care plan type, priority level (High, Medium, Low), status (Active, Completed, Discontinued), and date range using start and end date pickers. Multiple filters can be applied simultaneously and the result set updates dynamically to reflect the combined filter criteria. A "Clear Filters" button resets all active filters and restores the full care plan list for the resident.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider enters a keyword in the search bar, Then the system must filter the list to show only care plans with matching text in title, goals, or interventions.

2. If the Provider selects a care plan type filter, Then the system must display only care plans of the selected type.

3. If the Provider selects a priority filter, Then the system must display only care plans matching the selected priority level (e.g., High).

4. If the Provider selects a status filter, Then the system must display only care plans matching the selected status (Active, Completed, or Discontinued).

5. If the Provider applies multiple filters simultaneously, Then the system must display only care plans matching all applied filter criteria.
   Else if no care plans match the applied filters or search term, Then the system must display a "No matching care plans found" message.

6. When the Provider clicks "Clear Filters", Then the system must reset all active filters and restore the complete care plan list.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:

2. Verify that 2. Verify that selecting a care plan type from the filter dropdown narrows the list to only matching types.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that selecting a priority level filter "High" displays only care plans with High priority.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that selecting a status filter "Active" displays only Active care plans.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that applying a date range filter of 01/01/2025–03/31/2025 shows only care plans with start dates within that range.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that combining multiple filters simultaneously returns only care plans matching all criteria.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that clicking "Clear Filters" resets all active filters and restores the complete care plan list.

Negative:
8. Verify that 8. Verify that searching with a keyword that does not match any care plan displays a "No matching care plans found" message.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that applying conflicting filters (e.g., a date range with no records) returns an empty result with an appropriate message.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the search bar is positioned above the care plan list and accepts free-text input.
11. Verify that 11. Verify that filter dropdowns for type, priority, and status are clearly labeled and display selectable options.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that the "Clear Filters" button is visible when one or more filters are active.

Edge Cases:

User Story: As a Provider , I should be able to add Events of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Event creation form allows the Provider to record a new event for a resident within the ADH Patient Charting module, capturing clinical or behavioral incidents that occur during the care day. The form includes fields for event title, event type dropdown (such as Fall, Behavioral Incident, Medical Emergency, Seizure, or Other), event date and time pickers, severity dropdown (Minor, Moderate, Severe), a detailed description text area, and an action taken text area for documenting the immediate response. An optional witness name field and a file attachment area for supporting documents or images are also available. The form validates that required fields including event title, type, date and time, and description are completed before allowing submission. Upon successful save, the event record is timestamped with the authoring Provider identity and appears in the resident Face Sheet Events section.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider fills in all required fields (title, type, date, time, description) and clicks "Save", Then the system must create the event record and display a success confirmation message.

2. If the Provider clicks "Save" without entering an event title, Then the system must display an inline validation error "Event title is required".
   Else if the Provider clicks "Save" without selecting an event type, Then the system must display an inline validation error "Event type is required".

3. If the Provider clicks "Save" without setting an event date and time, Then the system must display inline validation errors on the date and time fields.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Save" without entering a description, Then the system must display an inline validation error "Description is required".

4. If the Provider attaches a file and saves, Then the system must store and associate the attached file with the event record.
   Else if the Provider uploads an unsupported file type, Then the system must display an error indicating the file format is not supported.

5. If the Provider clicks "Cancel", Then the system must discard all entered data and navigate back to the previous view without saving.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that the Event creation form loads with all fields (title, type, date, time, severity, description, action taken, witness, file attachment) displayed and editable.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that entering all required fields and clicking "Save" creates a new event with the correct timestamp and authoring Provider.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that the newly created event appears in the resident Face Sheet Events section card.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that selecting a severity level "Severe" saves correctly with the event record.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that attaching a valid PDF file to the event stores and associates the file with the event record.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that the action taken text area accepts and saves the Provider's documented response.

Negative:
7. Verify that 7. Verify that clicking "Save" without an event title displays an inline validation error "Event title is required".
8. Verify that 8. Verify that clicking "Save" without selecting an event type displays an inline validation error "Event type is required".
9. Verify that 9. Verify that clicking "Save" without a description displays an inline validation error "Description is required".

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the event type dropdown lists all available event categories (Fall, Behavioral Incident, Medical Emergency, Seizure, Other).
11. Verify that 11. Verify that the date and time pickers render correctly and allow proper selection.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that the file attachment area supports drag-and-drop and file browse functionality.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that 13. Verify event creation when the description text area contains the maximum allowed character length.
14. Verify that 14. Verify behavior when the Provider uploads an unsupported file type such as .exe in the attachment area.

Security:
15. Verify that 15. Verify that only authenticated Providers with charting permissions can access the Event creation form for a resident.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the Event creation form loads with all fields and dropdown options within 2 seconds.
Verify saving a new event with a file attachment completes and returns a success confirmation within 4 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider , I should be able to edit Events of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Event edit form allows the Provider to modify an existing event record for a resident within the ADH Patient Charting module. When the Provider opens an event for editing, the form is pre-populated with all previously saved data including event title, type, date and time, severity, description, action taken, witness name, and any attached files. The Provider can update any editable field and add or remove file attachments as needed. A "Reason for Edit" text field is required before saving to maintain a complete audit trail of all modifications to event records. The form enforces the same validation rules as the creation form, ensuring required fields remain populated. Upon saving, the system updates the event record with the new values, records the modification timestamp and editing Provider identity, and displays a success confirmation message.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider modifies fields and provides a valid reason for edit, Then the system must update the event record and display a success confirmation.

2. If the Provider clicks "Save" without entering a reason for edit, Then the system must display an inline validation error "Reason for edit is required" and prevent submission.

3. If the Provider clears the event title field during editing, Then the system must display a validation error "Event title is required" on save attempt.
   Else if the Provider clears the description field during editing, Then the system must display a validation error "Description is required" on save attempt.

4. If the Provider adds a new file attachment during editing and saves, Then the system must associate the new file with the event record.
   Else if the Provider removes an existing file attachment during editing and saves, Then the system must disassociate the file from the event record.

5. If the Provider clicks "Cancel" after making changes, Then the system must discard all unsaved modifications and navigate back to the previous view.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that the event edit form loads pre-populated with all existing event data including title, type, date, time, severity, description, action taken, witness, and attachments.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that modifying the event title and saving with a reason for edit updates the record correctly.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that modifying the severity level from Minor to Severe saves the updated value.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that adding a new file attachment during editing is saved with the event record.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that removing an existing file attachment during editing disassociates it from the event record.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that the modification timestamp and editing Provider identity are recorded in the audit trail.

Negative:
7. Verify that 7. Verify that clicking "Save" without entering a reason for edit displays an inline validation error "Reason for edit is required".
8. Verify that 8. Verify that clearing the event title and clicking "Save" displays a required field validation error.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that clearing the description field and clicking "Save" displays a required field validation error.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the "Reason for Edit" text field is prominently displayed and clearly labeled as required.
11. Verify that 11. Verify that pre-populated fields retain their formatting and values when the edit form loads.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that existing file attachments are displayed with options to view, download, or remove.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that 13. Verify edit behavior when two Providers attempt to edit the same event simultaneously and a concurrency conflict arises.
14. Verify that 14. Verify behavior when the Provider edits an event that was created more than 90 days ago.

Security:
15. Verify that 15. Verify that only the authoring Provider or a Provider with elevated charting permissions can edit the event record.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the event edit form loads with pre-populated data and attachments within 2 seconds.
Verify saving an edited event with updated attachments completes and displays the success confirmation within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add  pain scale for the residents.
Description of User Story: The Pain Scale creation form allows the Provider to record a new pain assessment for a resident within the ADH Patient Charting module. The form includes a visual numeric pain scale ranging from 0 (No Pain) to 10 (Worst Possible Pain) presented as clickable numbered icons or a slider control for intuitive selection. Additional fields include assessment date and time pickers, pain location body map or dropdown selection, pain type dropdown (such as Sharp, Dull, Throbbing, Burning, or Aching), duration field for how long the pain has been present, and a notes text area for additional clinical observations. The form validates that required fields including pain score, date and time, and pain location are completed before allowing submission. Upon successful save, the pain assessment is timestamped with the authoring Provider identity and appears in the resident Face Sheet with the recorded pain score prominently displayed.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider selects a pain score and fills in all required fields (date, time, pain location) and clicks "Save", Then the system must create the pain assessment record and display a success confirmation message.

2. If the Provider clicks "Save" without selecting a pain score, Then the system must display an inline validation error "Pain score is required".
   Else if the Provider clicks "Save" without setting an assessment date and time, Then the system must display inline validation errors on the date and time fields.

3. If the Provider clicks "Save" without selecting a pain location, Then the system must display an inline validation error "Pain location is required".

4. If the Provider selects a pain score of 0, Then the system must record the assessment as "No Pain" and save the record normally.

5. If the Provider clicks "Cancel", Then the system must discard all entered data and navigate back to the previous view without saving.

6. Verify that 6. Verify that entering pain duration of "2 hours" and notes saves the values correctly with the assessment.

9. Verify that 9. Verify that clicking "Save" without setting a date and time displays inline validation errors on both fields.

10. Verify that 10. Verify that the visual pain scale displays numbers 0 through 10 with descriptive labels (No Pain to Worst Possible Pain) and appropriate color gradients.
