13. Verify that 13. Verify that the Pain Scale list is accessible only to authenticated Providers with charting permissions for the selected resident.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the Pain Scale list loads and renders within 3 seconds for a resident with 100 or more assessment records.
Verify pagination navigation loads the next page of records within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to search and filter pain scale of the residents.
Description of User Story: The Pain Scale list view includes a search bar and filter controls that enable the Provider to locate specific pain assessment records efficiently for a resident in the ADH Patient Charting module. The search bar accepts free-text input and performs keyword matching against pain location and notes fields, updating results as the Provider types. Filter dropdown controls allow the Provider to narrow results by pain score range (0-3 Mild, 4-6 Moderate, 7-10 Severe), pain type, status (Active, Historical), and date range using start and end date pickers. Multiple filters can be applied simultaneously and the result set updates dynamically to reflect the combined criteria. A "Clear Filters" button resets all active filters and the search bar, restoring the complete pain assessment list for the resident.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider enters a keyword in the search bar, Then the system must filter the list to show only assessments with matching text in pain location or notes.

2. If the Provider selects a pain score range filter, Then the system must display only assessments with scores falling within the selected range (Mild 0–3, Moderate 4–6, Severe 7–10).

3. If the Provider selects a pain type filter, Then the system must display only assessments of the selected pain type (e.g., Sharp).

4. If the Provider selects a status filter, Then the system must display only assessments matching the selected status (Active or Historical).

5. If the Provider applies multiple filters simultaneously, Then the system must display only assessments matching all applied filter criteria.
   Else if no assessments match the applied filters or search term, Then the system must display a "No matching pain assessments found" message.

6. When the Provider clicks "Clear Filters", Then the system must reset all active filters and the search bar and restore the complete pain assessment list.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that entering a keyword such as "lower back" in the search bar filters the list to show only assessments with matching text in pain location or notes.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that selecting a pain score range filter "Severe (7–10)" narrows the list to matching assessments.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that selecting a pain type filter "Throbbing" displays only assessments with the corresponding pain type.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that selecting a status filter "Active" displays only Active assessments.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that applying a date range filter of 01/01/2025–03/31/2025 shows only assessments within that range.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that combining multiple filters simultaneously returns only assessments matching all criteria.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that clicking "Clear Filters" resets all active filters and restores the complete list.

Negative:
8. Verify that 8. Verify that searching with a keyword that does not match any assessment displays a "No matching pain assessments found" message.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that applying a date range with no assessments returns an empty result with an appropriate message.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the search bar is positioned above the list and accepts free-text input.
11. Verify that 11. Verify that filter dropdowns for score range, pain type, and status are clearly labeled and display selectable options.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that the "Clear Filters" button is visible when one or more filters are active.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that 13. Verify search behavior when the Provider enters special characters such as "!$%" in the search bar.
14. Verify that 14. Verify filter behavior when the Provider selects the Severe score range (7–10) and no assessments have scores in that range.

Security:
15. Verify that 15. Verify that search and filter operations only return pain assessment records the authenticated Provider is authorized to view for the selected resident.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify search results update within 2 seconds after the Provider enters a keyword for a resident with 100 or more pain assessments.
Verify applying multiple filters simultaneously returns filtered results within 2 seconds.

User Story:
As a Provider, I should be able to view the Face Sheet section details in the respective cards for a resident.
-Tickets
-Allergies
-Medication
-Vitals
-Progress Notes
-Incidents
-Activities
Description of User Story: The Face Sheet screen within the ALF Patient Charting module presents the Provider with a comprehensive summary view of a resident organized into distinct card-based sections. Each card represents a specific clinical or operational domain including Tickets, Allergies, Medication, Vitals, Progress Notes, Incidents, and Activities, displaying the most recent or relevant data points in a compact card layout. The Tickets card shows the count of open and resolved tickets with the most recent ticket subject and date. The Allergies card lists known allergies with severity indicators such as mild, moderate, or severe using color-coded badges. The Medication card displays currently active medications with dosage and frequency, while the Vitals card shows the latest recorded vitals including blood pressure, heart rate, temperature, and oxygen saturation with timestamps. The Progress Notes card presents the most recent note entry with author and date, the Incidents card shows recent incident reports with severity levels, and the Activities card displays scheduled and completed activities for the resident.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider navigates to the Face Sheet for a resident, Then the system must display all seven cards (Tickets, Allergies, Medication, Vitals, Progress Notes, Incidents, Activities) with their respective summary data.

2. If the resident has recorded allergies, Then the system must display all allergies with their severity badges on the Allergies card.
   Else if the resident has no recorded allergies, Then the system must display a "No Known Allergies" label on the Allergies card.

3. If the resident has active medications, Then the system must display the medication list with dosage and frequency on the Medication card.
   Else if the resident has no active medications, Then the system must display "No Active Medications" on the Medication card.

4. If vitals have been recorded within the last 24 hours, Then the system must display the latest vitals with a current timestamp on the Vitals card.
   Else if no vitals have been recorded within the last 24 hours, Then the system must display the last available vitals with a warning indicator showing the elapsed time since the last recording.

5. When the Provider clicks on any card, Then the system must navigate to the detailed view for that specific section.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that the Face Sheet loads with all seven cards displayed for a resident with complete data across all sections.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that the Allergies card displays known allergies such as "Penicillin - Severe" with correct severity color-coded badges.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that the Medication card shows active medications such as "Metformin 500mg twice daily" with dosage and frequency details.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that the Vitals card displays the most recent vitals including BP 120/80, HR 72, Temp 98.6°F with accurate timestamps.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that clicking on a card navigates to the detailed view of that section.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that the Tickets card shows the correct count of open and resolved tickets with the most recent ticket details.

Negative:
7. Verify that 7. Verify that the Allergies card displays "No Known Allergies" when no allergies are recorded for the resident.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that the Medication card displays "No Active Medications" when the resident has no active prescriptions.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that the Vitals card shows a warning indicator when no vitals have been recorded within the last 24 hours.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that all seven cards are rendered in a responsive card layout with consistent spacing and alignment on the ALF Patient Charting screen.
11. Verify that 11. Verify that severity badges on the Allergies card use distinct colors for mild, moderate, and severe levels (e.g., yellow, orange, red).
12. Verify that 12. Verify that each card header includes the section name and a clickable link or icon for navigation to the detailed view.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that the Face Sheet displays correctly when a resident has data in only some sections and empty data in others.
14. Verify that 14. Verify that the Medication card handles truncation or scrolling when more than 20 active medications are present.

Security:
15. Verify that 15. Verify that only authenticated Providers with charting access permissions can view the Face Sheet section for a resident.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the Face Sheet loads all seven cards with summary data within 3 seconds for a resident with extensive records.
Verify clicking on a card navigates to the detailed section view within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add services of the resident
Description of User Story: The Add Services modal allows the Provider to assign one or more facility services to a resident within the ALF Patient Charting module. The modal opens with a search bar at the top that enables the Provider to type and filter available services in real time from the Services List. The Services List section displays all available facility services such as Laundry, Meals, Housekeeping, Transportation, Personal Care Assistance, and Recreational Activities, each with a checkbox for selection. As the Provider selects services, a cost summary panel on the right side of the modal dynamically updates to show the selected service names, individual costs, and a total cost calculation. The Provider can select multiple services simultaneously using the checkboxes and review the aggregated cost before confirming. The modal footer contains Cancel and Save buttons where Cancel discards all selections and closes the modal, and Save persists the selected services to the resident record.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens the Add Services modal, Then the system must display the search bar, Services List with checkboxes, and cost summary panel.

2. If the Provider types a search query in the search bar, Then the system must filter the Services List to display only services whose names match the search text.
   Else if the search query does not match any services, Then the system must display a "No services found" message in the Services List area.

3. If the Provider selects one or more services, Then the system must update the cost summary panel with the selected service names, individual costs, and total cost.
   Else if the Provider deselects a previously selected service, Then the system must remove it from the cost summary panel and recalculate the total cost.

4. If the Provider clicks "Save" with at least one service selected, Then the system must persist the services to the resident record and close the modal.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Save" without selecting any services, Then the system must display a validation message indicating at least one service must be selected.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that the Add Services modal opens with a search bar, Services List with checkboxes, and cost summary panel.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that typing "Laundry" in the search bar filters the Services List in real time to display only matching services.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that selecting a service checkbox such as "Meals - $150/month" updates the cost summary panel with the service name and cost.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that selecting multiple services such as "Laundry", "Housekeeping", and "Transportation" displays all selected services with individual costs and correct total.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that clicking "Save" persists all selected services to the resident record and closes the modal.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that deselecting a service removes it from the cost summary and recalculates the total correctly.

Negative:
7. Verify that 7. Verify that clicking "Save" without selecting any service displays a validation message requiring at least one selection.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that entering a search query such as "XYZ Service" that matches no services displays "No services found".
9. Verify that 9. Verify that clicking "Cancel" discards all selections and closes the modal without saving.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the search bar is positioned at the top of the modal with placeholder text such as "Search services".
11. Verify that 11. Verify that the Services List displays services like Laundry, Meals, and Housekeeping with checkboxes aligned consistently.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that the cost summary panel is displayed on the right side of the modal with clear formatting for service names and costs.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that the cost summary panel handles the full list with scrolling when the Provider selects all available services.
14. Verify that 14. Verify that the cost summary recalculates correctly each time when the Provider rapidly selects and deselects services.

Security:
15. Verify that 15. Verify that only authenticated Providers with service management permissions can access the Add Services modal for a resident.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the Add Services modal loads the complete Services List within 2 seconds.
Verify the cost summary panel updates dynamically within 1 second after selecting or deselecting a service.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit services of the resident
Description of User Story: The Edit Services functionality allows the Provider to modify previously assigned services for a resident within the ALF Patient Charting module. When the Provider selects a service entry from the services list and clicks the edit action, the Edit Services modal opens pre-populated with the current service selections and their associated cost details. The Provider can add new services by checking additional checkboxes from the Services List or remove existing services by unchecking previously selected items. The search bar at the top allows the Provider to filter the Services List to locate specific services quickly. The cost summary panel dynamically recalculates the total cost as services are added or removed. The modal footer contains Cancel and Save buttons where Cancel reverts to the original service selections and Save persists the updated services to the resident record with an audit log entry recording the change.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When the Provider opens the Edit Services modal, Then the system must display pre-populated checkboxes for all currently assigned services with their costs in the summary panel.

2. If the Provider adds a new service by checking its checkbox, Then the system must update the cost summary panel to include the new service and recalculate the total cost.
   Else if the Provider removes an existing service by unchecking its checkbox, Then the system must remove the service from the cost summary panel and recalculate the total cost.

3. If the Provider clicks "Save" with at least one service remaining selected, Then the system must persist the updated service list and create an audit log entry.
   Else if the Provider attempts to remove all services leaving none selected, Then the system must display a validation message that at least one service must remain assigned.

4. If the Provider searches for a service in the search bar, Then the system must filter the list while preserving the checked state of all currently selected services.

5. If the Provider clicks "Cancel", Then the system must revert all changes and close the modal with the original service assignments intact.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that the Edit Services modal opens with pre-populated checkboxes for currently assigned services.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that the cost summary panel reflects the current service costs upon modal load.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that adding a new service such as "Personal Care Assistance" updates the cost summary and recalculates the total.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that removing an existing service such as "Laundry" updates the cost summary and recalculates the total.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that clicking "Save" persists updated services and creates an audit log entry.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that using the search bar to filter services retains the checked states of already selected services.

Negative:
7. Verify that 7. Verify that attempting to remove all services displays a validation message requiring at least one service to remain.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that clicking "Cancel" reverts all changes and retains the original service assignments.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that clicking "Save" with no changes made does not create a duplicate audit log entry.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the Edit Services modal layout matches the Add Services modal with search bar, Services List, and cost summary panel.
11. Verify that 11. Verify that pre-selected services are visually distinguished with checked checkboxes upon modal load.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that the Cancel and Save buttons are positioned in the modal footer with appropriate styling.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that the modal handles cost recalculation correctly when the cost of a service has changed since the original assignment.
14. Verify that 14. Verify that a previously assigned service that has been deactivated in the system is displayed with a deactivated indicator.

Security:
15. Verify that 15. Verify that only authenticated Providers with service management permissions can edit services for a resident.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the Edit Services modal loads with pre-populated data within 2 seconds.
Verify saving updated services persists changes and updates the services list within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to view list of services for the resident.
Description of User Story: The Services List screen within the ALF Patient Charting module displays a comprehensive table of all services currently assigned to a resident. The list view presents each service entry as a row containing the service name, category, assigned date, cost, status (Active or Inactive), and the name of the Provider who assigned the service. The table header includes column labels with sortable functionality allowing the Provider to sort by any column in ascending or descending order. A summary row at the bottom of the table displays the total number of active services and the aggregate monthly cost. The list view includes action buttons for Add, Edit, and View Details operations on each service row. Pagination controls at the bottom of the table allow the Provider to navigate between pages when the number of services exceeds the default display limit.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the resident has one or more assigned services, Then the system must display all services in the table with their corresponding details including name, category, assigned date, cost, status, and assigning provider.
   Else if the resident has no assigned services, Then the system must display an empty state message such as "No services assigned to this resident".

2. When the Provider clicks a column header to sort, Then the system must re-order the table rows based on the selected column and toggle sort direction on each subsequent click.

3. If the total number of services exceeds the default page size, Then the system must display pagination controls at the bottom of the table.

4. If the Provider clicks "Add", Then the system must open the Add Services modal for the resident.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Edit" on a service row, Then the system must open the Edit Services modal pre-populated with that service entry data.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that the services list table loads with correct columns for service name, category, assigned date, cost, status, and assigning provider.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that clicking a column header such as "Cost" sorts the table in ascending order, and clicking again sorts in descending order.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that the summary row displays the total active services count and aggregate monthly cost accurately.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that pagination controls navigate between pages when services exceed the default page size of 10 or 20 entries.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that clicking "View Details" opens the detailed view of the selected service.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that clicking "Add" opens the Add Services modal.

Negative:
7. Verify that 7. Verify that an empty state message "No services assigned to this resident" is displayed when no services are assigned.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that the list does not display services from other residents in the same facility.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that an appropriate error message is displayed when the services list API call returns an error.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the table layout displays columns with consistent alignment and readable font sizes.
11. Verify that 11. Verify that status values display as colored badges distinguishing Active (green) from Inactive (gray) services.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that the summary row is visually distinct from regular table rows with a highlighted background.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that the table handles a resident with 100 or more assigned services with proper pagination.
14. Verify that 14. Verify that table rows with missing or null values for optional fields display a placeholder such as "—" instead of blank.

Security:
15. Verify that 15. Verify that only authenticated Providers with charting access can view the services list for a resident.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the services list table loads with all entries within 2 seconds for a resident with up to 50 services.
Verify pagination navigation between pages completes within 1 second.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to search and filter services of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Services List screen includes search and filter capabilities that allow the Provider to quickly locate specific services assigned to a resident. A search bar at the top of the services table enables the Provider to type keywords that filter the list in real time by matching against service name, category, or assigning provider name. Advanced filter controls are available through a filter dropdown or panel that allows the Provider to filter services by status (Active, Inactive, All), category (Personal Care, Housekeeping, Meals, Transportation, Recreational, Other), date range for the assigned date, and cost range. Applied filters are displayed as removable chips or tags above the table so the Provider can see and manage active filter criteria. A "Clear All Filters" button resets all filter selections and restores the full services list. The search and filter operations work together so the Provider can combine text search with dropdown filters for precise results.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider enters a search keyword, Then the system must filter the services list to show only rows where the service name, category, or assigning provider matches the keyword.
   Else if the search keyword yields no matching results, Then the system must display a "No matching services found" message in the table area.

2. If the Provider selects a status filter, Then the system must display only services matching the selected status.

3. If the Provider selects multiple filters simultaneously, Then the system must apply all filters in combination and display only services matching all criteria.

4. If the Provider removes a filter chip, Then the system must remove that specific filter and refresh the table with the remaining filters applied.

5. If the Provider clicks "Clear All Filters", Then the system must reset all filters and search text and display the complete services list.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that typing "Housekeeping" in the search bar filters the services list in real time to display matching services.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that the search bar matches against category and assigning provider name in addition to service name.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that selecting the "Active" status filter shows only services with Active status.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that selecting a category filter such as "Meals" narrows the list to services in that category only.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that applied filters are displayed as removable chips above the table.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that clicking "Clear All Filters" resets all filters and restores the full services list.

Negative:
7. Verify that 7. Verify that a "No matching services found" message is displayed when the search keyword such as "XYZ" matches no services.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that applying conflicting filters resulting in no matching records displays an empty state message.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that the search bar handles special characters such as "<script>" without causing system errors.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the search bar is positioned at the top of the services table with a search icon and placeholder text such as "Search by name, category, or provider".
11. Verify that 11. Verify that filter chips are displayed as removable tags with an "x" icon for each applied filter.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that the filter dropdown or panel displays all available filter options with clear labels.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that combining a text search with multiple dropdown filters such as status "Active" and category "Meals" returns correct results.
14. Verify that 14. Verify that typing a partial keyword such as "House" incrementally updates the list to show "Housekeeping" and related services.

Security:
15. Verify that 15. Verify that search and filter operations do not expose services belonging to other residents.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the search bar filters results in real time within 500 milliseconds of each keystroke.
Verify applying multiple filters simultaneously refreshes the table within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add support or maintenance ticket for facility issues.
Description of User Story: The Add Ticket form allows the Provider to create a support or maintenance ticket for facility issues affecting a resident within the ALF Patient Charting module. The form includes fields for ticket subject as a text input, issue category as a dropdown with options such as Plumbing, Electrical, HVAC, Furniture, Housekeeping, Safety, and Other, and a priority level selector with options Low, Medium, High, and Urgent. A detailed description text area allows the Provider to explain the issue with a minimum of 30 characters to ensure adequate detail. The form includes a location or room field auto-populated with the resident assigned room number and a file attachment section where the Provider can upload photos or documents related to the issue. The form footer contains Cancel and Submit buttons where Cancel discards the form and Submit creates the ticket, assigns it a unique ticket number, and sets the initial status to Open.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider fills all required fields and clicks "Submit", Then the system must create the ticket with a unique number, set status to Open, and display a confirmation message.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Submit" without entering a subject, Then the system must display a validation error on the subject field.
   Else if the Provider enters a description with fewer than 30 characters, Then the system must display a validation error indicating the minimum character requirement.
   Else if the Provider selects "Other" as the issue category, Then the system must enable an additional text field for specifying the custom category.
   Else if the Provider uploads a file exceeding the maximum allowed size, Then the system must display an error message indicating the file size limit.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Cancel", Then the system must discard all entered data and close the form without creating a ticket.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that the Add Ticket form opens with all required fields displayed including subject, category, priority, description, location, and file attachment.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that submitting a completed form creates a ticket with a unique ticket number such as "TKT-00123" and Open status.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that the location field is auto-populated with the resident assigned room number such as "Room 204".
4. Verify that 4. Verify that uploading photos or documents such as a JPEG or PDF attaches them to the ticket with file names and sizes displayed.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that selecting "Other" as category enables an additional text field for custom category entry.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that the priority selector displays Low, Medium, High, and Urgent options.

Negative:
7. Verify that 7. Verify that submitting the form without entering a subject displays a validation error on the subject field.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that submitting with a description of fewer than 30 characters displays a minimum character validation error.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that uploading a file exceeding the maximum size limit such as a file over 10 MB displays an error message.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the category dropdown displays all predefined options (Plumbing, Electrical, HVAC, Furniture, Housekeeping, Safety, Other).
11. Verify that 11. Verify that the priority selector visually distinguishes Low, Medium, High, and Urgent levels with colors or icons.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that the Cancel and Submit buttons are positioned in the form footer with appropriate styling.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that the form handles submission when the description field contains the maximum allowed number of characters.
14. Verify that 14. Verify that uploading multiple files simultaneously totaling near the attachment size limit completes without error.

Security:
15. Verify that 15. Verify that only authenticated Providers with ticket creation permissions can access and submit the Add Ticket form.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the Add Ticket form loads with all fields and dropdown options within 2 seconds.
Verify submitting a ticket with file attachments creates the record and returns a confirmation within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit support or maintenance ticket for facility issues.
Description of User Story: The Edit Ticket form allows the Provider to modify an existing support or maintenance ticket for facility issues within the ALF Patient Charting module. When the Provider selects an existing ticket and clicks the edit action, the form opens pre-populated with the current ticket details including subject, category, priority, description, location, attached files, and current status. The Provider can update any editable field such as the subject, category, priority level, and description text while the ticket number and creation date remain read-only. The status field can be updated through a dropdown with options such as Open, In Progress, On Hold, Resolved, and Closed. The Provider can add new file attachments or remove previously attached files. The form footer contains Cancel and Save buttons where Cancel reverts to the original values and Save persists the updates with an audit trail entry recording the changes made, the timestamp, and the Provider who made the edit.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider modifies ticket fields and clicks "Save", Then the system must persist the updates and create an audit trail entry with the changes, timestamp, and Provider name.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Save" without making any changes, Then the system must close the form without creating an audit trail entry.

2. If the Provider changes the status to "Resolved" or "Closed", Then the system must require a resolution comment before allowing the save.

3. If the Provider attempts to edit a ticket with "Closed" status, Then the system must display a warning that the ticket must be reopened before editing.

4. If the Provider removes all attachments and the ticket requires documentation, Then the system must display a warning but allow saving.

5. If the Provider clicks "Cancel", Then the system must revert all changes and close the form without persisting any modifications.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that the Edit Ticket form opens with all fields pre-populated from the existing ticket.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that modifying the subject, category, priority, and description updates the form values.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that changing the status dropdown to "In Progress" records the new status value.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that clicking "Save" persists updates and creates an audit trail entry.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that adding a new attachment appends it to the existing attachment list.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that removing an existing attachment updates the attachment list.

Negative:
7. Verify that 7. Verify that clicking "Cancel" reverts all changes and closes the form without saving.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that changing status to "Resolved" without entering a resolution comment prevents saving.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that attempting to edit a "Closed" ticket displays a warning that it must be reopened first.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the ticket number and creation date are displayed as read-only fields.
11. Verify that 11. Verify that the status dropdown displays all available status options (Open, In Progress, On Hold, Resolved, Closed).
12. Verify that 12. Verify that the Cancel and Save buttons are positioned in the form footer with consistent styling.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that the form handles the case where two Providers attempt to edit the same ticket simultaneously with an appropriate conflict message.
14. Verify that 14. Verify that the form displays an appropriate indicator when the original ticket category has been deactivated in the system.

Security:
15. Verify that 15. Verify that only authenticated Providers with ticket edit permissions can modify existing tickets.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the Edit Ticket form loads with pre-populated data within 2 seconds.
Verify saving ticket updates persists changes and returns confirmation within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to view list of support or maintenance ticket for facility issues.
Description of User Story: The Tickets List screen within the ALF Patient Charting module displays a comprehensive table of all support and maintenance tickets associated with a resident. Each row in the table presents the ticket number, subject, category, priority level with color-coded indicators, current status, creation date, last updated date, and the name of the Provider who created the ticket. The table supports column-based sorting allowing the Provider to sort tickets by any column in ascending or descending order. A status summary bar above the table shows the count of tickets grouped by status (Open, In Progress, On Hold, Resolved, Closed) with clickable badges that act as quick filters. Each row includes action buttons for View Details and Edit operations. Pagination controls at the bottom allow navigation when the ticket count exceeds the default page size.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the resident has one or more tickets, Then the system must display all tickets in the table with their corresponding details.
   Else if the resident has no tickets, Then the system must display an empty state message such as "No tickets found for this resident".

2. When the Provider clicks a status badge in the summary bar, Then the system must filter the table to show only tickets with that status.

3. If the Provider clicks "View Details" on a ticket, Then the system must open the detailed view showing the full ticket information and history.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Edit" on a ticket, Then the system must open the Edit Ticket form pre-populated with the ticket data.

4. If the total tickets exceed the default page size, Then the system must display pagination controls at the bottom of the table.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able search and filter tickets for a resident.
Description of User Story: The Tickets List screen includes search and filter capabilities that enable the Provider to quickly locate specific support or maintenance tickets for a resident. A search bar at the top of the tickets table allows the Provider to type keywords that filter the list in real time by matching against ticket number, subject, category, or description content. Advanced filter controls allow the Provider to filter tickets by status (Open, In Progress, On Hold, Resolved, Closed), priority level (Low, Medium, High, Urgent), category (Plumbing, Electrical, HVAC, Furniture, Housekeeping, Safety, Other), and date range for the creation or last updated date. Applied filters are shown as removable chips above the table, and a "Clear All Filters" button resets all selections to restore the complete list. The search and filter operations work in combination allowing the Provider to narrow down tickets with precision.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider enters a search keyword, Then the system must filter the tickets list to show only rows matching the keyword by ticket number, subject, category, or description.
   Else if the search keyword yields no results, Then the system must display a "No matching tickets found" message.

2. If the Provider selects a priority filter, Then the system must display only tickets matching the selected priority level.

3. If the Provider applies multiple filters simultaneously, Then the system must apply all filters in combination and display only tickets matching all criteria.

4. If the Provider removes a filter chip, Then the system must remove that filter and refresh the table with remaining filters.

5. If the Provider clicks "Clear All Filters", Then the system must reset all filters and search text and display the complete tickets list.
Test Scenarios:

4. Verify that 4. Verify that selecting a status filter such as "In Progress" displays only tickets with that status.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that applying a date range filter such as "March 1 – March 31, 2026" shows only tickets created or updated in that range.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that applied filters are displayed as removable chips above the table.

9. Verify that 9. Verify that the search bar handles special characters such as "& < >" without causing system errors.

11. Verify that 11. Verify that filter chips display with an "x" icon for removal and clear labeling of the applied filter.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that the "Clear All Filters" button is visible when at least one filter is applied.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add inventory items of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Add Inventory Items screen within the ALF Patient Charting module allows the Provider to record personal belongings and inventory items brought by or assigned to a resident. The form includes fields for item name as a text input, item category as a dropdown with options such as Clothing, Electronics, Medical Equipment, Personal Documents, Valuables, Furniture, and Other, and quantity as a numeric input. A condition field provides options such as New, Good, Fair, and Poor to document the item condition at the time of intake. An optional serial number or identifying mark text field allows the Provider to record unique identifiers for valuable items. A photo upload section enables the Provider to attach images of the item for documentation purposes. The form footer contains Cancel and Save buttons where Cancel discards the entry and Save creates a new inventory record linked to the resident with a recorded date and the Provider name who logged the item.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider fills all required fields and clicks "Save", Then the system must create the inventory record with the item details, date, and Provider name.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Save" without entering an item name, Then the system must display a validation error on the item name field.
   Else if the Provider enters a quantity of zero or a negative number, Then the system must display a validation error indicating the quantity must be at least 1.
   Else if the Provider selects "Other" as the item category, Then the system must enable an additional text field for specifying the custom category.
   Else if the Provider uploads a photo exceeding the maximum allowed file size, Then the system must display an error message indicating the file size limit.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Cancel", Then the system must discard all entered data and close the form without saving.
Test Scenarios:

2. Verify that 2. Verify that submitting a completed form creates an inventory record with correct item details such as "Wheelchair - Medical Equipment - Qty: 1 - Condition: Good", recorded date, and Provider name.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that entering an optional serial number such as "SN-WC-2024-001" saves it as part of the inventory record.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that uploading photos attaches images to the inventory record with file names and previews displayed.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that selecting "Other" as category enables a custom category text field for free-text entry.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that the condition dropdown displays New, Good, Fair, and Poor options.

Negative:
7. Verify that 7. Verify that submitting without an item name displays a validation error on the item name field.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that entering a quantity of 0 or a negative value such as -1 displays a validation error requiring a minimum quantity of 1.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that uploading a file exceeding the maximum allowed size such as a file over 5 MB displays an error message.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the category dropdown displays all predefined options (Clothing, Electronics, Medical Equipment, Personal Documents, Valuables, Furniture, Other).
11. Verify that 11. Verify that the quantity field accepts only numeric values with a stepper control for increment and decrement.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that the photo upload section displays image thumbnails with file names after upload.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that the form handles submission when the item name and serial number fields contain the maximum allowed number of characters.
14. Verify that 14. Verify that uploading multiple photos simultaneously for a single inventory item completes without error.

Security:
15. Verify that 15. Verify that only authenticated Providers with inventory management permissions can add inventory items for a resident.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the Add Inventory Item form loads with all fields and dropdown options within 2 seconds.
Verify saving an inventory item with photo attachments completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit inventory items of the resident.
Description of User Story: The Edit Inventory Item form allows the Provider to modify the details of an existing inventory record for a resident within the ALF Patient Charting module. When the Provider clicks the edit action on an inventory item, the form opens pre-populated with the current item details including name, category, quantity, condition, serial number, and attached photos. The Provider can update any field including changing the item condition to reflect wear or damage, adjusting the quantity, updating the category, or modifying the serial number. The photo section allows the Provider to add new images or remove previously attached photos. A notes field is available to document the reason for the edit such as item condition change or quantity adjustment. The form footer contains Cancel and Save buttons where Cancel reverts to the original values and Save persists the updates with an audit trail entry recording the changes, timestamp, and Provider who made the modification.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider modifies fields and clicks "Save", Then the system must persist the updates, create an audit trail entry, and display a success confirmation.
   Else if the Provider clicks "Save" without making any changes, Then the system must close the form without creating an audit trail entry.

2. If the Provider changes the condition from a better state to a worse state such as from "Good" to "Poor", Then the system must require an edit note explaining the condition change.

3. If the Provider reduces the quantity to zero, Then the system must display a confirmation prompt asking whether to mark the item as removed from inventory.

4. If the Provider removes all photos from an item that previously had photo documentation, Then the system must display a warning but allow saving.

5. If the Provider clicks "Cancel", Then the system must revert all modifications and close the form.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that the Edit Inventory Item form opens with all fields pre-populated from the existing record.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that modifying the item name, category, quantity, and condition such as changing condition from "Good" to "Fair" updates the form values.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that adding a new photo appends it to the existing photo list.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that removing an existing photo updates the photo list.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that clicking "Save" with changes persists updates and creates an audit trail entry.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that entering an edit note such as "Item damaged during transport" is saved as part of the audit trail.

Negative:
7. Verify that 7. Verify that clicking "Cancel" reverts all changes without saving.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that reducing quantity to 0 displays a confirmation prompt asking whether to mark the item as removed from inventory.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that leaving mandatory fields blank displays inline validation errors.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the edit form layout matches the add form with consistent field arrangement and pre-populated values.
11. Verify that 11. Verify that the notes field is positioned below the main fields with a label indicating its purpose such as "Reason for edit".
12. Verify that 12. Verify that pre-populated photos display as thumbnails with individual remove buttons.

Edge Cases:

14. Verify that 14. Verify that the form handles the case where the original category has been deactivated in the system since the item was added.

Security:
15. Verify that 15. Verify that only authenticated Providers with inventory management permissions can edit inventory items for a resident.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the Edit Inventory Item form loads with pre-populated data and photo thumbnails within 2 seconds.
Verify saving inventory item updates persists changes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add face-to-face assessment notes with CNA + RN approval workflow.
Description of User Story: The Add Face-to-Face Assessment Note form within the ALF Patient Charting module allows the Provider to create a clinical assessment note that follows a structured CNA and RN approval workflow. The form includes sections for vital signs at the time of assessment, a comprehensive narrative text area for documenting the resident physical and cognitive observations, a functional status checklist covering activities of daily living such as bathing, dressing, eating, mobility, and toileting with rating scales. A care recommendations section allows the Provider to document suggested care plan adjustments or interventions. The form requires the CNA who conducted the face-to-face assessment to digitally acknowledge the note by entering their credentials and timestamp before it routes to the RN for final review and approval. The form footer contains Cancel, Save Draft, and Submit for Approval buttons where Submit initiates the CNA acknowledgment step in the workflow.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider completes all required fields and clicks "Submit for Approval", Then the system must initiate the CNA acknowledgment step and set the note status to "Pending CNA Approval".
   Else if the Provider clicks "Submit for Approval" with incomplete required fields, Then the system must display validation errors on each incomplete field and prevent submission.

2. If the Provider clicks "Save Draft", Then the system must save all current entries without validation and set the status to "Draft".

3. If the Provider enters vital signs outside normal clinical ranges such as BP above 180/120, Then the system must display a warning indicator but allow saving the values.

4. If the Provider clicks "Cancel" after entering data, Then the system must display a confirmation prompt before discarding.

5. If a draft note already exists for the same date and resident, Then the system must display a prompt asking whether to continue editing the existing draft or create a new note.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that the Add Assessment Note form opens with sections for vital signs, narrative, functional status, and care recommendations.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that entering vital signs such as BP 120/80, HR 72, Temp 98.6°F records the values with the assessment timestamp.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that selecting functional status ratings such as "Independent" for bathing and "Assistance Required" for mobility records correct values for each ADL.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that clicking "Submit for Approval" initiates the CNA acknowledgment workflow step and sets status to "Pending CNA Approval".
5. Verify that 5. Verify that clicking "Save Draft" saves partial entries with Draft status without triggering validation.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that entering care recommendations such as "Increase physical therapy sessions" persists the documented interventions.

Negative:
7. Verify that 7. Verify that submitting with incomplete required fields such as missing vital signs displays validation errors and prevents submission.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that clicking "Cancel" after entering data displays a confirmation prompt before discarding the note.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that entering vital signs outside normal ranges such as BP 200/130 displays warning indicators while still allowing the values to be saved.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the vital signs section displays labeled input fields for blood pressure, heart rate, temperature, respiratory rate, and oxygen saturation.
11. Verify that 11. Verify that the functional status checklist displays ADL items such as Bathing, Dressing, Eating, Mobility, and Toileting with selectable rating scales.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that Cancel, Save Draft, and Submit for Approval buttons are positioned in the form footer.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that a prompt is displayed when a draft note already exists for the same date and the Provider attempts to add a new note.
14. Verify that 14. Verify that the form handles submission correctly when the narrative text area contains the maximum allowed character count.

Security:
15. Verify that 15. Verify that only authenticated Providers with assessment note permissions can create face-to-face assessment notes.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the Add Assessment Note form loads with all sections within 2 seconds.
Verify submitting the note for approval processes the workflow transition within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit face-to-face assessment notes with CNA + RN approval workflow.
Description of User Story: The Edit Face-to-Face Assessment Note form allows the Provider to modify an existing assessment note within the ALF Patient Charting module while maintaining the integrity of the CNA and RN approval workflow. When the Provider clicks edit on an existing note, the form opens pre-populated with the current assessment data including vital signs, narrative observations, functional status ratings, and care recommendations. The Provider can update any section of the note provided it has not yet received final RN approval. If the note has already been approved by the CNA, editing resets the CNA acknowledgment and the note must go through the approval workflow again. An edit reason text field requires the Provider to document why the modification is being made. The form footer contains Cancel and Save buttons where Save persists the updates and triggers the appropriate workflow step based on the note current status.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the note status is "Draft", Then the system must allow full editing without resetting any workflow approvals.

2. If the note status is "Pending CNA Approval" and the Provider edits it, Then the system must save the changes and keep the status as "Pending CNA Approval".
   Else if the note has received CNA approval and the Provider edits it, Then the system must reset the CNA acknowledgment and set the status back to "Pending CNA Approval".

3. If the note has received final RN approval, Then the system must prevent editing and display a message that approved notes cannot be modified.

4. If the Provider clicks "Save" without entering an edit reason, Then the system must display a validation error requiring the edit reason.

5. If the Provider clicks "Cancel" after making changes, Then the system must display a confirmation prompt before discarding edits.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that the Edit form opens with all sections pre-populated from the existing assessment note.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that modifying vital signs such as updating BP from 120/80 to 130/85, narrative, and functional status updates the form values.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that editing a CNA-approved note resets the CNA acknowledgment and sets the status to "Pending CNA Approval".
4. Verify that 4. Verify that entering an edit reason such as "Corrected BP reading" records it in the audit trail.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that clicking "Save" persists updates and triggers the appropriate workflow step.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that editing a "Draft" note allows full modification without triggering any workflow reset.

Negative:
7. Verify that 7. Verify that attempting to edit an RN-approved note displays a message stating that approved notes cannot be modified.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that clicking "Save" without entering an edit reason displays a validation error requiring the reason.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that clicking "Cancel" after making changes displays a confirmation prompt.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the edit form layout matches the add form with consistent field arrangement and pre-populated values.
11. Verify that 11. Verify that the edit reason field is prominently displayed with a clear label such as "Reason for Edit (required)".
12. Verify that 12. Verify that the note current workflow status such as "Pending CNA Approval" is displayed at the top of the form.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that the form handles the scenario where the CNA who originally acknowledged the note is no longer active in the system.
14. Verify that 14. Verify that the form displays a concurrent edit warning when the Provider edits a note that is simultaneously being reviewed by the CNA.

Security:
15. Verify that 15. Verify that only authenticated Providers with assessment note edit permissions can modify existing notes.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the Edit Assessment Note form loads with pre-populated data within 2 seconds.
Verify saving edits and triggering the workflow transition completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to view list of face-to-face assessment notes with CNA + RN approval workflow.
Description of User Story: The Face-to-Face Assessment Notes List screen within the ALF Patient Charting module displays a comprehensive table of all assessment notes for a resident with their CNA and RN approval workflow statuses. Each row presents the assessment date, note summary or excerpt, CNA acknowledgment status with the CNA name and timestamp, RN approval status with the RN name and timestamp, the creating Provider name, and the overall workflow status. The workflow status column uses color-coded badges showing Draft, Pending CNA Approval, Pending RN Approval, Approved, or Rejected. A workflow progress indicator on each row visually represents the current step in the approval chain. The table supports sorting by assessment date and workflow status. Each row includes action buttons for View Details and Edit where Edit is available only for notes that have not received final RN approval. A filter bar allows the Provider to filter notes by workflow status.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the resident has one or more assessment notes, Then the system must display all notes in the table with their workflow statuses.
   Else if the resident has no assessment notes, Then the system must display an empty state message such as "No face-to-face assessment notes found".

2. If a note has received final RN approval, Then the system must disable the "Edit" button on that row and show only "View Details".
   Else if a note has "Draft" or "Pending" status, Then the system must enable both "View Details" and "Edit" buttons on that row.

3. If the Provider selects a workflow status filter, Then the system must display only notes matching the selected status.

4. If the Provider clears the filter, Then the system must display all assessment notes in the table.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that the assessment notes table loads with correct columns for date, note summary, CNA status with name and timestamp, RN status with name and timestamp, creating provider, and workflow status.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that workflow status badges display with correct color coding such as gray for Draft, yellow for Pending CNA Approval, orange for Pending RN Approval, green for Approved, and red for Rejected.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that clicking "View Details" opens the complete assessment note with full approval information including CNA and RN signatures.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that clicking "Edit" on a note with "Pending CNA Approval" status opens the Edit Assessment Note form.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that filtering by workflow status "Approved" displays only approved assessment notes.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that the workflow progress indicator accurately represents the current approval step for each note.

Negative:
7. Verify that 7. Verify that an empty state message "No face-to-face assessment notes found" is displayed when no notes exist for the resident.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that the "Edit" button is disabled on notes that have received final RN approval.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that the notes list does not display assessment notes from other residents.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that workflow status badges use distinct colors (e.g., gray for Draft, yellow for Pending CNA, orange for Pending RN, green for Approved, red for Rejected).
11. Verify that 11. Verify that the workflow progress indicator displays as a visual step tracker such as a multi-step progress bar on each row.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that the filter bar is positioned above the table with clear workflow status options.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that the table handles a resident with 100 or more assessment notes with proper pagination.
14. Verify that 14. Verify that the table correctly displays a note that is in transition between CNA and RN approval steps.

Security:
15. Verify that 15. Verify that only authenticated Providers with charting access can view the assessment notes list for a resident.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the assessment notes table loads with all entries and workflow statuses within 3 seconds.
Verify filtering by workflow status refreshes the table within 1 second.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to upload CNA signature for face-to-face note.
Description of User Story: The CNA Signature Upload functionality within the ALF Patient Charting module enables the Provider to capture and attach the CNA electronic signature to a face-to-face assessment note as part of the approval workflow. When a note is in "Pending CNA Approval" status, the Provider or CNA can open the signature upload panel which presents two options: a digital signature pad where the CNA can draw their signature using a mouse or touch input, and a file upload option for uploading a pre-captured signature image. The signature panel displays the CNA name, credentials, and the current date and time as part of the acknowledgment record. A confirmation checkbox requires the CNA to attest that they have reviewed the assessment note and confirm the accuracy of the documented observations. The panel footer contains Cancel and Submit Signature buttons where Submit captures the signature, updates the note status to "Pending RN Approval", and records the CNA acknowledgment with timestamp in the audit trail.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the CNA draws a signature on the digital pad and the confirmation checkbox is checked, Then the system must enable the Submit Signature button and allow submission.
   Else if the CNA uploads a signature image file, Then the system must validate the file format and display the image in the preview area.

2. If the uploaded file is not a valid image format such as PNG, JPG, or JPEG, Then the system must display an error message indicating acceptable file formats.

3. If the CNA clicks "Submit Signature" without providing any signature, Then the system must display a validation error requiring either a drawn or uploaded signature.

4. If the confirmation checkbox is not checked when the CNA clicks "Submit Signature", Then the system must display a validation error requiring the attestation checkbox to be checked.

5. If the CNA clicks "Submit Signature" with a valid signature and checked confirmation checkbox, Then the system must attach the signature, update the note status to "Pending RN Approval", and record the acknowledgment with timestamp.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that the CNA signature upload panel opens for notes in "Pending CNA Approval" status.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that drawing a signature on the digital pad captures and displays it in the preview area.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that uploading a valid signature image file such as a PNG or JPG displays it in the preview area.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that checking the confirmation checkbox and submitting with a valid signature attaches the signature and updates note status to "Pending RN Approval".
5. Verify that 5. Verify that the CNA name, credentials, and current date/time are displayed on the signature panel.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that the CNA acknowledgment with timestamp is recorded in the audit trail after successful submission.

Negative:
7. Verify that 7. Verify that submitting without checking the confirmation checkbox displays a validation error.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that submitting without providing any drawn or uploaded signature displays a validation error.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that uploading a non-image file format such as a PDF or DOCX displays an error message about acceptable formats.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the digital signature pad area is large enough for legible signature drawing with clear and redo controls.
11. Verify that 11. Verify that the file upload option includes a file browse button with accepted format labels such as "PNG, JPG, JPEG".
12. Verify that 12. Verify that the confirmation checkbox text clearly states the attestation of review and accuracy of documented observations.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that switching from a drawn signature to a file upload clears the drawn signature from the preview area.
14. Verify that 14. Verify that the panel displays an appropriate message when the note transitions to a different status while the CNA is in the process of signing.

Security:
15. Verify that 15. Verify that only the assigned CNA or authorized Provider can upload the CNA signature for a specific assessment note.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the CNA signature upload panel loads with the digital pad and upload option within 2 seconds.
Verify submitting the signature updates the note status and records the audit trail within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to route to RN for final approval and signature.
Description of User Story: The Route to RN functionality within the ALF Patient Charting module allows the Provider to forward a face-to-face assessment note to the assigned RN for final review, approval, and electronic signature. When a note has received CNA acknowledgment and is in "Pending RN Approval" status, the Provider can initiate the RN routing action which opens a routing panel displaying the list of available RNs assigned to the facility or unit. The Provider selects the target RN from the list, adds optional routing comments for the RN reviewer, and clicks "Route to RN" to send the note for final approval. The RN receives a notification with a link to the assessment note review screen where they can review the complete note including CNA signature, add reviewer comments, and either approve with electronic signature or reject with mandatory rejection reason. Upon RN approval, the note status updates to "Approved" with the RN signature and timestamp recorded. Upon rejection, the note returns to the Provider with the rejection reason for correction.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider selects an RN from the available list and confirms routing, Then the system must forward the note to the RN and send a notification with a link to the review screen.
   Else if no RN is selected when the Provider clicks "Route to RN", Then the system must display a validation error requiring an RN selection.

2. If the RN approves the note with electronic signature, Then the system must update the status to "Approved" and record the RN signature with timestamp.

3. If the RN rejects the note, Then the system must require a mandatory rejection reason before allowing the rejection to be submitted.
   Else if the RN clicks reject without entering a rejection reason, Then the system must display a validation error requiring the reason.

4. If the note is rejected and returned to the Provider, Then the system must set the status back to "Draft" and include the rejection reason in the Provider notification.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that the routing panel opens and displays a list of available RNs assigned to the facility with name, credentials, and availability status.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that selecting an RN such as "RN Jane Smith" and clicking "Route to RN" forwards the note and sends a notification to that RN.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that the RN review screen displays the complete assessment note including CNA signature, vital signs, narrative, and functional status ratings.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that the RN can approve the note with an electronic signature, updating the note status to "Approved" with the RN name and timestamp recorded.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that the RN can reject the note with a mandatory rejection reason such as "Vital signs require re-verification", returning the note to "Draft" status.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that optional routing comments entered by the Provider such as "Please review the mobility rating" are visible to the RN on the review screen.

Negative:
7. Verify that 7. Verify that clicking "Route to RN" without selecting an RN displays a validation error.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that the RN cannot submit a rejection without entering a rejection reason.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that a note that has not received CNA acknowledgment cannot be routed to the RN.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the RN list displays each RN name, credentials, and availability status such as "Available" or "Unavailable".
11. Verify that 11. Verify that the RN review screen includes Approve and Reject action buttons prominently displayed.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that the optional routing comments field is displayed below the RN selection with placeholder text such as "Add comments for the RN reviewer".

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that 13. Verify that an appropriate message is displayed when the selected RN becomes unavailable after routing but before completing the review.
14. Verify that 14. Verify that the system handles the case where the Provider routes the same note to a different RN after the first assigned RN has not responded.

Security:
15. Verify that 15. Verify that only authenticated RNs with approval permissions can approve or reject routed assessment notes.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the routing panel loads the list of available RNs within 2 seconds.
Verify the RN approval or rejection updates the note status and sends notifications within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add a new Resident as a Respite Care Resident at the time of admission
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to add a new resident specifically as a Respite Care Resident during the admission process within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. On the Admission Form, the provider selects the care type as "Respite Care" which flags the resident for short-term, temporary stay management with an expected discharge date. The form captures essential demographic details, emergency contacts, insurance information, medical history, and the planned duration of the respite stay. Upon successful admission, the resident appears in the Resident List with a "Respite Care" care type indicator, distinguishing them from long-term residents such as ALF or Memory Care. This capability is critical for facilities that provide temporary relief care for primary caregivers, ensuring that respite residents are tracked separately with appropriate stay duration limits and billing configurations. Without this feature, the provider would have no way to properly classify and manage short-term respite admissions distinct from permanent placements.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the provider selects "Respite Care" as the care type on the Admission Form and fills all required fields and clicks Save, Then the system must create the resident with care type "Respite Care" and display it in the Resident List.
   Else if the provider does not select a care type and clicks Save, Then the system must display a validation error "Care Type is required".

2. If the provider enters a valid expected discharge date that is after the admission date, Then the system must accept and store the date.
   Else if the expected discharge date is before or equal to the admission date, Then the system must display a validation error "Expected discharge date must be after admission date".

3. If the provider enters all mandatory fields (name, DOB, gender, emergency contact, admission date, expected discharge date), Then the system must enable the Save button and allow form submission.
   Else if any mandatory field is missing, Then the system must disable the Save button and highlight the missing fields.

4. If the facility has respite care beds available, Then the system must allow the admission to proceed.
   Else if no respite care beds are available, Then the system must display a notification informing the provider of bed unavailability.

5. If the resident record is successfully created, Then the system must generate a unique resident ID and display a success message with the resident details.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking "Add New Resident" opens the Admission Form with a Care Type dropdown containing "Respite Care" as an option.
2. Verify that selecting "Respite Care" displays respite-specific fields: planned admission date, expected discharge date, and reason for respite stay.
3. Verify that filling all required fields and clicking Save successfully creates the resident with "Respite Care" care type.
4. Verify that the newly added Respite Care Resident appears in the Resident List with a "Respite Care" indicator.
5. Verify that the resident details page shows all entered information correctly after admission.

Negative:
6. Verify that saving without selecting a care type displays a validation error.
7. Verify that entering an expected discharge date before the admission date shows a validation error.
8. Verify that submitting the form with missing mandatory fields (name, DOB, emergency contact) displays appropriate validation messages.
9. Verify that entering invalid characters in the name field is rejected by the system.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify the Admission Form layout displays all fields in a logical grouping with clear labels.
11. Verify the "Respite Care" badge is visually distinct in the Resident List (e.g., a different color from ALF or MC badges).
12. Verify date picker controls are properly rendered for admission and discharge date fields.
13. Verify the Save and Cancel buttons are clearly visible and properly positioned on the form.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify behavior when adding a respite resident with the same name and DOB as an existing resident — the system should warn about a potential duplicate.
15. Verify that setting the expected discharge date to the same day as admission is handled appropriately.
16. Verify behavior when the provider navigates away from a partially filled form — the system should prompt to save or discard changes.

Security:
17. Verify that only authenticated and authorized providers can access the Add New Resident admission form.
18. Verify that resident PII data is transmitted securely over HTTPS during form submission.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Admission Form loads completely within 2 seconds after clicking "Add New Resident".
2. Verify that saving a new Respite Care Resident completes and displays the success confirmation within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit a new patient as a Respite Care Resident at the time of admission
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to edit the details of a recently added Respite Care Resident during or shortly after the admission process within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. The provider can access the resident record from the Resident List or the admission confirmation screen and modify any previously entered information such as demographic details, emergency contacts, insurance data, expected discharge date, or reason for respite stay. The Edit Resident Form pre-populates all existing data, allowing the provider to make corrections or updates without re-entering the entire form. This capability is essential because admission data often needs to be corrected due to typos, incomplete information provided by the family, or changes in the planned respite stay duration. Upon saving edits, the system updates the resident record and reflects changes across all views including the Resident List, resident profile, and any associated care plans. Without this feature, providers would need to delete and re-create resident records to fix admission errors, which could disrupt room assignments and billing.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the provider clicks "Edit" on a Respite Care Resident record, Then the system must open the Edit Resident Form pre-populated with all current resident data.
   Else if the resident record is locked by another user, Then the system must display a message "Record is currently being edited by another user".

2. If the provider modifies valid fields and clicks Save, Then the system must update the resident record and display a success message.
   Else if the provider clears a mandatory field and clicks Save, Then the system must display validation errors and prevent the update.

3. If the provider changes the expected discharge date to a date after the admission date, Then the system must accept and store the updated date.
   Else if the new expected discharge date is before the admission date, Then the system must show a validation error "Expected discharge date must be after admission date".

4. If the provider clicks Cancel after making changes, Then the system must display a confirmation dialog asking to discard or continue editing.
   Else if the provider clicks Cancel without making any changes, Then the system must close the form without a confirmation dialog.

5. If the edit is saved successfully, Then the updated information must be reflected in the Resident List, resident profile, and associated care plans.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking "Edit" on a Respite Care Resident opens the Edit Form pre-populated with all current data.
2. Verify that modifying the expected discharge date and saving updates the record successfully.
3. Verify that changing the emergency contact and saving reflects the update in the resident profile.
4. Verify that editing the resident name and saving displays the updated name in the Resident List.
5. Verify that modifying the reason for respite stay and saving stores the new reason correctly.

Negative:
6. Verify that clearing the resident name field and attempting to save displays a validation error.
7. Verify that entering an expected discharge date before the admission date shows a validation error.
8. Verify that attempting to edit a record locked by another user displays an appropriate message.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify the Edit Form layout matches the Add Form layout with all fields pre-populated.
10. Verify the Save and Cancel buttons are clearly visible on the Edit Form.
11. Verify that modified fields are visually distinguishable (e.g., highlighted or marked) before saving.
12. Verify the confirmation dialog appears when clicking Cancel after making changes.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that editing a resident record immediately after creation retains all admission data.
14. Verify behavior when two providers attempt to edit the same resident simultaneously.
15. Verify that rapid consecutive edits do not cause data corruption or loss.

Security:
16. Verify that only authorized providers can access the Edit Resident Form.
17. Verify that edit actions are logged in the audit trail with the provider identity and timestamp.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Edit Resident Form loads with pre-populated data within 2 seconds.
2. Verify that saving edits to a Respite Care Resident record completes within 3 seconds.
