13. Verify that 13. Verify the one-click "Complete" and "Review" action buttons are visible and properly styled.
14. Verify that 14. Verify the dashboard is responsive and renders correctly on tablet and desktop screen sizes.

Edge Cases:
15. Verify that 15. Verify dashboard behavior when a task transitions from "Upcoming" to "Overdue" while the manager is viewing the dashboard — a refresh or auto-update should move it to the Overdue category.
16. Verify that 16. Verify behavior when hundreds of overdue tasks exist — the dashboard should paginate or virtualize the list without performance degradation.
17. Verify that 17. Verify that applying all filters simultaneously returns correctly intersected results.

Security:
18. Verify that 18. Verify that only authenticated compliance managers can access the dashboard.
19. Verify that 19. Verify that API endpoints do not return compliance data for facilities the manager is not authorized to view.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the compliance dashboard loads with all categorized tasks within 3 seconds.
Verify that applying or changing filters refreshes the dashboard within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a compliance manager, I should be able to create recurring compliance tasks with flexible schedules (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, annually) that automatically generate on the specified intervals, assign to responsible staff, and send reminders before due dates to ensure timely completion.
Description of User Story: This story enables the compliance manager to create recurring compliance tasks with configurable recurrence schedules within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. The manager selects a task type (e.g., fire drill, refrigerator temperature check, generator test), chooses a recurrence frequency (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, or annually), assigns a responsible staff member, and configures reminder settings such as how many days before the due date the reminder should be sent. Once saved, the system automatically generates individual task instances at the specified intervals, each with its own due date, assigned staff, and status tracking. Reminders are sent via in-app notifications and optionally via email to the assigned staff before each task's due date, ensuring no compliance activity is missed. This automated scheduling eliminates the manual effort of creating each task instance individually, reducing human error and ensuring consistent compliance monitoring across all facilities. Without this feature, the compliance manager would have to manually create each compliance task for every interval, which is unsustainable for facilities with dozens of recurring regulatory requirements.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the compliance manager selects a valid recurrence frequency and fills all required fields and clicks Save, Then the system must create the recurring task and generate task instances based on the selected frequency.
   Else if any required field is missing, Then the system must show validation errors and prevent saving.

2. If the recurrence frequency is set to "Daily," Then the system must generate a new task instance every day starting from the start date.
   Else if the frequency is "Quarterly," Then the system must generate a new task instance every 3 months.

3. If a reminder is configured for N days before the due date, Then the system must send a notification to the assigned staff exactly N days before.
   Else if no reminder is configured, Then the system must not send any advance notification but still track the task.

4. If the compliance manager edits the recurrence pattern, Then all future (ungenerated or pending) task instances must adopt the new pattern.
   Else if the manager edits only a single task instance, Then only that specific instance must be modified without affecting the recurring series.

5. If the compliance manager deletes a recurring task, Then the system must prompt for confirmation and offer options to delete only future instances or the entire series.
   Else if the manager cancels the deletion, Then no changes must be made.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that clicking "Create Recurring Task" opens the form with all required fields.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that creating a daily recurring task generates task instances for each day.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that creating a weekly recurring task generates task instances at 7-day intervals.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that creating a monthly recurring task generates task instances on the same date each month.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that creating a quarterly recurring task generates task instances every 3 months.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that creating an annual recurring task generates task instances once per year.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that assigned staff receives a reminder notification at the configured advance time.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that editing a recurring task updates all future instances.

Negative:
9. Verify that 9. Verify that saving without selecting a recurrence frequency displays a validation error.
10. Verify that 10. Verify that saving without assigning a staff member displays a validation error.
11. Verify that 11. Verify that setting a start date in the past is handled appropriately (warning or prevention).

UI Scenarios:
12. Verify that 12. Verify the recurrence frequency dropdown lists all options: Daily, Weekly, Monthly, Quarterly, Annually.
13. Verify that 13. Verify the staff assignment field provides a searchable dropdown of available staff members.
14. Verify that 14. Verify the reminder settings field allows input of number of days with a clear label.
15. Verify that 15. Verify the form layout is logical with clear grouping of task details, schedule, and assignment sections.

Edge Cases:
16. Verify that 16. Verify behavior when a monthly recurring task is set for the 31st but the month has only 30 or 28 days — the system should handle gracefully (e.g., schedule on the last day of the month).
17. Verify that 17. Verify behavior when the assigned staff member is deactivated — the task should still exist but the manager should be notified to reassign.
18. Verify that 18. Verify that deleting a recurring task offers options to delete only future instances or the entire series.

Security:
19. Verify that 19. Verify that only compliance managers can create, edit, or delete recurring compliance tasks.
20. Verify that 20. Verify that reminder notifications are sent only to the assigned staff and compliance manager, not to unauthorized users.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that creating a recurring task and generating the first task instance completes within 3 seconds.
Verify that the system generates up to 365 daily task instances without timeout or failure within 10 seconds.

User Story: As a staff member, I should be able to digitally record compliance activities including fire drills, refrigerator temperature checks, generator tests, emergency equipment inspections, and facility safety walkthroughs with date/time stamps, staff signatures, and recorded values or observations.
Description of User Story: This story enables staff members to digitally record various compliance activities within the Nemi Care Facility Portal, replacing traditional paper-based logs with a structured digital logging system. The staff member selects the type of compliance activity (fire drill, refrigerator temperature check, generator test, emergency equipment inspection, or facility safety walkthrough), enters the required data fields specific to that activity type, provides recorded values or observations, and signs the log entry digitally. Each log entry is automatically stamped with the current date and time, and the staff member's identity is captured from their authenticated session, ensuring accountability and traceability. The digital log supports different data entry formats depending on the activity type — numerical values for temperature checks, pass/fail indicators for equipment inspections, and free-text observations for safety walkthroughs. This digitization is critical for maintaining accurate, tamper-proof compliance records that can be quickly retrieved during regulatory audits, eliminating the risk of lost, illegible, or incomplete paper logs. Without this feature, staff would continue using paper forms that are prone to errors, difficult to search, and impossible to aggregate for trend analysis across facilities.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the staff member selects a compliance activity type from the dropdown, Then the form must dynamically display fields specific to that activity type.
   Else if no activity type is selected, Then the form must display a prompt "Please select an activity type to continue."

2. If the staff member fills all required fields, provides a digital signature, and clicks Submit, Then the system must save the log entry with an automatic date/time stamp and the staff member's identity.
   Else if any required field is missing, Then the system must highlight the missing fields and prevent submission.

3. If the recorded temperature value for a refrigerator check falls outside the acceptable range, Then the system must flag the entry with a warning alert.
   Else if the temperature is within the acceptable range, Then the system must save the entry normally without an alert.

4. If the staff member records an equipment inspection with a "Fail" result, Then the system must automatically create a follow-up task or alert the compliance manager.
   Else if the result is "Pass," Then the system must save the entry and mark it as compliant.

5. If the staff member attempts to submit a log entry without providing a digital signature, Then the system must display a validation error "Digital signature is required."
   Else if the signature is provided, Then the system must accept and store the signature as part of the log entry.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that the "Record Activity" button opens the compliance log form with an activity type dropdown.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that selecting "Fire Drill" displays fields for drill duration, number of participants, evacuation time, and observations.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that selecting "Refrigerator Temperature Check" displays fields for refrigerator ID, temperature value, acceptable range, and observations.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that selecting "Generator Test" displays fields for generator ID, test result (pass/fail), runtime duration, and observations.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that selecting "Emergency Equipment Inspection" displays fields for equipment type, condition (pass/fail), and observations.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that selecting "Facility Safety Walkthrough" displays fields for areas inspected, issues found, and free-text observations.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that submitting a complete log entry saves it with the correct date/time stamp, staff name, and digital signature.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that the submitted log entry appears in the log history with all recorded values.

Negative:
9. Verify that 9. Verify that submitting without selecting an activity type is prevented with a validation message.
10. Verify that 10. Verify that submitting without a digital signature displays "Digital signature is required."
11. Verify that 11. Verify that entering a non-numeric value in the temperature field shows a validation error.

UI Scenarios:
12. Verify that 12. Verify the form dynamically changes fields based on the selected activity type without a page reload.
13. Verify that 13. Verify the digital signature pad renders correctly and captures the staff member's signature.
14. Verify that 14. Verify the date/time stamp is displayed in a readable format on the submitted log entry.
15. Verify that 15. Verify the log history list is sortable by date, activity type, and staff member.

Edge Cases:
16. Verify that 16. Verify behavior when a temperature value is recorded at exactly the boundary of the acceptable range.
17. Verify that 17. Verify that the digital signature remains intact and viewable after saving and reopening the log entry.
18. Verify that 18. Verify behavior when the staff member loses internet connectivity mid-form — data should be saved locally and synced when connectivity resumes.

Security:
19. Verify that 19. Verify that only authenticated staff members can record compliance activities.
20. Verify that 20. Verify that submitted log entries cannot be modified or deleted by the staff member after submission (tamper-proof).
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the compliance log form loads with dynamic fields within 2 seconds after selecting an activity type.
Verify that submitting a log entry with a digital signature completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a staff member, I should be able to attach supporting evidence to compliance logs including photos (equipment readings, facility conditions, completed repairs), scanned documents (inspection reports, certificates, vendor invoices), and notes to create a complete audit trail for regulatory reviews.
Description of User Story: This story enables staff members to attach supporting evidence files and notes to existing compliance log entries within the Nemi Care Facility Portal, creating a comprehensive audit trail for regulatory reviews. The staff member can upload photos (such as equipment meter readings, facility condition snapshots, or completed repair images), scanned documents (such as inspection reports, certificates, or vendor invoices), and add textual notes to any compliance log entry. The system supports common file formats including JPEG, PNG, PDF, and DOCX with a configurable maximum file size, and each attachment is linked to the specific log entry with metadata including upload timestamp, file name, and the uploader's identity. The evidence attachments are organized within the log entry detail view, allowing compliance managers and auditors to review all supporting documentation alongside the recorded compliance data. This capability is essential for regulatory audits where inspectors require proof that compliance activities were actually performed as recorded, not just logged. Without this feature, staff would need to store evidence separately (in shared drives, email, or physical files), making it nearly impossible to correlate evidence with specific compliance events during audits.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the staff member uploads a file in a supported format (JPEG, PNG, PDF, DOCX) within the size limit, Then the system must attach the file to the log entry and display a success confirmation.
   Else if the file format is unsupported or the file exceeds the size limit, Then the system must reject the upload and display an appropriate error message.

2. If the staff member uploads multiple files to the same log entry, Then the system must attach all files and display them in a list within the evidence section.
   Else if the maximum number of attachments per log entry is reached, Then the system must display a message indicating the limit has been reached.

3. If the staff member adds a text note, Then the system must save the note with a timestamp and the staff member's identity.
   Else if the note field is left empty and Submit is clicked, Then the system must display a validation message "Note cannot be empty."

4. If a compliance manager views a log entry with attachments, Then the system must display all photos as thumbnails, documents as downloadable links, and notes inline with timestamps.
   Else if the log entry has no attachments, Then the evidence section must display "No evidence attached."

5. If the staff member attempts to delete an attachment after submission, Then the system must prevent deletion and display "Attachments cannot be removed from submitted log entries."
   Else if the log entry is still in draft state, Then the staff member may remove attachments.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that clicking "Attach Evidence" on a log entry opens the upload interface.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that uploading a JPEG photo attaches it to the log entry with correct metadata.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that uploading a PNG photo attaches it successfully.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that uploading a PDF document attaches it and it is viewable/downloadable.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that uploading a DOCX document attaches it and it is downloadable.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that adding a text note saves it with a timestamp and staff identity.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that the compliance manager can view all attached evidence in the log entry detail view.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that multiple files can be attached to a single log entry.

Negative:
9. Verify that 9. Verify that uploading an unsupported file format (e.g., EXE, MP4) displays an error message.
10. Verify that 10. Verify that uploading a file exceeding the size limit is rejected with an appropriate error.
11. Verify that 11. Verify that submitting an empty note displays a validation message.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that attempting to delete an attachment from a submitted log entry is prevented.

UI Scenarios:
13. Verify that 13. Verify the upload interface supports drag-and-drop as well as file browse functionality.
14. Verify that 14. Verify uploaded photos are displayed as thumbnails in the evidence section.
15. Verify that 15. Verify uploaded documents show file name, type icon, and file size.
16. Verify that 16. Verify a progress indicator is shown during file upload.

Edge Cases:
17. Verify that 17. Verify behavior when uploading a file with a very long file name — the system should truncate the display name without losing the file.
18. Verify that 18. Verify behavior when the internet connection is lost during file upload — the system should show an error and allow retry.
19. Verify that 19. Verify that uploading a zero-byte file is handled gracefully with an appropriate error message.

Security:
20. Verify that 20. Verify that only authenticated staff members and compliance managers can upload and view evidence.
21. Verify that 21. Verify that uploaded files are scanned for malware or malicious content before being stored.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that uploading a 5 MB photo completes within 5 seconds on a standard connection.
Verify that the evidence section with 10 attachments loads within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a user, I should be able to complete predefined assessment templates such as pain scale, fall risk, and PHQ-9 so evaluations are standardized.
Description of User Story: This story enables the user to complete predefined, standardized assessment templates such as the pain scale, fall risk assessment, and PHQ-9 (Patient Health Questionnaire-9 for depression screening) within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. The system provides a library of predefined assessment templates, each containing a fixed set of questions, scoring criteria, and interpretation guidelines that conform to clinical standards. When the user initiates an assessment, they select the desired template, and the system presents each question sequentially or in a form layout, capturing the user's responses and automatically calculating the total score based on the predefined scoring algorithm. Upon completion, the system displays the calculated score along with its clinical interpretation (e.g., "Moderate Depression" for a PHQ-9 score of 10-14) and stores the result linked to the resident's record. Standardized assessments are critical in healthcare settings to ensure consistent evaluation across providers, enable accurate trend tracking over time, and meet regulatory documentation requirements. Without predefined templates, each provider might assess patients differently, making it impossible to compare results or detect deterioration in a resident's condition over time.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the user selects a predefined assessment template, Then the system must display all questions and response options as defined in that template without any modifications.
   Else if the template is not found or is corrupted, Then the system must display an error message "Assessment template unavailable. Please contact support."

2. If the user answers all required questions and clicks Submit, Then the system must calculate and display the total score with clinical interpretation.
   Else if the user leaves any required question unanswered and clicks Submit, Then the system must highlight the unanswered questions and prevent submission.

3. If the calculated PHQ-9 score is 0-4, Then the system must display "Minimal Depression."
   Else if the score is 5-9, Then "Mild Depression." Else if 10-14, Then "Moderate Depression." Else if 15-19, Then "Moderately Severe Depression." Else if 20-27, Then "Severe Depression."

4. If the user has previously completed the same assessment type for the same resident, Then the system must allow a new submission and store it as a separate entry for trend tracking.
   Else if this is the first assessment of that type for the resident, Then the system must create the initial record.

5. If the assessment is saved successfully, Then the system must display a success confirmation with the score summary.
   Else if the save fails, Then the system must display an error and retain the form data for retry.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify the Assessment Tools section displays a list of all predefined templates (Pain Scale, Fall Risk, PHQ-9).
2. Verify that 2. Verify selecting the PHQ-9 template opens a form with all 9 standardized questions.
3. Verify that 3. Verify answering all PHQ-9 questions and submitting calculates the correct total score.
4. Verify that 4. Verify the clinical interpretation is displayed correctly based on the calculated score.
5. Verify that 5. Verify the completed assessment is saved and linked to the resident's record.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that completing the Pain Scale assessment calculates and displays the correct score.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that completing the Fall Risk assessment calculates and displays the risk level.

Negative:
8. Verify that 8. Verify that submitting with unanswered required questions displays validation errors.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that an unavailable or corrupted template shows an appropriate error message.
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the system prevents submitting the same assessment twice within a very short time to avoid accidental duplicates.

UI Scenarios:
11. Verify that 11. Verify the assessment form displays questions in a readable, well-structured layout.
12. Verify that 12. Verify response options (radio buttons, checkboxes, sliders) match the template definition.
13. Verify that 13. Verify the score summary and interpretation are displayed clearly after submission.
14. Verify that 14. Verify the assessment form is scrollable for templates with many questions.

Edge Cases:
15. Verify that 15. Verify behavior when the user changes a response after initially selecting one but before submission — the score should recalculate.
16. Verify that 16. Verify that completing the same assessment type multiple times for the same resident stores each as a separate entry.
17. Verify that 17. Verify behavior when the browser is refreshed mid-assessment — the form should either retain data or warn about data loss.

Security:
18. Verify that 18. Verify that only authenticated users with appropriate roles can complete assessments.
19. Verify that 19. Verify that assessment data is transmitted securely over HTTPS and is not visible in browser network logs to unauthorized parties.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the assessment template form loads within 2 seconds after selection.
Verify score calculation and result display completes within 1 second after submission.

User Story: As a user, I should be able to create or use custom assessment scales so the system supports practice-specific needs.
Description of User Story: This story enables the user to create custom assessment scales tailored to their practice-specific clinical needs within the Nemi Care Facility Portal, going beyond the predefined standardized templates. The user can design a new assessment by defining a name, adding custom questions with various input types (multiple choice, numeric scale, text, checkbox, dropdown), configuring scoring rules for each question, and setting interpretation thresholds for the total score. Custom assessment scales can be saved as reusable templates, making them available for the entire care team to use consistently across residents and facilities. Existing custom scales can be selected from the assessment library and used in the same workflow as predefined templates, including automatic score calculation and interpretation. This capability is essential for healthcare practices that use proprietary evaluation tools, specialty-specific screening instruments, or facility-customized checklists that are not part of the standard assessment library. Without this feature, facilities would be limited to only the predefined templates and would need to track custom evaluations outside the system, fragmenting the clinical record.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the user clicks "Create Custom Assessment," Then the system must open the assessment builder interface with fields for name, description, and a question editor.
   Else if the user does not have permission to create assessments, Then the "Create Custom Assessment" button must be hidden or disabled.

2. If the user adds questions with valid input types and scoring weights and clicks "Save Template," Then the system must save the custom assessment and make it available in the library.
   Else if the assessment name is empty or a duplicate name exists, Then the system must display a validation error.

3. If a custom assessment template is selected for completion, Then the system must present questions and calculate the score using the custom scoring rules.
   Else if the template has been deactivated, Then the system must not allow new assessments using that template.

4. If the user edits a custom template, Then future assessments must use the updated version.
   Else if past assessments were completed with the older version, Then those historical records must remain unchanged.

5. If the user attempts to delete a custom template that has been used for completed assessments, Then the system must prevent deletion and suggest deactivation instead.
   Else if no assessments have been completed using the template, Then the system must allow deletion after confirmation.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that clicking "Create Custom Assessment" opens the builder interface.
2. Verify that 2. Verify that adding a question with a numeric scale input type and scoring weight saves correctly.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that adding a multiple-choice question with response options and scoring saves correctly.
4. Verify that 4. Verify that saving a complete custom assessment template adds it to the assessment library.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that selecting and completing a custom assessment calculates the score correctly based on custom scoring rules.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that editing a custom template and saving updates the template for future use.

Negative:
7. Verify that 7. Verify that saving a custom assessment with an empty name displays a validation error.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that saving a custom assessment with duplicate name shows a "Name already exists" error.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that saving a template with zero questions is prevented with a validation message.
10. Verify that 10. Verify that attempting to delete a template with completed assessments is blocked.

UI Scenarios:
11. Verify that 11. Verify the builder interface provides drag-and-drop reordering of questions.
12. Verify that 12. Verify that a live preview of the assessment form is displayed as questions are added.
13. Verify that 13. Verify input type options (numeric scale, text, checkbox, dropdown, multiple choice) are available in the question editor.
14. Verify that 14. Verify the saved custom template is visually distinguished from predefined templates in the library (e.g., "Custom" badge).

Edge Cases:
15. Verify that 15. Verify behavior when a custom assessment has a very large number of questions (e.g., 50+) — the builder and form should remain usable.
16. Verify that 16. Verify that deactivating a custom template prevents new assessments but allows viewing completed ones.
17. Verify that 17. Verify behavior when scoring rules produce a decimal total — the system should handle rounding correctly.

Security:
18. Verify that 18. Verify that only users with the "Create Assessment" permission can access the builder.
19. Verify that 19. Verify that custom assessment data is stored securely and accessible only to authorized users.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the custom assessment builder loads within 2 seconds.
Verify that saving a custom template with 30 questions completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a user, I should be able to view and complete the assessment step by step
Description of User Story: This story enables the user to view and complete the initial assessment for a resident in a step-by-step wizard format within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. The assessment is divided into logical sections (e.g., personal information, medical history, functional abilities, cognitive assessment, nutritional status, social history) and each section is presented as a separate step in a wizard-style interface with clear navigation (Next, Previous, and a step indicator showing progress). The user can complete one section at a time, and the wizard validates each section before allowing progression to the next step, ensuring data completeness and accuracy at each stage. Completed steps are visually marked (e.g., green checkmark) while incomplete or skipped steps retain a different indicator, giving the user a clear picture of what has been done and what remains. This step-by-step approach is essential for initial assessments that are lengthy and complex, as it breaks down an overwhelming form into manageable sections and reduces errors caused by form fatigue. Without this feature, users would be presented with a single monolithic form that is difficult to navigate, increasing the likelihood of incomplete assessments and data entry errors.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the user clicks "Next" after completing all required fields on the current step, Then the wizard must validate and navigate to the next section.
   Else if required fields are missing, Then the wizard must display validation errors and remain on the current step.

2. If the user clicks "Previous," Then the wizard must navigate to the prior step with all previously entered data intact.
   Else if the user is on the first step, Then the "Previous" button must be disabled or hidden.

3. If a step is completed successfully, Then the step indicator must show a green checkmark for that step.
   Else if a step is skipped or incomplete, Then the step indicator must show an incomplete marker.

4. If the user clicks the step indicator to jump to a non-adjacent step, Then the system must allow navigation only if all preceding steps are completed.
   Else if preceding steps are incomplete, Then the system must display a message "Please complete the previous sections first."

5. If the user clicks "Finish" on the last step and all sections are valid, Then the system must save the assessment and mark it as complete.
   Else if any section has validation errors, Then the system must highlight the problematic sections and prevent submission.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify the initial assessment opens in a step-by-step wizard with a visible step indicator.
2. Verify that 2. Verify completing all required fields on a step and clicking "Next" navigates to the next section.
3. Verify that 3. Verify clicking "Previous" returns to the prior step with all data retained.
4. Verify that 4. Verify completed steps display a green checkmark in the step indicator.
5. Verify that 5. Verify clicking "Finish" on the last step saves and completes the assessment.

Negative:
6. Verify that 6. Verify clicking "Next" with missing required fields displays validation errors and stays on the current step.
7. Verify that 7. Verify attempting to jump to a future step when preceding steps are incomplete is blocked.
8. Verify that 8. Verify clicking "Finish" when any section has errors highlights the problematic sections.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that 9. Verify the step indicator clearly shows all assessment sections with their completion status.
10. Verify that 10. Verify the current step is highlighted differently from completed and pending steps.
11. Verify that 11. Verify "Next," "Previous," and "Finish" buttons are clearly visible and properly positioned.
12. Verify that 12. Verify the wizard layout is responsive and works on tablet screen sizes.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that 13. Verify behavior when the user completes all steps, then goes back and changes data in an earlier step — subsequent steps should remain accessible.
14. Verify that 14. Verify behavior when the browser is closed mid-assessment and reopened — data should be recoverable if auto-save is implemented.
15. Verify that 15. Verify the wizard handles an assessment with a very large number of sections (e.g., 15+).

Security:
16. Verify that 16. Verify that only authenticated users with assessment permissions can view and complete the initial assessment.
17. Verify that 17. Verify that resident data entered in the assessment is transmitted securely over HTTPS.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the step-by-step wizard loads with the first section within 2 seconds.
Verify navigation between steps (Next/Previous) completes within 1 second.

User Story: As a user, I should be able to view the completion percentage based on the filled assessment details
Description of User Story: This story enables the user to view a real-time completion percentage that reflects how much of the initial assessment has been filled out for a resident within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. The completion percentage is calculated based on the number of required and optional fields that have been filled across all sections of the assessment, and it updates dynamically as the user enters or clears data. A progress bar or percentage indicator is displayed prominently on the assessment screen (e.g., in the header or step indicator area), giving the user immediate visual feedback on how close they are to completing the assessment. The percentage accounts for all assessment sections, not just the current step, providing a holistic view of the overall assessment completion status. This feature is essential for tracking assessment progress, especially when assessments are completed over multiple sessions, as it allows users and supervisors to quickly identify partially completed assessments that need attention. Without this feature, users would have no easy way to determine how much of a lengthy assessment has been completed or how much work remains, leading to abandoned or incomplete assessments.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If all required fields across all sections are filled, Then the completion percentage must display 100%.
   Else if some fields are empty, Then the percentage must reflect the proportion of filled fields to total required fields.

2. If the user fills a new required field, Then the percentage must increase immediately without requiring a page refresh.
   Else if the user clears a previously filled field, Then the percentage must decrease immediately.

3. If the assessment is reopened from a saved draft, Then the completion percentage must be recalculated based on the saved data and displayed correctly.
   Else if the draft has no data saved, Then the percentage must display 0%.

4. If optional fields are also filled, Then the system may include them in the percentage calculation or display a separate indicator for optional field completion.
   Else if only required fields are considered, Then optional fields must not affect the main completion percentage.

5. If the completion percentage reaches 100%, Then the system must enable the "Submit" or "Finish" button.
   Else if the percentage is below 100%, Then the system may allow saving as draft but must prevent final submission.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that the completion percentage is displayed on the assessment screen when it loads.
2. Verify that 2. Verify the percentage starts at 0% for a new blank assessment.
3. Verify that 3. Verify the percentage increases when a required field is filled.
4. Verify that 4. Verify the percentage reaches 100% when all required fields are completed.
5. Verify that 5. Verify the percentage is correct when reopening a saved draft assessment.

Negative:
6. Verify that 6. Verify the percentage decreases when a filled required field is cleared.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that the percentage does not exceed 100% regardless of how many optional fields are filled.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that a corrupted or missing field does not crash the percentage calculation.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that 9. Verify the completion percentage is displayed as a progress bar or prominent label on the assessment screen.
10. Verify that 10. Verify the percentage updates in real-time without a page reload.
11. Verify that 11. Verify the progress bar changes color as completion increases (e.g., red at low, yellow at mid, green at high).
12. Verify that 12. Verify the percentage is visible at all times during the assessment, even when scrolling.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that 13. Verify the percentage calculation when an assessment has a mix of required and optional fields.
14. Verify that 14. Verify behavior when a required field type is a multi-select and only partial selections are made.
15. Verify that 15. Verify the percentage rounds correctly when the field count results in non-integer percentages (e.g., 33.33%).

Security:
16. Verify that 16. Verify that the completion percentage data cannot be manipulated via browser developer tools to bypass submission validation.
17. Verify that 17. Verify that the percentage calculation is performed server-side or validated server-side before allowing final submission.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the completion percentage recalculates and updates within 500 milliseconds of a field change.
Verify that an assessment with 100+ fields displays the percentage without noticeable UI lag.

User Story: As a user, I should be able to save as draft the assessment at any point without losing data.
Description of User Story: This story enables the user to save the initial assessment as a draft at any point during the data entry process within the Nemi Care Facility Portal, ensuring that no entered data is lost even if the assessment cannot be completed in a single session. The "Save as Draft" button is available on every step of the assessment wizard, and clicking it saves all data entered up to that point — including partially completed sections — without requiring validation of mandatory fields. When the user returns to the assessment later, all previously entered data is restored exactly as it was saved, allowing the user to continue from where they left off. The system also supports auto-save functionality that periodically saves draft data at regular intervals (e.g., every 60 seconds) to protect against unexpected session loss or browser crashes. This capability is critical for initial assessments that may take 30-60 minutes to complete, as clinical staff are often interrupted by urgent patient care needs and cannot always finish an assessment in one sitting. Without this feature, users would lose all progress if they navigate away or experience a browser crash, forcing them to re-enter all data from scratch.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the user clicks "Save as Draft," Then the system must save all entered data without mandatory field validation and display a success confirmation.
   Else if the save fails due to a server error, Then the system must display an error message and prompt the user to retry.

2. If the user reopens a drafted assessment, Then the system must restore all previously saved data and position the user at the step where they left off.
   Else if the draft data is corrupted, Then the system must notify the user and offer the option to start fresh.

3. If auto-save triggers after the configured interval, Then the system must save silently in the background without interrupting the user.
   Else if the auto-save fails, Then the system must display a subtle warning indicator (e.g., a small icon) without blocking the user's workflow.

4. If the user clicks "Save as Draft" multiple times, Then each save must overwrite the previous draft with the latest data.
   Else if the user clicks "Submit" (final submission), Then the draft status must be cleared and the assessment must be marked as complete.

5. If the browser crashes during assessment entry, Then the last auto-saved draft must be available when the user logs back in.
   Else if no auto-save occurred before the crash, Then the system must inform the user that no draft data is available.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that "Save as Draft" is available on every step of the assessment wizard.
2. Verify that 2. Verify clicking "Save as Draft" saves all entered data and shows a success confirmation.
3. Verify that 3. Verify that drafts are saved without requiring mandatory field validation.
4. Verify that 4. Verify reopening a drafted assessment restores all previously saved data.
5. Verify that 5. Verify auto-save triggers at the configured interval and saves data in the background.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that after a browser crash, reopening the assessment restores the last auto-saved draft.

Negative:
7. Verify that 7. Verify that if the server is unreachable during "Save as Draft," an error message is displayed.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that corrupted draft data triggers a notification with an option to start fresh.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that clicking "Save as Draft" with absolutely no data entered still creates a valid draft record.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that 10. Verify the "Save as Draft" button is clearly visible on each step of the wizard.
11. Verify that 11. Verify the "Draft saved successfully" confirmation appears as a toast notification and auto-dismisses.
12. Verify that 12. Verify an auto-save indicator (e.g., "Last saved at 10:32 AM") is displayed on the assessment screen.
13. Verify that 13. Verify the assessment list marks drafted assessments differently from completed ones (e.g., "Draft" badge).

Edge Cases:
14. Verify that 14. Verify that rapid consecutive clicks on "Save as Draft" do not create multiple draft entries or cause data corruption.
15. Verify that 15. Verify behavior when the session expires while the user is filling in the assessment — auto-saved data should be recoverable after re-login.
16. Verify that 16. Verify that saving a draft with special characters in text fields preserves the characters correctly.

Security:
17. Verify that 17. Verify that draft assessment data is stored securely and accessible only to authorized users.
18. Verify that 18. Verify that auto-save network requests are encrypted over HTTPS.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that "Save as Draft" completes and shows confirmation within 2 seconds.
Verify that auto-save operates in the background without causing UI lag or noticeable delay.

User Story: As a user, I should be able to review & download the completed assessment, with a preview of all filled sections
Description of User Story: This story enables the user to review and download a completed initial assessment with a full preview of all filled sections within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. After the assessment is marked as complete, the user can access a read-only review screen that displays all assessment sections and their responses in a consolidated, printable format. The review screen provides a comprehensive view of the entire assessment — including personal information, medical history, functional abilities, cognitive assessment, nutritional status, and all other sections — allowing the user to verify the data before sharing or archiving. The user can download the completed assessment as a PDF document that maintains professional formatting with the facility branding, resident information header, section headings, and all responses laid out in a clean, readable format. This review and download capability is essential for sharing completed assessments with external parties such as physicians, family members, insurance companies, or regulatory auditors who need a portable document. Without this feature, users would need to manually transcribe or screenshot the assessment data for external sharing, which is error-prone and unprofessional.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the assessment is marked as complete, Then the "Review" and "Download" options must be available.
   Else if the assessment is still in draft status, Then the "Review" option may be available for preview but the "Download" option must be disabled or show a warning.

2. If the user clicks "Download as PDF," Then the system must generate a well-formatted PDF with all assessment data and initiate the file download.
   Else if PDF generation fails, Then the system must display an error message "Unable to generate PDF. Please try again."

3. If the assessment has all sections filled, Then the review screen must display all sections with their responses.
   Else if some optional sections were left blank, Then those sections must be displayed with a "Not Completed" or "N/A" indicator.

4. If the user clicks "Print," Then the print layout must exclude navigation elements and render only the assessment content in a printer-friendly format.
   Else if the browser print dialog is cancelled, Then the user must be returned to the review screen.

5. If the downloaded PDF is opened on different devices or PDF readers, Then the formatting must be consistent and readable across all standard PDF viewers.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that clicking "Review" on a completed assessment opens the read-only preview with all sections.
2. Verify that 2. Verify the review screen displays all section headings, questions, and responses accurately.
3. Verify that 3. Verify clicking "Download as PDF" generates and downloads a PDF of the assessment.
4. Verify that 4. Verify the downloaded PDF contains facility branding, resident name, assessment date, and all responses.
5. Verify that 5. Verify the print layout produces a clean, readable document.

Negative:
6. Verify that 6. Verify that "Download" is disabled or shows a warning for draft assessments.
7. Verify that 7. Verify that if PDF generation fails, an appropriate error message is displayed.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that blank optional sections are shown with "Not Completed" or "N/A" in the review.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that 9. Verify the review screen is read-only and no fields are editable.
10. Verify that 10. Verify the review screen is scrollable with a clear table of contents or section anchors for quick navigation.
11. Verify that 11. Verify the PDF download button is clearly visible and labeled.
12. Verify that 12. Verify the print preview excludes navigation menus, buttons, and other non-content elements.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that 13. Verify behavior when an assessment has a very large number of sections and data — the PDF should generate without errors.
14. Verify that 14. Verify the PDF handles special characters, long text responses, and embedded images correctly.
15. Verify that 15. Verify behavior when downloading the PDF on a slow internet connection — a loading indicator should be shown.

Security:
16. Verify that 16. Verify that only authenticated users with review permissions can access the review screen and download the PDF.
17. Verify that 17. Verify that the downloaded PDF does not contain hidden metadata that exposes system internals.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the review screen loads all sections within 3 seconds for a completed assessment.
Verify PDF generation and download initiates within 5 seconds for a full assessment.

User Story: As a system, once the resident is admitted, the system should automatically generate an initial care plan based on the completed assessment.
Description of User Story: This story enables the system to automatically generate an initial care plan for a resident once the initial assessment is completed and the resident is formally admitted within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. When the assessment is submitted and the admission process is finalized, the system analyzes the assessment data — including medical conditions, functional limitations, cognitive status, nutritional needs, fall risk level, and other clinical findings — and automatically creates a care plan with relevant care goals, interventions, and responsible staff assignments. The generated care plan uses predefined rules and templates that map specific assessment findings to appropriate care plan components; for example, a high fall risk score triggers fall prevention interventions, and a depression screening score above threshold triggers mental health monitoring goals. The auto-generated care plan serves as a starting point that clinical staff can review, customize, and approve rather than building from scratch, significantly reducing the time to establish care for a newly admitted resident. This automation is essential for ensuring that no critical care need identified during assessment is overlooked when creating the care plan, as manual care plan creation is prone to human error and omission. Without this feature, staff would need to manually read through the entire assessment and create each care plan component individually, which is time-consuming and risks missing critical interventions.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the initial assessment is complete and the resident is admitted, Then the system must automatically generate a care plan within a defined timeframe (e.g., within 1 minute of admission).
   Else if the assessment is incomplete at the time of admission, Then the system must not generate a care plan and must flag the assessment as pending.

2. If the assessment data includes a high fall risk score, Then the care plan must include fall prevention interventions.
   Else if the fall risk is low, Then fall prevention interventions may be omitted or listed as optional.

3. If the assessment data includes a PHQ-9 score of 10 or higher, Then the care plan must include mental health monitoring goals.
   Else if the PHQ-9 score is below 10, Then mental health monitoring is optional.

4. If the auto-generated care plan is reviewed by clinical staff and they approve it, Then the care plan must be activated.
   Else if the staff edits the care plan before approval, Then the modified version must be saved and activated.

5. If the system fails to generate the care plan due to a processing error, Then it must log the error, notify the compliance manager, and allow manual care plan creation.
   Else if generation succeeds, Then the care plan must appear in the resident's profile under the Care Plan section.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that completing an assessment and admitting the resident triggers automatic care plan generation.
2. Verify that 2. Verify the auto-generated care plan contains care goals derived from the assessment data.
3. Verify that 3. Verify the care plan includes fall prevention interventions when the fall risk score is high.
4. Verify that 4. Verify the care plan includes mental health monitoring when the PHQ-9 score indicates moderate or higher depression.
5. Verify that 5. Verify the care plan includes nutritional interventions when the assessment identifies nutritional deficiencies.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that clinical staff can edit the auto-generated care plan before approval.
7. Verify that 7. Verify the approved care plan appears in the resident's profile under the Care Plan section.

Negative:
8. Verify that 8. Verify that the care plan is not generated if the assessment is incomplete at the time of admission.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that if care plan generation fails, an error is logged and the compliance manager is notified.
10. Verify that 10. Verify that the system does not generate duplicate care plans if the admission event is triggered multiple times.

UI Scenarios:
11. Verify that 11. Verify the auto-generated care plan is displayed in a readable format with clearly labeled goals, interventions, and assignments.
12. Verify that 12. Verify an "Auto-Generated" badge or label is displayed on the care plan to distinguish it from manually created plans.
13. Verify that 13. Verify the "Edit" and "Approve" buttons are available on the auto-generated care plan.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify that 14. Verify behavior when the assessment has conflicting data (e.g., low fall risk but mobility issues noted in observations) — the care plan should err on the side of caution.
15. Verify that 15. Verify that care plan generation handles assessments with missing optional sections gracefully.
16. Verify that 16. Verify behavior when the mapping rules do not cover a specific assessment finding — the system should include a generic "Review Required" item.

Security:
17. Verify that 17. Verify that only authorized clinical staff and compliance managers can view and edit the auto-generated care plan.
18. Verify that 18. Verify that the care plan generation process logs are auditable and tamper-proof.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the care plan is auto-generated within 60 seconds of the resident's admission being finalized.
Verify that viewing the auto-generated care plan loads within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a user, details from the completed assessment should auto-populate into relevant subsections of the move-in form (e.g., resident info, care, allergies).
Description of User Story: This story enables the system to automatically populate relevant subsections of the move-in form with data from the completed initial assessment within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. When the user opens the move-in form after an initial assessment has been completed for the resident, the system identifies data points from the assessment that map to fields in the move-in form — such as resident personal information, care needs, allergies, medical conditions, emergency contacts, and functional limitations — and pre-fills those fields automatically. The auto-populated data is editable, allowing the user to review and make corrections if needed, but it eliminates the need to re-enter information that was already captured during the assessment. Field mappings between the assessment and the move-in form are predefined, covering common fields such as resident name, date of birth, gender, primary diagnosis, allergy list, dietary restrictions, mobility status, and cognitive status. This data auto-population is essential for reducing duplicate data entry, minimizing transcription errors, and speeding up the move-in process for newly admitted residents. Without this feature, staff would need to manually reference the completed assessment and re-type all relevant details into the move-in form, which is time-consuming, error-prone, and creates a poor user experience.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the initial assessment is complete and the user opens the move-in form, Then the system must auto-populate all mapped fields from the assessment data.
   Else if the assessment is not yet complete, Then the move-in form must open with blank fields and display a note "Assessment data not available for auto-population."

2. If a mapped field in the assessment has data, Then the corresponding move-in form field must be pre-filled with that data.
   Else if a mapped field in the assessment is blank, Then the corresponding move-in form field must remain empty for manual entry.

3. If the user edits an auto-populated field in the move-in form, Then the modified value must be saved in the move-in form only.
   Else if the user does not modify the field, Then the assessment data must be saved as-is in the move-in form.

4. If the assessment is updated after the move-in form has been auto-populated, Then the move-in form must not retroactively change unless the user explicitly triggers a re-sync.
   Else if the user triggers a re-sync, Then the system must update the move-in form with the latest assessment data and flag changed fields.

5. If the move-in form is saved with auto-populated data, Then the original assessment record must remain unchanged and independent.
   Else if there is a conflict between assessment data and manually entered data, Then the move-in form must use the manually entered value.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 1. Verify that opening the move-in form after a completed assessment auto-populates the Resident Info fields (name, DOB, gender).
2. Verify that 2. Verify the Allergies section is auto-populated with the allergy list from the assessment.
3. Verify that 3. Verify the Care section is auto-populated with care needs, primary diagnosis, and mobility status.
4. Verify that 4. Verify the cognitive status is auto-populated from the cognitive assessment section.
5. Verify that 5. Verify that editing an auto-populated field and saving stores the modified value.
6. Verify that 6. Verify that saving the move-in form does not alter the original assessment data.

Negative:
7. Verify that 7. Verify that if the assessment is incomplete, the move-in form opens with blank fields and a note indicating assessment data is unavailable.
8. Verify that 8. Verify that if a mapped assessment field is blank, the corresponding move-in field remains empty.
9. Verify that 9. Verify that re-triggering auto-population does not overwrite manually entered data without user confirmation.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that 10. Verify auto-populated fields are visually indicated (e.g., a subtle background color or icon) to distinguish them from manually entered data.
11. Verify that 11. Verify the move-in form layout clearly organizes auto-populated data by section (Resident Info, Care, Allergies).
12. Verify that 12. Verify that a "Data sourced from assessment" label or tooltip is shown on auto-populated fields.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that 13. Verify behavior when the assessment is updated after the move-in form has been saved — the move-in form should not change retroactively.
14. Verify that 14. Verify behavior when the assessment contains data in a different format than expected by the move-in form field (e.g., date format mismatch).
15. Verify that 15. Verify auto-population when the assessment has extremely long text entries in observation fields.
16. Verify that 16. Verify behavior when the user opens the move-in form simultaneously in two browser tabs.

Security:
17. Verify that 17. Verify that auto-populated data is transmitted securely between the assessment and move-in form services.
18. Verify that 18. Verify that only authorized users can access the move-in form with auto-populated assessment data.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify the move-in form loads with auto-populated data within 3 seconds.
Verify that the auto-population mapping process does not add more than 1 second to the form load time.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to generate monthly Attendance Report for previous month's services.
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to generate a monthly Attendance Report that summarizes all services rendered during the previous month within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. The provider navigates to the Analytics & Reports section under ADH Billing, selects the target month and year, and clicks a Generate Report button to produce the Attendance Report. The generated report includes patient names, dates of attendance, service codes, units provided, and total billable hours for each participant across the selected month. The system automatically pulls attendance data from daily check-in/check-out records and cross-references with authorized service plans to ensure accuracy. This report is essential for the monthly billing cycle as it forms the basis for Medicaid claims submission and provider reimbursement. Without an automated generation mechanism, providers would need to manually compile attendance records, increasing the risk of billing errors and delayed submissions.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the provider selects a valid previous month and clicks Generate Report, Then the system must compile attendance data and display the monthly Attendance Report.
   Else if the provider selects the current month or a future month, Then the system must display a validation message "Only previous months can be selected for report generation."

2. If the selected month has attendance records for one or more patients, Then the report must display all patients with their attendance details, service codes, and billable hours.
   Else if the selected month has no attendance records, Then the system must display a message "No attendance data found for the selected month."

3. If the provider clicks Download after report generation, Then the system must export the report in the selected format (PDF or Excel).
   Else if the download fails due to a system error, Then the system must display an error message and allow the provider to retry.

4. If a patient has attendance records but their service plan was not authorized for that month, Then the report must flag those entries with a warning indicator.

5. If the provider regenerates a report for a month that was previously generated, Then the system must overwrite the previous version with the latest data.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that navigating to ADH Billing Reports displays the month/year selector and Generate Report button.
2. Verify that selecting a previous month and clicking Generate Report produces an Attendance Report with correct patient attendance data.
3. Verify that the generated report includes columns: Patient Name, Date of Service, Service Code, Units, Total Billable Hours.
4. Verify that clicking Download exports the report in PDF format with all data intact.
5. Verify that clicking Download exports the report in Excel format with all data intact.
6. Verify that regenerating a report for the same month reflects the most recent attendance data.

Negative:
7. Verify that selecting the current month and clicking Generate Report shows a validation message "Only previous months can be selected for report generation."
8. Verify that selecting a future month disables the Generate Report button or shows a validation error.
9. Verify that generating a report for a month with no attendance data displays "No attendance data found for the selected month."

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify the month/year selector is clearly labeled and defaults to the most recent previous month.
11. Verify the Generate Report button is prominently displayed and visually distinct.
12. Verify the report table is properly formatted with alternating row colors and readable column headers.
13. Verify the Download button displays format options (PDF, Excel) in a dropdown.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify report generation when a patient was admitted mid-month and only has partial attendance.
15. Verify report generation when a patient was discharged during the selected month.
16. Verify report accuracy when attendance records were retroactively edited after the month ended.

Security:
17. Verify that only authenticated providers with ADH Billing access can generate Attendance Reports.
18. Verify that the report data cannot be accessed by manipulating API query parameters to request unauthorized facility data.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that generating an Attendance Report for a month with 200+ patients completes within 5 seconds.
2. Verify that downloading the generated report in PDF or Excel format completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to submit monthly Attendance Report to state Medicaid.
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to submit a previously generated monthly Attendance Report directly to the state Medicaid agency through the Nemi Care Facility Portal. After generating and reviewing the Attendance Report, the provider clicks a Submit to Medicaid button which initiates the electronic submission process to the state's designated Medicaid portal or clearinghouse. The system validates the report data for completeness and compliance with state Medicaid formatting requirements before submission, flagging any discrepancies such as missing service codes or unauthorized service dates. Upon successful submission, the system records the submission timestamp, generates a confirmation reference number, and updates the report status to "Submitted." This electronic submission capability eliminates the need for manual paper-based or portal-based claim submissions, reducing processing time and human errors. Without this feature, providers would need to manually re-enter attendance data into the state Medicaid portal, a time-consuming process prone to transcription errors.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the provider clicks Submit to Medicaid and the report passes all validations, Then the system must transmit the report electronically and display a confirmation with reference number.
   Else if the report fails validation, Then the system must display a detailed error list and block submission.

2. If the submission is successful, Then the report status must update to "Submitted" with timestamp and reference number.
   Else if the submission fails due to a network or clearinghouse error, Then the system must display an error message and retain the report in "Ready to Submit" status.

3. If the provider attempts to submit a report that has already been successfully submitted, Then the system must prompt a confirmation dialog asking if they want to resubmit.
   Else if the provider cancels the resubmit prompt, Then the report status must remain unchanged.

4. If a report contains entries with unauthorized service dates, Then the system must flag those entries and exclude them from submission while allowing the remaining valid entries to be submitted.

5. If the provider does not have Medicaid submission permissions, Then the Submit to Medicaid button must be hidden or disabled.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that a generated Attendance Report displays the Submit to Medicaid button on the report detail view.
2. Verify that clicking Submit to Medicaid triggers validation and submits a valid report with a confirmation reference number.
3. Verify that after successful submission, the report status changes to "Submitted" with the correct date and reference number.
4. Verify that a report with validation errors displays the error list and prevents submission.
5. Verify that fixing validation errors and resubmitting completes successfully.

Negative:
6. Verify that submitting a report with missing service codes displays a validation error specifying the missing codes.
7. Verify that submitting a report with unauthorized service dates flags those entries and prevents their inclusion.
8. Verify that a network failure during submission displays an error message and does not change the report status.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify the Submit to Medicaid button is clearly visible and styled distinctly from other action buttons.
10. Verify that a loading indicator is displayed during the submission process.
11. Verify the confirmation dialog displays the reference number, submission date, and a success icon.
12. Verify the validation error list is presented in a scrollable panel with clear error descriptions.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify behavior when the provider clicks Submit to Medicaid multiple times rapidly — only one submission should be processed.
14. Verify behavior when submitting a report for a month where some patients were transferred between facilities.
15. Verify resubmission of a previously submitted report updates the reference number and timestamp.

Security:
16. Verify that only providers with Medicaid submission permissions can see and use the Submit to Medicaid button.
17. Verify that the submission payload is encrypted during transmission to the state Medicaid clearinghouse.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that the validation step for a report with 200+ patient entries completes within 3 seconds.
2. Verify that the electronic submission to the state Medicaid clearinghouse completes within 10 seconds under normal network conditions.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to track submission status and acknowledgment from state for monthly reports.
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to track the real-time status and acknowledgment of monthly Attendance Reports that have been submitted to the state Medicaid agency through the Nemi Care Facility Portal. After a report is submitted, the system periodically polls the state clearinghouse for status updates and displays the current status such as Submitted, Received, In Review, Accepted, Rejected, or Partially Accepted. The provider can view a Submission Tracker dashboard that lists all submitted reports with their current statuses, submission dates, reference numbers, and any acknowledgment messages from the state. When the state sends an acknowledgment (acceptance, rejection, or request for correction), the system updates the report status and sends a notification to the provider. This tracking capability provides transparency into the billing cycle and allows providers to quickly identify and address rejected or partially accepted reports. Without this feature, providers would need to manually log into the state Medicaid portal to check submission statuses, causing delays in resolving rejections and resubmissions.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the state acknowledges a submitted report as "Accepted," Then the system must update the report status to "Accepted" and record the acknowledgment date.
   Else if the state rejects the report, Then the system must update the status to "Rejected," display the rejection reason, and enable the Resubmit button.

2. If the state partially accepts a report, Then the system must mark accepted entries as "Accepted" and rejected entries as "Rejected" with individual rejection reasons.

3. If the provider clicks on a report in the Submission Tracker, Then the system must display the full status transition history with timestamps.
   Else if no status updates have been received yet, Then the system must display "Awaiting Acknowledgment" with the last checked timestamp.

4. If the provider has notification preferences enabled, Then the system must send in-app and email notifications on status changes.
   Else if notifications are disabled, Then the status update must still be visible on the Submission Tracker without sending alerts.

5. If the system cannot reach the state clearinghouse for a status check, Then it must display "Status Check Pending" and retry automatically on the next polling cycle.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Submission Tracker dashboard displays all submitted reports with Report Month, Submission Date, Reference Number, and Status columns.
2. Verify that a report acknowledged as "Accepted" by the state shows "Accepted" status with the acknowledgment date.
3. Verify that a rejected report displays the rejection reason and a Resubmit action button.
4. Verify that a partially accepted report shows individual entry statuses (accepted vs. rejected) with reasons.
5. Verify that clicking a report row opens the detail panel with the full status transition history.
6. Verify that the provider receives an in-app notification when a report status changes.

Negative:
7. Verify that the Submission Tracker displays "No submissions found" when no reports have been submitted.
8. Verify that when the clearinghouse is unreachable, the system shows "Status Check Pending" and does not display a false status.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that status values are color-coded (e.g., green for Accepted, red for Rejected, yellow for In Review).
10. Verify the Submission Tracker table supports sorting by Submission Date and Status columns.
11. Verify the detail panel shows a timeline visualization of status transitions.
12. Verify the notification badge on the navigation bar updates when a new status change occurs.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify behavior when a report is resubmitted after rejection — the tracker should show both the original and resubmission entries.
14. Verify behavior when the state sends multiple acknowledgments for the same report in quick succession.
15. Verify that the tracker correctly handles reports spanning a month boundary where services crossed two months.

Security:
16. Verify that providers can only view submission statuses for their own facility's reports.
17. Verify that the status polling endpoint is authenticated and does not expose report data to unauthorized users.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that the Submission Tracker dashboard loads with 50+ submitted reports within 3 seconds.
2. Verify that the detail panel with full status history loads within 2 seconds when a report row is clicked.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to view historical Attendance Reports for audit and reconciliation purposes.
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to access and view previously generated and submitted Attendance Reports for audit and reconciliation purposes within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. The provider navigates to the Historical Reports section under ADH Billing where they can search and filter reports by month, year, submission status, and facility. Each historical report is displayed in a read-only view with the same level of detail as the originally generated report, including patient names, attendance dates, service codes, units, and billable hours. The system maintains a complete audit trail showing who generated the report, when it was submitted, any modifications made, and the current status. This capability is critical for annual audits, state compliance reviews, and internal reconciliation where providers must demonstrate that billed services match documented attendance. Without historical report access, providers would need to regenerate reports from raw data, risking discrepancies if underlying records were updated after the original report was created.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the provider navigates to Historical Reports, Then the system must display all previously generated reports sorted by most recent date first.
   Else if no historical reports exist, Then the system must display "No historical reports available."

2. If the provider applies a filter for a specific month and year, Then only reports matching that period must be displayed.
   Else if no reports match the filter criteria, Then the system must display "No reports found for the selected criteria."

3. If the provider clicks on a historical report, Then the system must open it in read-only mode with complete attendance details and audit trail.

4. If the provider clicks Download on a historical report, Then the system must export the report as it existed at generation time, not with current data.
   Else if the download fails, Then the system must display an error and allow retry.

5. If the provider searches by reference number, Then the system must return the exact matching report.
   Else if the reference number does not match any report, Then the system must display "No report found with this reference number."
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Historical Reports page displays all previously generated Attendance Reports sorted by most recent first.
2. Verify that filtering by a specific month and year returns only matching reports.
3. Verify that filtering by submission status (e.g., Submitted, Accepted, Rejected) returns only matching reports.
4. Verify that clicking a historical report opens it in read-only mode with full attendance details.
5. Verify that the audit trail section shows generation date, generator name, submission date, and reference number.
6. Verify that downloading a historical report preserves the data as it was at generation time.
7. Verify that searching by reference number returns the correct report.

Negative:
8. Verify that applying filters with no matching results displays "No reports found for the selected criteria."
9. Verify that searching with an invalid reference number displays "No report found with this reference number."
10. Verify that historical reports cannot be edited — all fields must be read-only.

UI Scenarios:
11. Verify the Historical Reports page has clearly labeled filter dropdowns for Month, Year, and Status.
12. Verify the report list table is paginated when more than 20 reports exist.
13. Verify the audit trail section is visually separated from the report data with clear labels.
14. Verify the search bar with reference number input is prominently placed at the top of the page.

Edge Cases:
15. Verify that a historical report reflects the data at generation time even if patient attendance records were later modified.
16. Verify behavior when viewing a report for a month where a patient was transferred between facilities mid-month.

Security:
17. Verify that providers can only view historical reports belonging to their own facility.
18. Verify that the download endpoint does not allow unauthorized users to access report files by manipulating the URL.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that the Historical Reports page loads with 100+ reports within 3 seconds.
2. Verify that filtering and searching across historical reports returns results within 2 seconds.

Sub-Module: Live Census by Level of Care
User Story: As a provider, I should be able to view real-time census broken down by facility, program, and level of care to monitor capacity utilization.
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to view a real-time census dashboard that displays the current patient/resident count broken down by facility, program type, and level of care within the Nemi Care Facility Portal. The dashboard presents a hierarchical view where the provider can see total census at the facility level, then drill down into program-level census (e.g., ADH, ALF, Respite Care) and further into level-of-care breakdowns (e.g., Skilled Nursing, Personal Care, Supervisory Care). Each level displays the current occupied count, total capacity, available beds or slots, and a utilization percentage indicator with visual cues such as color-coded progress bars. The data refreshes automatically at configurable intervals or can be manually refreshed by the provider. This real-time census visibility is essential for operational decision-making such as intake planning, staffing adjustments, and ensuring regulatory compliance with maximum occupancy limits. Without this dashboard, providers would need to manually count patients across programs and levels, leading to delayed capacity decisions and potential regulatory violations.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the provider navigates to the Live Census dashboard, Then the system must display current census data for all facilities with occupied count, capacity, and utilization percentage.
