10. UI Scenario: The status badge on the user card and profile page updates immediately after the toggle action without requiring a page refresh.
11. Edge Case: Provider Admin rapidly toggles a user status multiple times within seconds; the system processes only the latest confirmed action and debounces intermediate clicks.
12. Edge Case: Provider Admin disables a user and immediately tries to re-enable before the disable action completes; system queues the actions sequentially and processes in order.
13. Security: Only users with "user_admin" permission can toggle enable/disable status; the toggle is hidden or grayed out for users without this permission.
Performance Scenarios:
1. The enable/disable toggle action completes and updates the user status badge within 2 seconds, including session termination for disabled users.
2. Loading the audit trail for a user with 100 or more enable/disable events renders within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a user, I should be able to upload and track licenses and credentials for each staff member so compliance requirements are met.
Description of User Story: The Credentialing Management screen provides a centralized interface for uploading, viewing, and tracking professional licenses and credentials for each staff member. The screen is accessible from the staff profile and displays a table of all uploaded credentials including credential type, issuing authority, license number, issue date, expiry date, and current status (Active, Expiring Soon, Expired). Users can add a new credential by clicking the "Add Credential" button which opens a modal with fields for credential type dropdown, license number, issuing authority, issue date, expiry date, and a file upload area for the credential document. The system automatically calculates the days remaining until expiry and color-codes the status column accordingly with green for active, yellow for expiring within 30 days, and red for expired. Each credential row includes action buttons for viewing the uploaded document, editing the record, and archiving expired credentials.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the user uploads a credential document exceeding the maximum file size (e.g., 10 MB), Then the system displays a file size error and rejects the upload.
2. If the user enters an expiry date that is in the past, Then the system accepts the record but immediately marks the status as "Expired" in red.
3. If the user attempts to save a credential without attaching a document, Then the system displays a warning but allows the save with a "Document Pending" indicator on the record.
4. If the user enters a duplicate credential type and license number combination for the same staff member, Then the system warns about the duplicate and asks whether to update the existing record.
5. If the user selects a credential type that does not require an expiry date (e.g., a degree certificate), Then the expiry date field becomes optional and the status defaults to "Active".
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: User adds a new RN license credential with license number, issuing state, issue date, expiry date, and uploads a PDF copy; the credential appears in the table with "Active" status in green.
2. Positive Functional: User views a credential expiring in 15 days and sees "Expiring Soon" status in yellow with "15 days remaining" displayed.
3. Positive Functional: User edits an existing credential to update the expiry date after renewal; the status changes from "Expiring Soon" back to "Active".
4. Positive Functional: User clicks the view action on a credential row and the uploaded document opens in a document viewer or downloads.
5. Negative: User attempts to save a credential with the license number field empty; system displays a validation error requiring the license number.
6. Negative: User uploads a file in an unsupported format (e.g., .exe); system rejects the upload with a file type validation error.
7. Negative: User enters an issue date later than the expiry date; system displays a date validation error indicating issue date must precede expiry date.
8. UI Scenario: The credentials table displays columns for credential type, license number, issuing authority, issue date, expiry date, and status with color-coded badges (green, yellow, red).
9. UI Scenario: The Add Credential modal displays all input fields in a clear form layout with the file upload area supporting drag-and-drop.
10. UI Scenario: Action buttons (View, Edit, Archive) on each credential row are visible as icon buttons aligned in the last column.
11. Edge Case: A staff member has 20 credentials uploaded; the table supports scrolling or pagination to display all records without layout issues.
12. Edge Case: A credential expires at exactly midnight; the system transitions the status from "Expiring Soon" to "Expired" at the correct boundary.
13. Security: Only users with "credential_manage" permission can add, edit, or archive credentials; users with "credential_view" can see the table but not modify records.
Performance Scenarios:
1. The Credentialing tab loads and displays all credentials for a staff member with up to 30 records within 2 seconds, including status calculations.
2. Uploading a 5 MB credential document and saving the record completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a user, I should receive alerts when staff credentials or licenses are nearing expiry so renewals can be completed on time.
Description of User Story: The Credential Expiry Alert system proactively notifies users when staff licenses or credentials are approaching their expiration dates, ensuring timely renewals and continuous compliance. The system evaluates all credential expiry dates daily and generates alerts at configurable threshold intervals such as 90 days, 60 days, 30 days, and 7 days before expiry. Alerts are delivered through multiple channels including in-app notification badges on the dashboard, email notifications to designated compliance officers, and a dedicated Expiring Credentials report accessible from the Credentialing module. The dashboard widget displays a summary count of credentials expiring within each threshold period with drill-down capability to view the specific staff members and credentials affected. Each alert includes the staff member name, credential type, expiry date, and days remaining, with a direct link to the credential record for initiating the renewal process.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If a credential expires today and has not been renewed, Then the system escalates the alert to "Expired" severity and sends an urgent notification to the compliance officer and the staff member supervisor.
2. If the compliance officer has already acknowledged an expiry alert, Then the system does not resend the same alert but retains it in the notification history.
3. If multiple credentials for the same staff member are expiring within the same threshold period, Then the system groups them into a single consolidated alert listing all affected credentials.
4. If the email notification delivery fails, Then the system retries once and logs the failure for the administrator to review.
5. If the alert threshold settings are changed to a shorter period than previously configured, Then existing alerts outside the new threshold are archived but not deleted.
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: A nursing license expiring in 28 days triggers a 30-day threshold alert; the compliance officer receives an email and in-app notification with the staff name, credential type, and expiry date.
2. Positive Functional: Dashboard widget displays "3 expiring within 30 days" and "1 expiring within 7 days"; admin clicks the 30-day count and sees the three specific credentials listed.
3. Positive Functional: User clicks on an expiry alert notification and is taken directly to the credential record where they can initiate the renewal by uploading the new document.
4. Positive Functional: Admin updates the alert threshold configuration to include a 45-day threshold; the next daily run generates alerts for credentials expiring within 45 days.
5. Negative: System attempts to send an email alert but the compliance officer email is invalid; the system logs the delivery failure and displays a notification to the admin.
6. Negative: User tries to dismiss an alert for a credential that has already expired; system prevents dismissal and requires the credential to be renewed or archived first.
7. Negative: Alert evaluation runs but no credentials are nearing expiry; no alerts are generated and the dashboard widget shows zero counts.
8. UI Scenario: The dashboard expiry widget displays threshold categories (90, 60, 30, 7 days) as color-coded sections with counts, using green, yellow, orange, and red respectively.
9. UI Scenario: In-app notification badges show the total count of pending expiry alerts on the bell icon in the navigation bar.
10. UI Scenario: The Expiring Credentials report table displays staff name, credential type, expiry date, days remaining, and a "Renew" action link in a sortable table.
11. Edge Case: A credential expiry date is updated to extend by one day past the 30-day threshold; the system clears the 30-day alert on the next daily evaluation.
12. Edge Case: Daylight saving time change occurs during the alert evaluation period; the system correctly calculates days remaining using UTC dates to avoid threshold miscalculation.
13. Security: Only users with "compliance_manage" or "credential_view" permissions receive expiry alerts; unauthorized users do not see the dashboard widget or receive notifications.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Daily credential expiry evaluation across 500 staff members with up to 10 credentials each completes within 30 seconds.
2. The dashboard expiry widget loads and displays aggregated counts within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a user, I should be able to upload documents like CPR certificates, TB tests, background checks, and licenses so staff compliance files are stored in one place.
Description of User Story: The Document Storage upload feature provides a centralized repository for storing staff compliance documents including CPR certificates, TB test results, background check reports, professional licenses, and other required documentation. The upload interface is accessible from each staff profile under a dedicated Documents tab, displaying an "Upload Document" button that opens a modal with fields for document type dropdown (CPR Certificate, TB Test, Background Check, License, Other), document title, effective date, expiry date if applicable, and a file upload area supporting drag-and-drop as well as browse functionality. The system accepts common file formats including PDF, JPG, PNG, and DOCX with a configurable maximum file size. Upon successful upload, the document appears in a categorized document list organized by document type, showing the file name, upload date, effective date, expiry date, and uploaded-by user information. The feature integrates with the credentialing module to automatically link license documents to their corresponding credential records.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the user uploads a file exceeding the maximum allowed size (e.g., 10 MB), Then the system rejects the upload and displays an error message specifying the file size limit.
2. If the user selects "Other" as the document type, Then a custom type text field appears requiring the user to specify the document category.
3. If the user attempts to upload a file in an unsupported format (e.g., .exe, .bat), Then the system blocks the upload and displays an error listing the accepted file formats.
4. If the user does not enter an expiry date for a document type that typically expires (e.g., CPR Certificate), Then the system displays a warning that the document may require expiry tracking but allows the save.
5. If the user uploads a document with the same title and type as an existing document for the same staff member, Then the system warns about the duplicate and offers to replace the existing version or keep both.
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: User uploads a CPR certificate PDF with title "CPR Cert 2026", effective date, and expiry date; the document appears in the Documents tab under CPR Certificate category with correct metadata.
2. Positive Functional: User uploads a TB test result as a JPG image; the system stores it and displays a thumbnail preview in the document list.
3. Positive Functional: User uploads a background check report and it appears in the document list with the upload date and uploader name recorded.
4. Positive Functional: User uploads a license document and links it to the corresponding credential record in the Credentialing module.
5. Negative: User attempts to upload a 15 MB file when the limit is 10 MB; system displays "File exceeds maximum size of 10 MB" error.
6. Negative: User tries to upload an .exe file; system rejects with "Unsupported file format. Accepted formats: PDF, JPG, PNG, DOCX".
7. Negative: User attempts to save the upload form without attaching a file; system displays a validation error requiring a file to be attached.
8. UI Scenario: The Documents tab displays documents grouped by category with collapsible sections for each document type (CPR Certificate, TB Test, Background Check, License, Other).
9. UI Scenario: The upload modal features a drag-and-drop zone with a dashed border, file browse button, and previews the selected file name before upload.
10. UI Scenario: Each document row in the list shows file icon, title, document type, upload date, effective date, expiry date, and uploaded-by in a clean table layout.
11. Edge Case: User uploads a document with a title containing special characters (e.g., "CPR Cert #2 - Renewal (2026)"); system accepts and displays the title correctly.
12. Edge Case: User uploads a 0 KB empty file; system rejects the upload with an error indicating the file is empty.
13. Security: Only users with "document_upload" permission can upload documents; users with "document_view" can see the document list but cannot upload or delete files.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Uploading a 5 MB PDF document and saving the metadata completes within 3 seconds.
2. The Documents tab loads and displays up to 50 documents per staff member within 2 seconds, including category grouping and metadata.

User Story: As a user, I should be able to view and download staff documents so they can be accessed whenever needed.
Description of User Story: The Document Viewer and Download feature enables users to access, preview, and download staff compliance documents stored in the centralized repository. From the Documents tab on a staff profile, users can click on any document to open it in an in-app document viewer that supports PDF rendering and image display without requiring external applications. The viewer includes zoom controls, page navigation for multi-page PDFs, and a full-screen mode for detailed review. A prominent "Download" button allows users to save a local copy of the document, and a "Download All" option enables bulk download of all documents for a staff member as a compressed ZIP file. The document list provides sorting options by upload date, document type, and expiry date, along with a search bar for locating specific documents by title or type. Each document entry shows the last accessed date and download count for audit tracking purposes.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the user clicks on an image document (JPG, PNG), Then the viewer displays the image with zoom and rotation controls instead of PDF page navigation.
2. If the user attempts to download a document that has been deleted or archived since the page loaded, Then the system displays an error message indicating the document is no longer available.
3. If the "Download All" action is triggered for a staff member with no documents, Then the system displays a message indicating there are no documents to download.
4. If the user clicks on a DOCX file, Then the system offers a download option since in-app rendering is not supported for Word documents.
5. If the document list contains more than 20 entries, Then the system implements pagination with page controls at the bottom of the list.
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: User clicks on a multi-page PDF license document, the in-app viewer opens, and the user navigates through all pages using page controls.
2. Positive Functional: User clicks "Download" on a CPR certificate and the PDF file downloads to their device with the correct file name.
3. Positive Functional: User clicks "Download All" for a staff member with 10 documents and receives a ZIP file containing all 10 documents.
4. Positive Functional: User sorts the document list by upload date in descending order and sees the most recently uploaded document at the top.
5. Negative: User attempts to open a document that has been corrupted; the viewer displays an error message with an option to download the raw file.
6. Negative: User clicks "Download All" for a staff member with no uploaded documents; system shows "No documents available for download".
7. Negative: User searches for a document with a title that does not exist; the document list shows "No documents found".
8. UI Scenario: The in-app document viewer displays a toolbar with zoom in, zoom out, page forward, page back, full-screen, and download buttons.
9. UI Scenario: The document list table shows columns for document title, type, upload date, expiry date, uploaded by, and action buttons (View, Download) in a consistent layout.
10. UI Scenario: The "Download All" button is positioned at the top of the Documents tab next to the search bar and is clearly labeled.
11. Edge Case: User opens a 100-page PDF in the viewer; the viewer loads the first page immediately and lazy-loads subsequent pages for smooth navigation.
12. Edge Case: User initiates "Download All" for a staff member with 50 documents totaling 200 MB; the system shows a progress indicator during ZIP generation.
13. Security: Only users with "document_view" permission can view and download documents; unauthorized users cannot access the Documents tab or trigger downloads.
Performance Scenarios:
1. The in-app document viewer opens and renders the first page of a 10-page PDF within 2 seconds.
2. Download All ZIP generation for 20 documents totaling 50 MB completes within 10 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add a Case Manager Details
Description of User Story: The Add Case Manager screen allows providers to create new case manager records within the system by capturing all essential professional and contact details. The form includes fields for first name, last name, email address, phone number, agency affiliation, assigned region or territory, specialization area, and NPI number if applicable. A profile photo upload area is available at the top of the form for adding a professional headshot. The agency affiliation field is linked to the Case Management Agency module, allowing selection from a dropdown of registered agencies or manual entry for unregistered agencies. The form footer contains "Cancel" and "Save" buttons, where Save validates all required fields including email format and phone number format before persisting the record. Upon successful creation, the system redirects to the Case Manager list view with a confirmation toast notification.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the user enters an email address that already exists in the system for another case manager, Then the system displays a duplicate email warning and prevents the save.
2. If the user leaves the required first name or last name field empty and clicks Save, Then the system highlights the empty fields and displays validation messages.
3. If the user types an agency name that does not match any registered agency, Then the system allows manual entry and tags the record with "Unregistered Agency" for follow-up.
4. If the user enters an NPI number, Then the system validates the format (10-digit numeric) before allowing the save.
5. If the user uploads a profile photo in an unsupported format, Then the system rejects the upload and displays the accepted image formats (JPG, PNG).
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: Provider fills in all required fields (first name, last name, email, phone, agency), selects a region, uploads a photo, clicks Save, and the case manager record is created with a success message.
2. Positive Functional: Provider creates a case manager with an NPI number; the system validates the 10-digit format and stores it correctly.
3. Positive Functional: Provider selects a registered agency from the dropdown and the agency contact details auto-populate on the form.
4. Positive Functional: Provider creates a case manager without an NPI (optional field) and the record is saved successfully.
5. Negative: Provider attempts to save with the email field empty; system displays "Email is required" validation error.
6. Negative: Provider enters an invalid email format (e.g., "john@"); system displays "Please enter a valid email address" error.
7. Negative: Provider enters a phone number with letters; system rejects with a phone format validation error.
8. UI Scenario: The Add Case Manager form displays the profile photo upload area at the top, followed by personal details fields, agency dropdown, region selector, and Save/Cancel buttons in the footer.
9. UI Scenario: Required fields are marked with asterisk indicators and field labels are clearly aligned with their input controls.
10. UI Scenario: The agency affiliation dropdown supports type-ahead search, filtering the agency list as the user types.
11. Edge Case: Provider creates a case manager and immediately navigates back to add another; the form resets to empty state without retaining previous entries.
12. Edge Case: Provider enters a first name and last name with leading/trailing spaces; the system trims whitespace before saving.
13. Security: Only users with "case_manager_manage" permission can access the Add Case Manager form; providers without this permission do not see the Add button.
Performance Scenarios:
1. The Add Case Manager form loads within 1.5 seconds, including the agency dropdown populated with up to 100 registered agencies.
2. Saving a new case manager record with photo upload completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to edit a Case Manager Details
Description of User Story: The Edit Case Manager screen allows providers to modify existing case manager records by updating their professional and contact information. The form opens in edit mode with all current data pre-populated including first name, last name, email, phone number, agency affiliation, assigned region, specialization, NPI number, and profile photo. Users can modify any field and the system tracks all changes in an audit log visible at the bottom of the form, recording the previous value, new value, timestamp, and the user who made the modification. The Save action validates all modified fields using the same validation rules as the Add form, and upon successful update, the user remains on the case manager detail page with a success confirmation. If the agency affiliation is changed, the system updates all linked client assignments to reflect the new agency relationship.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the user changes the email to one that already belongs to another case manager, Then the system displays a duplicate email error and blocks the save.
2. If the user clears a required field (e.g., first name) and clicks Save, Then the system highlights the field and displays a validation error.
3. If the user modifies the NPI number to an invalid format, Then the system displays a format validation error before allowing the save.
4. If the user changes the assigned region, Then the system logs the region change in the audit trail and updates the case manager visibility in region-filtered views.
5. If the user attempts to save without making any changes, Then the system displays a message indicating no modifications were detected and does not create an audit entry.
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: Provider opens a case manager record in edit mode, changes the phone number, clicks Save, and the record updates with the new phone number and an audit log entry.
2. Positive Functional: Provider changes the agency affiliation from "Agency A" to "Agency B", saves, and all linked client assignments reflect the new agency.
3. Positive Functional: Provider updates the specialization field and views the audit log showing the previous and new specialization values with the timestamp.
4. Positive Functional: Provider replaces the profile photo with a new image; the updated photo displays on the detail page after saving.
5. Negative: Provider changes the email to an invalid format; system displays a validation error and blocks the save.
6. Negative: Provider clears the last name field and clicks Save; system shows "Last name is required" validation error.
7. Negative: Provider enters a 5-digit NPI number; system rejects with "NPI must be a 10-digit number" error.
8. UI Scenario: The edit form pre-populates all fields with current data and displays editable inputs with the current values visible.
9. UI Scenario: The audit log section at the bottom of the form displays entries in a table with columns for date, field changed, old value, new value, and modified by.
10. UI Scenario: The Save and Cancel buttons are prominently displayed in the form footer, with Save as the primary action.
11. Edge Case: Two providers edit the same case manager record simultaneously; the system detects the conflict and warns the second provider that the record has been updated since they opened it.
12. Edge Case: Provider edits a case manager whose agency has been deactivated since the record was last viewed; the system warns about the inactive agency status.
13. Security: Only users with "case_manager_edit" permission can access edit mode; users with view-only access see the detail page without the Edit button.
Performance Scenarios:
1. The Edit Case Manager form loads with pre-populated data and audit history within 2 seconds for records with up to 30 audit entries.
2. Saving updated case manager details and refreshing the detail page completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to view list of Case Managers
Description of User Story: The Case Manager List screen presents a comprehensive table view of all case managers registered in the system, enabling providers to browse, review, and navigate to individual case manager profiles. The list displays key columns including case manager name with profile photo thumbnail, email address, phone number, agency affiliation, assigned region, status (Active/Inactive), and the number of assigned clients. The table supports column-based sorting by clicking on column headers, allowing providers to sort by name, agency, region, or client count. Pagination controls at the bottom of the table allow navigation through large datasets with configurable rows per page (10, 25, 50). A summary bar at the top shows the total count of case managers and a breakdown by status (Active vs Inactive), providing a quick overview of the workforce.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If there are no case managers registered in the system, Then the list displays a "No case managers found" message with an "Add Case Manager" button.
2. If a case manager has no profile photo, Then the name column displays a default avatar with the case manager initials.
3. If the case manager list exceeds 100 entries, Then the system defaults to 25 rows per page and displays pagination controls.
4. If a case manager status is "Inactive", Then their row is styled with a muted color and the status badge displays in grey.
5. If the Provider navigates to the list while a background data sync is in progress, Then the system displays the cached data with a refresh indicator.
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: Provider navigates to the Case Manager list and sees 30 case managers displayed in a table with all columns populated correctly.
2. Positive Functional: Provider sorts the list by agency affiliation and case managers are grouped alphabetically by their agency name.
3. Positive Functional: Provider clicks on a case manager row and is navigated to the detail page showing full profile information.
4. Positive Functional: Provider views the summary bar showing "Total: 30 | Active: 25 | Inactive: 5".
5. Negative: Provider accesses the Case Manager list when no case managers exist; system shows "No case managers found" with an option to add one.
6. Negative: Provider with insufficient permissions attempts to access the Case Manager list; system redirects to an access denied page.
7. Negative: Provider sorts by a column and reverses the sort order; the table correctly toggles between ascending and descending.
8. UI Scenario: The Case Manager list table displays profile photo thumbnails in the first column, aligned left of the case manager name, with consistent row heights.
9. UI Scenario: Pagination controls at the bottom show page numbers, previous/next buttons, and a rows-per-page dropdown.
10. UI Scenario: The summary bar at the top displays total count and status breakdown with Active in green and Inactive in grey badges.
11. Edge Case: A case manager is assigned to zero clients; the client count column displays "0" and the row remains visible in the list.
12. Edge Case: Two case managers have identical names; both appear in the list and are distinguishable by their email or agency affiliation.
13. Security: Only users with "case_manager_view" or higher permission can access the Case Manager list; unauthorized users are redirected to the dashboard.
Performance Scenarios:
1. The Case Manager list loads and renders within 2 seconds for up to 200 case manager records, including photo thumbnails and client counts.
2. Sorting the table by any column completes within 1 second without a full page reload.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to search & filter Case Manager
Description of User Story: The Case Manager Search and Filter feature provides advanced querying capabilities on the Case Manager List screen, allowing providers to quickly locate specific case managers using text search and multi-criteria filters. A search bar at the top of the list supports real-time text search by case manager name, email, phone number, or NPI number, filtering the table as the user types. Adjacent to the search bar, filter dropdowns allow narrowing results by agency affiliation, assigned region, status (Active/Inactive/All), and specialization area. Multiple filters can be applied simultaneously and the results update dynamically to reflect the combined criteria. Active filters are displayed as removable chips below the filter bar, and a "Clear All Filters" button resets all criteria to the default view. The filtered result count updates in real time next to the search bar to indicate how many case managers match the current criteria.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the search query returns no matching case managers, Then the table displays "No results found" with a suggestion to adjust the search or filter criteria.
2. If the user applies the "Inactive" status filter and no inactive case managers exist, Then the table displays "No inactive case managers found".
3. If the user removes a single filter chip, Then the results update to reflect the remaining active filters without resetting all criteria.
4. If the user types fewer than 2 characters in the search bar, Then the system waits until at least 2 characters are entered before initiating the filter to reduce unnecessary processing.
5. If the agency filter dropdown contains more than 20 agencies, Then the dropdown includes a type-ahead search to help locate the desired agency quickly.
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: Provider types "Smith" in the search bar and the list filters to show two case managers with "Smith" in their name.
2. Positive Functional: Provider selects "Region North" from the region filter and "Active" from the status filter; the list shows only active case managers in the North region.
3. Positive Functional: Provider applies an agency filter, sees the filter chip displayed below the filter bar, clicks the remove icon on the chip, and the agency filter is cleared while other filters remain.
4. Positive Functional: Provider clicks "Clear All Filters" after applying three filters; the full unfiltered list is restored with the result count updated.
5. Negative: Provider searches for "XYZABC" which matches no case manager; the table shows "No results found" message.
6. Negative: Provider applies conflicting filters (e.g., a specific agency and a region where that agency has no case managers); the table correctly shows zero results.
7. Negative: Provider types a single character in the search bar; the system does not filter until at least two characters are entered.
8. UI Scenario: The search bar displays a magnifying glass icon and placeholder text "Search by name, email, phone, or NPI" at the top of the list.
9. UI Scenario: Filter dropdowns for agency, region, status, and specialization are aligned horizontally next to the search bar with clear labels.
10. UI Scenario: Active filter chips are displayed as pill-shaped badges below the filter bar with the filter name and a close icon for removal.
11. Edge Case: Provider pastes a full email address into the search bar; the system handles the paste event and filters results immediately.
12. Edge Case: Provider applies all available filters simultaneously and the combined criteria result in exactly one case manager; the list displays the single result with correct pagination showing "1 of 1".
13. Security: Only users with "case_manager_view" permission can access the search and filter functionality; the filter controls are hidden for unauthorized users.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Real-time search filtering returns results within 500 milliseconds for a case manager list of up to 500 records.
2. Applying or removing a filter dropdown updates the table results within 1 second.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to add a case management agency
Description of User Story: The Add Case Management Agency screen allows providers to register new case management agencies in the system, establishing organizational entities that case managers can be affiliated with. The form captures the agency name, primary contact person, contact email, contact phone number, physical address (street, city, state, ZIP code), agency type or classification, license or registration number, and an optional notes field for additional details. The address section supports auto-complete functionality powered by address lookup to reduce manual entry errors. A "Save" button validates all required fields including email format, phone format, and address completeness before creating the agency record. Upon successful creation, the new agency becomes available in the agency affiliation dropdown throughout the Case Manager module. The form also includes a toggle for marking the agency as active or inactive at the time of creation.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the user enters an agency name that already exists in the system, Then the system displays a duplicate warning and asks the user to confirm or cancel the creation.
2. If the user leaves the agency name field empty and clicks Save, Then the system highlights the field and displays a validation error requiring the agency name.
3. If the user enters an invalid email format for the contact email, Then the system displays a format validation error on the email field.
4. If the auto-complete address lookup fails due to network issues, Then the system allows manual address entry and displays a note that auto-complete is unavailable.
5. If the user enters a license number that matches an existing agency, Then the system warns about the potential duplicate and requires confirmation to proceed.
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: Provider fills in agency name "Care Solutions Inc.", primary contact, email, phone, full address, and license number, clicks Save, and the agency is created successfully.
2. Positive Functional: Provider starts typing an address and selects a suggestion from the auto-complete dropdown; the address fields are populated automatically.
3. Positive Functional: Provider creates a new agency and verifies it appears in the Case Manager agency affiliation dropdown immediately.
4. Positive Functional: Provider creates an agency with the status toggle set to "Inactive"; the agency is saved with inactive status and displayed with a grey badge in the agency list.
5. Negative: Provider attempts to save without entering the agency name; system displays "Agency name is required" validation error.
6. Negative: Provider enters an invalid phone number format (e.g., "123"); system displays a phone format validation error.
7. Negative: Provider enters a duplicate agency name that matches an existing record; system warns about the duplicate and requires confirmation.
8. UI Scenario: The form layout displays agency details fields at the top, address section in the middle with auto-complete, and status toggle and Save/Cancel buttons in the footer.
9. UI Scenario: Required field indicators (asterisks) are visible on mandatory fields including agency name, contact email, and phone.
10. UI Scenario: The address auto-complete dropdown appears below the street address field as the user types, with matching suggestions highlighted.
11. Edge Case: Provider enters an address that auto-complete cannot resolve (e.g., a new construction address); the system accepts the manually entered address without validation failure.
12. Edge Case: Provider creates an agency with the notes field containing 2000 characters; the system stores the full text without truncation.
13. Security: Only users with "agency_manage" permission can access the Add Agency form; providers without this permission do not see the Add Agency button.
Performance Scenarios:
1. The Add Case Management Agency form loads within 1.5 seconds, including address auto-complete initialization.
2. Saving a new agency record and making it available in the Case Manager dropdown completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to view a list of all case management agencies
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to view a comprehensive list of all case management agencies that have been added to the Nemi Care system. The list displays key agency details such as agency name, contact person, phone number, email, address, and status (active/inactive) in a tabular format. Each row includes action icons for editing agency details. The list supports pagination for managing large datasets and displays the total count of agencies at the top. This is essential for Providers to quickly reference and manage their relationships with case management agencies that coordinate Medicaid referrals, resident assessments, and state-mandated services.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider navigates to the Case Management Agency section, Then the system displays a table listing all agencies with name, contact, phone, email, and status columns.
2. If the list contains more than 10 entries, Then pagination controls are shown. Else if the list has 10 or fewer entries, Then all entries are displayed on a single page without pagination.
3. If no agencies exist in the system, Then an empty state message with a prompt to add an agency is displayed.
4. If the Provider clicks on an agency row, Then the agency details are expanded or a detail view is opened.
5. If the data is still loading, Then a loading spinner is displayed. Else if the data has loaded, Then the table is rendered with all agency records.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify the agency list page loads and displays all added agencies in tabular format.
2. Verify each agency row shows name, contact person, phone, email, and status.
3. Verify pagination works correctly when navigating between pages.
4. Verify the total count of agencies is displayed at the top of the list.

Negative:
5. Verify empty state message is displayed when no agencies exist.
6. Verify the page handles API failure gracefully with an error message.

UI Scenarios:
7. Verify table headers are properly aligned and labeled.
8. Verify action icons (edit, view) are visible and clickable on each row.
9. Verify loading spinner is shown while data is being fetched.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify list displays correctly with 100+ agencies.
11. Verify behavior when agency name contains special characters or long text.

Security:
12. Verify only authorized users with Settings access can view the agency list.
13. Verify the API endpoint requires authentication token.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the agency list loads within 3 seconds for up to 100 agencies.
2. Verify pagination navigation responds within 1 second.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to edit case management agency details
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to modify existing case management agency information in the Nemi Care system. When the Provider clicks the edit icon on an agency row, an edit form is displayed pre-populated with the current agency details including name, contact person, phone, email, address, and status. The Provider can update any field and save the changes. All field validations are enforced on save, and a success confirmation is displayed upon successful update. This ensures that agency records remain accurate and up-to-date as contacts change or agency information needs correction.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider clicks the edit icon on an agency, Then the edit form opens with all current details pre-populated.
2. If the Provider modifies valid fields and clicks Save, Then the changes are persisted and a success message is shown. Else if validation fails, Then the form highlights the invalid fields with error messages.
3. If the Provider leaves the agency name empty, Then a "Agency name is required" error is displayed and the form does not submit.
4. If the Provider clicks Cancel, Then the form closes without saving any changes.
5. If the Provider enters an invalid email format, Then a "Please enter a valid email address" error is shown.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify clicking edit opens the form with pre-populated agency data.
2. Verify updating agency name and saving reflects the new name in the list.
3. Verify updating contact phone number saves correctly.
4. Verify success message "Agency updated successfully" is shown after save.

Negative:
5. Verify validation error when agency name is cleared and saved.
6. Verify validation error for invalid email format.
7. Verify validation error for invalid phone number format.
8. Verify cancel discards all unsaved changes.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify edit form fields match the current agency data exactly.
10. Verify Save and Cancel buttons are visible and properly styled.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify editing agency with very long name (200+ characters).
12. Verify concurrent edit by two users on the same agency.

Security:
13. Verify only users with edit permissions can access the edit form.
14. Verify XSS input in agency name is sanitized.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify edit form opens within 2 seconds after clicking the edit icon.
2. Verify save operation completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to search & filter Case Management agency
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to quickly locate specific case management agencies from the list using search and filter functionality. A search bar at the top of the agency list allows the Provider to type an agency name, contact person, or other keyword to instantly filter results. Additional filter options may include status (active/inactive). The search results update in real-time as the Provider types, narrowing down the list to matching agencies. This is critical for facilities working with many agencies to quickly find the right contact for Medicaid referrals or case coordination.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider enters text in the search bar, Then the agency list filters in real-time to show only matching results.
2. If the search text matches no agencies, Then a "No results found" message is displayed.
3. If the Provider selects "Active" from the status filter, Then only active agencies are shown. Else if "Inactive" is selected, Then only inactive agencies are shown.
4. If the Provider clears the search field, Then the full unfiltered list is restored.
5. If both search text and status filter are applied, Then only agencies matching both criteria are displayed.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify typing a partial agency name filters the list correctly.
2. Verify search by contact person name returns matching agencies.
3. Verify status filter "Active" shows only active agencies.
4. Verify clearing search restores the full list.

Negative:
5. Verify "No results found" message for non-matching search terms.
6. Verify search with only spaces does not trigger API calls.

UI Scenarios:
7. Verify search bar has placeholder text (e.g., "Search agencies...").
8. Verify filter dropdown displays available filter options.
9. Verify search results update in real-time as user types.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify search with special characters (e.g., "&", apostrophes).
11. Verify search behavior with very long input (500+ characters).

Security:
12. Verify search input is sanitized to prevent injection attacks.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify search results render within 500ms of typing.
2. Verify filter application completes within 1 second.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to add rooms with room type, floor number and service type
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to add new rooms to the facility configuration in the Nemi Care system. The add room form includes fields for room number, room type (Private, Semi-Private, Shared), floor number, service type (ADH, ALF, Memory Care, Independent Living), bed count, and room status (Available, Occupied, Maintenance). Upon saving, the new room appears in the room list and becomes available for resident room assignments during admission. This is essential for facility management to maintain an accurate digital representation of their physical space and capacity for occupancy tracking and billing.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider clicks "Add Room", Then the add room form opens with empty fields for room number, room type, floor, service type, bed count, and status.
2. If all required fields are valid and the Provider clicks Save, Then the room is created and added to the room list. Else if any required field is empty, Then the form shows validation errors for the missing fields.
3. If the room number already exists in the system, Then a "Room number already exists" error is displayed and the form does not submit.
4. If the Provider clicks Cancel, Then the form closes without creating a room.
5. If the Provider selects a service type, Then the room type dropdown filters to show only applicable room types for that service.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify adding a room with all valid fields creates the room successfully.
2. Verify the new room appears in the room list after creation.
3. Verify success message "Room added successfully" is displayed.
4. Verify room type dropdown shows Private, Semi-Private, Shared options.

Negative:
5. Verify validation error for empty room number.
6. Verify validation error for duplicate room number.
7. Verify form does not submit when service type is not selected.
8. Verify validation error for negative or zero bed count.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify Add Room form layout with all labeled fields.
10. Verify dropdowns for room type, service type, and status are functional.
11. Verify Save and Cancel buttons are visible.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify adding a room with floor number 0 or very high floor (e.g., 99).
13. Verify room number with alphanumeric characters (e.g., "A-101").

Security:
14. Verify only admin/provider users can add rooms.
15. Verify input fields are sanitized against XSS.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the Add Room form opens within 2 seconds.
2. Verify room creation saves within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to edit room details
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to modify existing room configuration details in the Nemi Care system. When the Provider clicks the edit icon on a room row, the edit form opens pre-populated with the current room details including room number, room type, floor, service type, bed count, and status. The Provider can update any field and save the changes with validation enforced. This allows facilities to reflect physical changes like renovations, service type reassignments, or status updates (e.g., marking a room as under maintenance) without needing to delete and re-create room entries.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider clicks edit on a room, Then the form opens with all current room details pre-populated.
2. If the Provider updates fields with valid data and clicks Save, Then changes are saved and success message is shown. Else if validation fails, Then error messages are displayed.
3. If the updated room number conflicts with an existing room, Then a "Room number already exists" error prevents saving.
4. If the Provider changes status to "Occupied" with no assigned resident, Then a warning is displayed.
5. If the Provider clicks Cancel, Then the form closes without any changes.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify editing room type from Private to Semi-Private saves correctly.
2. Verify updating floor number reflects in the room list.
3. Verify changing room status to Maintenance updates the room's availability.

Negative:
4. Verify validation error when room number is changed to a duplicate.
5. Verify validation error when required fields are cleared.
6. Verify cancel discards all unsaved changes.

UI Scenarios:
7. Verify edit form pre-populates all current room data correctly.
8. Verify Save and Cancel buttons are properly styled.

Edge Cases:
9. Verify editing a room that has an active resident assignment shows appropriate warnings.
10. Verify concurrent edit scenario on the same room.

Security:
11. Verify only users with room management permissions can edit rooms.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify edit form loads within 2 seconds.
2. Verify save operation completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As staff, I should be able to update room occupancy to occupied or vacant
Description of User Story: This feature enables facility staff to manually update the occupancy status of any room in the Assisted Living Facility to either "Occupied" or "Vacant". Staff can navigate to a specific room and toggle its occupancy status based on resident move-in or move-out events. When a room is marked as vacant, it becomes available for new resident assignments and any previously linked resident association is cleared. When a room is marked as occupied, it should be linked to a resident record. The system maintains an audit trail of all occupancy status changes including the timestamp and the staff member who performed the update. This ensures accurate real-time tracking of room availability across the facility.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the room status is changed from "Occupied" to "Vacant", Then the resident association must be cleared and the room must appear in the available pool.

2. If the room status is changed from "Vacant" to "Occupied", Then the system must require a resident to be linked before saving.

3. If the staff tries to set a room to "Occupied" without selecting a resident, Then an error message must be displayed preventing the save.

4. If the occupancy update is successful, Then the room list must refresh to reflect the new status.
   Else if the update fails due to a network or server error, Then the system must display an error and revert the status to its previous value.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that staff can update a room (e.g., "Room 105") from "Occupied" to "Vacant" and the status change is reflected on the dashboard.
2. Verify that staff can update a room from "Vacant" to "Occupied" by linking a resident (e.g., "John Doe") and the update is confirmed.
3. Verify that staff can view the audit log after a status change and the entry is recorded with correct timestamp and staff name.
4. Verify that after marking a room vacant, it appears in the available rooms filter.

Negative:
5. Verify that staff attempting to mark a room as "Occupied" without selecting a resident is blocked by the system.
6. Verify that staff attempting to mark a room as "Vacant" that is already vacant sees an appropriate message.
7. Verify that a network disconnection during occupancy update is handled gracefully and the room status is not corrupted.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the occupancy status toggle or dropdown displays correct options and current state.
9. Verify that the confirmation message appears with correct details after a successful update.
10. Verify that the room card color changes immediately after status update on the dashboard.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that staff rapidly toggling occupancy status multiple times results in the system processing only the final state correctly.
12. Verify that two staff members updating the same room occupancy simultaneously from different sessions are handled without data corruption.

Security:
13. Verify that only authorized staff with occupancy management permissions can update room status.
14. Verify that the occupancy update API validates authentication and rejects tampered requests.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that room occupancy status update should save and reflect on the UI within 2 seconds.
Verify that audit log retrieval for a room with 1000+ historical entries should load within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to view list of Room details
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to view a comprehensive list of all configured rooms in the facility. The room list displays key details in a tabular format including room number, room type, floor number, service type, bed count, current occupancy status, and assigned resident name (if occupied). The list supports pagination and shows the total room count. This provides facility administrators with a real-time view of room inventory, helping them manage occupancy, plan admissions, and identify available rooms for new residents across different service programs.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider navigates to Room & Bed Configuration, Then the system displays a table of all rooms with their details.
2. If a room is currently occupied, Then the assigned resident name is displayed in the row. Else if the room is available, Then the resident column shows "—" or is blank.
3. If no rooms exist, Then an empty state message is shown with a prompt to add rooms.
4. If the list has more entries than the page size, Then pagination controls are shown.
5. If the data is loading, Then a loading indicator is displayed.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify room list displays all configured rooms.
2. Verify each row shows room number, type, floor, service type, bed count, status.
3. Verify occupied rooms show assigned resident name.
4. Verify total room count is displayed.

Negative:
5. Verify empty state when no rooms are configured.
6. Verify error handling when API fails to load room data.

UI Scenarios:
7. Verify table column headers are properly labeled.
8. Verify status indicators use color coding (green=Available, red=Occupied, yellow=Maintenance).
9. Verify pagination controls work correctly.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify list with 500+ rooms loads and paginates correctly.
11. Verify rooms across multiple floors and service types display correctly.

Security:
12. Verify only authorized users can view room configuration.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify room list loads within 3 seconds for up to 200 rooms.
2. Verify pagination navigation responds within 1 second.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to Search & filter the rooms
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to search and filter the room list to quickly locate specific rooms. A search bar allows keyword-based search by room number, and filter dropdowns allow filtering by room type, floor number, service type, and status. Filters can be combined with search for precise results. Real-time filtering narrows the list as criteria are selected. This is critical for large facilities with many rooms across multiple floors and service types where quickly identifying available rooms or specific room details is essential for admissions and operations.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider types in the search bar, Then rooms are filtered by room number in real-time.
2. If the Provider selects "Private" from room type filter, Then only private rooms are shown. Else if "Semi-Private" is selected, Then only semi-private rooms are shown.
3. If multiple filters are combined, Then results match all applied criteria simultaneously.
4. If the Provider clicks "Clear Filters", Then all filters are removed and the full list is restored.
5. If no rooms match the criteria, Then "No rooms found" message is displayed.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify search by room number filters correctly.
2. Verify filter by room type works.
3. Verify filter by floor number works.
4. Verify filter by service type works.
5. Verify filter by status (Available/Occupied/Maintenance) works.

Negative:
6. Verify "No rooms found" for non-matching criteria.
7. Verify empty search does not break the list.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify search bar and filter dropdowns are properly positioned.
9. Verify clear filter button resets all applied filters.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify combined search + filter returns accurate results.
11. Verify search with special characters in room number.

Security:
12. Verify search input is sanitized.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify search results render within 500ms.
2. Verify filter application completes within 1 second.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to create rate card templates with room types, pricing tiers, and additional services so customized rate cards can be sent to leads.
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to create reusable rate card templates that define pricing for different room types, care levels, and additional services offered by the facility. The template builder allows the Provider to specify room type (Private, Semi-Private), base monthly rate, care level tiers (Level 1-5 with increasing rates), and add-on services (laundry, meals, therapy, personal care, social activities) with individual pricing. Once created, these templates can be attached to leads during the sales process, sent via email, and used as the basis for billing configuration during admission. This is essential for standardizing facility pricing, enabling consistent rate communication to prospective residents and their families.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider clicks "Create Rate Card", Then the template builder opens with sections for template name, room types, care tiers, and services.
2. If all required fields are filled and Save is clicked, Then the template is created and added to the list. Else if required fields are missing, Then validation errors are shown.
3. If the Provider enters a negative or zero rate, Then a "Rate must be a positive number" error is displayed.
4. If the Provider adds multiple room types, Then each room type can have its own base rate and care tier pricing.
5. If the Provider clicks Cancel, Then the form closes without creating a template.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify creating a rate card template with all required fields.
2. Verify adding multiple room types with different base rates.
3. Verify adding care level tiers (Level 1-5) with incremental pricing.
4. Verify adding additional services with individual prices.
5. Verify the template appears in the list after creation.

Negative:
6. Verify validation error for empty template name.
7. Verify validation error for negative rate values.
8. Verify template cannot be saved without at least one room type.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify template builder layout with clear sections for rooms, tiers, services.
10. Verify add/remove buttons for room types and services.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify creating a template with maximum number of services (20+).
12. Verify rate values with decimal precision (e.g., $5,499.99).

Security:
13. Verify only admin users can create rate card templates.
14. Verify pricing data is transmitted securely.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify template builder loads within 2 seconds.
2. Verify template save completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to view all rate card templates
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to view a list of all rate card templates created in the system. The list displays template name, associated room types, number of care tiers, creation date, last modified date, and status (active/inactive) in a tabular format. Each row provides quick access to view full template details or edit the template. This allows the Provider to review existing pricing structures, select appropriate templates for leads, and ensure pricing consistency across the facility.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider navigates to Rate Card Management, Then the system displays all rate card templates in a list.
2. If templates exist, Then each row shows name, room types, tiers, dates, and status. Else if no templates exist, Then an empty state message is shown.
3. If the Provider clicks on a template, Then the full details are displayed.
4. If the list has multiple templates, Then they are sorted by last modified date (newest first).
5. If the Provider clicks "Create Rate Card", Then the template builder opens.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify template list loads with all created templates.
2. Verify each row displays correct template details.
3. Verify clicking a template shows full pricing breakdown.

Negative:
4. Verify empty state when no templates exist.
5. Verify error handling for API failure.

UI Scenarios:
6. Verify table columns are properly labeled and aligned.
7. Verify sorting by last modified date.

Edge Cases:
8. Verify list with 50+ templates loads correctly.

Security:
9. Verify only authorized users can view rate card templates.
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify template list loads within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to edit rate card pricing at the facility level
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to modify existing rate card template pricing at the facility level. When the Provider clicks edit on a template, the template builder opens pre-populated with current pricing for all room types, care tiers, and additional services. The Provider can update any rate, add or remove room types, adjust care tier pricing, and modify service costs. Changes are saved and immediately reflected when the template is next used for leads or billing. This allows facilities to adjust pricing for market changes, annual rate increases, or service modifications without creating entirely new templates.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider clicks edit on a template, Then the editor opens with all current pricing pre-populated.
2. If the Provider updates rates and clicks Save, Then changes are persisted. Else if validation fails, Then errors are shown.
3. If the Provider adds a new tier, Then it is included in the template on save.
4. If the Provider removes a service, Then it is excluded from the template on save.
5. If the Provider clicks Cancel, Then no changes are saved.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify editing room type rate saves correctly.
2. Verify adding a new care tier to existing template.
3. Verify removing an additional service.
4. Verify updating service pricing.

Negative:
5. Verify validation for negative rate values.
6. Verify cancel discards changes.

UI Scenarios:
7. Verify editor pre-populates all current pricing.
8. Verify add/remove controls for tiers and services.

Edge Cases:
9. Verify editing a template currently in use by active leads.
10. Verify rate changes with very large values (e.g., $99,999.99).

Security:
11. Verify only admin users can edit rate card pricing.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify rate card editor loads within 2 seconds.
2. Verify save operation completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to create discount codes with either a percentage or fixed amount and set a time-bound expiration
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to create promotional discount codes that can be applied to resident billing. The discount creation form allows specifying a unique discount code, discount type (percentage or fixed amount), discount value, applicable service types, start date, expiration date, and optional usage limits. Percentage discounts reduce the total by a calculated percentage while fixed-amount discounts subtract a specific dollar amount. Time-bound expirations automatically deactivate the discount after the set date. This enables facilities to offer move-in specials, seasonal promotions, or negotiated rate reductions while maintaining pricing governance and audit trails.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider clicks "Add Discount", Then the discount creation form opens with fields for code, type, value, dates, and limits.
2. If the Provider selects "Percentage" type and enters a value, Then the discount is applied as a percentage reduction. Else if "Fixed Amount" is selected, Then the exact dollar amount is deducted.
3. If the expiration date has passed, Then the discount code is automatically deactivated and cannot be applied.
4. If the discount code already exists, Then a "Discount code already exists" error prevents creation.
5. If the Provider enters a percentage value greater than 100, Then a "Percentage cannot exceed 100%" error is shown.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify creating a percentage-based discount code.
2. Verify creating a fixed-amount discount code.
3. Verify expiration date auto-deactivates the discount.
4. Verify discount code appears in the active discounts list after creation.

Negative:
5. Verify validation for duplicate discount codes.
6. Verify validation for percentage > 100.
7. Verify validation for negative discount value.
8. Verify validation for expiration date before start date.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify discount type toggle between Percentage and Fixed Amount.
10. Verify date pickers for start and expiration dates.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify discount with same start and expiration date (single-day discount).
12. Verify discount with no usage limit (unlimited uses).

Security:
13. Verify only admin users can create discount codes.
14. Verify discount code cannot be guessed (sufficient length/complexity).
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify discount creation saves within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to view all active discounts with their assigned residents, expiration dates
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to view a comprehensive list of all active discount codes in the system along with their usage details. The list displays discount code, type (percentage/fixed), value, assigned residents, expiration date, usage count, and status in a tabular format. Each row allows the Provider to see which residents are currently benefiting from a discount and when the discount will expire. This provides visibility into active promotions, helps track discount utilization, and ensures discounts are properly managed before they expire or need renewal.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider navigates to the discounts section, Then all active discounts are displayed in a table.
2. If a discount has assigned residents, Then their names are shown. Else if no residents are assigned, Then "No residents" is displayed.
3. If a discount expires within 7 days, Then it is highlighted with a warning indicator.
4. If no active discounts exist, Then an empty state message is shown.
5. If the Provider clicks a discount row, Then detailed usage and assignment information is displayed.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify active discounts list loads with all active codes.
2. Verify assigned resident names are displayed for each discount.
3. Verify expiration dates are correctly displayed.
4. Verify near-expiry discounts are highlighted.

Negative:
5. Verify empty state when no active discounts exist.
6. Verify expired discounts do not appear in the active list.

UI Scenarios:
7. Verify table columns are properly labeled.
8. Verify warning highlighting for near-expiry discounts.

Edge Cases:
9. Verify discount assigned to 50+ residents displays correctly.
10. Verify discount expiring today is still shown as active.

Security:
11. Verify only authorized users can view discount details.
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify discount list loads within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to deactivate a discount code
Description of User Story: This story allows the Provider to deactivate an existing discount code within the Rate Card Management section of Settings. When a discount code is no longer valid or needs to be retired, the Provider can select the deactivate option on an active discount entry. The system prompts for confirmation before proceeding with deactivation to prevent accidental changes. Once deactivated, the discount code is marked as inactive and can no longer be applied to any resident billing or rate card calculations. The deactivated discount remains visible in the list for audit and historical reference but is clearly distinguished from active discounts. This functionality ensures Providers maintain accurate and current discount configurations for their facility billing.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider clicks Deactivate on an active discount code, Then a confirmation dialog is displayed. Else if the discount is already inactive, Then the Deactivate option is disabled or hidden.
2. If the Provider confirms the deactivation, Then the discount code status is updated to Inactive and a success message is shown. Else if the Provider cancels, Then no changes are made and the discount remains active.
3. If the discount code is currently assigned to residents, Then a warning message listing affected residents is shown before allowing deactivation. Else if no residents are assigned, Then deactivation proceeds with standard confirmation.
4. If the deactivation API call fails, Then an error message "Failed to deactivate discount code. Please try again." is displayed and the discount remains active.
5. If the Provider searches or filters the discount list after deactivation, Then the deactivated discount appears under inactive filters only.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify clicking Deactivate on an active discount code shows a confirmation dialog.
2. Verify confirming deactivation changes the discount status to Inactive.
3. Verify success message "Discount code deactivated successfully" is displayed after deactivation.
4. Verify the deactivated discount code is no longer available for new resident assignments.

Negative:
5. Verify cancelling the confirmation dialog does not deactivate the discount.
6. Verify appropriate error message when the deactivation API call fails.
7. Verify the Deactivate option is not available for already inactive discount codes.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify the confirmation dialog displays the discount code name and current details.
9. Verify the Inactive status badge is visually distinct from the Active badge.
10. Verify the Deactivate button is styled as a warning action.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify deactivation behavior when the discount is assigned to 50+ active residents.
12. Verify behavior when two users attempt to deactivate the same discount simultaneously.

Security:
13. Verify only authorized users with Rate Card Management permissions can deactivate discounts.
14. Verify the deactivation API endpoint requires a valid authentication token.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the deactivation confirmation dialog opens within 1 second.
2. Verify the deactivation operation completes and reflects in the list within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add Absence code
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to add a new Absence Code in the Settings section of Nemi Care. Absence codes are used to categorize and track resident absences from the facility such as hospital visits, home leave, or therapeutic outings. The Provider fills in an add form with details including the absence code name, code identifier, description, and applicable billing impact. Validation ensures all required fields are completed and that duplicate codes cannot be created. Upon successful addition, the new absence code appears in the absence code list and becomes available for selection when recording resident absences across the system.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider clicks the Add button, Then the add absence code form is displayed with empty fields.
2. If all required fields are filled with valid data and Save is clicked, Then the absence code is created and a success message is shown. Else if required fields are missing, Then validation errors are displayed for each missing field.
3. If the Provider enters a code identifier that already exists, Then a duplicate error message is displayed and the form does not submit.
4. If the Provider clicks Cancel, Then the form closes without saving any data.
5. If the save API call fails due to a server error, Then an error message "Failed to add absence code. Please try again." is displayed.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify clicking Add opens the absence code form with empty fields.
2. Verify entering valid data and saving creates the absence code successfully.
3. Verify the newly added absence code appears in the absence code list.
4. Verify success message "Absence code added successfully" is displayed after creation.

Negative:
5. Verify validation error when code name is left empty.
6. Verify validation error when a duplicate code identifier is entered.
7. Verify validation error when description exceeds maximum character limit.
8. Verify error message when the save API call fails.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify the add form layout is properly aligned with labeled fields.
10. Verify Save and Cancel buttons are visible and properly styled.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify adding an absence code with special characters in the name.
12. Verify form behavior when the user double-clicks the Save button rapidly.

Security:
13. Verify only authorized Providers can access the add absence code form.
14. Verify XSS payloads in the code name and description fields are sanitized.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the add absence code form opens within 2 seconds.
2. Verify the save operation completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to View list of Absence code
Description of User Story: This story allows the Provider to view a comprehensive list of all absence codes configured in the Nemi Care system. The list is displayed in a tabular format showing key details such as code name, code identifier, description, billing impact, and status (active or archived). Each row includes action icons for editing and archiving the absence code. The list supports pagination when the number of entries exceeds the page limit and displays the total count of absence codes. This view serves as the central management hub for absence codes, enabling Providers to quickly review and manage all configured absence categories for their facility.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider navigates to the Absence Code section, Then the system displays a table listing all absence codes with name, identifier, description, billing impact, and status.
2. If the list contains more than 10 entries, Then pagination controls are shown. Else if the list has 10 or fewer entries, Then all entries are displayed on a single page.
3. If no absence codes exist in the system, Then an empty state message with a prompt to add an absence code is displayed.
4. If the data is still loading, Then a loading spinner is displayed. Else if the data has loaded, Then the table is rendered with all absence code records.
5. If the Provider clicks on an absence code row, Then the details are displayed or expanded for that entry.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify the absence code list page loads and displays all configured codes in tabular format.
2. Verify each row shows code name, identifier, description, billing impact, and status.
3. Verify pagination works correctly when navigating between pages.
4. Verify the total count of absence codes is displayed.

Negative:
5. Verify empty state message is displayed when no absence codes exist.
6. Verify the page handles API failure gracefully with an error message.

UI Scenarios:
7. Verify table headers are properly aligned and labeled.
8. Verify action icons (edit, archive) are visible and clickable on each row.
9. Verify loading spinner is shown while data is being fetched.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify list displays correctly with 200+ absence codes.
11. Verify behavior when absence code name contains very long text.

Security:
12. Verify only authorized users with Settings access can view the absence code list.
13. Verify the API endpoint requires a valid authentication token.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the absence code list loads within 3 seconds for up to 100 entries.
2. Verify pagination navigation responds within 1 second.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to Edit Absence codes
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to modify existing absence code details in the Nemi Care system. When the Provider clicks the edit icon on an absence code row, an edit form opens pre-populated with the current absence code information including name, identifier, description, and billing impact. The Provider can update any editable field and save the changes. Field validations are enforced on save to ensure data integrity, and the code identifier may be read-only to prevent breaking existing references. A success confirmation is displayed upon successful update, and the changes are immediately reflected in the absence code list.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider clicks the edit icon on an absence code, Then the edit form opens with all current details pre-populated.
2. If the Provider modifies valid fields and clicks Save, Then the changes are persisted and a success message is shown. Else if validation fails, Then the form highlights invalid fields with error messages.
3. If the Provider leaves the code name empty, Then a "Code name is required" error is displayed and the form does not submit.
4. If the Provider clicks Cancel, Then the form closes without saving any changes.
5. If the save API call fails, Then an error message "Failed to update absence code. Please try again." is displayed and the original data remains unchanged.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify clicking edit opens the form with pre-populated absence code data.
2. Verify updating the code name and saving reflects the new name in the list.
3. Verify updating the description saves correctly.
