4. Verify success message "Absence code updated successfully" is shown after save.

Negative:
5. Verify validation error when code name is cleared and saved.
6. Verify the code identifier field is read-only and cannot be modified.
7. Verify error message when the save API call fails.
8. Verify cancel discards all unsaved changes.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify edit form fields match the current absence code data exactly.
10. Verify Save and Cancel buttons are visible and properly styled.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify editing an absence code with a very long name (200+ characters).
12. Verify concurrent edit by two users on the same absence code.

Security:
13. Verify only users with edit permissions can access the edit form.
14. Verify XSS input in the code name and description fields is sanitized.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the edit form opens within 2 seconds after clicking the edit icon.
2. Verify the save operation completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As  a Provider, I should be able to Archive Absence codes
Description of User Story: This story allows the Provider to archive absence codes that are no longer actively used in the Nemi Care system. Archiving removes the code from the active selection list without permanently deleting it, preserving historical data integrity for past records that reference the code. When the Provider clicks the archive action on an absence code, a confirmation dialog is displayed to prevent accidental archival. Once archived, the code is moved to an archived state and is no longer available for new absence entries. The Provider can still view archived codes through appropriate filters, ensuring full traceability and compliance with record-keeping requirements.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider clicks Archive on an active absence code, Then a confirmation dialog is displayed. Else if the code is already archived, Then the Archive option is disabled.
2. If the Provider confirms the archival, Then the absence code status is updated to Archived and a success message is shown. Else if the Provider cancels, Then no changes are made.
3. If the archived absence code is referenced in existing resident records, Then those records retain the code for historical reference.
4. If the archive API call fails, Then an error message "Failed to archive absence code. Please try again." is displayed and the code remains active.
5. If the Provider filters the list by Archived status, Then only archived absence codes are displayed.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify clicking Archive on an active absence code shows a confirmation dialog.
2. Verify confirming archive changes the absence code status to Archived.
3. Verify success message "Absence code archived successfully" is displayed.
4. Verify the archived code does not appear in active absence code dropdowns.

Negative:
5. Verify cancelling the confirmation dialog does not archive the code.
6. Verify error message when the archive API call fails.
7. Verify the Archive option is not available for already archived codes.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify the confirmation dialog displays the absence code name.
9. Verify the archived status badge is visually distinct from active status.
10. Verify the Archive button is styled as a cautionary action.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify archiving a code that is currently referenced in active resident absence records.
12. Verify behavior when two users attempt to archive the same code simultaneously.

Security:
13. Verify only authorized Providers can archive absence codes.
14. Verify the archive API endpoint requires a valid authentication token.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the archive confirmation dialog opens within 1 second.
2. Verify the archive operation completes and reflects in the list within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to Search and filter the Absence Codes
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to search and filter absence codes in the Settings section to quickly locate specific entries. The search functionality allows the Provider to type a keyword or code name in a search bar, and the list dynamically filters to show matching results. Filter options may include status (active, archived) and billing impact type. The search is case-insensitive and supports partial matching for ease of use. Combining search with filters narrows results further, helping Providers efficiently manage large lists of absence codes. The result count updates dynamically to reflect the number of matching entries.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider types a keyword in the search bar, Then the list filters dynamically to show matching absence codes. Else if the search bar is empty, Then the full list is displayed.
2. If the Provider selects a status filter, Then only absence codes with that status are shown. Else if no filter is applied, Then all statuses are displayed.
3. If both search and filter are applied, Then results matching both criteria are displayed.
4. If no results match the search or filter criteria, Then a "No results found" message is displayed.
5. If the Provider clears all search and filter criteria, Then the complete absence code list is restored.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify typing a keyword filters the absence code list dynamically.
2. Verify the search is case-insensitive and supports partial matching.
3. Verify applying the Active status filter shows only active codes.
4. Verify applying the Archived status filter shows only archived codes.
5. Verify combining search text with status filter narrows results correctly.

Negative:
6. Verify "No results found" message when search term matches no codes.
7. Verify the list restores fully when search bar is cleared.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify the search bar has a placeholder text like "Search absence codes...".
9. Verify filter dropdowns are clearly labeled and functional.
10. Verify the result count updates dynamically as filters are applied.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify search with special characters does not cause errors.
12. Verify search behavior with very long input text (500+ characters).

Security:
13. Verify search input is sanitized to prevent injection attacks.
14. Verify only authenticated users can access the search and filter functionality.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify search results update within 1 second of typing.
2. Verify filter application reflects results within 2 seconds for 200+ entries.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add Activities
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to add a new activity within the Activity Categories section of Settings. Activities represent scheduled programs, events, or engagements offered to residents such as recreation, therapy sessions, educational programs, or social events. The Provider fills in an add form with details including activity name, category, description, duration, and applicable facility or unit. Validation ensures all required fields are completed and duplicate activity names within the same category cannot be created. Upon successful addition, the activity appears in the activity list and becomes available for scheduling in the resident calendar and daily activity modules.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider clicks the Add button, Then the add activity form is displayed with empty fields.
2. If all required fields are filled with valid data and Save is clicked, Then the activity is created and a success message is shown. Else if required fields are missing, Then validation errors are displayed for each missing field.
3. If the Provider enters an activity name that already exists in the same category, Then a duplicate error message is displayed and the form does not submit.
4. If the Provider clicks Cancel, Then the form closes without saving any data.
5. If the save API call fails due to a server error, Then an error message "Failed to add activity. Please try again." is displayed.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify clicking Add opens the activity form with empty fields.
2. Verify entering valid data and saving creates the activity successfully.
3. Verify the newly added activity appears in the activity categories list.
4. Verify success message "Activity added successfully" is displayed after creation.

Negative:
5. Verify validation error when activity name is left empty.
6. Verify validation error when a duplicate activity name is entered in the same category.
7. Verify validation error when description exceeds the maximum character limit.
8. Verify error message when the save API call fails.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify the add form layout is properly aligned with labeled fields.
10. Verify Save and Cancel buttons are visible and properly styled.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify adding an activity with special characters in the name.
12. Verify form behavior when the user double-clicks the Save button rapidly.

Security:
13. Verify only authorized Providers can access the add activity form.
14. Verify XSS payloads in the activity name and description fields are sanitized.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the add activity form opens within 2 seconds.
2. Verify the save operation completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As Provider, I should be able to edit activities
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to modify existing activity details within the Activity Categories section of Settings. When the Provider clicks the edit icon on an activity row, an edit form opens pre-populated with the current activity information including name, category, description, duration, and applicable facility. The Provider can update any editable field and save the changes. Field validations are enforced on save to ensure data integrity, including preventing duplicate names within the same category. A success confirmation is displayed upon successful update, and the changes are immediately reflected in the activity list and any associated scheduling modules.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider clicks the edit icon on an activity, Then the edit form opens with all current details pre-populated.
2. If the Provider modifies valid fields and clicks Save, Then the changes are persisted and a success message is shown. Else if validation fails, Then the form highlights invalid fields with error messages.
3. If the Provider leaves the activity name empty, Then an "Activity name is required" error is displayed and the form does not submit.
4. If the Provider clicks Cancel, Then the form closes without saving any changes.
5. If the save API call fails, Then an error message "Failed to update activity. Please try again." is displayed and the original data remains unchanged.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify clicking edit opens the form with pre-populated activity data.
2. Verify updating the activity name and saving reflects the new name in the list.
3. Verify updating the description and duration saves correctly.
4. Verify success message "Activity updated successfully" is shown after save.

Negative:
5. Verify validation error when activity name is cleared and saved.
6. Verify validation error when changing name to a duplicate within the same category.
7. Verify error message when the save API call fails.
8. Verify cancel discards all unsaved changes.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify edit form fields match the current activity data exactly.
10. Verify Save and Cancel buttons are visible and properly styled.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify editing an activity with a very long name (200+ characters).
12. Verify concurrent edit by two users on the same activity.

Security:
13. Verify only users with edit permissions can access the edit form.
14. Verify XSS input in the activity name and description fields is sanitized.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the edit form opens within 2 seconds after clicking the edit icon.
2. Verify the save operation completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to Archive activities
Description of User Story: This story allows the Provider to archive activities that are no longer offered or relevant within the Activity Categories section of Settings. Archiving removes the activity from the active selection list without permanently deleting it, preserving historical records for past scheduling and attendance data. When the Provider clicks the archive action on an activity, a confirmation dialog is displayed to prevent accidental archival. Once archived, the activity is no longer available for new scheduling but remains viewable through archived filters. This ensures Providers can retire outdated programs while maintaining complete audit trails for compliance and reporting purposes.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider clicks Archive on an active activity, Then a confirmation dialog is displayed. Else if the activity is already archived, Then the Archive option is disabled.
2. If the Provider confirms the archival, Then the activity status is updated to Archived and a success message is shown. Else if the Provider cancels, Then no changes are made.
3. If the archived activity is referenced in existing schedules, Then those schedules retain the activity for historical reference.
4. If the archive API call fails, Then an error message "Failed to archive activity. Please try again." is displayed and the activity remains active.
5. If the Provider filters the list by Archived status, Then only archived activities are displayed.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify clicking Archive on an active activity shows a confirmation dialog.
2. Verify confirming archive changes the activity status to Archived.
3. Verify success message "Activity archived successfully" is displayed.
4. Verify the archived activity does not appear in active scheduling dropdowns.

Negative:
5. Verify cancelling the confirmation dialog does not archive the activity.
6. Verify error message when the archive API call fails.
7. Verify the Archive option is not available for already archived activities.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify the confirmation dialog displays the activity name.
9. Verify the archived status badge is visually distinct from active status.
10. Verify the Archive button is styled as a cautionary action.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify archiving an activity that is currently scheduled for future dates.
12. Verify behavior when two users attempt to archive the same activity simultaneously.

Security:
13. Verify only authorized Providers can archive activities.
14. Verify the archive API endpoint requires a valid authentication token.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the archive confirmation dialog opens within 1 second.
2. Verify the archive operation completes and reflects in the list within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to View  list of Activities
Description of User Story: This story allows the Provider to view a comprehensive list of all activities configured within the Activity Categories section of Settings. The list is displayed in a tabular format showing key details such as activity name, category, description, duration, applicable facility, and status (active or archived). Each row includes action icons for editing and archiving the activity. The list supports pagination when the number of entries exceeds the page limit and displays the total count of activities. This view serves as the central management hub for activity categories, enabling Providers to review all configured programs and events offered to residents at their facility.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider navigates to the Activity Categories section, Then the system displays a table listing all activities with name, category, description, duration, and status.
2. If the list contains more than 10 entries, Then pagination controls are shown. Else if the list has 10 or fewer entries, Then all entries are displayed on a single page.
3. If no activities exist in the system, Then an empty state message with a prompt to add an activity is displayed.
4. If the data is still loading, Then a loading spinner is displayed. Else if the data has loaded, Then the table is rendered with all activity records.
5. If the Provider clicks on an activity row, Then the details are displayed or expanded for that entry.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify the activity list page loads and displays all configured activities in tabular format.
2. Verify each row shows activity name, category, description, duration, and status.
3. Verify pagination works correctly when navigating between pages.
4. Verify the total count of activities is displayed.

Negative:
5. Verify empty state message is displayed when no activities exist.
6. Verify the page handles API failure gracefully with an error message.

UI Scenarios:
7. Verify table headers are properly aligned and labeled.
8. Verify action icons (edit, archive) are visible and clickable on each row.
9. Verify loading spinner is shown while data is being fetched.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify list displays correctly with 200+ activities.
11. Verify behavior when activity name contains very long text.

Security:
12. Verify only authorized users with Settings access can view the activity list.
13. Verify the API endpoint requires a valid authentication token.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the activity list loads within 3 seconds for up to 100 entries.
2. Verify pagination navigation responds within 1 second.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to Search & filter the Activities
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to search and filter activities within the Activity Categories section to quickly locate specific entries. The search functionality allows the Provider to type a keyword or activity name in a search bar, and the list dynamically filters to show matching results. Filter options may include status (active, archived), category, and facility. The search is case-insensitive and supports partial matching for ease of use. Combining search with filters narrows results further, helping Providers efficiently manage large lists of activities. The result count updates dynamically to reflect the number of matching entries.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider types a keyword in the search bar, Then the list filters dynamically to show matching activities. Else if the search bar is empty, Then the full list is displayed.
2. If the Provider selects a status filter, Then only activities with that status are shown. Else if no filter is applied, Then all statuses are displayed.
3. If both search and filter are applied, Then results matching both criteria are displayed.
4. If no results match the search or filter criteria, Then a "No results found" message is displayed.
5. If the Provider clears all search and filter criteria, Then the complete activity list is restored.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify typing a keyword filters the activity list dynamically.
2. Verify the search is case-insensitive and supports partial matching.
3. Verify applying the Active status filter shows only active activities.
4. Verify applying the Archived status filter shows only archived activities.
5. Verify combining search text with status filter narrows results correctly.

Negative:
6. Verify "No results found" message when search term matches no activities.
7. Verify the list restores fully when search bar is cleared.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify the search bar has a placeholder text like "Search activities...".
9. Verify filter dropdowns are clearly labeled and functional.
10. Verify the result count updates dynamically as filters are applied.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify search with special characters does not cause errors.
12. Verify search behavior with very long input text (500+ characters).

Security:
13. Verify search input is sanitized to prevent injection attacks.
14. Verify only authenticated users can access the search and filter functionality.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify search results update within 1 second of typing.
2. Verify filter application reflects results within 2 seconds for 200+ entries.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to add Weejly Menu
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to add a new weekly menu entry within the Weejly Menu section of Settings. Weekly menus define the meal plans and dietary offerings available to residents at the facility, typically organized by day and meal type (breakfast, lunch, dinner, snacks). The Provider fills in an add form with details including menu name, applicable week or date range, meal items per day, dietary category, and any allergen information. Validation ensures all required fields are completed and prevents duplicate menu entries for the same time period. Upon successful addition, the menu appears in the weekly menu list and can be associated with facility meal planning and resident dietary management.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider clicks the Add button, Then the add weekly menu form is displayed with empty fields.
2. If all required fields are filled with valid data and Save is clicked, Then the weekly menu is created and a success message is shown. Else if required fields are missing, Then validation errors are displayed for each missing field.
3. If the Provider enters a date range that overlaps with an existing menu, Then a duplicate error message is displayed and the form does not submit.
4. If the Provider clicks Cancel, Then the form closes without saving any data.
5. If the save API call fails due to a server error, Then an error message "Failed to add weekly menu. Please try again." is displayed.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify clicking Add opens the weekly menu form with empty fields.
2. Verify entering valid data and saving creates the weekly menu successfully.
3. Verify the newly added menu appears in the weekly menu list.
4. Verify success message "Weekly menu added successfully" is displayed after creation.

Negative:
5. Verify validation error when menu name is left empty.
6. Verify validation error when a duplicate date range is entered.
7. Verify validation error when meal items field is left empty.
8. Verify error message when the save API call fails.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify the add form layout is properly aligned with labeled fields.
10. Verify Save and Cancel buttons are visible and properly styled.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify adding a weekly menu with special characters in the name.
12. Verify form behavior when the user double-clicks the Save button rapidly.

Security:
13. Verify only authorized Providers can access the add weekly menu form.
14. Verify XSS payloads in the menu name and meal item fields are sanitized.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the add weekly menu form opens within 2 seconds.
2. Verify the save operation completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to Archive Weejly Menu
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to archive weekly menu items that are no longer in active use within the Nemi Care facility system. When the Provider selects a menu item and triggers the archive action, the item is moved to an archived state and removed from the active menu list. A confirmation dialog is presented before the archive operation to prevent accidental archival. Archived menus are retained in the system for historical reference and can be viewed through a separate archived items view. This functionality helps Providers maintain a clean and current weekly menu list by removing outdated or seasonal menus without permanently deleting them. The system displays a success notification upon successful archival of the menu item.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider clicks the archive icon on a menu item, Then a confirmation dialog is displayed before proceeding with the archive operation.
2. If the Provider confirms the archive action, Then the menu item is moved to archived status and removed from the active list. Else if the Provider cancels, Then no changes are made and the menu remains active.
3. If the archive operation succeeds, Then a success notification "Menu archived successfully" is displayed.
4. If the archive operation fails due to a server error, Then an error message is displayed and the menu item remains in its current state.
5. If the Provider attempts to archive a menu that is currently assigned to an active schedule, Then a warning message is displayed indicating the menu is in use.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify clicking the archive icon on a menu item displays the confirmation dialog.
2. Verify confirming the archive removes the menu item from the active list.
3. Verify success message "Menu archived successfully" is displayed after archiving.
4. Verify archived menu item appears in the archived menus section with the correct date.

Negative:
5. Verify cancelling the confirmation dialog does not archive the menu item.
6. Verify appropriate error message is shown when the archive API call fails.
7. Verify attempting to archive an already archived menu item is not possible.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify the confirmation dialog has clear "Confirm" and "Cancel" buttons.
9. Verify the archive icon is visible and identifiable on each menu row.
10. Verify the success notification auto-dismisses after a few seconds.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify archiving the last remaining menu item in the list displays the empty state.
12. Verify behavior when multiple users attempt to archive the same menu simultaneously.

Security:
13. Verify only authorized Providers with Settings permissions can archive menu items.
14. Verify the archive API endpoint requires a valid authentication token.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the archive operation completes within 2 seconds after confirmation.
2. Verify the active menu list refreshes within 1 second after a successful archive.

4. If the Provider clicks on a menu row, Then the menu details are expanded or a detail view is opened.

4. Verify the total count of menus is displayed at the top of the list.

User Story: As a Provider, I should be able to Search & filter the Weejly Menu
Description of User Story: This story enables the Provider to quickly locate specific weekly menu items from the list using search and filter functionality. A search bar at the top of the Weejly Menu list allows the Provider to type a menu name, meal type, or other keyword to instantly filter results. Additional filter options may include status (active/archived) and meal type categories. The search results update in real-time as the Provider types, narrowing down the list to matching menus. This is critical for facilities managing numerous weekly menus across different meal periods and dietary requirements, enabling quick access to the desired menu configuration.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Provider enters text in the search bar, Then the Weejly Menu list filters in real-time to show only matching results.
2. If the search text matches no menus, Then a "No results found" message is displayed.
3. If the Provider selects "Active" from the status filter, Then only active menus are shown. Else if "Archived" is selected, Then only archived menus are shown.
4. If the Provider clears the search field, Then the full unfiltered list is restored.
5. If both search text and a filter are applied, Then only menus matching both criteria are displayed.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify typing a partial menu name filters the list correctly.
2. Verify search by meal type returns matching menus.
3. Verify status filter "Active" shows only active menus.
4. Verify clearing search restores the full list.

Negative:
5. Verify "No results found" message for non-matching search terms.
6. Verify search with only spaces does not return incorrect results or trigger unnecessary API calls.

UI Scenarios:
7. Verify search bar has placeholder text (e.g., "Search menus...").
8. Verify filter dropdown displays available filter options clearly.
9. Verify search results update in real-time as the user types.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify search with special characters (e.g., "&", apostrophes) is handled properly.
11. Verify search behavior with very long input (500+ characters).

Security:
12. Verify search input is sanitized to prevent SQL injection and XSS attacks.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify search results render within 1 second for a list of 100+ menus.
2. Verify applying a filter refreshes the list within 1 second.

User Story: As a supervisor, I should be able to review and approve employee timesheets before payroll export, with ability to make adjustments and add notes.
Description of User Story: This story enables Supervisors to review and approve employee timesheets before they are exported for payroll processing within the Nemi Care facility system. The supervisor is presented with a list of pending timesheets that require approval, showing employee name, hours worked, shift details, and any flagged discrepancies. The supervisor can approve, reject, or request corrections on each timesheet, and has the ability to make manual adjustments to clock-in/out times with mandatory notes explaining the change. An approval workflow ensures that only reviewed and approved timesheets proceed to payroll export, maintaining accuracy and compliance with labor regulations. This provides a critical quality control checkpoint in the payroll process.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the Supervisor navigates to the approval workflow, Then a list of all pending timesheets is displayed sorted by submission date.
2. If the Supervisor clicks "Approve" on a valid timesheet, Then the timesheet moves to approved status and becomes eligible for payroll export. Else if there are unresolved discrepancies, Then a warning is shown prompting resolution before approval.
3. If the Supervisor modifies a time entry, Then a mandatory notes field is displayed requiring justification. Else if no notes are provided, Then the adjustment cannot be saved.
4. If the Supervisor clicks "Reject", Then a mandatory reason field is presented. Else if no reason is provided, Then the rejection cannot be submitted.
5. If all timesheets in a pay period are approved, Then the payroll export button becomes enabled for that period.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify the pending timesheets list displays all submitted timesheets awaiting approval.
2. Verify clicking on a timesheet opens the detailed view with clock-in/out times and total hours.
3. Verify approving a timesheet changes its status to "Approved" and shows a success message.
4. Verify the supervisor can adjust clock-in/out times with mandatory notes.
5. Verify rejecting a timesheet with a reason returns it to the employee.

Negative:
6. Verify that adjustments cannot be saved without providing notes.
7. Verify that rejection cannot be submitted without a reason.
8. Verify that a timesheet with unresolved discrepancies shows a warning before approval.
9. Verify that an already approved timesheet cannot be approved again.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify the pending timesheets list shows employee name, hours, dates, and status columns.
11. Verify Approve and Reject buttons are clearly visible and styled distinctly.
12. Verify the notes field appears when making adjustments or rejecting.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify behavior when a supervisor attempts to approve a timesheet that was just modified by the employee.
14. Verify handling of timesheets that span across pay periods.
15. Verify behavior when the supervisor modifies time to exceed the maximum allowed shift hours.

Security:
16. Verify only users with Supervisor role can access the approval workflow.
17. Verify all adjustment actions are logged in the audit trail with supervisor identity and timestamp.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the pending timesheets list loads within 3 seconds for up to 200 timesheets.
2. Verify the approve or reject action completes within 2 seconds.
3. Verify the detailed timesheet view renders within 2 seconds when opened.

User Story: As a user, I should be able to add a new payer/insurance Facility with all required details so I can maintain an updated list of accepted insurances.
Description of User Story: This story enables the user to add a new payer or insurance entity to the Nemi Care facility system with all required details such as payer name, payer ID, address, contact information, payer type, and associated plan codes. The add payer form includes all mandatory and optional fields organized in a structured layout, with field-level validations to ensure data integrity. Upon successful submission, the new payer is added to the master payer list and becomes available for selection during patient insurance assignment. A success notification confirms the addition. This functionality is fundamental for facilities to maintain an accurate and comprehensive list of accepted insurance payers for billing and claims processing.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the user clicks "Add New Payer", Then the add payer form is displayed with all required and optional fields.
2. If all required fields are filled with valid data and the user clicks Save, Then the payer is created and added to the master list. Else if required fields are missing, Then validation errors are shown for each missing field.
3. If the payer ID entered already exists in the system, Then a "Payer ID already exists" error is displayed and the form does not submit.
4. If the user clicks Cancel, Then the form closes without saving any data.
5. If the payer is successfully added, Then it becomes immediately available for selection in patient insurance assignment.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify clicking "Add New Payer" opens the add form with all required fields.
2. Verify entering valid data in all fields and saving creates the payer successfully.
3. Verify success message "Payer added successfully" is displayed after creation.
4. Verify the newly added payer appears in the payer list immediately.

Negative:
5. Verify validation errors are shown when required fields are left empty.
6. Verify duplicate payer ID triggers "Payer ID already exists" error.
7. Verify invalid email format in contact field shows validation error.
8. Verify cancel discards all entered data without saving.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify the add form layout is organized with clearly labeled fields.
10. Verify required fields are marked with an asterisk or similar indicator.
11. Verify Save and Cancel buttons are visible and properly styled.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify adding a payer with the maximum allowed character length in all fields.
13. Verify behavior when the network disconnects during form submission.

Security:
14. Verify only authorized users can access the add payer functionality.
15. Verify input fields are sanitized to prevent XSS and SQL injection.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the add payer form loads within 2 seconds.
2. Verify the save operation completes and payer is created within 3 seconds.

Negative:
5. Verify validation error when a required field is cleared and saved.
6. Verify validation error for duplicate payer ID.
7. Verify validation error for invalid contact email format.
8. Verify cancel discards all unsaved changes.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify edit form fields match the current payer data exactly.
10. Verify Save and Cancel buttons are visible and properly styled.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify editing a payer with very long name (200+ characters).
12. Verify concurrent edit by two users on the same payer record.

Security:
13. Verify only users with edit permissions can access the edit form.
14. Verify XSS input in payer name and other fields is sanitized.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the edit form opens within 2 seconds after clicking the edit icon.
2. Verify the save operation completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a user, I should be able to add and manage custom plan codes under each payer so that I can properly map each patient's plan to the correct insurance templates.
Description of User Story: This story enables the user to create and manage custom plan codes associated with each payer in the Nemi Care facility system. Under each payer record, the user can add one or more plan codes with details such as plan code identifier, plan name, plan type, effective date range, and associated billing templates. Existing plan codes can be edited or deactivated as needed. The plan code structure allows the system to accurately map patient insurance plans to the correct payer and billing template during claims submission. This hierarchical payer-plan organization is essential for healthcare billing accuracy and ensures that claims are processed with the correct plan-specific codes and templates.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the user navigates to the Plan Codes section of a payer, Then all existing plan codes for that payer are displayed in a list.
2. If the user clicks "Add Plan Code" and fills in valid data, Then the new plan code is created and associated with the payer. Else if required fields are missing, Then validation errors are displayed.
3. If the plan code identifier already exists under the same payer, Then a "Plan code already exists for this payer" error is displayed.
4. If the user edits a plan code and saves valid changes, Then the updates are persisted and a success message is shown.
5. If the user deactivates a plan code, Then it is marked inactive and no longer available for new patient assignments but remains visible for historical records.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify the plan codes list is displayed under a payer record.
2. Verify clicking "Add Plan Code" opens the form with all required fields.
3. Verify creating a plan code with valid data adds it to the payer.
4. Verify editing an existing plan code saves updates correctly.
5. Verify deactivating a plan code changes its status to inactive.

Negative:
6. Verify validation error for duplicate plan code identifier under the same payer.
7. Verify validation errors when required fields are left empty.
8. Verify invalid effective date range (start date after end date) is rejected.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify plan codes are displayed in a structured list with code, name, type, and status.
10. Verify Add, Edit, and Deactivate actions are accessible from the plan codes section.
11. Verify required fields are clearly marked on the plan code form.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify adding the maximum number of plan codes under a single payer.
13. Verify behavior when a plan code is deactivated while assigned to active patients.

Security:
14. Verify only authorized users can add or manage plan codes.
15. Verify plan code data is transmitted securely over HTTPS.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the plan codes list loads within 2 seconds for a payer with 50+ plan codes.
2. Verify adding a new plan code completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a user, I should be able to enter and store clearinghouse-specific payer IDs so that claims can be routed correctly for electronic submission.
Description of User Story: This story enables the user to enter and store clearinghouse-specific payer identification numbers for each payer in the Nemi Care facility system. Different clearinghouses may use different payer IDs for the same insurance company, and this feature allows the system to store these mappings accurately. The user can add a clearinghouse payer ID by selecting the clearinghouse and entering the corresponding payer ID value. These stored IDs are used during electronic claims submission to route claims to the correct payer through the appropriate clearinghouse. This ensures accurate claim routing and reduces rejections due to incorrect payer identification during electronic billing.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the user navigates to the Clearinghouse IDs section of a payer, Then all stored clearinghouse payer IDs are displayed.
2. If the user adds a new clearinghouse ID with valid data, Then the ID is saved and associated with the payer. Else if required fields are missing, Then validation errors are displayed.
3. If the clearinghouse-payer ID combination already exists, Then a duplicate error is displayed and the entry is not saved.
4. If the user edits a clearinghouse payer ID and saves, Then the updated value is persisted.
5. If the user deletes a clearinghouse payer ID, Then it is removed from the payer record and no longer used for claims routing.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify the clearinghouse IDs section displays existing IDs for a payer.
2. Verify adding a new clearinghouse payer ID with valid data saves correctly.
3. Verify the success message is displayed after saving a clearinghouse ID.
4. Verify editing an existing clearinghouse payer ID updates it correctly.

Negative:
5. Verify duplicate clearinghouse-payer ID combination is rejected with an error message.
6. Verify validation error when clearinghouse is not selected.
7. Verify validation error when payer ID field is left empty.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify the clearinghouse dropdown lists all available clearinghouses.
9. Verify the payer ID field accepts alphanumeric characters.
10. Verify Add, Edit, and Delete actions are visible for each clearinghouse ID entry.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify behavior when a clearinghouse is removed from the system that has stored payer IDs.
12. Verify adding a clearinghouse ID with special characters in the payer ID field.

Security:
13. Verify only authorized users can manage clearinghouse payer IDs.
14. Verify clearinghouse payer ID data is encrypted in transit and at rest.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the clearinghouse IDs section loads within 2 seconds.
2. Verify saving a new clearinghouse payer ID completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a user, I should be able to store payer IDs for multiple clearinghouses (e.g., Change Healthcare, ClaimMD, Availity) to support different submission routes.
Description of User Story: This story enables the user to store multiple clearinghouse-specific payer IDs for a single payer in the Nemi Care facility system, supporting different electronic claims submission routes simultaneously. Healthcare facilities often work with multiple clearinghouses such as Change Healthcare, ClaimMD, and Availity, and the same payer may have different identification numbers at each clearinghouse. This feature allows the user to maintain a comprehensive mapping of payer IDs across all utilized clearinghouses within a single payer record. The system uses the appropriate clearinghouse payer ID based on the selected submission route during claims processing, ensuring correct routing regardless of which clearinghouse is used.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the user adds payer IDs for multiple clearinghouses under the same payer, Then all IDs are stored and displayed in the clearinghouse IDs section.
2. If a claim is submitted through Change Healthcare, Then the system uses the Change Healthcare payer ID. Else if submitted through ClaimMD, Then the ClaimMD payer ID is used.
3. If the user removes one clearinghouse payer ID, Then only that specific mapping is deleted. Else the remaining clearinghouse IDs are unaffected.
4. If no clearinghouse payer ID is configured for a selected submission route, Then the system displays a warning that no payer ID is available for that clearinghouse.
5. If the user edits a specific clearinghouse payer ID, Then only that entry is updated without affecting other clearinghouse mappings.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify a payer can have payer IDs stored for multiple clearinghouses simultaneously.
2. Verify adding a Change Healthcare payer ID and a ClaimMD payer ID under the same payer.
3. Verify all stored clearinghouse IDs are displayed in the payer record.
4. Verify editing one clearinghouse payer ID does not affect others.
5. Verify removing one clearinghouse ID leaves others intact.

Negative:
6. Verify a warning is shown when submitting a claim through a clearinghouse with no configured payer ID.
7. Verify duplicate entries for the same clearinghouse under one payer are rejected.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify each clearinghouse entry shows the clearinghouse name and payer ID clearly.
9. Verify the interface supports adding, editing, and removing individual clearinghouse entries.
10. Verify the clearinghouse selection dropdown lists all supported clearinghouses.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify behavior when all clearinghouse IDs are removed from a payer.
12. Verify handling when a new clearinghouse is added to the system and existing payers need mapping.

Security:
13. Verify only authorized users can manage multiple clearinghouse mappings.
14. Verify audit trail records all changes to clearinghouse payer ID mappings.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify loading a payer record with 10+ clearinghouse IDs completes within 2 seconds.
2. Verify saving a new clearinghouse ID when multiple already exist completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a user, I should be able to mark a payer as active or inactive so I can control which payers appear during insurance selection for patients.
Description of User Story: This story enables the user to toggle the active or inactive status of a payer in the Nemi Care facility system, thereby controlling whether that payer appears in the insurance selection dropdown during patient registration and insurance assignment. When a payer is marked as inactive, it is hidden from active selection lists but remains in the system for historical reference and existing patient records. When a payer is reactivated, it becomes available again in selection lists. A confirmation dialog is presented before changing the status to prevent accidental modifications. This functionality helps facilities manage their accepted insurance list as payer contracts change, ensuring only currently accepted payers are available for new patient assignments.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the user clicks "Mark Inactive" on an active payer and confirms, Then the payer status changes to inactive and it is hidden from selection lists.
2. If the user clicks "Mark Active" on an inactive payer and confirms, Then the payer status changes to active and it appears in selection lists again.
3. If the user cancels the confirmation dialog, Then no status change is made.
4. If a payer is inactive and has existing patient assignments, Then those assignments remain valid. Else new assignments with that payer are not permitted.
5. If the status change fails due to a server error, Then an error message is displayed and the payer status remains unchanged.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify marking an active payer as inactive changes its status and shows a success message.
2. Verify an inactive payer does not appear in insurance selection dropdowns.
3. Verify reactivating an inactive payer makes it available in selection dropdowns again.
4. Verify existing patient assignments remain intact when a payer is marked inactive.

Negative:
5. Verify cancelling the confirmation dialog does not change the payer status.
6. Verify appropriate error message when the status change API fails.
7. Verify an inactive payer cannot be selected for new patient insurance assignments.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify the active/inactive toggle or button is clearly visible on the payer record.
9. Verify the confirmation dialog displays the payer name and requested action.
10. Verify inactive payers are visually distinguished in the payer list (e.g., greyed out or badge).

Edge Cases:
11. Verify behavior when all payers are marked inactive and a user tries to assign insurance.
12. Verify rapid toggling of active/inactive status does not cause data inconsistency.

Security:
13. Verify only authorized users can change payer active/inactive status.
14. Verify status change actions are recorded in the audit log.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the status toggle operation completes within 2 seconds.
2. Verify insurance selection dropdowns load within 2 seconds reflecting the correct active payers.

User Story: As a user, I should be able to search and filter payers by name, payer ID, plan code, or clearinghouse ID so I can quickly find the correct payer record.
Description of User Story: This story enables the user to quickly locate specific payer records from the payer management list using comprehensive search and filter functionality. A search bar at the top of the payer list allows the user to type a payer name, payer ID, plan code, or clearinghouse ID to instantly filter results. Additional filter options include payer status (active/inactive) and payer type. The search results update in real-time as the user types, narrowing down the list to matching payers. This is critical for facilities working with many insurance payers to quickly find the correct payer record for billing, claims processing, or administrative updates without scrolling through extensive lists.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the user enters text in the search bar, Then the payer list filters in real-time to show only matching results across name, payer ID, plan code, and clearinghouse ID.
2. If the search text matches no payers, Then a "No results found" message is displayed.
3. If the user selects "Active" from the status filter, Then only active payers are shown. Else if "Inactive" is selected, Then only inactive payers are shown.
4. If the user clears the search field, Then the full unfiltered payer list is restored.
5. If both search text and a status filter are applied, Then only payers matching both criteria are displayed.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify searching by partial payer name filters the list correctly.
2. Verify searching by payer ID returns the exact matching payer.
3. Verify searching by plan code returns all payers with that plan code.
4. Verify searching by clearinghouse ID returns the corresponding payer.
5. Verify status filter "Active" shows only active payers.

Negative:
6. Verify "No results found" message for non-matching search terms.
7. Verify search with only spaces does not return incorrect results.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify search bar has appropriate placeholder text (e.g., "Search by name, ID, plan code...").
9. Verify filter dropdown displays available filter options.
10. Verify search results update in real-time as the user types.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify search with special characters is handled properly.
12. Verify search behavior with very long input (500+ characters).
13. Verify combined search and filter with no matching results.

Security:
14. Verify search input is sanitized to prevent SQL injection and XSS attacks.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify search results render within 1 second for a list of 500+ payers.
2. Verify applying a filter refreshes the list within 1 second.

User Story: As a user, I should be able to add multiple plans under a single payer so that the system accurately reflects the payer-plan hierarchy.
Description of User Story: This story enables the user to associate multiple insurance plans under a single payer entity in the Nemi Care facility system, accurately reflecting the real-world payer-plan hierarchical structure. Insurance companies typically offer multiple plan types such as HMO, PPO, Medicare Advantage, and Medicaid managed care under the same payer umbrella. The user can add plans with details including plan name, plan code, plan type, coverage details, effective date range, and billing template association. Each plan is linked to its parent payer and can be individually managed. This hierarchy is essential for accurate patient insurance mapping, claims submission, and billing processes where the correct plan under the correct payer must be identified.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the user navigates to the Plans section of a payer, Then all existing plans for that payer are displayed in a list.
2. If the user clicks "Add Plan" and fills in valid data, Then the new plan is created and associated with the payer. Else if required fields are missing, Then validation errors are displayed.
3. If the plan code already exists under the same payer, Then a "Plan code already exists for this payer" error is displayed.
4. If the user adds multiple plans of different types (HMO, PPO), Then all plans are stored and displayed under the payer record.
5. If the user deactivates a plan, Then it is marked inactive and no longer available for new patient assignments but remains for historical reference.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify the plans list is displayed under a payer record.
2. Verify clicking "Add Plan" opens the form with all required fields.
3. Verify creating a plan with valid data adds it under the correct payer.
4. Verify multiple plans of different types can be added under the same payer.
5. Verify each plan displays name, code, type, and status in the list.

Negative:
6. Verify validation error for duplicate plan code under the same payer.
7. Verify validation errors when required fields are left empty.
8. Verify invalid effective date range (start date after end date) is rejected.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify plans are displayed in a structured list under the payer.
10. Verify Add Plan button is visible and accessible within the payer record.
11. Verify plan type dropdown lists all supported types (HMO, PPO, etc.).

Edge Cases:
12. Verify adding a large number of plans (50+) under a single payer.
13. Verify behavior when a plan is deactivated while assigned to active patients.

Security:
14. Verify only authorized users can add or manage plans under payers.
15. Verify plan data input fields are sanitized against injection attacks.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify the plans list loads within 2 seconds for a payer with 50+ plans.
2. Verify adding a new plan completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As a SuperAdmin, I should be able to view the audit log for files we have imported.
Description of User Story: The Data Import Audit Log screen provides SuperAdmins with a chronological record of all file import operations performed in the system. Each audit log entry displays the file name, import type (ICD-10, CPT, etc.), date and time of import, the user who initiated the import, total records processed, and the import status (Success, Partial, Failed). The log table supports sorting by date, file name, or status and includes a search bar and date range filter for narrowing results. A details expansion or drill-down allows the SuperAdmin to view granular information such as the count of records inserted, updated, skipped, or errored for each import. The screen is read-only and serves as a compliance and troubleshooting reference for all bulk data operations.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the SuperAdmin navigates to the audit log and import records exist, Then all entries are displayed in reverse chronological order with the most recent import at the top.
2. If no imports have been performed, Then the system displays an empty state message "No import records found" with guidance on how to perform a data import.
3. If the search query returns no matching entries, Then the system displays "No results match your search" with a clear filter option.
4. If an import entry has a "Failed" status, Then the row is highlighted in red and includes a link to view the error details.
5. If the audit log data fails to load due to a server error, Then the system displays an error message with a Retry button.
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin navigates to the Audit Log page and sees a table listing five recent imports with correct file names, dates, users, record counts, and statuses.
2. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin clicks on a "Partial" status import entry and sees the detail view showing 950 records inserted, 30 skipped, and 20 errored with error descriptions.
3. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin searches for "ICD" in the search bar and the table filters to show only ICD-10 related import entries.
4. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin applies a date range filter for the last 7 days and only imports within that period are displayed.
5. Negative: SuperAdmin navigates to the audit log with no imports performed; system displays "No import records found" empty state.
6. Negative: SuperAdmin searches for a non-existent file name; system displays "No results match your search" with a clear filter option.
7. Negative: Audit log data fails to load due to a timeout; system shows an error message with a Retry button.
8. UI Scenario: The audit log table displays columns for File Name, Import Type, Date/Time, Initiated By, Records Processed, and Status with proper alignment and sortable headers.
9. UI Scenario: Failed import rows are highlighted with a red background or red status badge for quick visual identification.
10. UI Scenario: The date range filter provides a calendar picker with preset options (Today, Last 7 Days, Last 30 Days, Custom Range).
11. Edge Case: An import with zero records processed (empty file upload) displays "0 records" in the Records Processed column with a "No Data" note.
12. Edge Case: SuperAdmin views an audit log entry where the initiating user account has since been deactivated; the entry still shows the original username with a "Deactivated" indicator.
13. Security: Only authenticated SuperAdmins can access the audit log; non-SuperAdmin users attempting to access via URL manipulation are redirected to an access-denied page.
Performance Scenarios:
1. The audit log page loads and renders up to 50 entries within 2 seconds of navigation including all status indicators and filters.
2. Expanding an import entry to view detailed record counts loads within 1 second.

User Story:
As a SuperAdmin, I should be able to bulk upload the below data like
 - ICD-10 codes
 - CPT code
Description of User Story: The Bulk Upload screen allows SuperAdmins to import large datasets of ICD-10 codes and CPT codes into the system through CSV or Excel file uploads. The interface presents a file upload area with drag-and-drop support and a browse button, along with a dropdown selector to specify the data type being imported (ICD-10, CPT). Before processing, the system validates the uploaded file for correct format, required columns, data types, and duplicate entries, displaying a validation summary with counts of valid, duplicate, and invalid records. The SuperAdmin can review the validation results and choose to proceed with the import or cancel. Upon confirmation, the system processes the file in the background and displays a progress indicator, notifying the SuperAdmin upon completion with a summary of records inserted, updated, and skipped.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the SuperAdmin uploads a file with a valid structure and all records pass validation, Then the system enables the "Proceed with Import" button and displays a green validation summary.
2. If the uploaded file contains duplicate entries (codes already in the system), Then the system flags them in the validation summary and allows the SuperAdmin to choose whether to skip or overwrite duplicates.
3. If the uploaded file has an unsupported format (e.g., PDF, DOC), Then the system rejects the upload and displays "Unsupported file format. Please upload a CSV or Excel file."
4. If the uploaded file exceeds the maximum allowed size, Then the system rejects the upload and displays the maximum file size limit.
5. If the import process fails midway due to a server error, Then the system rolls back the partial import, notifies the SuperAdmin of the failure, and logs the error in the audit log.
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin selects "CPT code" data type, uploads a valid CSV with 500 CPT codes, validation passes, and all 500 records are imported successfully.
2. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin uploads an ICD-10 CSV with 100 new codes and 20 duplicates; validation summary shows 100 valid and 20 duplicates; SuperAdmin chooses to skip duplicates and 100 records are imported.
3. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin drags and drops an Excel file onto the upload area; system accepts the file and begins validation.
4. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin uploads a file, reviews validation results showing 5 invalid records with specific row-level errors, fixes the file, and re-uploads successfully.
5. Negative: SuperAdmin uploads a PDF file; system rejects it with "Unsupported file format" error.
6. Negative: SuperAdmin uploads a CSV with missing required columns (e.g., code, description); system displays specific column-level validation errors.
7. Negative: SuperAdmin uploads an empty CSV file (headers only, no data rows); system displays "No data records found in the uploaded file."
8. UI Scenario: The upload area displays a dashed border with a cloud upload icon, "Drag & Drop" text, and a "Browse Files" button centered within the zone.
9. UI Scenario: The validation summary displays a card with color-coded counts: green for valid, yellow for duplicates, and red for invalid records.
10. UI Scenario: The progress indicator shows a percentage bar during import processing with an estimated time remaining.
11. Edge Case: SuperAdmin uploads a CSV with 50,000 records; the system handles the large file with background processing and does not time out.
12. Edge Case: SuperAdmin uploads a file with special characters or unicode in code descriptions; the system imports them correctly without encoding issues.
13. Security: Only authenticated SuperAdmins can access the bulk upload functionality; the upload endpoint validates authentication and authorization before accepting any file.
Performance Scenarios:
1. File validation for a CSV with up to 10,000 records completes within 5 seconds and displays the validation summary.
2. Bulk import of 5,000 valid records completes background processing within 30 seconds with real-time progress updates.

User Story: As a SuperAdmin, I should be able to download CSV template file.
Description of User Story: The Download CSV Template feature allows SuperAdmins to download pre-formatted CSV template files for each supported data import type (ICD-10 codes, CPT codes, and other bulk data categories). The download section is accessible from the Data Import settings page and presents a list of available templates with the data type name, a brief description of expected columns, and a download button for each. Clicking the download button initiates an immediate file download of the CSV template containing the correct column headers, sample data rows for reference, and any formatting instructions in a comments row. The templates ensure that SuperAdmins have the exact file structure required for a successful bulk upload, reducing import errors caused by incorrect formatting.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the SuperAdmin clicks the download button and the template file is available, Then the browser initiates an immediate file download without navigating away from the page.
2. If the template file fails to generate due to a server error, Then the system displays an error notification "Unable to download template. Please try again." with a Retry option.
3. If the SuperAdmin has a pop-up blocker that prevents the download, Then the system provides an alternative direct download link.
4. If a new data type is added to the system but the template is not yet configured, Then that data type appears in the list with a "Template Coming Soon" label and a disabled download button.
5. If the SuperAdmin is on a slow connection, Then the system shows a download progress indicator for larger template files.
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin clicks download for "CPT Code Template" and receives a CSV file with columns Code, Description, Category, and two sample data rows.
2. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin downloads the "ICD-10 Codes Template" and the file contains correct headers with formatting instructions in a comments row.
3. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin downloads multiple templates sequentially; each file downloads correctly with the appropriate name and content.
4. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin opens the downloaded template in Excel and all columns are correctly separated and labeled.
5. Negative: Template download fails due to a server error; system displays an error message with a Retry option.
6. Negative: SuperAdmin clicks download but the browser blocks the download; system provides an alternative direct link.
7. Negative: SuperAdmin attempts to download a template for a data type that is not yet configured; the download button is disabled with a "Coming Soon" label.
8. UI Scenario: Each template entry in the list shows the data type name in bold, a brief description of expected columns below it, and a download icon button aligned to the right.
9. UI Scenario: The download button displays a file icon with a downward arrow, and changes to a loading spinner while the file is being generated.
10. UI Scenario: The templates section has a clear heading "Download Templates" with an informational note explaining the purpose of templates.
11. Edge Case: SuperAdmin downloads a template while offline; the system displays a network error with a prompt to check the connection and retry.
12. Edge Case: SuperAdmin downloads the same template multiple times; the browser handles duplicate file names by appending a number suffix.
13. Security: Template download endpoints require SuperAdmin authentication; unauthenticated requests return a 401 Unauthorized response.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Clicking the download button initiates the file download within 1 second for template files under 1 MB.
2. The templates list renders with all available template entries within 1 second of navigating to the Data Import page.

User Story: As a SuperAdmin, I should be able to view a list of all added CPT codes
Description of User Story: The CPT Code Catalog screen displays a comprehensive list of all CPT (Current Procedural Terminology) codes that have been added to the system, presented in a searchable and sortable table format. Each row shows the CPT code number, description, category or section, fee schedule amount if configured, and active/inactive status. The table supports column sorting by code number, description, or category, and includes a search bar for filtering by code number or keyword. A count indicator at the top displays the total number of CPT codes in the catalog. Pagination controls allow navigation through large datasets, and an export option enables SuperAdmins to download the current catalog view as a CSV file for offline reference.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the catalog contains CPT codes, Then the system displays them in a paginated table with all columns populated and the total count shown.
2. If no CPT codes have been added to the system, Then the system displays an empty state message "No CPT codes found. Use Data Import to add codes." with a link to the bulk upload page.
3. If the search query returns no matching codes, Then the system displays "No CPT codes match your search" with a clear filter option.
4. If a CPT code has an inactive status, Then its row is visually muted with an "Inactive" badge in the status column.
5. If the CPT code data fails to load, Then the system displays an error message with a Retry button.
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin navigates to the CPT Code Catalog and sees a table with 200 CPT codes displaying code number, description, category, fee, and active status.
2. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin searches for "99213" in the search bar and the table filters to show the specific CPT code entry with its description.
3. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin sorts the table by category and sees codes grouped alphabetically by their category section.
4. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin clicks Export and downloads a CSV file containing all CPT codes that match the current filter criteria.
5. Negative: SuperAdmin navigates to the catalog with no CPT codes added; system displays "No CPT codes found" empty state with a link to bulk upload.
6. Negative: SuperAdmin searches for a non-existent code; system displays "No CPT codes match your search" message.
7. Negative: CPT code data fails to load; system shows an error message with a Retry button.
8. UI Scenario: The table displays columns for Code, Description, Category, Fee, and Status with consistent widths and sortable column headers with sort direction indicators.
9. UI Scenario: The search bar is positioned above the table with a magnifying glass icon and placeholder text "Search by code or keyword".
10. UI Scenario: Pagination controls at the bottom show page numbers, previous/next buttons, and a rows-per-page selector.
11. Edge Case: The catalog contains 10,000+ CPT codes; pagination handles the dataset smoothly without degraded table rendering.
12. Edge Case: A CPT code description contains very long text (over 200 characters); the table truncates with an ellipsis and provides a tooltip on hover.
13. Security: Only authenticated SuperAdmins can access the CPT Code Catalog; unauthorized users are redirected to an access-denied page.
Performance Scenarios:
1. The CPT Code Catalog page loads and renders the first page of results (up to 50 codes) within 2 seconds.
2. Search filtering returns results within 500 milliseconds as the SuperAdmin types in the search bar.

User Story: As a SuperAdmin, I should be able to view a list of all added ICD 10 codes
Description of User Story: The ICD-10 Code Catalog screen provides SuperAdmins with a complete listing of all ICD-10 (International Classification of Diseases, 10th Revision) codes stored in the system, displayed in a structured and searchable table. Each row shows the ICD-10 code, description, category or chapter, code type (diagnosis or procedure), and active/inactive status. The table supports sorting by code, description, or category and includes a global search bar for filtering by code number, keyword, or category. A total count indicator at the top displays the number of ICD-10 codes in the system. Pagination handles large volumes of codes, and an export button allows SuperAdmins to download the filtered or full catalog as a CSV file for reporting or offline review.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the catalog contains ICD-10 codes, Then the system displays them in a paginated table with the total count shown at the top.
2. If no ICD-10 codes have been added, Then the system displays an empty state message "No ICD-10 codes found. Use Data Import to add codes." with a link to the bulk upload page.
3. If the search returns no matching codes, Then the system displays "No ICD-10 codes match your search" with a clear filter option.
4. If an ICD-10 code has an inactive status, Then its row appears visually muted with an "Inactive" badge.
5. If the data fails to load due to a server error, Then the system displays an error message with a Retry button.
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin navigates to the ICD-10 Code Catalog and sees a table with 500 codes showing code, description, category, type, and status correctly.
2. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin searches for "E11" and the table filters to show all ICD-10 codes starting with E11 (Type 2 Diabetes related codes).
3. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin sorts the table by code in ascending order and sees codes listed from A00 through Z99 categories.
4. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin clicks Export and receives a CSV file with all ICD-10 codes currently displayed based on the active filter.
5. Negative: SuperAdmin navigates to the catalog with no ICD-10 codes added; system displays "No ICD-10 codes found" empty state with bulk upload link.
6. Negative: SuperAdmin searches for a non-existent code "ZZZZZ"; system displays "No ICD-10 codes match your search" with a clear filter option.
7. Negative: ICD-10 data fails to load due to a network error; system displays an error message with Retry button.
8. UI Scenario: The table displays columns for Code, Description, Category, Type, and Status with proper alignment, sortable headers, and alternating row colors for readability.
9. UI Scenario: The search bar is positioned at the top with a magnifying glass icon and placeholder text "Search by code, description, or category".
10. UI Scenario: Pagination controls at the bottom include page numbers, next/previous buttons, and a configurable rows-per-page dropdown.
11. Edge Case: The catalog contains 70,000+ ICD-10 codes; the system paginates efficiently and search filtering remains responsive.
12. Edge Case: An ICD-10 code description contains special characters or medical abbreviations; the table renders them correctly.
13. Security: Only authenticated SuperAdmins can view the ICD-10 Code Catalog; unauthorized access attempts are denied and logged.
Performance Scenarios:
1. The ICD-10 Code Catalog page loads and renders the first page of results (up to 50 codes) within 2 seconds even with 70,000+ total codes.
2. Search filtering across the full ICD-10 catalog returns results within 1 second of the SuperAdmin typing a search query.

User Story: As a SuperAdmin, I should be able to search or filter any data.
Description of User Story: The Master Management Search and Filter feature provides SuperAdmins with a unified search capability across all master data categories in the system, including CPT codes, ICD-10 codes, payers, staff records, practice details, and other configurable data sets. The interface presents a global search bar at the top of the Master Management section that searches across all categories simultaneously, along with advanced filter options to narrow results by data category, status (active/inactive), date added, and custom attributes. Search results are displayed in a categorized list showing the data type, matching record details, and quick-action links to view or edit each result. The system supports both exact match and partial match (contains) searching to accommodate various lookup needs.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the SuperAdmin enters a search term and matching records exist across multiple categories, Then the system displays results grouped by category with a count for each.
2. If the search term returns no results in any category, Then the system displays "No results found for your search" with suggestions to broaden the search criteria.
3. If the SuperAdmin applies multiple filters simultaneously (category + status + date range), Then the system applies all filters cumulatively and displays only records matching all criteria.
4. If the SuperAdmin clears the search bar, Then the system resets the results view to the default master management landing page.
5. If the search fails due to a server error, Then the system displays an error notification with a Retry option.
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin searches for "99213" and sees results showing the matching CPT code with its description and a link to view details.
2. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin searches for "diabetes" and sees results from ICD-10 codes category showing all diabetes-related codes.
3. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin filters by category "Payers" and status "Active" to see only active payer records.
4. Positive Functional: SuperAdmin clicks on a search result for an ICD-10 code and is navigated to the ICD-10 Code Catalog detail view.
5. Negative: SuperAdmin searches for a random string "xyz123abc" that matches no records; system displays "No results found" with guidance to broaden the search.
6. Negative: SuperAdmin applies conflicting filters (e.g., category "CPT Codes" + date range with no imports); system shows empty results with a suggestion to adjust filters.
7. Negative: Search functionality encounters a server timeout; system displays an error with a Retry button.
8. UI Scenario: The global search bar spans the full width of the Master Management header with a prominent magnifying glass icon and placeholder text "Search across all master data".
9. UI Scenario: Search results are grouped by category with collapsible sections, each showing a count badge (e.g., "CPT Codes (3)").
10. UI Scenario: Advanced filter controls are accessible via a "Filters" button that expands a panel with category dropdown, status toggle, and date range pickers.
11. Edge Case: SuperAdmin searches with a single character; the system requires a minimum of 2 characters and displays a hint message.
12. Edge Case: SuperAdmin searches for a term that matches thousands of records; the system paginates results and displays the most relevant matches first.
13. Security: Search results are restricted to data the SuperAdmin has permission to view; the system does not expose records from restricted categories.
Performance Scenarios:
1. Global search returns categorized results within 2 seconds for queries across all master data categories.
2. Applying or removing filters updates the results within 1 second without a full page reload.

User Story: As a user, I should have the app automatically switch to my local language based on my location so I can use the system comfortably
Description of User Story: The Automatic Language Detection feature detects the user's preferred language based on their geographic location or browser/device language settings and automatically switches the application interface to that language upon login or first access. The system uses the browser's locale settings or IP-based geolocation as the primary detection method and maps the detected locale to the nearest supported language in the system. If the detected language is supported, the entire interface (navigation menus, labels, buttons, tooltips, and static content) renders in that language without requiring manual selection. A language preference indicator in the user profile or header displays the currently active language with an option to override the automatic selection. The user's language preference is persisted in their profile so subsequent logins remember the chosen language.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. If the user's browser language matches a supported language in the system, Then the interface automatically renders in that language on first access.
2. If the user's browser language is not supported (e.g., a rare dialect), Then the system falls back to the closest supported language or defaults to English.
3. If the user manually overrides the detected language in their profile, Then the manual selection takes priority over auto-detection for all future sessions.
4. If the user clears their browser data and accesses the app again, Then the system uses the language preference stored in their user profile on the server.
5. If the geolocation-based detection and browser language detection return conflicting results, Then the browser language setting takes priority as it is more likely to reflect the user's actual preference.
Test Scenarios:
1. Positive Functional: User with browser language set to French logs in for the first time and the entire interface renders in French.
2. Positive Functional: User sees the language indicator showing "Francais" in the header and clicks it to see other available language options.
3. Positive Functional: User overrides auto-detected French to English via the language selector; the interface switches to English immediately and persists the preference.
4. Positive Functional: User logs out and logs back in; the system remembers the manually selected English preference and does not revert to French.
5. Negative: User's browser language is set to an unsupported language (e.g., Swahili); the system defaults to English and shows a prompt to select a preferred language.
6. Negative: Geolocation service is unavailable; the system falls back to browser language detection without errors.
7. Negative: User accesses the app from a shared computer with a different browser language; the system uses the user's stored profile preference over the browser setting.
